definition.en: Basically the inner reality is the creative source of the physical one.
definition.zh: 基本上，内在实相是物质实相的创造性源头。

definition.en: The act of perception itself helps form the perceived event and is a part of it.
definition.zh: 感知行为本身有助于形成被感知的事件，并且是它的一部分。

definition.en: The rockbed reality is the one in which the perceiver is focused.
definition.zh: 基岩实相是感知者聚焦于其中的那个实相。

definition.en: Reality is above all practical, so when you expand your concepts concerning the nature of reality, you are apt then to find yourselves scandalized, appalled, or simply disoriented.
definition.zh: 实相首先是实用的，所以当你扩展关于实相本质的概念时，你很可能会发现自己感到震惊、惊骇，或者干脆迷失方向。

definition.en: Basic reality is not by nature terrifying. It is however vibrant and direct to the extreme, and you must be prepared before you experience such an ultrasonic action.
definition.zh: 基本实相本质上并不可怕。然而它极度充满活力和直接，你必须做好准备才能体验这种超音速般的行动。

explanation: 实相并非单一的固定存在，而是由感知者主动参与构建的多维动态系统。物理世界只是意识聚焦于某一特定频率时呈现的“节目”，而内在意识才是创造物理现实的源头。通过调整意识焦点，人可以感知到通常被忽略的另类事实——这些事实指向一个更深层、更具支持性的内在实相，它比物理假设更优先存在。感知行为本身就在塑造被感知的事件，而非被动接收客观世界。时间与空间并非如人们以为的那样绝对，梦境意识中清晰展现着实相的基本性质。每个现实系统都被概率簇环绕，这些概率可能存在于完全不同的宇宙中，与时空无关。身份既可以是粒子化的个体，也可以如波浪般包含其他意识。扩展对实相的理解会带来实际体验的转变，甚至可能令人震惊或迷失，

quotes.text: If, however, you learn to know yourself better in daily life to become more fully aware even of your earthly life, then you will indeed receive other information that hints of a deeper, more supportive reality, in which physical existence rests. You will find yourself having experiences that do not fit recognized facts. These can add up to an alternate set of facts, pointing toward a different kind of reality, and give evidence for an inner existence that takes precedence over the physical assumptions. A certain kind of discretion and understanding is necessary, however. Basically the inner reality is the creative source of the physical one. Yet to some extent, the physical rules are also inviolate -- at their level.
quotes.translation: 然而，若你在日常生活中学会更好地认识自己，更充分地觉察甚至是你的人世生活，那么你确实会接收到其他信息，这些信息暗示着一个更深层、更具支撑性的实相，物质存在便安驻其中。你会发现自己的经历与公认的事实不符。这些经历可以累积成另一套事实，指向一种不同的实相，并为优先于物质假设的内在存在提供证据。不过，某种程度的谨慎与理解是必要的。基本上，内在实相是物质实相的创造性源头。然而在某种程度上，物质规则在其层面上也是不可侵犯的。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: To some extent the greater expression of consciousness can be experienced under usual waking conditions, but only when a personality is flexible enough and secure enough to alter the focus of consciousness. This way, other unperceived data become available. The unknown reality is not beyond your experience, therefore. Any of your scientific or religious disciplines could benefit from a study of the dreaming consciousness, for there the basic nature of reality exists as clearly as you can perceive it. The inner condition of dreaming is valid. You find yourselves in other times and places because basically neither time nor space exists as you suppose.7
quotes.translation: 在某种程度上，意识的更大表达可以在通常的清醒状态下体验到，但只有当人格足够灵活、足够安全以改变意识的焦点时才能如此。这样一来，其他未被感知的数据就变得可得。因此，未知的实相并非超出你的经验。你们的任何科学或宗教学科都可以从对做梦意识的研究中受益，因为在那里，实相的基本本质如你所感知的那样清晰存在。做梦的内在状态是有效的。你发现自己处于其他时空，因为基本上，时间与空间都不像你假设的那样存在。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Here's Seth from the 750th session, held on June 25, 1975, two months after he finished Volume 2. In it he not only sums up his motives in producing "Unknown" Reality, but comments on another one of his basic ideas that I think it important to stress every so often; this time, perception is involved. "The 'Unknown' Reality was written to give ... individuals glimpses into alternate patterns of reality. It was meant to serve as a map that would lead, not into another objectified universe per se, but into inner roads of consciousness. These inner roads or strands of consciousness bring elements into play so that it becomes possible to realize that the content of a given objectified universe may actually be perceived quite differently. You are part of what you perceive. When you alter the focus of your perception you automatically change the objectified world. It is not simply that you perceive it differently while it remains the same, regardless of your experience. The act of perception itself helps form the perceived event and is a part of it."
quotes.translation: 以下是赛斯在1975年6月25日第750次课上的发言，距他完成第二卷已两个月。在这次课中，他不仅总结了他撰写《未知的实相》的动机，还评论了他的另一个基本观点，我认为这一点需要时常强调；这次涉及的是感知。"《未知的实相》的写作是为了让……个体瞥见实相的其他模式。它旨在作为一张地图，不是导向另一个客观化的宇宙本身，而是导向意识的内在道路。这些内在道路或意识线索将各种元素带入运作，从而使人有可能认识到，一个特定客观化宇宙的内容实际上可能被以截然不同的方式感知。你是你所感知的一部分。当你改变感知的焦点时，你自动改变了客观化的世界。并非仅仅是你以不同方式感知它，而它保持不变，与你的体验无关。感知行为本身有助于形成被感知的事件，并且是它的一部分。"
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Every so often I've thought of averaging Jane's dictation time for Seth Speaks and Personal Reality in the same way, but haven't done so. I'm somewhat puzzled to note, however, that her very short working times for the Seth books seem to be either ignored or taken for granted by practically everyone -- or, perhaps, those factors just aren't understood in terms of ordinary linear time. Maybe I'm alone in my interest here, for even Jane doesn't express any great curiosity about the time she has invested in the Seth material; she just delivers it. But given her abilities, I think her speed of production is a close physical approach to, or translation of, Seth's idea that basically all exists at once -- that really there is no time, and that the Seth books, for example, are "there" to be had in final form for just the tuning in. (In Section 3 of this volume, Note 2 for Session 692 contains information on another way by which we can move closer to Seth's idea of simultaneity from our physical reality, but that method grows out of material not discussed here.)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: In my other books I used many accepted ideas as a springboard to lead readers into other levels of understanding. Here, I wish to make it clear that this book4 will initiate a journey in which it may seem that the familiar is left far behind. Yet when I am finished, I hope you will discover that the known reality is even more precious, more "real," because you will find it illuminated both within and without by the rich fabric of an "unknown" reality now seen emerging from the most intimate portions of daily life. Give us a moment. (Pause at 11:35.) Your concepts of personhood are now limiting you personally and en masse, and yet your religions, metaphysics, histories, and even your sciences are hinged upon your ideas of who and what you are. Your psychologies do not explain your own reality to you. They cannot contain your experience. Your religions do not explain your greater reality, and your sciences leave you [just] as ignorant about the nature of the universe in which you dwell.
quotes.translation: 在我其他的书中，我使用了许多公认的观念作为跳板，引导读者进入其他理解层次。在这里，我希望明确说明，这本书将开启一段旅程，在这段旅程中，熟悉的事物似乎被远远抛在身后。然而，当我完成时，我希望你会发现已知的实相更加珍贵、更加"真实"，因为你会看到它内外都被"未知"实相的丰富织锦所照亮，而这织锦如今正从日常生活最私密的角落浮现。给我们一点时间。（11:35停顿。）你们关于人格的概念现在正限制着你们个人和整体，然而你们的宗教、形而上学、历史，甚至你们的科学，都依赖于你们对自己是谁、是什么的看法。你们的心理学没有向你们解释你们自己的实相。它们无法容纳你们的经验。你们的宗教没有解释你们更大的实相，而你们的科学让你们对自己所居住的宇宙的本质同样无知。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: The rockbed reality is the one in which the perceiver is focused. From that standpoint all others would seem peripheral. Taking that for granted, however, any given reality system will be surrounded by its probability clusters. These can almost be thought of as satellites. Time and space need not be connected, however -- that is, the attractions that exist between a reality and any given probability cluster may have nothing to do with time and space at all. The closest probability satellite to any given reality may, for example, be in an entirely different universe altogether. (Pause.) In that regard, you may find brethren more or less like yourselves outside of your own universe -- as you think of it -- rather than inside it. You imagine your universe as extending outward in space (and backwards in time). You think of it as an exteriorized manifestation, expanding perhaps, but in an exterior rather than an interior fashion.1
quotes.translation: 基岩实相是感知者聚焦其中的那个实相。从这个立场来看，所有其他实相似乎都是边缘的。然而，以此为前提，任何给定的实相系统都会被其概率簇所包围。这些几乎可以被视为卫星。然而，时间和空间不一定相连——也就是说，存在于一个实相与任何给定概率簇之间的吸引力可能完全与时间和空间无关。例如，最接近任何给定实相的概率卫星可能完全处于一个完全不同的宇宙中。（停顿。）在这方面，你可能会发现与你或多或少相似的同胞存在于你的宇宙之外——如你所想的那样——而不是之内。你想象你的宇宙在空间中向外延伸（并在时间中向后延伸）。你把它视为一种外在化的显化，或许在扩张，但以外在而非内在的方式。

quotes.text: You know where physical reality is, then, on the dial of your multidimensional television set. While focused within that living scene you can learn to travel through it, leaving the "surface" picture intact and whole. In a way you program yourself, going about your daily duties as conscientiously and effectively as usual -- but at the same time you discover an additional portion of your own reality. This does not diminish the physical self. Instead, in fact, it enriches it. You discover that the psyche has many aspects. While fully enjoying the physical aspect you find that there is some part of you left over, so to speak; and that part can travel into other realities. It can also then return, bringing the physically oriented self "snapshots" of its journeys. These snapshots are usually interpreted in terms of your home program. Otherwise, they might make no sense to the physical self.
quotes.translation: 那么，在你的多维电视机的刻度盘上，你知道物质实相在哪里。当聚焦于那个活生生的场景中时，你可以学会穿越它，保持"表面"画面完整无损。在某种程度上，你对自己进行编程，像往常一样认真而有效地履行日常职责——但同时，你发现了自己实相的一个额外部分。这并不会削弱物质自我。事实上，反而会丰富它。你发现心灵有许多面向。在充分享受物质面向的同时，你发现你身上还有一部分剩余，可以这么说；而那一部分可以旅行到其他实相。它也可以随后返回，为面向物质的自我带来其旅程的"快照"。这些快照通常根据你的家庭程序来解读。否则，它们可能对物质自我毫无意义。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The development freed Ruburt from many old limitations, and allowed him to at last have practical experience with the unknown reality in intimate terms. Ruburt's library does exist as surely as this room does. It also exists as unsurely as this room. It is one thing to be theoretically convinced that other worlds exist, and to take a certain comfort and joy from the idea. It is quite another thing to find yourself in such an environment, and to feel the worlds coincide. Reality is above all practical, so when you expand your concepts concerning the nature of reality, you are apt then to find yourselves scandalized, appalled, or simply disoriented. So in this work I am presenting you not only with probabilities as conjecture, but, often, showing you how such probabilities affect your daily lives, and giving examples of the ways in which Ruburt's and Joseph's lives have been so touched.
quotes.translation: 这一发展使鲁柏摆脱了许多旧有的限制，并最终让他得以亲密地体验未知实相。鲁柏的图书馆确实存在，就像这个房间一样确定无疑；它也同样不确定地存在，就像这个房间一样。理论上相信其他世界的存在，并从中获得某种安慰与喜悦是一回事；而发现自己置身于这样的环境中，感受到诸世界交汇重叠，则完全是另一回事。实相首先是实用的，因此当你扩展对实相本质的概念时，你很可能会发现自己感到震惊、骇然，或仅仅是迷失方向。所以在这部作品中，我不仅将可能性作为推测呈现给你们，还常常展示这些可能性如何影响你们的日常生活，并举例说明鲁柏和约瑟的生活是如何被如此触动的。

quotes.text: I have thus far stayed clear of many important and vital subjects, involving mass realities, because first of all the importance of the individual was to be stressed, and his power to form his private events. Only when the private nature of reality was emphasized sufficiently would I be ready to show how the magnification of individual reality combines and enlarges to form vast mass reactions -- such as, say, the initiation of an obviously new historical and cultural period; the rise or overthrow of governments; the birth of a new religion that sweeps all others before it; mass conversions; mass murders in the form of wars; the sudden sweep of deadly epidemics; the scourge of earthquakes, floods, or other disasters; the inexplicable appearance of periods of great art or architecture or technology.
quotes.translation: 迄今为止，我避免涉及许多涉及大众实相的重要且关键的主题，因为首先要强调个体的重要性，以及他塑造个人事件的能力。只有当个体实相的本质得到充分强调之后，我才会准备展示个体实相的放大如何结合并扩展，形成庞大的大众反应——例如，一个显然全新的历史和文化时期的开启；政府的兴衰；一种新宗教的诞生，它将所有其他宗教扫荡殆尽；大规模皈依；以战争形式出现的大规模屠杀；致命流行病的突然蔓延；地震、洪水或其他灾难的祸害；伟大艺术、建筑或技术时期的莫名出现。

quotes.text: (From the 775th session for May 10, 1976:) Bits of your consciousnesses,34 Joseph and Ruburt, go out through these books. I am not speaking symbolically. Those portions will mix with the consciousness of others. Portions of your intent and purpose become theirs. My own psychological reality is not particleized. My identity includes the identities of many others, and they each operate in their own fashion. In those terms I am a wave formation. More specifically, however, and to a lesser degree, each physical person operates partially as a particleized being, and partially in terms of a wave.35 But identity, being itself inviolate, is on the other hand everchanging -- and there is, in the larger framework of reality, no contradiction.
quotes.translation: （摘自1976年5月10日第775节：）约瑟和鲁柏，你们意识的一部分通过这些书散发出去。我并非象征性地说话。那些部分将与他人的意识混合。你们意图和目的的一部分变成了他们的。我自己的心理实相并非粒子化的。我的身份包含了许多其他身份，它们各自以自身的方式运作。用那些术语来说，我是一种波的形式。然而，更具体地说，在较小程度上，每个物质人部分地作为粒子化的存在运作，部分地以波的形式运作。但身份本身是神圣不可侵犯的，另一方面又是不断变化的——在实相的更大框架内，这并不矛盾。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: I do not mean to mislead you. Basic reality is not by nature terrifying. It is however vibrant and direct to the extreme, and you must be prepared before you experience such an ultrasonic action. A direct experience of reality involves complete use of all the inner senses to an integrated cognizance field. A very weak analogy can be found if you imagine at one time hearing the most exciting and moving music imaginable, while simultaneously smelling the strongest but not necessarily unpleasant odor, viewing the most emotionally-charged scene while feeling intense and vivid bodily sensations. In normal life you even turn down outer-sense stimuli for the sake of simplicity, and to enable you to focus upon those stimuli close at hand.
quotes.translation: 我无意误导你们。基本实相本质上并不可怕。然而，它极其充满活力且直接，你们在体验如此超音速的行动之前必须做好准备。对实相的直接体验涉及所有内在感官的完全运用，形成一个整合的认知场。一个非常薄弱的类比是，如果你想象同时听到所能想象的最激动人心、最动人的音乐，同时闻到最强烈但不一定令人不快的气味，观看最充满情感的场景，同时感受强烈而鲜明的身体感觉。在正常生活中，为了简单起见，你甚至会调低外在感官的刺激，以便能够专注于手边的刺激。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: As a rule you have enough difficulty dealing with the day's occurrences, much less next week's, and so in the sequence of events the reality of probable actions is usually hidden from your view. (Pause.) This more complex reality is an ever-existing property of your personal creaturehood. Beside this, in your terms you exist as a creature more than once. In each of your "reincarnational" existences you are faced with the same relationship with probabilities. In each case, also, the nature of the conscious mind sets up its own territory-of-identity (with hyphens) that it regards as its own. This provides a clear focus in which "present" action can be considered. These incarnations are all simultaneous.
quotes.translation: 通常来说，你们处理当天发生的事情已经足够困难，更不用说下周的了，因此在事件的序列中，可能行动的实相通常隐藏于你们的视野之外。（停顿。）这种更复杂的实相是你们个人存在的一个永恒属性。除此之外，用你们的说法，你们不止一次地作为存在而存在。在你们每一次的"转世"存在中，你们都面临着与可能性的相同关系。在每种情况下，意识心智的本质也建立了它自己视为己有的身份领域（带连字符）。这提供了一个清晰的焦点，在其中"当下"的行动可以被考虑。这些转世都是同时存在的。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: When I say that a person misinterprets sense data, I mean that the fine balance between mind and matter becomes overstrained in one direction. There are, then, certain events that connect the world. Though when everything is said and done these events come from outside of the world's order, nevertheless they appear as constants within it. Their reality is the result of the most precise balancing of forces so that certain mental events appear quite real, and others are peripheral. You have dusk and dawn. If in the middle of the night, and fully awake, you believe it is sunrise in physical terms, and cannot differentiate between your personal reality and the physical one, then that balance is disturbed.
quotes.translation: 当我说一个人误解了感官资料时，我的意思是心智与物质之间的精妙平衡在一个方向上变得过度紧张。那么，存在着某些连接世界的事件。尽管当一切尘埃落定，这些事件来自世界秩序之外，但它们却作为常数出现在其中。它们的实相是力量最精确平衡的结果，使得某些心智事件显得非常真实，而其他事件则处于边缘。你们有黄昏和黎明。如果在午夜时分，完全清醒，你在物质层面上相信那是日出，并且无法区分你的个人实相与物质实相，那么那种平衡就被扰乱了。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: hen he originally gave us the 812th session some 16 months ago, Seth told us it would "be part of a chapter later in the book...." [See my opening notes for Session 814.] The 812th session was held following an encounter Jane and I had with an unexpected visitor, although these excerpts aren't personal at all. They fit in well with material in Mass Events, however -- indeed, as soon as Seth confirmed Jane's headings for Part 3 and Chapter 6, we knew that we'd found the place to present this material. We made our decision without consulting Seth, by the way.
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: In sense terms he would learn little about an orange, though he might be able to isolate its elements, predict where others might be found, theorize about its environment -- but the greater "withinness" of the orange is not found any place inside of its skin either. The seeds are the physical carriers of future oranges, but the blueprints for that reality are what formed the seeds. In such dilemmas you are always brought back to the question of which came first, and begin another merry chase. Because you think in terms of consecutive time, it seems that there must have been a first egg, or seed.1 The blueprints for reality exist, however, in dimensions without such a time sequence.
quotes.translation: 从感官层面来说，他对橙子的了解微乎其微，尽管他或许能分离其元素、预测其他橙子可能出现的位置、对其生长环境提出理论——但橙子更深的"内在性"也并非存在于其果皮之内的任何地方。种子是未来橙子的物理载体，但形成种子的却是那实相的蓝图。面对这样的困境，你总会回到"先有鸡还是先有蛋"的问题，开始另一场愉快的追逐。因为你以连续时间的方式思考，所以似乎必然存在第一颗蛋或第一粒种子。然而，实相的蓝图存在于没有这种时间序列的维度中。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 703
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Other realities quite as legitimate as your own, quite as vital, quite as "real," coexist with your own, and in the terms of your understanding, "in the same space"--but of course in terms of your experience those spaces and realities would appear to be quite separate. No systems are closed, however, so that basically (underlined) the living grid of perception that causes one world or reality is also "wired into" all other such systems. There is a give-and-take between them.
quotes.translation: 其他实相与你的实相同样合理、同样充满活力、同样"真实"，它们与你的实相共存，用你理解的话说，"在同一个空间里"——但根据你的经验，那些空间和实相看起来会是完全分离的。然而，没有任何系统是封闭的，因此从根本上（强调）来说，导致一个世界或实相的感知活网格也"连接"到所有其他此类系统。它们之间存在相互给予与接收。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: These dream locations are realities. They do exist, even though they do not exist in space as you know it, and certainly they do not take up space in the skull. There would be no room for anything else. As a brief byline here, I mentioned once the Crucifixion, saying that it was an actuality and a reality, although it did not take place in your time. It took place where time is not as you know it. It took place in the same sort of time in which a dream takes place, and its reality was felt undeniably by generations, and was reacted to. Not being a physical reality, it influenced the world of physical matter in a way that no purely physical reality ever could.
quotes.translation: 这些梦境地点是实相。它们确实存在，尽管它们并不存在于你所知的空间中，当然也不占据颅骨内的空间——否则就没有空间容纳其他任何东西了。作为简短的附注，我曾提到过"受难"事件，说它是一个事实和实相，尽管它并未发生在你们的时间中。它发生在时间并非如你所知的地方。它发生在与梦境相同类型的时间中，其真实性被世世代代无可否认地感受到并做出反应。虽然不是物理实相，但它以任何纯粹物理实相永远无法做到的方式影响了物质世界。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Reality is indeed not necessarily that which is constant within the various appearances of reality through all systems, as it is the perception of the whole picture of reality, or the sum of all reality as seen within the various systems. This involves quite a complicated point, and implies a complicated position; for true reality would not be completely either the reality of an automobile, say, as it appears within the physical system, or as it appears within the electrical system. It would not be that which appears identical to the two systems, but it would be indeed the sum of the realities of all systems, as applied to our weary automobile.
quotes.translation: 实相确实不一定是所有系统中实相各种显现中恒定不变的东西，而是对实相整体画面的感知，或是在各个系统中所见的所有实相的总和。这涉及一个相当复杂的观点，并暗示了一个复杂的立场；因为真正的实相既不完全是一辆汽车在物理系统中显现的实相，也不完全是在电力系统中显现的实相。它不是两个系统中显现相同的东西，而是所有系统实相的总和，应用于我们那辆疲惫的汽车。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Your material reality is formed through joint cooperation. Period. Your own ideas, objectified, become a part of the physical environment. In this vast cooperative venture the thoughts and feelings of each living being take root, so to speak, springing up as objectified data. I said (in the 708th session) that each system of reality uses its own codified system. This effectively provides a sort of framework. Generally speaking, then, you agree to objectify certain inner data privately and en masse at any given "time." In those terms the airplane objectified the inner idea of flying in "your" time, and not in A.D. 1500, for example.
quotes.translation: 你们的物质实相是通过共同合作形成的。句号。你们自己的想法，被客观化后，成为物质环境的一部分。在这庞大的合作事业中，每个生灵的思想和情感可以说都扎根生长，作为客观化的数据涌现出来。我曾（在第708节中）说过，每个实相系统都使用自己的编码系统。这有效地提供了一种框架。那么，一般来说，在任何给定的"时间"，你们同意私下和集体地将某些内在数据客观化。用那些术语来说，飞机在"你们的"时间中客观化了飞行的内在想法，而不是在公元1500年。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 710
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: The unknown reality: Much of that reality is unknown simply because your beliefs close you off from your own knowledge. The reaches of your own consciousness are not limited. Because you accept the idea of a straight-line movement of time, you cannot see before or after what you think of as your birth or death,* yet your greater consciousness is quite aware of such experience. Ideally it is possible not only to remember "past" lives, but to plan future ones now. In greater terms, all such lives happen at once. Your present neurological structure makes this seem impossible, yet your inner consciousness is not so impeded.
quotes.translation: 未知的实相：那个实相的大部分之所以未知，仅仅是因为你们的信念将你们与自己的知识隔绝开来。你们自己意识的疆界并不受限。因为你们接受了时间直线运动的观念，所以你们无法看到你们认为的出生或死亡之前或之后的事物，*然而你们更伟大的意识完全知晓这样的经验。理想情况下，不仅有可能记住"过去"的转世，而且现在就可以规划未来的转世。从更宏大的角度来看，所有这些转世同时发生。你们当前的神经结构使这看似不可能，但你们的内在意识并未受到如此阻碍。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 708
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Some of the "particles" the theoretical physicists have discovered--and/or created--in their gigantic particle accelerators have unbelievably short life-spans in our terms, vanishing, it seems, almost before they're born. I like to think of such research from the particle's point of view, though, a consideration I haven't seen mentioned in the few scientific journals I read. Keep in mind that according to the Seth material the merest particle is basically conscious in its own way. Mesons are classes of particles produced from the collisions of protons. Did a meson, for example, choose to participate in an atom-smashing experiment in order to merely peek in on our gross physical reality for much less than the billionth of a second it exists with that identity, before it decays into electrons and photons? From its viewpoint, our reality might be as incomprehensible to it as its reality is to us--yet the two inevitably go together.
quotes.translation: 理论物理学家在巨型粒子加速器中发现的（或创造的）某些"粒子"，以我们的时间尺度衡量，其寿命短得令人难以置信，几乎在诞生前就已消失。但我更愿从粒子自身的角度看待这类研究——这是我在所读的少数科学期刊中从未见过的观点。请记住，根据赛斯资料，最微小的粒子本质上都具有其独特意识。介子是质子碰撞产生的粒子类别。举例而言，某个介子是否为了短暂窥探我们粗钝的物质实相，而选择参与原子撞击实验？它仅以该身份存在不足十亿分之一秒，便衰变为电子和光子。从它的视角看，我们的实相或许与它的实相同样难以理解——然而两者必然共存。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 1. In Volume 1 of "Unknown" Reality, I wrote in Note 7 for Session 681 that atoms are "processes" rather than things. The classical conception of the typical atom as being composed of a neat nucleus of indivisible protons and neutrons circled by electrons is largely passé, although for convenience's sake we may still describe the atom that way. (In those terms, the one exception is the hydrogen atom, which evidently consists of but one proton and one electron cloud, or "smear.") For the simple purposes of this note, then, I'm leaving out considerations involving quantum mechanics, which concept repudiates the idea of "particles" to begin with. (And surely that notion involves more than a little of the psychic, or "irrational." What a heretical thought from the scientific viewpoint!) But each atom of whatever element is an amazingly complicated, finely balanced assemblage of forces and particles woven together in exquisite detail--one of the more basic examples of the unending and stupendous creativity, order, and design of nature, or consciousness, or All That Is.

quotes.text: In that respect you cannot rip apart your events to find the reality behind them, for that reality is not so much a glue that holds events together, but is invisibly entwined within your own psychological being. There are obvious differences between what you think of as waking and dream events. You differentiate definitely between the two, making great efforts to see that they are neatly divided. In your world, conventional and practical sanity and physical manipulation are dependent upon your ability to discriminate, accepting as real only those events with which others more or less agree.
quotes.translation: 就此而言，你无法通过撕裂事件来寻找其背后的实相，因为那实相并非维系事件的胶水，而是无形地交织在你自身的心理存在中。你所谓的清醒事件与梦境事件存在明显差异。你严格区分两者，竭力确保它们泾渭分明。在你的世界里，传统认知、实际理智与物质操控，都取决于你辨别真伪的能力——只接受那些与他人大致认同的事件为真实。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The EE units are quite simply incipient forms of reality: seeds automatically given birth, suited for different environments, some appearing within the physical framework, and some not conforming at all to its prerequisites. Now some systems of reality are "bounded" with centers of faster-than-light particles. These begin to slow down at a rhythmic rate toward the peripheries, in your terms over great distances, until actually the outside slower particles to some extent imprison the center masses even though they move much more quickly, but within a confined area.
quotes.translation: 电磁能量单位（EE units）不过是实相的初始形态：自动诞生的种子，适应不同环境，有些显现在物质框架内，有些则完全不符合其先决条件。某些实相系统以超光速粒子为中心"界域"。这些粒子有节奏地向边缘减速——以你的术语而言，跨越巨大距离——直至外围较慢粒子在某种程度上囚禁中心质量，尽管它们运动更快，却局限于有限区域。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The "true facts" are that you exist in this life and outside it simultaneously. You are "between lives" and "in lives" at once. The deeper dimensions of reality are such that your thoughts and actions not only affect the life you know, but also reach into all of those other simultaneous existences. What you think now is unconsciously perceived by some hypothetical 14th-century self. The psyche is open-ended. No system is closed, psychological systems least of all. Your life is a dreaming experience to other portions of your greater reality which focus elsewhere.
quotes.translation: "真实情况"是：你同时存在于此生与此生之外。你既"处于生命间隙"，又"处于生命之中"。实相的更深维度使你的思想与行动不仅影响你所知的生命，更触及所有其他同时存在。你此刻的念头会被某个假设的14世纪自我无意识地感知。心灵是开放性的。没有封闭的系统，心理系统尤其如此。对你更大实相中聚焦于他处的部分而言，你的生命是一场梦境体验。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: As mentioned (in the 614th session in Chapter Two), the first important step is to realize that your beliefs about reality are just that -- beliefs about reality and not necessarily attributes of reality. You must make a clear distinction between you and your beliefs. You must then realize that your beliefs are physically materialized. What you believe to be true in your experience is true. To change the physical effect you must change the original belief -- while being quite aware that for a time physical materializations of the old beliefs may still hold.
quotes.translation: 如前所述（第二章第614节），首要步骤是认识到：你对实相的信念仅仅是信念，而非实相的本质属性。你必须清晰区分自我与信念。继而必须认识到信念会物质化。你经验中相信为真的事物即为真。要改变物质效应，必须改变原始信念——同时清楚意识到，旧信念的物质化产物可能暂时持续存在。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Those who understand thoroughly that reality is self-created will have least difficulty. Those who have learned to understand and operate in the mechanics of the dream state will have great advantage. A belief in demons is highly disadvantageous after death, as it is during physical existence. A systematized theology of opposites is also detrimental. If you believe, for example, that all good must be balanced by evil, then you bind yourself into a system of reality that is highly limiting, and that contains within it the seeds of great torment.
quotes.translation: 彻底理解实相乃自我创造者，将面临最少困难。学会理解并运作梦境机制者，将拥有巨大优势。对恶魔的信念在死后极为不利，正如在物质存在期间一样。系统化的二元对立神学同样有害。例如，若你相信所有善必须由恶来平衡，便将自己束缚在高度局限的实相系统中，其中蕴含着巨大痛苦的种子。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now. If the basic personality is fairly well balanced, then his existence in the dream reality will reinforce his physical existence. The opposite will also hold true. You are involved in a juggling of realities. The dream reality is simply the nearest reality with which you are concerned outside of your physical preoccupation. It is necessary to see the personality as it operates within both realities, if you are interested in viewing the personality as a whole. And even then unless you delve deeply you will fall short.
quotes.translation: 现在。如果基本人格相当平衡，那么他在梦实相中的存在将会强化他的物质存在。反之亦然。你正参与一场实相的杂耍。梦实相只是你除了物质专注之外所关心的最近实相。若你有兴趣将人格视为一个整体，就必须观察人格在这两种实相中的运作方式。即便如此，除非你深入探究，否则仍会有所不足。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: It is only because their realities are so distorted that the difficulty is discovered. I mentioned many sessions back that your physical universe, so taken for granted, is actually at least as diverse and multitudinous as the dream system. Within the physical universe you merely focus upon similarities and ignore the vast differences that exist. Each reality is completely unique for every individual, and through his own actions he attempts therefore to communicate the nature of this reality of his to his fellows.
quotes.translation: 正是因为他们的实相如此扭曲，困难才会被发现。许多节课前我曾提到，你们视为理所当然的物质宇宙，实际上至少与梦系统一样多样且繁复。在物质宇宙中，你们只聚焦于相似之处，而忽略了存在的巨大差异。每个实相对每个个体而言都是完全独特的，而个体通过自身的行为试图向同伴传达自己实相的本质。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The horror and the results of mismanagement, and the vulnerability, are the teaching methods that each consciousness has accepted before entering your system. There is no way out but to learn or to ruin the entire system. In no other field of reality are the terms so drastic. For this reason the inner self withholds much of its knowledge. There must be no leaning upon the very basic fact that behind and within the system there is relief. You must believe in the physical reality and accept the vulnerability.
quotes.translation: 恐惧、管理不当的结果以及脆弱性，是每个意识在进入你们系统之前就已接受的教学方法。除了学习或毁掉整个系统之外，别无出路。在实相的其他领域，条件从未如此严苛。正因如此，内在自我保留了它的大部分知识。绝不能依赖那个基本事实——即系统背后和内部存在解脱。你必须相信物质实相并接受脆弱性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: To some extent you can actualize portions of your own unknown reality, and draw them into the experienced area of your life. There is an obvious relationship between one note and another in a musical composition. Now in terms of physical families and in larger terms of countries, there is a relationship between realities, which constantly change as the notes do. To some extent your reality is picked up by your contemporaries. They accept it or not according to the particular theme or focus of their lives.
quotes.translation: 在某种程度上，你可以实现自己未知实相的部分，并将它们带入你生命中的经验领域。音乐作品中一个音符与另一个音符之间存在着明显的关系。现在，就物质家族而言，以及更广泛地就国家而言，实相之间存在着一种关系，这种关系像音符一样不断变化。在某种程度上，你的实相会被同时代的人所接收。他们根据自己生活的特定主题或焦点来接受或不接受它。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 735
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: The root assumptions that govern physical reality are indeed valid, but within physical reality alone. They do not apply elsewhere. There is a natural tendency to continue judging inner experience against these root assumptions however. The tendency, with further experience, will fade. This interpretation of inner reality into physical terms is at first automatic, and far beneath conscious levels. Inner experience, you see, must to some extent be colored by the physical system while you exist within it.
quotes.translation: 支配物质实相的根本假设确实是有效的，但仅限于物质实相之内。它们不适用于其他地方。然而，人们自然倾向于继续根据这些根本假设来判断内在经验。随着进一步的经验，这种倾向会逐渐消退。将内在实相转化为物质术语的过程最初是自动的，且远在意识层面之下。你看，当你存在于物质系统之内时，内在经验必然会在一定程度上受到该系统的着色。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: It will involve an expansion of consciousness, not physical knowledge... You will directly and simply perceive more... I cannot make Ruburt find all the words. Your God is part of a larger reality. We see what we can see... This larger reality is also a part of our dreams: it is more important and vital than breath, for you are all part of this individually. There is a give and take between you and the stars on a physical basis, just as there is also a connection between selves and what you call a god.
quotes.translation: 这将涉及意识的扩展，而非物质知识……你将直接而简单地感知更多……我无法让鲁柏找到所有词语。你们的上帝是更大实相的一部分。我们能看到我们能看到的……这个更大的实相也是我们梦境的一部分：它比呼吸更重要、更充满活力，因为你们每个人都是其中的一部分。在物质基础上，你们与星辰之间存在着给予与索取，正如自我与你们所谓的上帝之间也存在着联系一样。

quotes.text: In the body's spontaneous functioning you see the easy mobility of the soul, the "going with that which I am," which is an indication of the soul's inner freedom and yet innate sense of direction. All portions of the body's reality are versions in flesh of the soul's reality, even as all segments of the exterior universe mirror an internal one. The latter is as alive and natural and changing as the exterior world. Physical phenomena is only a portion of what nature is, and all realities are natural.
quotes.translation: 在身体的自发运作中，你看见灵魂的轻松流动性，看见“随顺我所是者”，这暗示了灵魂的内在自由与与生俱来的方向感。身体实相的所有部分都是灵魂实相在血肉中的版本，正如外在宇宙的所有片段都映照着一个内在宇宙。后者与外在世界一样鲜活、自然且不断变化。物质现象只是自然的一部分，而所有实相都是自然的。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: To make this clear: When you dissect an animal, for instance, you are still dealing only with the "inside" of exterior reality, or with another level of outsideness. (Pause.) In a manner of speaking, when you probe the heavens with your instruments you are doing the same thing. There is a difference between this and the "withinness" out of which all matter springs. It is there that the blueprints for reality are found. There are various ways of studying reality. Let us take a very simple example.
quotes.translation: 为说清楚这点：举例来说，当你解剖一只动物时，你仍然只处理外在实相的“内部”，或另一个层次的外在性。（停顿）从某种角度说，当你用仪器探测天空时，你也在做同样的事。这与所有物质从中涌现的“内在性”有所不同。实相的蓝图正是在那里被找到的。研究实相有各种方法。让我们举一个非常简单的例子。

quotes.text: Your physical life and your dreaming life are so intimately connected that it can be misleading to say what I am about to say, colon: that waking experience springs from the unknown dream reality. On the one hand the statement is indeed true. On the other hand, the intricate inner workings make it impossible to separate one from the other. "Reality" operates basically, however, in a way that is perceived more clearly in the dream state. Freedom from time and place, the wider kind of communication, the great mobility of consciousness -- all of these experiences under dreaming conditions are characteristic of the basic nature of reality -- whereas your waking experience provides limitations that are indicators of certain conditions only. Period.
quotes.translation: 你的物质生活与你的梦境生活如此紧密相连，以至于我接下来要说的话可能会造成误导：清醒体验源自未知的梦境实相。一方面，这句话确实为真。另一方面，复杂的内在运作使得两者无法分离。然而，“实相”基本上以一种在梦境状态中被更清晰感知的方式运作。不受时间与地点的束缚、更广泛的沟通方式、意识的高度流动性——所有这些在梦境条件下的体验都是实相基本本质的特征——而你的清醒体验则提供了仅指示特定条件的限制。句号。

quotes.text: The Emotional impact behind them determines their strength. They are passed on telepathically. You recall however that individuals receive in certain electromagnetic patterns, or pattern ranges, far better than they do in others. Some individuals therefore will simply not telepathically receive the dominating reality assumptions, but will be tuned to different ranges, highly unsatisfactory to them. It becomes quickly apparent that their view of reality is entirely different from their neighbors.
quotes.translation: 它们背后的情感冲击决定了其强度。它们通过心电感应传递。然而，你记得个体在某些电磁模式或模式范围内接收信息的效果远胜于其他模式。因此，有些个体根本不会心电感应地接收主导性的实相假设，而是调频到不同的范围，这让他们极不满意。很快就能明显看出，他们对实相的看法与邻居完全不同。

quotes.text: You begin to manipulate the dream state, or rather you begin to manipulate yourself within dream reality, which is something different. It is an entirely new environment. In ordinary dreaming, without the awareness of usual waking consciousness, perception of dream reality is limited and instinctive. All in all you get along quite well. You are like a child in objective reality. When you learn to take waking awareness into the dream condition, you are reaching adolescence, so to speak.
quotes.translation: 你开始操控梦境状态，或者更确切地说，你开始在梦境实相中操控自己——这是两回事。这是一个全新的环境。在普通梦境中，没有通常清醒意识的觉知，对梦境实相的感知是有限且本能的。总的来说，你应付得相当不错。你就像客观实相中的孩子。当你学会将清醒意识带入梦境状态时，可以说你正在进入青春期。

quotes.text: In the various levels of existence the inner and outer egos begin to merge. Gradually direct experience of inner reality is spoon-fed by the inner ego through the mouth of the subconscious to the outer ego. The division between the inner and outer egos is necessary for other reasons than the nature of direct experience itself. That is, the outer ego is shielded from direct experience of reality because it could not take the impact of such experience. But this is not the only reason.
quotes.translation: 在存在的各个层次中，内在与外在自我开始融合。逐渐地，内在自我通过潜意识之口，将内在实相的直接体验像喂食般传递给外在自我。内在与外在自我的区分，除了直接体验本身的性质外，还有其他原因。也就是说，外在自我被屏蔽于实相的直接体验之外，因为它无法承受这种体验的冲击。但这并非唯一的原因。

quotes.text: The intellect is basically able to handle many kinds of information, and information systems. It is far more flexible than you presently allow it to be. It can handle several (pause) main world views at once, realizing that they are each methods of perceiving and approaching reality. To some degree historically speaking, that sort of situation operated in the past when -- comparatively speaking, now -- people realized that there was indeed an inner world of complexity and richness that could be approached in certain fashions, one that existed alongside with the physical world, so that the two intersected. Certain approaches worked in one area, and others worked in the inner reality.
quotes.translation: 智力基本上能够处理多种信息和信息系统。它远比你们目前所允许的更为灵活。它可以同时处理几种（停顿）主要的世界观，认识到它们各自都是感知和接近实相的方法。从历史角度来看，在某种程度上，过去曾出现过这种情况——相对而言，现在——人们意识到确实存在一个复杂而丰富的内在世界，可以通过某些方式接近，它与物质世界并存，两者相互交织。某些方法在一个领域有效，而另一些方法则适用于内在实相。

quotes.text: Reality is not limited. There is no past, present and future. These only appear to those who exist within three-dimensional reality. Since I am no longer within it, I can perceive what you do not see. But there is a part of you that is not imprisoned within three-dimensional reality, and that part of you knows that there is no time, that there is only an eternal now; and that part of you that knows is the whole self, the inner personality that knows all of your lives.
quotes.translation: 实相不受限制。没有过去、现在和未来。这些只存在于三维实相中的人看来如此。既然我不再处于其中，我能感知你们所看不见的。但你们有一部分并不受困于三维实相，那部分知道没有时间，只有永恒的当下；那部分知道的就是整体自己，是知晓你们所有生命的内在人格。

As would be expected of a gathering, class sessions were full of energy and repartee and questions and serious thinking both with and without Seth.

quotes.text: It is really a building up of idea into a whole pattern that can be perceived by the camouflage senses. Any reality therefore will be variously perceived, and the nature of the reality will necessarily be distorted in the very attempt to perceive it. Here again we have our creative tension, whereby a new reality is formed as a result of the distortion itself. Within your system colors may be perceived as sound. Their connections with human moods is only too apparent.
quotes.translation: 这实际上是将观念构建成一个可以被伪装感官感知的整体模式。因此，任何实相都会被以不同方式感知，而实相的本质在试图感知它的过程中必然会被扭曲。这里我们再次有了创造性的张力，扭曲本身由此形成新的实相。在你们的系统中，颜色可能被感知为声音。它们与人类情绪的联系显而易见。

quotes.text: As you have an ego, fully conscious, directed toward the physical world, you also have what I call an inner ego, directed toward inner reality. You have, in other words, a portion of yourself that is fully conscious in Framework 2. The ego in your ordinary world, which again we will call Framework 1, is uniquely equipped to deal with that environment. It manipulates with rules of cause and effect and consecutive moments. It deals with an objectified reality. It can stretch its capacities, becoming far more aware of inner events than it is normally allowed to do, but its main purpose is to deal with the world of effects, to encounter events.
quotes.translation: 正如你们有一个完全有意识、面向物质世界的自我，你们也有一个我称之为内在自我的部分，面向内在实相。换句话说，你们有一部分在架构二中完全有意识。你们普通世界中的自我，我们再次称之为架构一，拥有独特的能力来处理那个环境。它按照因果律和连续时刻运作。它处理客观化的实相。它可以扩展其能力，比通常允许的更加意识到内在事件，但其主要目的是处理结果的世界，遭遇事件。

quotes.text: Because of the great organizing nature of these basic units, there are also psychological structures that are quite capable of holding their own identities while being aware of any given number of probable selves. Life after death has great meaning in your reality, because death is a part of it. Your greater reality obviously transcends both your births and your deaths. The idea of one universe alone is basically nonsensical. Your reality must be seen in its relationship to others.8 Otherwise you are always caught in questions like "How did the universe begin?" or "When will it end?" All systems are constantly being created.
quotes.translation: 由于这些基本单位具有强大的组织性，也存在心理结构，它们完全能够保持自身身份，同时意识到任何给定数量的可能自己。死后生命在你们的实相中具有重大意义，因为死亡是它的一部分。你们更大的实相显然超越了你们的出生和死亡。只有一个宇宙的想法基本上是无稽之谈。你们的实相必须在其与其他实相的关系中看待。否则你们总是被困在诸如“宇宙如何开始？”或“它何时结束？”之类的问题中。所有系统都在不断被创造。

quotes.text: Now nevertheless the dream world, the mind and the reality universe do exist. They exist in a climate that we will call the value climate of psychological reality. This is the medium. This takes the place of what you call space. This value climate of psychological reality is a quality which makes all existences and consciousness possible. It is one of the most powerful principles behind or within the vitality that itself composes from itself all other phenomena.
quotes.translation: 然而，梦境世界、心灵和实相宇宙确实存在。它们存在于一种我们称之为心理实相价值氛围的环境中。这就是媒介。它取代了你们所谓的空间。这种心理实相的价值氛围是一种品质，使所有存在和意识成为可能。它是生命力背后或内部最强大的原则之一，生命力本身从自身构成所有其他现象。

quotes.text: The rational approach of course suits certain kinds of people better than others, even while it still carries its disadvantages. You have been living in an industrialized, scientific society, so that the benefits and the great disadvantages of the rational approach appear everywhere in the social and political world. Artists of any kind find such an approach the least friendly, for it directly contradicts the vast thrust of man's creativity in several important areas. You, however, and Ruburt, do have evidence that hardbed reality is quite different. In the past you have both felt at some disadvantage yourselves, feeling our work to be theoretically fascinating, creatively valid, but not necessarily containing any statement about any kind of "scientifically valid" hardbed reality. (All with much emphasis.
quotes.translation: 理性方法当然更适合某些类型的人，但即便如此它仍带有其弊端。你们一直生活在工业化、科学化的社会中，因此理性方法的益处与重大弊端在社会和政治世界中随处可见。任何类型的艺术家都认为这种方法最不友善，因为它直接违背了人类创造力在几个重要领域的巨大推力。然而，你和鲁柏确实有证据表明，硬邦邦的现实截然不同。过去你们俩都曾感到自己处于某种劣势，认为我们的工作在理论上引人入胜、在创造上真实有效，但未必包含任何关于"科学上有效"的硬邦邦现实的陈述。（语气强调）

quotes.text: I feel no great responsibility for any of your beings. [If I did] then I would be denying you your own power, and therefore seemingly building my own ... I am here because I enjoy it. I am a teacher, and because I am a teacher I love to teach. A person who loves to teach needs people who love to learn. That is why I am here and why you are here ... My view of reality is different from your own, and that is fine, and so I can teach. A true teacher allows you to learn from yourself. I enjoy the great vitality and exuberance of your reality, and our city will have joy and exuberance. Now joy sounds quite acceptable, but (with amusement) our city will also have fun -- which in many spiritual circles is not so acceptable!
quotes.translation: 我对你们任何存有都不感到负有重大责任。若我如此，那便是否认你们自身的力量，从而看似在建立我自己的力量……我在此是因为我享受其中。我是一位老师，正因是老师，我热爱教学。热爱教学的人需要热爱学习的人。这就是我在此的原因，也是你们在此的原因……我对现实的看法与你们不同，这很好，因此我能教学。真正的老师让你从自身学习。我享受你们现实的巨大活力与丰沛，我们的城市将充满喜悦与丰沛。喜悦听起来相当可接受，但（带着戏谑）我们的城市也将充满乐趣——这在许多灵性圈子里可没那么可接受！

It is still individual. quotes.translation: 这个电性模式包含了人格及其尘世时间的所有经验。它随后能加入或参与内在自体。换言之，尽管自我最初由内在自体采用，且是物质遗传与环境的产物，但它不会消亡；其存在从物质现实转变为电性现实。它仍是个体化的。没有任何个体性会丧失，但它成为内在自体的一部分，其经验被添加到构成内在自体的众多人格的总经验中。

quotes.text: There may or may not be disorientation on your part, according to your beliefs and development. Now I do not necessarily mean intellectual development. The intellect should go hand in hand with the emotions and intuitions, but if it pulls against these too strongly, difficulties can arise when the newly freed consciousness seizes upon its ideas about reality after death, rather than facing the particular reality in which it finds itself. It can deny feeling, in other words, and even attempt to argue itself out of its present independence from the body.
quotes.translation: 根据你们的信念与发展，你们可能会或不会感到迷失方向。我并非特指智力发展。智力应与情感和直觉携手并进，但若它过于强烈地对抗这些，当新获解放的意识紧抓住死后现实的观念，而非面对它发现自己所处的特定现实时，困难就可能出现。换言之，它可能否认感受，甚至试图通过争辩来摆脱当前脱离身体的独立状态。

quotes.text: You may find some of your most cherished conceptions to be misconceptions in the light of your new experiences. Since explorations are highly personal, you will most likely begin them from the framework of your current beliefs. Symbols may be utilized, and these may change their meaning for you as you progress. The symbols may evolve, therefore. In the beginning of this work I "warned" the reader that here in these sessions we would go beyond ideas of one god and one self.3 I stated that your ideas of personhood would be expanded. As "Unknown" Reality is being produced Ruburt and Joseph are having their own experiences, and uncovering the nature of the unknown reality as it applies to them.
quotes.translation: 根据新经验的光照，你们可能会发现某些最珍视的观念其实是误解。由于探索极具个人性，你们很可能从当前信念的框架开始。符号可能被运用，随着你们进步，这些符号对你们的意义可能改变。因此符号可能演化。在这项工作的开端，我"警告"读者，在此这些课中我们将超越一神与一自体的观念。我曾声明你们关于人格的观念将被扩展。随着《未知的实相》被创作，鲁柏和约瑟正经历他们自己的体验，并揭示未知现实适用于他们自身的本质。

quotes.text: We have to some extent explained the reality of the personality within the electrical system. We have to some extent explained its basic origin within inner reality. We have explained the fact that the potentialities of the personality and of the self are basically unlimited. But during its alliance within the physical system, it is diffused within the cellular structure, and interrelating actions between the biological system, the electrical system, and the personality structure actually form the reality of the human individual.
quotes.translation: 我们在某种程度上解释了电性系统内人格的实相，也在某种程度上解释了其在内在实相中的基本起源。我们已说明人格与自体潜能基本上无限的事实。但在其与物质系统联盟期间，它分散于细胞结构之中，而生物系统、电性系统与人格结构之间的互动，实际上形成了人类个体的实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Imagine if you can the figures or inhabitants in the painting having psychological reality, all within the set limits prescribed by the given space. Imagine in other words consciousness, growth, reality and expansion, having nothing to do with expansion of space in your terms, but an almost complete freedom of psychological realities, and you will come at least within the realm of understanding what I mean by an expanding universe that has nothing to do with the expanding universe of which your scientists speak.
quotes.translation: 请想象画中人物或居民拥有心理实相，全在既定空间设定的界限之内。换句话说，请想象意识、成长、实相与扩张，与你们所谓的空间扩张毫无关系，而是心理实相几乎完全的自由。这样你至少能进入我所指的"扩张宇宙"的领域——这与你们科学家所说的扩张宇宙截然不同。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Every thought therefore has this kind of reality, which is the only reality, basically speaking. （Long pause.） Sense data itself has a reality independent of any given object. This sense data has its own intensity. If you can tune into that intensity, so to speak, you will automatically translate it into physical data, and you will perceive a physical object.
quotes.translation: 因此每个念头都具有这种实相，而基本上说，这是唯一的实相。（长停顿）感官资料本身具有独立于任何特定物体的实相。这种感官资料有其自身的强度。如果你能调频进入那个强度，可以说，你会自动将其转化为物质资料，并感知到一个物质物体。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: Regardless of your circumstances, your condition in life, your training or your aptitudes, at your own threshold you stand at the center of all realities--for at your center all existences intersect. You are everywhere part of them, and they are of you. Each portion of the universe carries the knowledge of all other parts, and each point of a reality is (underlined) that reality's center. You are, then, centered in the universe.
quotes.translation: 无论你的环境、生活状况、训练或才能如何，在你自己的门槛上，你站在所有实相的中心——因为在你的中心，所有存在交汇。你处处是它们的一部分，它们也是你的一部分。宇宙的每一部分都承载着其他所有部分的知识，而实相的每一点都是（强调）该实相的中心。因此，你以宇宙为中心。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Two and a half years ago I wrote in the closing notes for Session 869, in Chapter 10 of Mass Events, that she had finally received from Seth the full title for this book: Dreams, "Evolution," and Value Fulfillment. Mass Events was published eight months ago, and we have been receiving a steady flow of inquiries as to when Dreams will be issued. We're glad to try to answer questions like that, but our correspondents cover many topics and express many viewpoints--and some are hard for us to handle. As we waited for the session Jane gave me two very long letters she had received yesterday. I felt sad after reading them. We think that both writers express extreme points of view, and that both are much too adulatory of us. To answer one letter would draw its author to our doorstep at once: "I am your Seth," and: "I will visit you as soon as I hear from you." The writer of the other letter, while praising our work, is caught up in questions of conventional religion: "I keep wondering over and over again whether Seth is a demon or a deception. What do you have to say about this?" We don't feel like justifying ourselves.

quotes.text: As your perceptive mechanisms insist that objects are solid, for example, so they insist that such a thing as space exists. Now what your senses tell you about the nature of matter is entirely erroneous, and what they tell you about space is equally wrong -- wrong in terms of basic reality, but quite in keeping of course with three-dimensional concepts. (Humorously): In out-of-body experiences from the living state, many of the problems are encountered, in terms of space, that will be met after death. And in such episodes, therefore, the true nature of time and space becomes more apparent. After death it does not take time to go through space, for example. Space does not exist in terms of distance. This is illusion. There are barriers, but they are mental or psychic barriers. For example, there are intensities of experience that are interpreted in your reality as distance in miles.
quotes.translation: 例如，正如你的感知机制坚持物体是固态的，它们也坚持空间这种东西存在。现在，你的感官告诉你关于物质本质的信息完全错误，它们告诉你关于空间的信息也同样错误——就基本实相而言是错误的，但当然完全符合三维概念。（幽默地）：在活体状态下的出体经验中，会遇到许多关于空间的问题，这些问题在死后也会遇到。因此，在这种经历中，时间与空间的真实本质变得更加明显。例如，死后穿越空间不需要时间。空间不存在于距离的层面。这是幻相。存在障碍，但它们是心智或心灵的障碍。例如，存在经验的强度，在你们的实相中被解释为以英里计的距离。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The inner self is embarked upon an exciting endeavor, in which it learns how to translate its reality into physical terms. The conscious mind is brilliantly attuned to physical reality, then, and often so dazzled by what it perceives that it is tempted to think physical phenomena is a cause, rather than a result. Deeper portions of the self always serve to remind it that this is not the case. When the conscious mind accepts too many false beliefs, particularly if it sees that inner self as a danger, then it closes out these constant reminders. When this situation arises the conscious mind feels itself assailed by a reality that seems greater than itself, over which it has no control. The deep feeling of security in which it should be anchored is lost.
quotes.translation: 内在自体正投入一项激动人心的努力，在其中它学习如何将其实相转化为物质术语。因此，意识心被精妙地调谐到物质实相，并常常被它所感知的事物所迷惑，以至于它倾向于认为物质现象是原因，而非结果。自体的更深层部分总是提醒它事实并非如此。当意识心接受了太多错误信念，特别是当它视内在自体为危险时，它就会关闭这些持续的提醒。当这种情况发生时，意识心感到被一个似乎比它自身更大的实相所攻击，它无法控制这个实相。它本应扎根其中的深层安全感便丧失了。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Your own value system then is built up of your beliefs about reality, and those beliefs form your experience. Suppose you believe that to be "good" you must try to be perfect. You may have been told, or read, that the spirit is perfect, and hence thought that your duty was to reproduce that perfect spirit in flesh as best you could. To this end you attempt to deny all imperfect thoughts and emotions. Your own "negative" thoughts appall you. You may believe also what I have told you -- that your thoughts create your reality -- so you become all the more frightened at mental or actual expressions of an aggressive nature. You may be so concerned about hurting someone else that you hardly dare move. Trying to be perfect all the time can be far more than a nuisance: It can be disastrous because of your misunderstanding.
quotes.translation: 你的价值体系是由你对实相的看法所构成的，而这些看法形成了你的经验。假设你相信要成为"好人"就必须努力追求完美。你可能被告知或读到过，灵性是完美的，因此认为你的职责就是尽可能在肉体中复制那个完美的灵性。为此，你试图否认所有不完美的思想和情感。你自己的"负面"思想让你惊恐。你可能也相信我曾告诉你的——你的思想创造你的实相——所以你更加害怕任何攻击性本质的心理或实际表达。你可能如此担心伤害别人，以至于几乎不敢动弹。一直试图完美绝不仅仅是个麻烦：由于你的误解，它可能是灾难性的。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The physical reality into which you are born is not nearly as solid or predetermined or definite as it appears to be. Instead there is a field of rich interaction. Your consciousness must be focused at one particular range of frequencies before it can even perceive matter, much less solidity. In sleep your consciousness fluctuates between ranges of intensities, literally flowing into and out of the physical-matter grouping, and forming from more plastic "pre-matter" (with a hyphen) stages, the final shape that matter will take in your world. The same applies to events, where some will be crystallized in physical terms and others will not. The deep portions of your own being are aware of those purposes and intents that are uniquely yours. Unconsciously, then, you have within you what you might think of as a set of blueprints for the particular kind of physical reality you want to materialize. You are the architect.
quotes.translation: 你出生其中的物质实相并不像它看起来那样坚实、预定或确定。相反，它是一个丰富的互动场域。你的意识必须聚焦在某个特定的频率范围，才能感知物质，更不用说坚实性了。在睡眠中，你的意识在强度范围之间波动，实际上流入和流出物质群组，并从更具可塑性的"前物质"阶段形成物质将在你世界中呈现的最终形态。这同样适用于事件，有些会在物质层面结晶，有些则不会。你存在的深层部分知道那些独特属于你的目的和意图。因此，在无意识中，你内心有你想要物质化的特定物质实相的蓝图。你就是建筑师。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: I think it important to periodically remind the reader of certain of Seth's basic ideas throughout both volumes of "Unknown" Reality. As an example, I'll continue with the subject of time -- but Seth's time now -- and couple it with his notions of a durability that is at the same time spontaneous and simultaneous, as he's explained to us more than once. The durability is achieved through constant expansion in terms of value fulfillment. Part of my paragraph of commentary following the 724th session, in Volume 2, fits in here: "As he [Seth] quite humorously commented in the 14th session for January 8, 1964, '... for you have no idea of the difficulties involved in explaining time to someone who must take time to understand the explanation.' Yet Seth's simultaneous time isn't an absolute, for, as he also told us in that session: 'While I am not affected by time on your plane, I am affected by something resembling time on my plane ... To me time can be manipulated, used at leisure and examined. To me your time is a vehicle, one of several by which I can enter your awareness. It is therefore still a reality of some kind to me [my emphasis]. Otherwise I could not utilize it in any way whatsoever.'"
quotes.translation: 我认为定期提醒读者赛斯在《未知的实相》两卷中的某些基本概念很重要。例如，我将继续讨论时间主题——但现在是赛斯的时间——并将其与他关于持久性的概念结合起来，这种持久性同时是自发和同时的，正如他多次向我们解释的那样。这种持久性是通过价值完成方面的持续扩张实现的。我在第724节课后的评论段落（第二卷）中有一部分与此相关："正如他（赛斯）在1964年1月8日的第14节课中相当幽默地评论道，'……因为你不知道向一个必须花时间理解解释的人解释时间有多困难。'然而赛斯的同时时间并不是绝对的，因为他在那节课中也告诉我们：'虽然我不受你们层面时间的影响，但我受到我层面类似时间的东西影响……对我来说，时间可以被操控、随意使用和检视。对我来说，你们的时间是一种交通工具，是我进入你们意识的几种方式之一。因此它对我仍然是某种实相（我的强调）。否则我根本无法以任何方式利用它。'"
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Every so often I've thought of averaging Jane's dictation time for Seth Speaks and Personal Reality in the same way, but haven't done so. I'm somewhat puzzled to note, however, that her very short working times for the Seth books seem to be either ignored or taken for granted by practically everyone -- or, perhaps, those factors just aren't understood in terms of ordinary linear time. Maybe I'm alone in my interest here, for even Jane doesn't express any great curiosity about the time she has invested in the Seth material; she just delivers it. But given her abilities, I think her speed of production is a close physical approach to, or translation of, Seth's idea that basically all exists at once -- that really there is no time, and that the Seth books, for example, are "there" to be had in final form for just the tuning in. (In Section 3 of this volume, Note 2 for Session 692 contains information on another way by which we can move closer to Seth's idea of simultaneity from our physical reality, but that method grows out of material not discussed here.)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: "In Seth Speaks I tried to describe certain extensions of your own reality in terms that my readers could understand. In The Nature of Personal Reality, I tried to extend the boundaries of individual existence as it is usually experienced ... to give the reader hints that would increase practical, spiritual and physical enjoyment and fulfillment in daily life. Those books were dictated by me in a more or less straight narrative style. In 'Unknown' Reality I went further, showing how the experiences of the psyche splash outward into the daylight, so to speak. I hope that [in those two books] through my dictation and through Ruburt's and Joseph's experiences, the reader can see the greater dimensions that touch ordinary living, and sense the psyche's greater magic. 'Unknown' Reality required much more work on Joseph's part, and that additional effort in itself was a demonstration that the psyche's events are very difficult to pin down in time. Seemingly its action goes out in all directions.... As Joseph did his notes, it became apparent that some events ... seemed to have no beginning or end."
quotes.translation: "在《赛斯说》中，我试图用读者能理解的术语描述你们自己实相的某些延伸。在《个人实相的本质》中，我试图扩展通常体验到的个体存在的边界……给读者一些提示，以增加日常生活中的实际、灵性和身体享受与满足。那些书是我以或多或少直接的叙事风格口述的。在《未知的实相》中，我更进一步，展示了心灵的经验如何向外溅射到日光中，可以这么说。我希望（在这两本书中）通过我的口述以及鲁柏和约瑟的经历，读者能看到触及日常生活的更大维度，并感受到心灵的更大魔力。《未知的实相》需要约瑟做更多工作，而这额外的努力本身就证明了心灵的事件很难在时间中固定下来。看似它的行动向各个方向扩散……当约瑟做笔记时，很明显有些事件……似乎没有开始或结束。"
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: To me, some of the most important material in Volume 1 is Jane's information on her sensing of other neurological pulses as they're connected with probable events, and how she picked up those pulses by bypassing her direct, or ordinary neurological impact. See her work in appendixes 4 and 5. Seth also discussed such neurological changeovers in Session 685, among others. I think this kind of material offers a rich source for future scientific investigation.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Any discussion of the unknown reality must necessarily involve certain usually dismissed hypotheses about the characteristics of consciousness itself. The world as you know it is the result of a complicated set of "codes" (as given at the beginning of the last session), each locked in one to the other, each one in those terms dependent upon the others. Your precise perceived universe in all of its parts, then, results from coded patterns, each one fitting perfectly into the next. Alter one of these and to some extent you step out of that context (underlined). Any event of any kind that does not directly, immaculately intersect with your space-time continuum, does not happen, in your terms, but falls away. It becomes probable in your system but seeks its own "level," and becomes actualized as it falls into place in another reality whose "coded sequence" fits its own. Period.
quotes.translation: 任何关于未知实相的讨论都必须涉及某些通常被忽视的关于意识本身特征的假设。你所知的世界是一套复杂"密码"的结果（如上一节课开头所述），每个密码相互锁定，每个密码在那些术语中依赖于其他密码。因此，你精确感知的宇宙及其所有部分，都源于编码模式，每个模式完美地契合下一个。改变其中一个，在某种程度上你就脱离了那个背景（下划线）。任何不直接、完美地与你时空连续体相交的事件，用你的话说，就不会发生，而是脱落。它在你的系统中成为可能的，但寻求自己的"层面"，并在落入另一个其"编码序列"适合它的实相时被实现。句号。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 709
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: (Slowly:) The simple picture of the universe that you see on our screen, therefore, represents a view from your own now perspective -- but each star, planet, galaxy or whatever is made up of other reference points in which, to put it simply, the same patterns have different kinds of reality. True space travel would of course be time-space travel,5 in which you learned how to use points in your own universe as "dimensional clues" that would serve as entry points into other worlds. Otherwise you are simply flying like an insect around the outside of the television set, trying to light on the fruit, say, that is shown upon the screen -- and wondering, like a poor bemused fly, why you cannot. You use one main focus in your reality. In the outside world this means that you have a "clear picture." (Humorously:) There is no snow! That physical program is the one you are acting in, alive in, and it is the one shown on the screen. The screen is the part of your psyche upon which you are concentrating. You not only tune in the picture but you also create the props, the entire history of the life and times, hyphen -- but in living three-dimensional terms, and "you" are within that picture.
quotes.translation: （缓慢地：）因此，你在屏幕上看到的宇宙简单画面，代表了你从自己当下视角看到的景象——但每颗恒星、行星、星系或其他东西都由其他参考点组成，简单来说，在这些参考点中，相同的模式具有不同种类的实相。真正的太空旅行当然是时空旅行，在其中你学会如何利用自己宇宙中的点作为"维度线索"，这些线索将成为进入其他世界的入口点。否则你就像一只昆虫在电视机外部飞来飞去，试图落在屏幕上显示的水果上——然后像一只困惑的苍蝇一样纳闷为什么做不到。你在你的实相中使用一个主要焦点。在外部世界，这意味着你有一个"清晰的画面"。（幽默地：）没有雪花！那个物质节目是你正在其中表演、生活的节目，也是屏幕上显示的节目。屏幕是你正在专注的心灵部分。你不仅调谐到画面，还创造了道具、整个生命和时代的历史——但以活生生的三维术语来说，"你"就在那个画面之内。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 713
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Your thoughts, for example, and your intents, have their own validity and force. You set them into motion, but then they follow their own laws and realities. All creativity comes from the psyche. I [recently] suggested a project to Ruburt's class -- one that will ultimately illuminate many of the points I am making in "Unknown" Reality. I suggested that Ruburt's students create a "city"9 at another level of reality. This is not to be a pie-in-the sky sort of thing, or some "heaven" hanging suspended above, but a very valid meeting place between worlds. A psychic marketplace, for example, where ideas are exchanged, a place of psychic commerce, a pleasant environment with quite definite coordinates, established as an "orbiting satellite" on the outskirts of your world.
quotes.translation: 例如，你的思想和意图本身具有其有效性和力量。你启动它们，但它们随后遵循自身的法则与实相。所有创造力皆源自心灵。我（最近）向鲁柏的班级建议了一个计划——这个计划最终将阐明我在《未知的实相》中提出的许多观点。我建议鲁柏的学生在另一层实相中创造一座"城市"9。这并非空中楼阁或悬于上方的某种"天堂"，而是一个极其有效的世界间交汇点。例如，一个交换思想的灵性市场，一处灵性交易的场所，一个具有明确坐标的宜人环境，作为你们世界边缘的"轨道卫星"而建立。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 713
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: If, however, you pause first and wait a moment, you can begin to glimpse the environment that serves as a stage: the natural landscape of the dream reality. In waking life, if you want to disconnect yourself from an event or place, you try to move away from it in space. In dream reality events occur in a different fashion, and places spring up about you. If you meet with people or events not of your liking, then you must simply move your attention away from them, and they will disappear as far as your experience is concerned. In physical reality you can move fairly freely through space, but you do not travel from one city to another, for example, unless you want to. Intent is invoked. This is so obvious that its significance escapes you: but it is intent that moves you through space, and that is behind all of your physical locomotion. You utilize ships, automobiles, trains, airplanes, because you want to go to another place, and certain vehicles work best under certain conditions.
quotes.translation: 然而，如果你先暂停片刻并等待，便能开始瞥见作为舞台的环境：梦境实相的自然景观。在清醒生活中，若想脱离某个事件或地点，你会试图在空间中远离它。在梦境实相中，事件以不同方式发生，地点在你周围涌现。若遇到不喜的人或事，你只需将注意力移开，它们便会从你的经验中消失。在物质实相中，你可以相当自由地在空间中移动，但除非你愿意，否则你不会从一个城市旅行到另一个城市。意图被调动起来。这显而易见，以至于其意义被你忽略：正是意图驱动你穿越空间，并存在于你所有身体运动背后。你使用船只、汽车、火车、飞机，因为你想去另一个地方，而某些交通工具在特定条件下最为适用。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 721
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: You are not alone in physical reality, so obviously your picture of the world is also affected by the world views of others, and you play a part in their experiences. There is a constant waking give-and-take. The same give-and-take occurs in the dream state, however. You affect your world through your dreams, then, as much as you do through your waking activities. In terms of time, lapses had to occur as various species physically matured and developed. They did so in response to inner impetus. The many languages that are now known originated in what you can call, from your point of view, nonwaking reality. Words, again, are related to the neurological structure, and languages follow that pattern. In the dream state many kinds of communication occur, and there are inner translations. Two people with different languages can speak together quite clearly in certain dreams, and understand each other perfectly. They may each translate the communication into their familiar language.
quotes.translation: 你在物质实相中并非孤身一人，因此你对世界的看法显然也受到他人世界观的影响，而你在他们的经验中也扮演着角色。清醒状态中存在着持续的给予与接受。然而，同样的给予与接受也发生在梦境状态中。因此，你通过梦境影响你的世界，其程度不亚于通过清醒活动的影响。就时间而言，随着各种物种在物质上成熟和发展，必须经历时间的流逝。它们是对内在驱力的回应而如此发展的。从你的观点来看，如今已知的许多语言起源于你们可称为非清醒实相的状态。词语再次与神经结构相关，语言遵循这一模式。在梦境状态中，存在多种沟通方式，并有内在的翻译。两个使用不同语言的人在某些梦境中可以清晰地交谈，并完全理解对方。他们可能各自将沟通内容翻译成自己熟悉的语言。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 723
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: The freshness of dream experience lies in its direct nature. Your cultural world view does not have any clear understanding of the nature of dreams, so that their direct, clear expression is not recalled often in the morning. (Pause.) At night you tune in to dreaming reality simply by closing out so-called waking reality, but the same kind of dream experience continues beneath your focus in waking life. Dreaming, you are still aware of your daily experience, but it is seemingly peripheral. Waking, your dream experience is peripheral also, but you are less aware of that condition. Both together represent the dimensions of your consciousness, and they exist simultaneously. You can and often do work out in dreams the challenges of daily life. In waking life you are also working out challenges set for yourselves in the dream state. Obviously, then, your consciousness is equipped to function in the known and unknown realities, and the divisions that you have set up are quite arbitrary.
quotes.translation: 梦境经验的新鲜感在于其直接性。你的文化世界观对梦的本质缺乏清晰理解，因此其直接、清晰的表达在早晨往往不被记起。（停顿）夜晚，你仅通过排除所谓的清醒实相来调谐进入梦境实相，但同样的梦境经验在清醒生活中仍持续存在于你的焦点之下。做梦时，你仍意识到日常经验，但它看似是边缘的。清醒时，你的梦境经验也是边缘的，但你对此状态的认识更少。两者共同代表你意识的维度，且它们同时存在。你可以在梦境中解决日常生活的挑战，且经常如此。在清醒生活中，你也在解决自己在梦境状态中设定的挑战。因此，显然你的意识装备齐全，能在已知与未知实相中运作，而你所设定的划分完全是武断的。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 723
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Past associations merge with present reality and form a pattern. Mentally, a part of you actually starts out upon each street -- a projected mental image. Period. As you stand there, then, in this case two such projected images go out onto streets Three and Four. To some extent these images experience "what will happen" if you yourself take one direction or the other. That information is returned to you instantaneously, and you make your decision accordingly. Say you choose Street Four. Physically you begin to walk in that direction. Street Four becomes your physical reality. You accept that experience in your prime sequence of events. You have, however, already sent out an energized mental image of yourself into Street Three, and you cannot withdraw that energy.
quotes.translation: 过去的联想与当前实相融合，形成一种模式。在心理上，你的某一部分实际上会投射到每条街道上——一个投射的心理影像。句号。当你站在那里时，在这种情况下，两个这样的投射影像会分别前往第三街和第四街。在某种程度上，这些影像会体验"如果你自己选择某个方向将会发生什么"。这些信息会即时返回给你，你据此做出决定。假设你选择了第四街。你开始在物理上朝那个方向行走。第四街成为你的物质实相。你在主要事件序列中接受那个经验。然而，你已经将自身的一个充满能量的心理影像发送到第三街，并且你无法撤回那股能量。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 741
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: In your reality, the "Unknown" Reality we have just finished is the only version of that manuscript. Instead it is, of course, the only version you recognize. When we are working on such a project here (in your reality), we are working on probable books also, and those are as real as your official one. In ways too difficult to explain now, your probabilities are connected by certain themes, intents, purposes. Some of these appear as subsidiary interests in your own lives, for example. Others may well be recognized by you as prime concerns, and still others may be so latent that you are unaware of them. So we have been working on a probable "Unknown" Reality -- in fact, on many probable "Unknown" Realities. Not mere versions, but variations.
quotes.translation: 在你的实相中，我们刚刚完成的《未知的实相》是该手稿的唯一版本。实际上，它当然是你唯一认可的版本。当我们在此（你的实相中）从事这样的计划时，我们也在处理可能的书籍，而那些书籍与你官方版本一样真实。以现在难以解释的方式，你的可能性通过某些主题、意图、目的相连。例如，其中一些以次要兴趣出现在你自己的生活中。另一些你可能认为是主要关注点，而还有一些可能如此潜在，以至于你毫无察觉。因此，我们一直在处理一个可能的《未知的实相》——事实上，是许多可能的《未知的实相》。不仅仅是版本，而是变奏。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 744
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: The creative act is your closest experience to direct cognition. While your consciousness thinks of itself in physical terms, whether you are living or dead, then you will still largely utilize thinking patterns with which you are familiar.
