Your consciousness is cellularly attuned in life, in that it perceives its own reality through cellular function that forms the bodily apparatus. The psyche is larger than that physically attuned consciousness, however. It is the larger context in which you exist. It is intertwined with your own reality as you think of it. On those occasions when you are able to alter your focus momentarily, then the psyche's greater experiences come into play. You are able to at least sense your existence apart from its cellular orientation. The experience, however, is circular, and therefore very difficult to verbalize or to organize into your normal patterns of information.
quotes.translation: 创造行为是你最接近直接认知的经验。当你的意识以物质角度思考自身时，无论你认为自己是生是死，你仍会大量运用熟悉的思维模式。你的意识在生命中是细胞性地调谐的，因为它通过构成身体装置的细胞功能来感知自身的实相。然而，心理体比那个物质调谐的意识更为广大。它是你存在的更大脉络。它与你所认为的自身实相交织在一起。当你能够暂时改变焦点时，心理体的更大经验就会发挥作用。你至少能够感知到脱离细胞取向的自身存在。然而，这种经验是循环性的，因此极难用言语表达或组织成你惯常的信息模式。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Consciousness within the body knows that its existence is within the body's context, and apart from it at the same time. In ordinary life during the day consciousness often takes a recess, so to speak -- it daydreams, or otherwise experiences itself as somewhat apart from the body's reality. At night, in sleep, the self's consciousness takes longer, freer recesses from physical reality, and does this as spontaneously as the body itself walks. These experiences are not hypothetical. They happen to each person. On such occasions, each person is to some extent aware of a kind of comprehension that is not dependent upon the accumulation of data, but of a deeper kind of experience and direct encounter with the reality from which the world emerges.
quotes.translation: 身体内的意识知道自己的存在既在身体脉络之内，又同时独立于它。在日间的寻常生活中，意识常常可以说是在休息——它做白日梦，或以其他方式体验自身与身体实相稍作分离。夜晚睡眠时，自我意识从物质实相中取得更长、更自由的休息，其自发性如同身体行走一般。这些经验并非假设。它们发生在每个人身上。在那些时刻，每个人都多少意识到一种不依赖数据积累的理解，而是一种更深层的经验，直接与世界从中涌现的实相相遇。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Mountains rose and tumbled. Oceans filled. Tidal waves thundered. Islands appeared. The seasons themselves were not stable. In your terms the magnetic fields themselves fluctuated--but all of the species were there at the beginning, though in the same fashion, for as the dream world broke through into physical reality there was all of the tumultuous excitement and confusion with which a mass creative event is achieved. There was much greater plasticity, motion, variety, give-and-take, as consciousness experimented with its own forms. The species and environment together formed themselves in concert, in glorious combination, so that each fulfilled the requirements of its own existence while adding to the fulfillment of all other portions of physical reality (all very intently, and with many gestures).
quotes.translation: 山脉升起又崩塌。海洋充盈。海啸轰鸣。岛屿出现。季节本身并不稳定。用你们的话说，磁场本身也在波动——但所有物种一开始就存在，尽管以同样的方式，因为当梦境世界突破进入物质实相时，伴随着一场大规模创造事件得以完成的所有喧嚣兴奋与混乱。存在着更大的可塑性、运动、多样性、互动，因为意识在实验自己的形式。物种与环境共同和谐地形成自身，以辉煌的组合方式，使得每个物种满足自身存在的需求，同时增添物质实相所有其他部分的圆满（这一切都非常专注，并伴随着许多姿态）。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You are subjectively "alive" before your birth. You will be subjectively alive after your death. Your subjective life is now interpreted through the specialized state of consciousness that you call the waking one, in which you recognize as real only experience that falls within certain space and time coordinates. Your greater reality exists outside those coordinates, and so does the reality of the universe. (Pause.) You create lives for yourselves, changing them as you go along, as a writer might change a book, altering the circumstances, changing the plots. The writer only knows that he or she creates without understanding the spontaneous order with which the creativity happens. The processes occur at another level of consciousness (underlined).
quotes.translation: 你在出生前就主观地"活着"。你在死后也将主观地活着。你的主观生命现在通过你称为清醒状态的特殊意识状态来诠释，在这种状态中，你只承认落在特定时空坐标内的经验为真实。你的更大实相存在于那些坐标之外，宇宙的实相也是如此。（停顿。）你们为自己创造生命，在过程中不断改变它们，如同作家可能修改一本书，改变情境，变换情节。作家只知道自己在创造，却不了解创造力发生的自发秩序。这些过程发生在另一个意识层面（强调）。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Those "properties" are the faculties of the imagination, creativity, telepathy, clairvoyance, and dreaming, as well as the functions of logic and reason. You know that you dream. You know that you think. Those are direct experiences. (Pause.) Anytime you use instruments to probe into the nature of reality, you are looking at a kind of secondary evidence, no matter how excellent the instruments may be. The subjective evidence of dreaming, for example, is far more "convincing" and irrefutable than is the evidence for an expanding universe, black holes, or even atoms and molecules themselves. Although instruments can indeed be most advantageous in many ways, they still present you with secondary rather than primary tools of investigation--and they distort the nature of reality far more than the subjective attributes of thoughts, feelings, and intuitions do.
quotes.translation: 那些"属性"是想象力、创造力、心灵感应、千里眼和做梦的能力，以及逻辑和理性的功能。你知道自己做梦。你知道自己思考。这些都是直接经验。（停顿。）每当你使用仪器探究实相的本质时，你看到的是某种间接证据，无论仪器多么精良。例如，做梦的主观证据远比膨胀宇宙、黑洞甚至原子和分子本身的证据更"令人信服"且无可辩驳。尽管仪器在许多方面确实极为有利，但它们呈现给你的仍是间接而非直接的调查工具——而且它们对实相本质的扭曲远甚于思想、感受和直觉的主观属性。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Ironically, as individuals and nations we talk about casting off old beliefs while cherishing them as long as possible. Why have large segments of consciousness chosen to operate in such a fashion? I think we're creating a probable reality in which consciousness has the absolute freedom to explore all facets of itself--every one we can think of, and therefore create. Within our national orientations, within our religious and secular, scientific and artistic structures, we are choosing to go to the extremes of "good" and "bad," and to deal with the consequences, all stewing together in what seems like an impossible mix of reason and emotion, learning and joy, pain and violence, and life and death. Naturally, many of us don't like certain facets of our creations, yet we must deal with all of them if we are to make any sense out of our reality. Otherwise, our growing will be too limited; we'll remain slaves to our animosities.
quotes.translation: 讽刺的是，作为个人和国家，我们谈论着抛弃旧信念，同时却尽可能长久地珍视它们。为什么意识的大部分选择以这种方式运作？我认为我们正在创造一个可能性实相，其中意识拥有绝对自由去探索自身的所有面向——每一个我们能想到的，并因此创造。在我们国家的取向中，在我们宗教与世俗、科学与艺术的结构中，我们选择走向"善"与"恶"的极端，并处理其后果，所有这一切都在看似不可能的理性与情感、学习与喜悦、痛苦与暴力、生与死的混合中一同煎熬。自然，我们许多人并不喜欢自己创造的某些面向，但如果我们想从自己的实相中找出任何意义，就必须处理所有面向。否则，我们的成长将过于受限；我们将继续成为自身敌意的奴隶。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: The ordinary violence involved with these events leads me to comment upon the theological concept of privation theory, and the military one of perception theory--for again, I think the two are closely related, not only to each other but to the points I've made in this note. In mundane terms, both represent longstanding distortions in perception of the great basic creativity of All That Is. I suggest that the reader review Seth's material on the basically creative use of violence as I quoted it in Chapter 10; see Note 2 for Session 933.

quotes.text: The material in these last sessions will be basic for further discussions. We are going much deeper into the meaning of reality, and the aspects of reality. Our present procedure is working out very well, and it is enabling me to discuss matters that were too complicated for our earlier method. Before we close I want you to understand that your experiments in psychological time add to your mobility and subconscious manipulation within the electrical universe. And all psychic experience must have reality here, that is, within the electrical system. There is no psychic experience which does not have such electric reality. Your psychological experiments give you familiar ground there to walk upon, to travel on; grounds of reference and even of safety.
quotes.translation: 这些最后几节的材料将成为后续讨论的基础。我们将更深入地探讨实相的意义及其各个面向。目前的方法进展顺利，使我能够讨论早期方法难以处理的复杂议题。在结束前，我希望你们明白：心理时间的实验能增强你们在电性宇宙中的机动性与潜意识操控能力。所有心灵经验都必须在此——即电性系统内——具有实相。没有任何心灵经验不具备这种电性实相。你们的心理实验为你们提供了熟悉的基础，可供行走、旅行；提供了参照系，甚至安全的保障。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The entity should therefore not be thought of as completed. The electric universe, of which you know so little, is a reality, and yet it is also a symbol of another reality. For behind even this electrical universe there is a reality which cannot be explored in terms of speech; for all consciousness, while having an electric reality, has a reality beyond even this. It is the vitality of the universe, and all universes, which causes the electrical reality, and the vitality of the universe, composing everything that is, cannot be touched. It is touch, but touch not as it is conceived of in your terms. This vitality is the most immediate and intimate aspect that composes the camouflage appearance within all fields. And yet it is by far more than the camouflage.
quotes.translation: 因此，不应将存有视为已完成之物。你们知之甚少的电性宇宙是一种实相，但它同时也是另一种实相的象征。因为即便在这电性宇宙背后，仍存在无法用言语探索的实相；所有意识虽具有电性实相，却拥有超越此层面的实相。正是宇宙及所有宇宙的生命力造就了电性实相，而构成万物的宇宙生命力本身无法被触及。它是触觉本身，却非你们概念中的触觉。这种生命力是构成所有场域中伪装表象最直接、最内在的面向。然而它远不止是伪装。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Action is more like growth than force. It is a by-product of any reality, and a part of all reality. You should be able to see many implications here when you read this material over. Again, action involves more than movement, as you think of movement, for value fulfillment is action. A dream involves action. Not only the action within the dream, but the action of dreaming itself. You may here get a glimmering of the connection between certain types of action and distance, as I have mentioned it briefly. There is always action within action, and any reality or any experience is instantaneous action. Motion is the type of action with which you are most familiar, but motion attains its importance within the physical field only because of your particular outer senses. For much action is entirely unperceived by you, particularly on a conscious level.
quotes.translation: 行动更像成长而非力量。它是任何实相的副产品，也是所有实相的一部分。当你们重读这些材料时，应能从中看出诸多意涵。再次强调，行动不仅包含你们所理解的移动，因为价值完成本身就是行动。梦境包含行动——不仅是梦中的行动，更是做梦这个行为本身。在此你们或许能隐约窥见某些类型的行动与距离之间的关联，正如我先前简要提及的。行动之中永远存在行动，任何实相或经验都是瞬间的行动。运动是你们最熟悉的行动类型，但运动在物质场域中的重要性仅源于你们特定的外在感官。因为许多行动完全不被你们感知，尤其在意识层面。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Now I have told you that you may legitimately visit not only the past, present and future as it existed, or will exist in physical terms; but you may also visit realities which never existed in physical terms. In our earliest sessions I emphasized that the intensity regulated the duration of an experience. Now, many events that were only imagined and never took place, physically speaking, many such events still exist. They are simply not a part of your definition of reality. You may therefore visit a museum which was planned in the 16th century, but never built. Such a museum has a reality as real, you see, as the house in which you live. Ordinarily you only perceive physical reality. In projections you may visit other realities such as these also--which you may be tempted to call imaginary, but they are not.
quotes.translation: 我曾告诉你们，你们不仅可以合法造访过去、现在与未来——无论是物质层面已存在或将存在的时空；更可造访从未在物质层面存在的实相。在早期课程中，我强调强度决定了经验的持续时间。如今，许多仅被想象而从未在物质层面发生的事件依然存在。它们只是不在你们对实相的定义范畴内。因此，你们可以造访一座16世纪规划却从未建造的博物馆。这座博物馆的实相，与你们居住的房屋同样真实。通常你们只感知物质实相。在投射中，你们也能造访这类实相——你们或许会称之为"想象"，但它们并非如此。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: You must learn to use this mechanism consciously for your own purposes, for it is extremely handy. Many of you do not pay attention to your own experience, subjectively speaking, so you drift in and out of clear focus in this reality, barely realizing it. Often your daily program is not nearly as clear or well-focused as it should be, but full of static; and while this may annoy you, you often put up with it or even become so used to the lack of harmony that you forget what a clear reception is like. However, in this world you are surrounded by familiar objects, details, and ideas, and your main orientation is physical so that you can operate through habit alone even when you are not as well focused within your reality as you should be.
quotes.translation: 你们必须学会有意识地运用这个机制为己所用，因为它极为便利。从主观角度而言，许多人并未关注自身经验，因此在这实相中清晰聚焦与模糊游离交替出现，却几乎未曾察觉。你们的日常程序往往缺乏应有的清晰度与聚焦，充斥着杂讯；尽管这令人困扰，你们却常默默忍受，甚至逐渐习惯这种不和谐，以致忘记清晰接收的状态为何。然而在这个世界，你们被熟悉的事物、细节与观念环绕，主要取向是物质性的，因此即便未能在实相中充分聚焦，仍可凭习惯运作。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Each particular "station" of consciousness perceives in a different kind of reality, and as mentioned earlier (in Session 711, for instance), you usually tune in to your home station most of the time. If you turn your focus only slightly away, the world appears differently; and if that slightly altered focus were the predominant one, then that is how the world would seem to be. Each aspect of the psyche perceives the reality upon which it is focused, and that reality is also the materialization of a particular state of the psyche projected outward. You can learn to encounter other realities by altering your position within your own psyche.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: As the intense experience of great joy or pain often blots out everything else, so would the direct experience of reality blot out, only completely, the outer ego who experienced it directly. At your level the sieve of the subconscious is a necessity and that is one of the main reasons why Ruburt dissociates during our sessions, even though his experience of inner reality is received second handed, so to speak, through me.
quotes.translation: 正如极乐或剧痛的强烈体验常会抹去其他一切，对实相的直接体验也会彻底抹去——且是彻底地——那个直接体验它的外在自我。在你们的层面，潜意识的筛网是必要的，这也是鲁柏在我们课程中必须解离的主要原因——即便他对内在实相的体验，可以说是通过我间接接收的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Ruburt has been working with alterations of consciousness (for Psychic Politics), and wondering about the basic validity of religion. He has been trying to reconcile intellectual and emotional knowledge. James is far from one of his favorite writers, yet Ruburt's interests, intent, and desire were close enough so that under certain conditions he could experience the world view held by James. The unknown reality is unknown only because you believe it must be hidden. Once that belief is annihilated, the other quite-as-legitimate views of reality can appear to your consciousness, and worlds just as valid as your own swim into view.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏一直在处理意识的变化（为《心灵政治》一书做准备），并思考宗教的基本有效性。他试图调和理智知识与情感知识。詹姆斯远非他最喜欢的作家之一，但鲁柏的兴趣、意图和渴望足够接近，以至于在某些条件下，他能体验到詹姆斯所持的世界观。未知的现实之所以未知，只因为你相信它必定是隐藏的。一旦那个信念被摧毁，其他同样合理的现实观点就能出现在你的意识中，与你自己的世界同样真实的世界便会浮现眼前。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: I said (in sessions 711 and 716, for instance) that your normal focus of consciousness can be compared to your home station. So far, exercises have been described that will gently lead you away from concentration upon this home base, even while its structure is strengthened at the same time. You can also call this home station or local program your world view, since from it you perceive your reality. To some extent it represents your personal focus, through which you interpret most of your experience. As I mentioned (in Session 715, for instance), when you begin to move away from that particular organization, strange things may start to happen. You may be filled with wonder, excitement, or perplexity. You may be delighted or appalled, according to whether or not your new perceptions agree or disagree with your established world view.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Each character, however, or portion of a scene, would represent in fragmented form another quite valid program [or reality, in brackets]. In the dream state, then, you are sometimes aware of too many stations. When you try to make them fit into your recognized picture of reality, they may seem chaotic. There are ways to bring the picture into focus: There are ways to tune into those other quite-natural frequencies, so that they present you with a more expansive view both of the world as you define it, and of its greater aspects. Period. The psyche is not encased, in your case (amused), within a frame too fragile to express it. Only your beliefs about the psyche and about the body limit your experience to its present degree.
quotes.translation: 然而，每个角色或场景的一部分，都会以碎片化的形式代表另一个同样有效的程序[或现实，括号内]。因此，在梦境状态中，你有时会意识到太多的频道。当你试图让它们符合你认可的现实画面时，它们可能显得混乱。有办法让画面聚焦：有办法调谐到那些同样自然的频率，使它们为你呈现一个更广阔的世界观，既包括你定义的世界，也包括其更宏大的方面。句号。心灵并非被禁锢在一个过于脆弱而无法表达它的框架内，就你的情况而言（逗趣地）。只有你对心灵和身体的信念，才将你的体验限制在目前的程度。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Session 757 was devoted to other matters that Jane and I were interested in.)

quotes.text: You make your own reality. So, programmed ahead of time, they perceived [data] according to the psychic conventions that had been established. There are tigers in Asia, but you can travel through Asia and if you do not want to you'll never see a tiger. It's according to where you go. In the unknown reality your thoughts are instantly made apparent and real, materialized according to your beliefs. There, if you believe in demons, you will see them -- without ever realizing that they are part of the environment of your psyche, formed by your beliefs, and thrown out as mirages over a very real environment that you do not perceive. You will believe the psychic tour books and go hunting for demons instead of tigers.
quotes.translation: 你创造你自己的现实。因此，预先编程好的他们根据已建立的心灵惯例来感知[数据]。亚洲有老虎，但你可以穿越亚洲，如果你不想，就永远不会看到老虎。这取决于你去哪里。在未知的现实中，你的思想会立即变得明显而真实，根据你的信念物质化。在那里，如果你相信恶魔，你就会看到它们——却从未意识到它们是你心灵环境的一部分，由你的信念形成，并像海市蜃楼一样投射在你未曾感知的非常真实的环境之上。你会相信心灵旅游指南，去猎寻恶魔而不是老虎。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (There were clear-cut connections between her creative performance today and her reception of the outline for The Way Toward Health last March; see the notes at 10:45, as well as Note 8, for Session 713; and in Volume 1, see Appendix 7. But there was even more creative expression to come through Jane this evening, not only in the session itself of course, but after it, as I try to explain in the concluding notes.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: There is no end to our environment. In your terms there would be no lack of space or time in which to operate. Now this would put tremendous pressure on any consciousness without proper background and development. We do not have one simple, cozy universe in which to hide. We are still alert to other quite alien systems of reality that flash on the very outskirts of consciousness as we know it. There are far more various kinds of consciousness than there are physical forms, each with its own patterns of perception, dwelling within its own camouflage system. Yet all of these have inner knowledge of the reality that exists within all camouflage and that composes any reality, by whatever name it is called.
quotes.translation: 我们的环境没有尽头。用你们的话说，不会有缺乏操作空间或时间的情况。这会给任何没有适当背景和发展的意识带来巨大压力。我们没有一个简单、舒适的宇宙可以躲藏。我们仍然对意识边缘闪现的其他完全陌生的现实系统保持警觉，正如我们所知的那样。存在的意识种类远比物质形态多得多，每种都有其自身的感知模式，居住在自己的伪装系统内。然而，所有这些意识都内在知晓存在于所有伪装之内的现实，以及构成任何现实的东西，无论它被称为什么名字。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now it is easier perhaps for some of you to understand the simple stories and parables of beginnings of which I have spoken. But the time has come for mankind to take several steps further, to expand the nature of his own consciousness by trying to comprehend a more profound version of reality. You have outgrown the time of children's tales. When your own thoughts have a form and reality, when they have validity even in other systems of reality of which you are unaware, then it is not difficult to understand why other systems of probabilities are also affected by your own thoughts and emotions -- nor why the actions of the probable gods are not affected by what happens in other dimensions of existence.
quotes.translation: 现在，你们中有些人也许更容易理解我讲过的那些关于开端的简单故事和寓言。但人类是时候再迈出几步，通过试图理解一个更深刻的现实版本，来扩展自身意识的本质。你们已经超越了童话故事的时代。当你们自己的思想具有形式和现实，甚至在你们未曾意识到的其他现实系统中也具有有效性时，就不难理解为什么其他概率系统也会受到你们自己思想和情感的影响——也不难理解为什么可能的神祇们的行动不受其他存在维度中发生的事情的影响。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: If you are normally capable of dealing with physical reality, you will encounter no difficulties in alterations of consciousness, or leaving your home station. Be reasonable, however: If you have difficulties in New York City, you are most apt to encounter them in a different form no matter where else you might travel. A change of environment might help clear your head by altering your usual orientation, so that you can see yourself more clearly, and benefit. The same applies when you leave your home station. Here the possible benefits are far greater than in usual life and travel, but you are still yourself. It is impossible not to structure reality in some fashion. Reality implies a structuring.
quotes.translation: 如果你通常能够处理物质现实，你在意识变化或离开你的本营时就不会遇到困难。然而，要合理：如果你在纽约市遇到困难，那么无论你旅行到哪里，你都很可能以不同的形式遇到它们。改变环境可能通过改变你惯常的取向来帮助你理清思绪，这样你就能更清楚地看到自己，并从中受益。当你离开本营时也是如此。这里可能的益处远大于平常生活和旅行，但你仍然是你自己。不可能不以某种方式构建现实。现实本身就意味着一种构建。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 719
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: I am trying to tell you that numbers are only symbols for your kind of reality. They are not multidimensional symbols. Your physicists can theorize to a certain point. The blockage occurs because the symbols used to portray reality are themselves limited by the reality that you know. They are like keys that will only fit your own doors. You can open endless doors with them, but only doors within your own system.
quotes.translation: 我试图告诉你，数字只是你们这种实相世界的符号。它们并非多维符号。你们的物理学家可以理论化到某个程度。阻碍之所以发生，是因为用来描绘实相的符号本身就被你们所知的实相所限制。它们就像只能打开你们自己房门的钥匙——虽然可以开启无数扇门，但始终局限于你们自己的系统之内。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Now, indeed you are. And... as a piece or chunk of physical matter... You are indeed immediate and here. You do not understand the ways in which you project the physical matter of yourself into this room, however. When you understand that completely and fully, you will no longer be within physical reality. But that is of little notice. You will never notice the difference. In any reality, you create the image that you see. And the reality that follows this one will seem as physical to you as this... and as real. But you will have freedoms within it that you do not have now.... Not unless at 8 o'clock in the morning you leap from the rooftops and fly through the windows to your death.
quotes.translation: 此刻你确实在此。作为一块物理物质...你确实即时存在于这里。然而你并不理解自己是如何将物理性的自我投射到这个房间的。当你完全彻底理解这一点时，你将不再处于物理实相之中。但这无关紧要——你永远不会察觉其中的差异。在任何实相中，你都在创造你所见的影像。紧随其后的实相对你而言将如当前这般物理化...这般真实。但你在其中将拥有现在所没有的自由...除非你在清晨八点从屋顶跃下，穿过窗户坠向死亡。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... Your dream experience represents a pivotal reality, like the center of a wheel. Your physical world is one spoke. You are united with all of your other simultaneous existences through the nature of the dream state. The unknown reality is there presented to your view, and there is no biological, mental, or psychic reason why you cannot learn to use and understand your own dreaming reality.
quotes.translation: 请稍候...你的梦境体验代表着一个枢纽实相，如同车轮的中心。你的物质世界是其中一根轮辐。你通过梦境的本质与所有其他同时存在的自我相连。未知的实相就在那里向你展现，没有任何生物、心智或灵性理由能阻止你学习运用和理解自己的梦境实相。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 721
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: I am a personified energy source -- but so are you. I have had many lives, in your terms, yet in other terms I have not lived physically, but rather lent or loaned my energy to lives that rose from my reality but were not me. In the same way you give birth to dream images of your own -- hardly aware that you have done so, unconscious of the fact that you have provided impetus for a kind of psychological reality that quite escapes your notice. The dream stories you begin continue on their own. No dream is stillborn. Each chapter of this book is written in such a way that the ideas presented will activate your own intuitions, and open pathways between your dreaming and waking states.
quotes.translation: 我是人格化的能量源头——但你也是。用你们的话说，我经历过许多世生命，但从其他角度而言，我从未以物理形态存在过，而是将能量借予那些源自我的实相却非我本身的生命。正如你孕育自己的梦境影像——几乎意识不到自己已这么做，也未曾察觉你为某种完全逃过你注意的心理实相提供了动力。你开启的梦境故事会自行延续。没有哪个梦是死胎。本书每章都以能激活你直觉的方式撰写，并打通你梦境与清醒状态之间的通道。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: He sees more than you do, or more than his mother does, because he does not yet realize that you only accept certain patterns and reject others. By the time he is born he has already learned to accept his parents' idea of what reality is. In a large sensehe begins to train himself to focus only upon what you would call physical reality, though he still partially perceives other fields that you do not accept.
quotes.translation: 他比你们或他母亲看得更多，因为他尚未意识到你们只接受特定模式而排斥其他。到他出生时，他已学会接受父母对实相的定义。从广义上说，他开始训练自己只聚焦于你们所谓的物理实相，尽管他仍能部分感知你们不接纳的其他场域。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Many of these we have discussed in our sessions, and there is no time now to tell you. Look where nothing seems to be, for no place is truly empty. Where there appears to be nothing, there will be no distortion. Within that seeming nothingness, reality can show itself if you know how to look. That which appears full, that space which appears full, is misleading, for reality is already given to a rigid form.
quotes.translation: 我们在课程中讨论过许多这类内容，现在无暇细述。请看向看似空无之处，因为没有任何空间是真正空虚的。在看似空无的地方，将没有扭曲。在那片看似虚无之中，若你懂得如何观看，实相便会显现。那些看似充盈的空间、看似饱满的场域反而具有误导性，因为实相已被固化为僵硬的形态。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: There may be other terms I could use, in some ways more advantageous than the term, "the dream world." I am emphasizing this dream connection, however, because the dream state is one familiar to each reader, and it represents your closest touchstone to the kind of subjective reality from which your physical world emerges. The dream state appears chaotic, shadowy, suspicious, or even meaningless, precisely because in life you are so brilliantly focused in daily reality that dreams appear to be staticky objective background noise, left over from when you sleep. But that is how physical experience would seem to someone not focused in it, or inexperienced with its organization.
quotes.translation: 或许有其他术语在某些方面比"梦境世界"更合适。但我之所以强调梦境关联，是因为每个读者都熟悉梦境状态，它代表着你最接近那种主观现实的试金石——你的物质世界正是从这种主观现实中浮现的。梦境看似混乱、朦胧、可疑甚至毫无意义，恰恰因为你在清醒时如此专注地聚焦于日常现实，以至于梦境在你眼中不过是睡眠时残留的、充满杂音的客观背景噪音。但若有人未曾聚焦于物质体验，或对其组织方式不熟悉，物质体验在他们眼中也会如此。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In a reality that is inconceivably multidimensional, the old concepts of God are relatively meaningless. Even the term, a supreme being, is in itself distortive, for you naturally project the qualities of human nature upon it. If I told you that God was an idea, you would not understand what I meant, for you do not understand the dimensions in which an idea has its reality, or the energy that it can originate and propel. You do not believe in ideas in the same way that you believe in physical objects, so if I tell you that God is an idea, you will misinterpret this to mean that God is less than real -- nebulous, without reality, without purpose, and without motive action.
quotes.translation: 在难以想象的多维现实中，关于上帝的旧有概念相对毫无意义。甚至"至高存在"这个术语本身就有扭曲性，因为你自然会用人性的特质投射其上。若我告诉你上帝是一个理念，你不会理解我的意思，因为你并不理解理念赖以存在的维度，也不理解理念所能激发和推动的能量。你对理念的信任程度不同于对物质实物的信任，所以若我告诉你上帝是理念，你会误解为上帝不够真实——虚无缥缈、缺乏实相、没有目的、没有动机行动。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: We gave you the pattern by which your physical selves are formed. We gave you the pattern by which you learned to form your physical reality. We gave you the patterns intricate, involved, and blessed from which you form the reality of each physical thing you know. The most minute cell within your brain has been made from the patterns of consciousness, which we have given you. We gave you the pattern upon which you formed your entire physical universe... and the comprehension that exists within each cell, the knowledge that each cell has, the desire for organization was given by us. The entire webwork was initiated by us. We taught you to form the reality that you know.
quotes.translation: 我们给了你形成物质自我的模式。我们给了你学习构建物质现实的模式。我们给了你那些精妙、复杂且蒙受祝福的模式，你从中构建出你所知的每件物质事物的实相。你脑中最微小的细胞都由我们给予的意识模式构成。我们给了你构建整个物质宇宙的模式……每个细胞内存在的理解力、每个细胞拥有的知识、对组织性的渴望，都由我们赋予。整个网络架构由我们启动。我们教导你构建你所知的实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Creativity connects waking and dreaming reality, and is in itself a threshold in which the waking and dreaming selves merge to form constructs that belong equally to each reality. You cannot begin to understand how you form the physical events of your lives unless you understand the connections between creativity, dreams, play, and those events that form your waking hours. In one respect dreams are a kind of structured unconscious play. Your mind dreams in joyful pleasure at using itself, freed from the concerns of practical living. Dreams are the mind's free play. The spontaneous activity, however, is at the same time training in the art of forming practical events.
quotes.translation: 创造力连接着清醒与梦境现实，它本身就是一个门槛，在此清醒与梦境的自我融合，形成同时属于两种现实的建构。除非你理解创造力、梦境、嬉戏与构成清醒时光的那些事件之间的关联，否则你无法开始理解自己如何构建生活中的物质事件。从某方面说，梦境是一种有结构的无意识嬉戏。你的心灵在运用自身时欢愉地做梦，摆脱了实际生活的挂虑。梦境是心灵的自由嬉戏。然而这种自发活动同时也是构建实际事件的艺术训练。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: As Ruburt said after reading the body of the book, the interior drama is the "real" one. Christ became the Crucified, Judas became the betrayer, though Christ was not crucified and Judas did not betray him. The reality, therefore, was in the myth. The reality was the myth. In such cases the interior events will always predominate, regardless of the physical facts, which are only symbols for those events.
quotes.translation: 正如鲁柏读完本书正文后所说，内在戏剧才是"真实"的。基督成了被钉十字架者，犹大成了背叛者——尽管基督并未被钉十字架，犹大也并未背叛他。因此，实相存在于神话之中。实相就是神话。在此类情形中，内在事件永远占据主导地位，无论物质事实如何——物质事实只是这些事件的象征。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: New paragraph: The blueprints for reality will not be found in the exterior universe. Some other civilizations experimented with a different kind of science than the one with which you are familiar. They met with varying degrees of success in their attempts to understand the nature of reality, and it is true that their overall goals were different than yours. Such people were focusing their consciousnesses in a completely different direction. Your own behavior, customs, sciences, arts, and disciplines are in a way uniquely yours, yet they also provide glimpses into the ways in which various groupings of abilities can be used to probe into the "unknown" reality.
quotes.translation: 新段落：实相的蓝图不会在外在宇宙中找到。有些其他文明实验过与你所熟悉的不同类型的科学。他们在理解实相本质的尝试中取得了不同程度的成功，而且他们的总体目标确实与你的不同。这类人将他们的意识聚焦在完全不同的方向上。你自己的行为、习俗、科学、艺术和学科在某种程度上是独一无二的，但它们也提供了窥见各种能力组合如何被用来探索"未知"实相的途径。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 700
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: As far as self and not-self are concerned, the unit of self is organized, as you know, by the inner ego, which directs the whole energy field. The outer ego directs the manipulation of this gestalt in the physical universe. The outer ego is rather more tied to physical properties, and yet it can directly experience inner reality by a change of focus through aligning itself with the inner ego, focusing its energies with the inner rather than the outer senses. This is by far the most advantageous method of experiencing inner reality, because the outer ego is therefore consciously aware of what has been going on, and can use such knowledge in its own sphere.
quotes.translation: 就自我与非我而言，自我单元是由内在自我组织的，如你所知，它引导着整个能量场。外在自我则指挥这个完形在物质宇宙中的运作。外在自我与物质属性关联较深，但它可以通过调整焦点，与内在自我对齐，将能量聚焦于内在而非外在感官，从而直接体验内在实相。这是体验内在实相最有利的方法，因为外在自我因此能清醒地意识到所发生的一切，并在其自身领域运用这些知识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: You must perceive what you do of reality through your physical senses, but your physical senses distort reality. They present reality to you in their own way. The physical senses can only perceive reality a little bit at a time, and so it seems to you that one moment exists, and is gone forever, and the next moment comes, and like the one before it disappears. But everything in the universe exists at one time, simultaneously, and the first words ever spoken still ring throughout the universe; and in your terms the last words ever spoken have been said time and time again, for there is no ending and no beginning. It is only your perception that is limited.
quotes.translation: 你必须通过物质感官来感知你所认知的实相，但物质感官会扭曲实相。它们以自身的方式向你呈现实相。物质感官每次只能感知实相的一小部分，因此对你而言，一个瞬间存在，然后永远消失，下一个瞬间到来，又像前一个一样消逝。但宇宙中的一切同时存在于一个时间点，最初说出的话语至今仍在宇宙中回响；而用你的话说，最后说出的话语已被反复言说无数次，因为既无终点也无起点。受限的只是你的感知。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

source_raw.title: (And once again, as she'd done for the session of last December 28, she wanted tonight's material to be presented as part of regular Session 393. I was glad to do it--and note that Jane's class sessions usually are in marked contrast to those we hold for ourselves, for books or whatever in the future. As would be expected of a gathering, class sessions were full of energy and repartee and questions and serious thinking both with and without Seth.

quotes.text: The medium perceives so clearly the reality of the surviving personality that the medium to some extent directly perceives that reality. You see the correlation here with what I told you in the past about experiencing concepts. That is one thing. To experience the reality of another does not necessarily mean that the medium negates her own personality--only that momentarily she allows it to perceive as directly as possible the experience of what it is to be the other. This involves a high degree of cooperation from the other person who does not exist in your physical terms, an opening of his reality to the medium rather than an invasion of the medium.
quotes.translation: 灵媒如此清晰地感知到幸存人格的实相，以至于灵媒在某种程度上直接感知到那个实相。你看到这与过去我告诉你的关于体验概念的内容之间的关联。这是一回事。体验他人的实相并不一定意味着灵媒否定自己的人格——只是暂时允许它尽可能直接地感知成为他人是什么体验。这需要那个不存在于你物质层面的人的高度配合，他向灵媒敞开自己的实相，而非入侵灵媒。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Those who perceive this set of data in such a way that agreement is reached are called sane. But none of you perceives the same reality. Those whose ideas and perceptions of reality are completely out of line with the general agreement are called insane. The terms only show that reality as you know it is the result of private perceptions. Reality is built up as a result of these private perceptions.
quotes.translation: 那些以达成共识的方式感知这组数据的人被称为神智正常。但你们中没有一个人感知到相同的实相。那些对实相的观念和感知完全偏离普遍共识的人被称为神智失常。这些术语只表明，你所知的实相是私人感知的结果。实相正是由这些私人感知构建而成的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Now, in the world you (to me) early formed your own beliefs and strategies. In midlife you were presented with our sessions -- or [the two of] you presented yourselves with them, if you prefer. You recognized the overall vitality of our material (long pause) -- but again, you did not realize that it meant a complete reorientation of your attitudes. You did not realize that you were being presented, not merely with an alternate view of reality, but with the closest approximation you could get of what reality was, and how it worked, and what it meant.
quotes.translation: 现在，在你们的世界里，你（对我而言）很早就形成了自己的信念和策略。中年时，你被呈现了我们的课程——或者如果你愿意，也可以说是你们自己呈现了这些课程。你认出了我们材料的整体活力（长停顿）——但同样，你没有意识到这意味着你态度的彻底重新定向。你没有意识到，你被呈现的不仅仅是对实相的另一种看法，而是你能获得的最接近实相本质、运作方式及其意义的近似描述。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: For much more Seth material on the counterpart concept, see Session 732 and Appendix 25 in Volume II of The "Unknown" Reality (Published by Amber-Allen Publishing, Inc., San Rafael, CA).

quotes.text: From the point of view of the perceiver these would be unofficial events, and yet they could serve as important clues to the nature of reality. The separate programs existing at once each have their own schedules, and from your reality you could not play them all at once. It seems to you that you are outside of the psyche, so you think of someone as yourself operating this radio from that external position. From your point of view you could not pick up the grocer's escapades and the symphony, for instance, if both came through at 8 o'clock in the evening, without switching from one to the other: You would have to choose which program you wanted.
quotes.translation: 从感知者的角度来看，这些是非官方事件，但它们可以作为理解实相本质的重要线索。同时存在的不同节目各有其时间表，从你的实相出发，你无法同时播放所有节目。在你看来，你似乎处于心灵之外，所以你认为有某个"你"从那个外部位置操作这台收音机。从你的角度看，例如，如果杂货店的冒险故事和交响乐都在晚上8点播放，你无法在不切换频道的情况下同时接收两者：你必须选择你想要哪个节目。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 711
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: I shall of course take advantage of the opportunity, but then you will have time to speak to me at your leisure. There are many points to be considered, and these matters certainly cannot be covered in one evening such as this. I hinted at our last discussion that it is indeed within the ability of the human personality to become aware of other realities while still keeping contact with physical reality. Manipulation in the physical universe is of course a necessity, but there are ways by which the human individual can become aware of other quite valid realities, and still maintain balance and control within his own more usual field of activity.
quotes.translation: 我当然会利用这个机会，但之后你会有闲暇时间与我交谈。有许多要点需要考量，这些事当然不可能在这样一个晚上全部涵盖。我在上次讨论中暗示过，人类人格确实有能力在保持与物质实相接触的同时，觉察到其他实相。在物质宇宙中运作固然是必要的，但人类个体仍有方法可以觉察到其他同样有效的实相，同时在自己较惯常的活动领域中维持平衡与控制。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: They can impress others with greater import through their communications. They can move from inner to outer reality with easy ability. They know instinctively how to use symbolism. They are highly creative on an unconscious level, constantly forming psychic frameworks beneath normal consciousness that can be used both by themselves and others in dream and trance states. They often appear to others in the dream condition, and they help dreamers in the manipulation of inner reality. They form images with which the dreamers can relate, images that can be used as bridges and then as gateways into kinds of consciousness more separated from your own.
quotes.translation: 他们能通过沟通以更重大的影响力打动他人。他们能轻松自如地从内在实相转向外在实相。他们本能地懂得如何运用象征符号。他们在无意识层面具有高度创造力，不断在正常意识之下建构心灵架构，这些架构可供他们自己及他人在梦境与出神状态中使用。他们常在梦境状态中出现在他人面前，并帮助做梦者运作内在实相。他们形成做梦者能够关联的意象，这些意象可作为桥梁，进而成为通往与你们自身意识更为分离的各类意识的通道。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: To close your eyes to them in an ignorant fashion, to wash your hands of them, so to speak, is equally shortsighted. To pretend such situations do not exist, out of fear of them, will only bring the feared reality closer. It is far better to situate yourself firmly in your own reality, acknowledge it as your own, encourage your strength and creativity, and from that vantage point view those areas of the world or of your own society that need constructive help. Purposefully in your own life, in your daily dialogues with others, in your relationships through your groups or clubs, reinforce as well as you can the strength and abilities of others.
quotes.translation: 以无知的方式对它们视而不见，或可说置身事外，同样是短视的。出于恐惧而假装这些情境不存在，只会让所恐惧的实相更接近。更好的做法是坚定地立足于自己的实相，承认它属于自己，激发自身的力量与创造力，并从这个有利位置观察世界或社会中需要建设性帮助的领域。在你自己的生活中，在与他人日常的对话中，通过团体或俱乐部的各种关系，尽你所能强化他人的力量与能力。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now, using an analogy only, let me explain that your thoughts and feelings also give off shadows (intently) that we will here call hallucinations.2 They are quite valid. They have as strong a part to play in dream reality as shadows do in the physical world. They are beautiful in themselves. They add to the entire picture. A shadow of a tree cools the ground. It affects the environment. So hallucinations alter the environment, but in a different way and at another level of reality. In the dream world hallucinations are like conscious shadows. They are not passive, nor is their shape dependent upon their origin. They have their own abilities.
quotes.translation: 现在，仅以类比方式说明，你们的思绪与情感也会投下阴影（专注地），我们在此称之为幻觉。它们是相当有效的。它们在梦境实相中所扮演的角色，就如同阴影在物质世界中所扮演的角色一样重要。它们本身是美丽的。它们为整体画面增添了色彩。一棵树的阴影能使地面凉爽。它影响环境。同样地，幻觉改变环境，但以不同的方式，在实相的另一层次上。在梦境世界中，幻觉就像有意识的阴影。它们不是被动的，它们的形状也不取决于其起源。它们拥有自身的能力。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 720
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: There are tests to pass before entry. There are rituals to be acted out. Now, all of this is highly distorted. Any attempt to so rigorously and precisely express inner reality is bound to be abortive, highly misleading, and in your terms sometimes dangerous; for you do create your own reality and live it according to your inner beliefs. Therefore, be careful also of those beliefs that you accept.
quotes.translation: 进入之前有考验要通过。有仪式要执行。现在，这一切都是高度扭曲的。任何试图如此严格而精确地表达内在实相的做法，都注定是徒劳的、极具误导性的，并且用你们的说法有时是危险的；因为你们确实创造了自己的实相，并根据内在信念来活出它。因此，也要小心你们所接受的信念。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The first Seth excerpt is in keeping with the idea of creating bridges between the two volumes of "Unknown" Reality by lifting something out of one for inclusion in the other. Once more from the 743rd session in Volume 2: "No book entitled The "Unknown" Reality can hope to make that reality entirely known. It remains nebulous because it is consciously unrealized. The best I can do is to point out areas that have been relatively invisible, to help you explore, actually, different facets of your own consciousness ... I am well aware that the book raises many more questions than it presents answers for, and this has been my intent...."
quotes.translation: 第一段赛斯摘录符合在《未知的实相》两卷之间建立桥梁的理念，即从一卷中提取内容纳入另一卷。再次引用第二卷第743节的内容："一本名为《未知的实相》的书，不可能指望让那实相完全被知晓。它仍然是模糊的，因为它是有意识地未被实现的。我所能做的最好的事，是指出那些相对不可见的领域，以帮助你们实际上探索自己意识的不同面向……我很清楚，这本书提出的问题远多于它给出的答案，而这正是我的意图……"
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Every so often I've thought of averaging Jane's dictation time for Seth Speaks and Personal Reality in the same way, but haven't done so. I'm somewhat puzzled to note, however, that her very short working times for the Seth books seem to be either ignored or taken for granted by practically everyone -- or, perhaps, those factors just aren't understood in terms of ordinary linear time. Maybe I'm alone in my interest here, for even Jane doesn't express any great curiosity about the time she has invested in the Seth material; she just delivers it. But given her abilities, I think her speed of production is a close physical approach to, or translation of, Seth's idea that basically all exists at once -- that really there is no time, and that the Seth books, for example, are "there" to be had in final form for just the tuning in. (In Section 3 of this volume, Note 2 for Session 692 contains information on another way by which we can move closer to Seth's idea of simultaneity from our physical reality, but that method grows out of material not discussed here.)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Now in your dream, you were quite clearly seeing the threshold between physical reality and the magical dimension in which that physical reality has its source. You were being shown -- or showing yourself -- the difference in the rules or assumptions between the two. The dog's desire for food led him to walk magically through the door, for the desires of the natural creature are satisfied (pause) with an ease that has nothing to do with your ideas of work. What I am getting at is the introduction of the concepts of a different kind of work -- very valuable, vital work that is performed at another level and in a different fashion.
quotes.translation: 在你的梦境中，你清晰地看见了物质实相与它源起的魔法维度之间的门槛。你被展示——或说向自己展示——两者规则或假设的差异。狗对食物的渴望让它魔法般穿过门，因为自然生物的欲望（停顿）以与你工作观念无关的轻松得到满足。我要引入的是另一种工作的概念——在另一层面以不同方式进行的、极具价值且至关重要的活力工作。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth speaks in Session 826, held for Mass Events on March 8, 1978:

quotes.text: On one level they could be said not to exist, and yet they do exist. While in your dream, you are able to see and touch and move about in these locations. It is only when you awake that they escape you. This should be considered along with our material on the expanding universe, since dream locations represent, certainly, a reality, even a framework that has no existence in your space; and measured purely along the lines used to measure your space, you would receive no hint at all of their existence or reality. Measured purely in terms of your camouflage conceptions, many things which you know to exist would seem not to exist.
quotes.translation: 在某一层面，它们可以说并不存在，然而它们确实存在。当你身处梦境时，你能够看见、触摸并在这些地点移动。只有当你醒来时，它们才离你而去。这一点应与我们关于宇宙膨胀的资料一并考虑，因为梦境地点无疑代表一种实相，甚至是一个在你空间中不存在的架构；若纯粹以测量你空间的标准来衡量，你完全不会得到它们存在或实相的线索。若纯粹以你的伪装概念来衡量，许多你知道存在的事物似乎并不存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Clear understanding or effective exploration of the unknown reality can be achieved only when you are able to leave behind you many "facts" that you have accepted as criteria of experience. "Unknown" Reality is also written in such a way that it will, I hope, bring many of your cherished beliefs about existence into question. Then you will be able to look even at this existence with new eyes.
quotes.translation: 只有当你能够放下许多你视为经验标准的"事实"时，才能清晰理解或有效探索未知实相。"未知"实相也以这样的方式书写，我希望它能让你许多珍视的存在信念受到质疑。这样你便能以新的眼光审视这存在本身。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 715
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: It is very possible for one dreamer who is a Speaker, to go to the aid of another individual who is having some difficulties in an inner reality within the dream state. The idea of guardian angels of course is highly connected here. A good Speaker is as effective within one reality as he is within the other, creating psychic frameworks within physical reality as well as within interior environments. Many artists, poets, and musicians are Speakers, translating one world in terms of another, forming psychic structures that exist in both with great vitality -- structures that may be perceived from more than one reality at once.
quotes.translation: 作为传讯者的做梦者，完全有可能去帮助另一个在梦境状态内在实相中遇到困难的个体。守护天使的概念自然与此高度相关。一位优秀的传讯者在一种实相中与在另一种实相中同样有效，在物质实相与内在环境中都创造心灵架构。许多艺术家、诗人与音乐家都是传讯者，以另一种世界的语言翻译这个世界，形成在两者中都充满活力地存在的心灵结构——这些结构可同时从不止一种实相中被感知。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It is quite true that your physical senses create the reality that they perceive. A tree is something far different to a microbe, a bird, an insect, and a man who stands beneath it. I am not saying that the tree only appears to be different. It is different. You perceive its reality through one set of highly specialized senses. This does not mean that its reality exists in that form in any more basic way than it exists in the form perceived by the microbe, insect, or bird. You cannot perceive the quite valid reality of that tree in any context but your own. This applies to anything within the physical system that you know.
quotes.translation: 确实，你的物质感官创造了它们所感知的实相。一棵树对微生物、鸟、昆虫和树下的人来说，是截然不同的存在。我并非说树只是看起来不同。它确实不同。你通过一套高度专门化的感官感知它的实相。这并不意味着它的实相以那种形式存在，比它以微生物、昆虫或鸟感知的形式更为基本。你无法在任何其他脉络中感知那棵树相当有效的实相，只能在你自己的脉络中。这适用于你所知的物质系统内的一切。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Since you have all kinds of thoughts there are reasons for having them, as you have all kinds of geography. Within your reality it is as foolish to deny the existence of certain thoughts as it would be, say, to pretend that deserts do not exist. In following such a course you deny dimensions of experience and diminish your reality. This does not mean that you have to collect what you think of as negative thoughts, any more than it means that you should spend a month in a desert if you do not like them. Period. It does mean that within nature as you understand it, nothing is meaningless or to be pretended out of existence.
quotes.translation: 既然你有各种思想，就有拥有它们的理由，正如你有各种地理。在你的实相中，否认某些思想的存在，就像假装沙漠不存在一样愚蠢。遵循这样的路线，你否定了经验的维度，削弱了你的实相。这并不意味着你必须收集你认为的负面思想，正如它并不意味着如果你不喜欢沙漠就必须在那里待一个月。句号。它确实意味着，在你所理解的自然中，没有什么是无意义的，或可以被假装不存在。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: No action basically is ever completed. This statement will later lead us into further realms. All possibilities are open to an action, an electrical action. And again, within it are an infinity of variations and depths of intensities, which give it a reality in many dimensions simultaneously. Where strong intensities are felt, then the actuality will be said to project within the field of reality which falls within the range of that particular intensity group.
quotes.translation: 任何行动本质上都从未完成。这句话将引领我们进入更深的领域。所有可能性都对一个行动敞开——一个电性行动。而在这行动之内，又蕴含着无限的变化与强度层次，使其同时在多个维度中具有实相。当感受到强烈强度时，其现实性便会被认为投射在属于该特定强度群组范围内的实相场域中。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Now some of you who read this book will immediately and intuitively grasp what I am saying, for you will have already suspected that you are viewing experience through highly distorted, though colorful, figurative lenses. Remember also that if physical reality is in a larger sense an illusion, it is an illusion caused by a greater reality. The illusion itself has a purpose and a meaning.
quotes.translation: 现在，阅读本书的各位中，有些人会立即凭直觉领会我的意思，因为你们早已怀疑自己正透过高度扭曲却色彩斑斓的具象化透镜来观察经验。也请记住：若说物质实相在更大意义上是幻相，那它也是由更伟大的实相所引发的幻相。这幻相本身自有其目的与意义。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The man who wrote wants to live largely in his own world. He hurts no one. He supports himself a good deal of the time. His view of reality is eccentric from most viewpoints. He adds a flavor to the world that would be missing otherwise, and through his very eccentricity, to some extent he shows other people that their rigid views of reality may indeed have chinks in them here and there.
quotes.translation: 那位写信者渴望主要活在自己的世界里。他不伤害任何人，大部分时间能自给自足。他对实相的看法在多数人眼中显得古怪。他为世界增添了原本会缺失的独特风味，而正是通过这种古怪，他在某种程度上向他人揭示：他们僵化的实相观确实可能存在处处裂隙。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You will in all cases attempt to construct as physical reality your inner conception of what reality is. Your physical environment and conditions are a mirror of your own basic conceptions of reality. If the environment changes it is because your inner conceptions have changed, and no smallest alteration is made within physical reality, that has not first been made within the inner self.
quotes.translation: 你们在任何情况下都会试图将内在对实相的概念建构为物质实相。你们的物质环境与状况，正是你们对实相基本概念的镜像。若环境改变，必因内在概念已然转变；物质实相中任何细微变化，无不是先在内在自我中发生。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: History, as you know it, represents but one single light upon which you focus. You interpret the events that you see therein, and you project upon its glimmer your interpretation of events that may occur. So entranced is your concentration, that when you wonder about the nature of reality you automatically confine your question to this one small flickering moment that you call physical reality. When you ponder upon the aspects of God, you unthinkingly speak of the creator of that one light. That light is unique, and if you truly understood what it was, you would indeed understand the nature of true reality.
quotes.translation: 你们所知的历史，仅代表你们聚焦的那一束光。你们诠释其中所见的事件，并将对可能发生事件的诠释投射于其微光之上。你们的专注如此着迷，以至于当你们思索实相本质时，会不自觉地只将问题局限在这个你们称为物质实相的短暂闪烁瞬间。当你们沉思神的面向时，会不假思索地谈论那束光的创造者。那束光独一无二——若你们真正理解它是什么，便确实能领悟真实实相的本质。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Such religious dramas focus, direct, and, hopefully, clarify aspects of inner reality that need to be physically represented. (Long pause, eyes closed.) These do not only appear within your own system. Many are also projected into other systems of reality. Religion per se, however, is always the external facade of inner reality. The primary spiritual existence alone gives meaning to the physical one. In the most real terms, religion should include all of the pursuits of man in his search for the nature of meaning and truth. Spirituality cannot be some isolated, specialized activity or characteristic.
quotes.translation: 这类宗教戏剧聚焦、引导并（但愿能）澄清需要以物质形式呈现的内在实相面向。（长停顿，闭目。）它们不仅出现在你们自己的系统中，许多也被投射到其他实相系统里。然而宗教本身始终是内在实相的外在表象。唯有原初灵性存在才能赋予物质存在意义。在最真实的意义上，宗教应包含人类探索意义与真理本质的所有追求。灵性绝不能是某种孤立、专门的活动或特质。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: "The 'Unknown' Reality itself is a product of the unknown reality of the mind, of course, since I produced it entirely in a trance state, as Seth. In a way the two volumes are the products of an inner psychic 'combustion' -- the spark that is lit in our world, as Seth's reality strikes mine -- or vice versa. For me, this is an accelerated state. I would compare it to a higher state of wakefulness rather than to the sleep usually associated with trance -- but a different kind of wakefulness, in which the usual world seems to be the one that is sleeping. My attention is not blunted. It is elsewhere.
quotes.translation: “未知”实相本身自然是心灵的未知实相的产物，因为我完全在出神状态中以赛斯的身份创作了它。在某种程度上，这两卷书是内在心灵“燃烧”的产物——那是在我们世界中点燃的火花，当赛斯的实相冲击我的实相时——反之亦然。对我来说，这是一种加速状态。我会将其比作更高的清醒状态，而非通常与出神关联的睡眠——但这是另一种清醒，在其中寻常世界反而像是沉睡的那个。我的注意力并未迟钝，它只是去了别处。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Every so often I've thought of averaging Jane's dictation time for Seth Speaks and Personal Reality in the same way, but haven't done so. I'm somewhat puzzled to note, however, that her very short working times for the Seth books seem to be either ignored or taken for granted by practically everyone -- or, perhaps, those factors just aren't understood in terms of ordinary linear time. Maybe I'm alone in my interest here, for even Jane doesn't express any great curiosity about the time she has invested in the Seth material; she just delivers it. But given her abilities, I think her speed of production is a close physical approach to, or translation of, Seth's idea that basically all exists at once -- that really there is no time, and that the Seth books, for example, are "there" to be had in final form for just the tuning in. (In Section 3 of this volume, Note 2 for Session 692 contains information on another way by which we can move closer to Seth's idea of simultaneity from our physical reality, but that method grows out of material not discussed here.)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Give us a moment.... Master events are those (long pause) whose main activity takes place in inner dimensions. (Long pause.) Such events are t-o-o (spelled) multidimensional to appear clearly in your reality, so that you see or experience only parts of them. They are source events. Their main thrust is in what you can call the vaster dimension of dreams, the unknown territory of inner reality. The terms you use make no difference. The original action, however, of such events is unmanifest--not physical. Those events then "subsequently" show themselves in time and space, with extraordinary results.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……主要事件是那些（长时间停顿）其主要活动发生在内在维度的事件。（长时间停顿。）这类事件太（拼写）多维了，无法清晰显现在你们的实相中，因此你们只能看到或体验到它们的一部分。它们是源头事件。其主要推力存在于你们可称为更广阔的梦境维度中，即内在实相的未知领域。你们用什么术语无关紧要。然而，这类事件的原始行动是未显化的——非物质的。那些事件随后“在时间与空间中显现自身”，带来非凡的结果。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: A portion of you, therefore, is aware of each and every dream encounter and experience. Dreams are no more hallucinatory than your physical life is. Your waking physical self is the dreamer, as far as your dreaming self is concerned: You are the dreamer it sends on its way. Your daily experiences are the dreams that it dreams, so when you look at your dreaming self or consider it, you do so with a highly prejudiced eye, taking it for granted that your "reality" is real, and its reality is illusion.
quotes.translation: 因此，你们的一部分知晓每一个梦境遭遇与体验。梦境并不比你们的物质生活更具幻觉性。就你们的做梦自身而言，你们清醒的物质自我才是做梦者：你们是它派遣上路的做梦者。你们日常的体验是它所做的梦，所以当你们审视做梦自身或考虑它时，你们带着高度偏见的目光，理所当然地认为你们的“实相”是真实的，而它的实相是幻象。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Emotion and imagination are being considered as far superior. The displaced powers of consciousness are still being assigned to the unconscious, and great efforts are being made to reach what seem to be normally inaccessible areas of awareness. To this end drugs are utilized, cults set up, and there are methods and training manuals galore. Period. Yet there is nothing basically inaccessible about such "inner knowledge or experience." It can all be quite conscious, and utilized to enrich the reality that you know. The conscious mind is not some prodigal child or poor relative of the self. It can quite freely focus into inner reality when you understand that it can. You, again, have a conscious mind. You can change the focus of your own consciousness.
quotes.translation: 情感与想象力被认为远为优越。被置换的意识能力仍被归于无意识，人们正付出巨大努力去触及那些看似通常无法触及的意识领域。为此，药物被利用，教派被建立，还有大量方法与训练手册。句号。然而，这类“内在知识或体验”本质上并无不可触及之处。它们完全可以是有意识的，并用于丰富你们所知的实相。有意识心智并非自我的某个浪子或穷亲戚。当你们理解它能够时，它可以相当自由地聚焦于内在实相。再次强调，你们拥有有意识心智。你们可以改变自身意识的焦点。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: "Art is not a specifically human endeavor, though man likes to believe that this is so. Art is above all a natural characteristic. I try to straddle your definitions--but flowers, for example, in a fashion see themselves as their own artistic creations. They have an esthetic appreciation of their own colors--a different kind, of course, than your perception of color. But nature seeks to outdo itself in terms that are most basically artistic, even while those terms may also include quite utilitarian purposes. The natural man, then, is a natural artist. In a sense, painting is man's natural attempt to create an original but coherent, mental yet physical interpretation of his own reality--and by extension to create a new version of reality for his species."
quotes.translation: “艺术并非人类特有的努力，尽管人类喜欢相信如此。艺术首先是一种自然特性。我试图跨越你们的定义——但举例来说，花朵以某种方式视自身为自己的艺术创作。它们对自己的色彩有审美欣赏——当然，这与你们对色彩的感知不同。但自然在最根本的艺术层面上寻求超越自身，即便这些层面也可能包含相当实用的目的。因此，自然人是天生的艺术家。从某种意义上说，绘画是人类自然的尝试，去创造一种对自身实相原创而连贯、既精神又物质的诠释——并由此为他的物种创造实相的新版本。”
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: When you are dealing with normal waking reality, you are operating at one level of the many that are native to your psyche. When you are dreaming, from your viewpoint you are entering other levels of reality quite as native to your psyche, but usually you are still experiencing those events through your current "waking station" The dreams that you remember are colored or altered or even censored to a certain extent. There is no inherent psychological or biological necessity for this. Your ideas and beliefs, however, about the nature of reality, and sanity, have resulted in such a schism.
quotes.translation: 当你们处理正常的清醒实相时，你们是在你们心灵本具的众多层次中的一个层次上运作。当你们做梦时，从你们的视角看，你们进入了同样本具于你们心灵的其他实相层次，但通常你们仍通过当前的“清醒站”体验那些事件。你们记得的梦境在某种程度上被着色、改变甚至审查。这并无内在的心理或生物学必要性。然而，你们关于实相与心智健全本质的观念与信念导致了这样的分裂。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: All of them cast certain "atmospheric conditions" or reflections that color physical events as you know them. Some of your own feelings are propelled into a reality within such systems, adopting within that framework their own mass and form. In the creation and maintenance of your normal reality, you focus your daily waking consciousness so that it becomes effective within the ranges necessary. Ideas and feelings that you want made physical carry within them the mechanisms that will put them in the proper range, well within the electromagnetic field necessary for physical development.
quotes.translation: 它们全都投射出某种"氛围状态"或反照，为你们所知的物质事件染上色彩。你们自己的某些感受会被推进这类系统中的实相里，在该架构内获得自身的质量与形态。在创造与维持你们正常实相的过程中，你们聚焦日常清醒意识，使其在必要的范围内发挥效力。你们想要物质化的想法与感受，其内在携带着能将它们置于适当范围的机制，完全处于物质发展所需的电磁场之内。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Much of the remainder of "Unknown" Reality, then, will deal with an inside look at the nature of reality, and with some exercises that will allow you to see yourself and your world from another perspective. Later I intend to say far more about some civilizations that, in your terms, came before your own (but see the last sentence in Note 4). Before you can understand their orientation, we will have to speak about various alternate kinds of consciousness and out-of-body experience. These will help you to understand how other kinds of cultures could operate in ways so alien to your own.
quotes.translation: 因此，"未知的"实相其余大部分内容，将探讨实相本质的内在面貌，并提供一些练习，使你们能从另一个视角看待自身与世界。稍后我打算更详细地谈论某些文明——按你们的说法，它们存在于你们文明之前（但请参阅注4最后一句）。在你们能理解它们的取向之前，我们必须先讨论各种交替意识状态与出体经验。这些将帮助你们理解，其他类型的文化何以能以与你们截然不同的方式运作。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 709
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: If you tell yourself that sexual feeling is wrong, and organize your daily programming in that fashion, then when you "meditate," or dispense with that orientation, you may suddenly find yourself presented with material that you consider unsavory. You cannot deny the reality of the psyche, or those natural feelings that you experience in the flesh. When you begin to alter your perception, then, and your habitual picture of reality drops away, you may well find yourself encountering in distorted fashion elements of your own reality that you have up to then studiously denied or ignored.
quotes.translation: 如果你告诉自己性感受是错误的，并以此方式组织日常程序，那么当你"冥想"或放弃这种取向时，可能会突然发现自己面对的是你认为不悦的材料。你无法否认心灵实相，也无法否认你在肉身中体验到的那些自然感受。当你开始改变感知，习惯性的实相图景脱落时，你很可能会以扭曲的形式遭遇自己一直刻意否认或忽视的实相元素。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Each particular "station" of consciousness perceives in a different kind of reality, and as mentioned earlier (in Session 711, for instance), you usually tune in to your home station most of the time. If you turn your focus only slightly away, the world appears differently; and if that slightly altered focus were the predominant one, then that is how the world would seem to be. Each aspect of the psyche perceives the reality upon which it is focused, and that reality is also the materialization of a particular state of the psyche projected outward. You can learn to encounter other realities by altering your position within your own psyche.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: When the race is in deepest stress and faced with great problems, it will call up someone like Christ. It will seek out and indeed from itself produce the very personalities necessary to give it the strength it needs. It makes little difference what circumstances are involved. The myth will grow to a reality, and the reality is far stronger than any purely physical event could be.
quotes.translation: 当人类处于最深重的压力、面临巨大问题时，会召唤出像基督这样的存在。它会寻找、甚至从自身产生出能赋予其所需力量的特定人格。具体情境如何并不重要。神话将成长为实相，而实相比任何纯粹物质事件都强大得多。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Myth is not a distortion of fact, but the womb through which fact must come. Myth involves an intrinsic understanding of the nature of reality, couched in imaginative terms, carrying a power as strong as nature itself. Myth-making is a natural psychic characteristic, a psychic element that combines with other such elements to form a mythical representation of inner reality. That representation is then used as a model upon which your civilizations are organized, and also as a perceptual tool through whose lens you interpret the private events of your life in their historical context.
quotes.translation: 神话并非对事实的扭曲，而是事实必须经由其诞生的子宫。神话包含对实相本质的内在理解，以充满想象力的语言表达，携带着与自然本身同样强大的力量。神话创作是自然的心理特性，是一种心灵元素，与其他此类元素结合，形成内在实相的神话性再现。这种再现随后被用作组织你们文明的模型，也作为感知工具——你们通过其透镜，在历史脉络中解读个人生活的私密事件。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: eth's statement at the end of dictation for the 815th session, "I will try to begin work on our book in a more predictable fashion," reflected his good intent, but things didn't turn out that way. Jane and I let the holiday season intervene to some extent, and Session 816, which came through the day after Christmas, didn't concern book work at all. Then, starting in early January, we held eight sessions having to do with questions we'd put off dealing with for a long time; some were personal and some were not. We stayed with our Monday-Saturday evening session routine, though, and kept busy with all of our other projects.1
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Consider this analogy: The self as a moving circle, such as a Ferris wheel. A tree in front of the wheel will represent physical reality. The whole self, or the whole wheel, is composed of many selves in various positions, as the many people who sit on the Ferris wheel. As the wheel turns you call the person or the self who faces the tree the ego, simply because this is the portion that faces physical reality, represented by our tree. But the self who faces the tree one moment is not the self that faces it the next moment, and the operator of the wheel is never in evidence, you see.
quotes.translation: 试想这个类比：自我如同移动的圆环，比如摩天轮。轮前的树代表物质实相。整个自我或整个轮子，由处于不同位置的众多自我组成，就像摩天轮上坐着的许多人。当轮子转动时，你们称面向树的那个人或自我为"自我"，仅仅因为这是面向物质实相（由我们的树代表）的部分。但此刻面向树的那个自我，下一刻就不再是面向它的那个自我，而轮子的操作者从未显现，你们明白吗。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: You cooperate together to form the physical reality that you know, telepathically, through ways and means that are unknown to you. You weave webs of psychic reality that then coalesce into physical reality. You do not necessarily weave them alone, but together. Your thoughts intertwine with those of others. You are responsible for your own thoughts. You need to learn the power of thought and emotion, but this should fill you with the joy of creativity. Once you realize that your thoughts form reality, then you are no longer a slave to events. You simply have to learn the methods.
quotes.translation: 你们共同合作，以你们未知的方式和手段，通过心灵感应形成你们所知的物质实相。你们编织出心灵实相的网络，随后这些网络凝聚成物质实相。你们未必是独自编织，而是共同编织。你们的思想与他人的思想交织在一起。你们要为自己的思想负责。你们需要学习思想和情感的力量，但这应让你们充满创造的喜悦。一旦你们意识到思想构成实相，就不再是事件的奴隶。你们只需学会方法。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: We will take your physical system as an example. There is general and mass focus of all self structures within that system, binding enough and cohesive enough so that the appearance of a permanent and all-pervading physical reality is maintained. The ego part of the structure concentrates with great intensity, directing itself within those coordinates that form physical reality.
quotes.translation: 我们将以你们的物质系统为例。在该系统内，所有自体结构存在普遍而集中的焦点，足够凝聚和紧密，以维持一个永恒且无所不在的物质实相的表象。结构中的自我部分高度集中，在构成物质实相的坐标内定向自身。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: The inner references of reality involve a different kind of experience entirely, with organizational patterns that mix and merge at every conceivable point. You tune your consciousness while you sleep as one might tune a piano, so that in waking reality, it clearly perceives the proper notes and values that build up into physical experience. Those inner fields of reference in which you have your existence are completely changing themselves as your experience is added to them, and your own (long pause) identity was couched in those references before birth as you understand it.
quotes.translation: 实相的内在参照涉及完全不同的经验类型，其组织模式在每个可想象的点上混合与融合。你们在睡眠中调整意识，如同调校钢琴，以便在清醒实相中清晰感知构成物质经验的正确音符与价值。你们所存在的那些内在参照场域，随着你们经验的加入而完全改变自身，而你们自己的（长停顿）身份在你们所理解的出生之前就已寓于这些参照之中。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 4. In the Preface for Volume 1 of Dreams, see Session 881, with its Note 1. Then I suggest a review in chapters 1 and 2 of all of the material pertinent to evolution and creationism.
