quotes.text: Now: Dictation: As per Ruburt's notes, each system of reality is indeed surrounded by its probable realities, though any one of those "probable realities" can be used as the hub, or core reality; in which case all of the others will then be seen as probable. In other words, relativity certainly applies here.
quotes.translation: 现在：口授：如鲁柏笔记所述，每个实相系统确实被其可能实相所环绕，尽管这些"可能实相"中的任何一个都可作为核心或中心实相；在这种情况下，所有其他实相将被视为可能的。换言之，相对性当然适用于此。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 712
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Root assumptions are those built-in ideas of reality of which I spoke -- those agreements upon which you base your ideas of existence. Space and time, for example, are root assumptions. Each system of reality has its own set of such agreements. When I communicate within your system, I must use and understand the root assumptions upon which it is based. As a teacher it is part of my job to understand and use these, and I have had existences in many such systems as a part of what you may call my basic training; though in your terms my associates and I had other names for them.
quotes.translation: 根本假设是我所谈到的那些内置的实相观念——你们赖以建立存在观念的那些协议。例如，空间和时间就是根本假设。每个实相系统都有自己的一套此类协议。当我在你们的系统内沟通时，我必须使用并理解其赖以存在的根本假设。作为教师，理解并运用这些是我的职责之一，而我在许多此类系统中存在过，作为你们所谓基础训练的一部分；尽管按你们的说法，我和我的同伴们对这些有别的称呼。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: What you call dimensions represent states in which reality is perceived. You perceive reality in three dimensions, and you have a glimpse of reality in a fourth dimension. There are many dimensions however in all directions. These heavenly bodies represent moment points in other systems. As they are projected into your system however, they are only perceived in terms of matter.
quotes.translation: 你们所谓的维度代表感知实相的状态。你们在三维中感知实相，并对第四维度的实相有一瞥。然而，各个方向都存在许多维度。这些天体代表其他系统中的瞬间点。但当它们投射到你们的系统时，仅以物质的形式被感知。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

source_raw.title: （Once before, in the first and 18th envelope experiments, an object was used that was similar but not identical by any means. In the first experiment in the 179th session, Seth came through with one accurate impression. In the 18th experiment, held during the 212th session, a few more impressions were obtained; but overall the results were interfered with by other factors in a way that doesn't seem to happen these days. See Session 179 in Volume 4, and Session 212 in Volume 5.

quotes.text: When you leave this dimension, then you concentrate upon the knowledge that you have gained. If you still do not realize that you create the reality that you know, then you return and again you learn to manipulate and again and again you see the results of your own inner reality as you meet it objectified. You teach yourself the lesson until you have learned it. And when you have learned it, then you have begun to learn how to handle the consciousness that is yours intelligently and well. And then you can form images for the benefit of others, and lead them and guide them.
quotes.translation: 当你离开这个维度时，便会专注于所获得的知识。若你仍未领悟自己创造所知的实相，便会重返人间继续学习操控，一次又一次目睹内在实相外显为具体结果。你不断自我教导直至学会这堂课。当你真正学会后，便开始懂得如何明智妥善地运用属于你的意识。届时你便能为他人的利益塑造意象，引领并指引他们。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: (Slower now): All of this material now given must be considered along with the fact that beneath these developments there are the eternal aspects and creative characteristics of a force that is both undeniable and intimate. All That Is, in other words, represents the reality from which all of us spring. (Pause, one of several.) All That Is, by its nature, transcends all dimensions of activity, consciousness, or reality, while being a part of each.
quotes.translation: （语速放缓）此刻所授的一切内容，必须结合以下事实来理解：在这些发展表象之下，存在着一种既不容否认又亲密无间的力量，它拥有永恒的特质与创造特性。换言之，"一切万有"代表了我们所有存在的本源实相。（停顿数次）"一切万有"的本质超越所有活动、意识或实相的维度，同时又参与其中每个维度。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It is impossible to tell you of the emotional reality of such an experience. You will have to discover it for yourself. Such bridge beliefs often allow you to perceive the "invisible" beliefs mentioned this evening, and these can then appear to you as a revelation. On second thought, however, you will realize that another belief blocked that one from your view, but that you were always aware of it; and that in a strange way it was also invisible because you took it for granted. You did not consider it a belief about reality but as reality itself, and never questioned it.
quotes.translation: 我无法向你描述这种体验的情感实相，你必须亲自发现。这类桥梁信念常能让你觉察到今晚提及的"隐形"信念，它们会以启示的形式显现。但细想之下，你会意识到是另一种信念遮蔽了它，而你始终对其心知肚明；奇妙的是，它之所以隐形，恰因你视其为理所当然。你未曾将它视为关于实相的信念，而是直接当作实相本身，从未质疑。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: A true understanding of the way in which an idea becomes physical matter would result in a complete revamping of your so-called modern technology, and in buildings, roads, and other structures that would far outlast those you now have. While the psychic reality behind physical matter is ignored, then you cannot use those methods effectively that do exist, nor can you take advantage of them. You cannot understand the psychic reality that is the true impetus for your physical existence unless you first realize your own psychic reality, and independence from physical laws.
quotes.translation: 真正理解意念转化为物质的方式，将彻底革新你们所谓的现代科技，创造出远胜现今的建筑、道路及其他结构。若忽视物质背后的心灵实相，便无法有效运用既存的方法，更无法善加利用。除非你先认识到自身的心灵实相与独立于物理法则的本质，否则无法理解那真正驱动物质存在的心灵实相。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Thoughts, as you know, contain their own electromagnetic reality. This means that each thought is an electromagnetic system in fact and not just in theory. Such a system is the basis for your physical system and these are highly valid. Theoretically only, with drugs it would be possible to be lost within an idea, caught in its electromagnetic reality, and forced to follow the ramifications and developments of the idea in various guises. The value fulfillment system would be changed. There would be development within that system, but you would be lost to your own system.
quotes.translation: 如你所知，思想本身蕴含电磁实相。这意味着每个思想事实上都是电磁系统，而非仅存于理论。这类系统是物质系统的基础，具有高度有效性。理论上，借助药物可能迷失于某个意念中，被困在其电磁实相里，被迫追随该意念以各种伪装呈现的分支与发展。价值完成系统将因此改变，虽然系统内部仍有发展，但你会与自身系统失联。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Facts are a very handy but weak brew of reality. They immediately consign certain kinds of experiences as real and others as not. The psyche, however, will not be so limited. It exists in a medium of reality, a realm of being in which all possibilities exist. It creates myths the way the ocean creates spray. Myths are originally psychic fabrications of such power and strength that whole civilizations can rise from their source. They involve symbols and known emotional validities that are then connected to the physical world, so that that world is never the same again.
quotes.translation: 事实是实相中便捷却淡薄的酿造物。它们立即将某些经验归类为真实，其他则否。然而心灵不会如此受限，它存在于实相媒介中，那是所有可能性共存的存有领域。心灵创造神话，犹如海洋制造浪花。神话最初是心灵编织的产物，其力量与强度足以让整个文明从源头崛起。它们包含象征与已知的情感有效性，继而与物质世界相连，使那个世界从此焕然一新。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: eth's statement at the end of dictation for the 815th session, "I will try to begin work on our book in a more predictable fashion," reflected his good intent, but things didn't turn out that way. Jane and I let the holiday season intervene to some extent, and Session 816, which came through the day after Christmas, didn't concern book work at all. Then, starting in early January, we held eight sessions having to do with questions we'd put off dealing with for a long time; some were personal and some were not. We stayed with our Monday-Saturday evening session routine, though, and kept busy with all of our other projects.1
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: I have told you that exterior reality is a materialization of inner electromagnetic reality. I have told you that all experience is electromagnetically coded. The inner senses pick up this data directly, you see, as in last Friday's episode. Some translation must occur if you are to become aware consciously of such material, or if it is to impress the physical senses at all.
quotes.translation: 我曾告诉过你，外在实相是内在电磁实相的物质化。我也告诉过你，所有经验都以电磁方式编码。内在感官直接接收这些资料，就像上周五的插曲那样。若要你有意识地觉察这类资料，或要让它在任何程度上影响物质感官，就必须发生某种翻译过程。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... You are a part of nature that has learned to make choices, a part of nature that naturally and automatically produces dreams and beliefs about which you then organize your reality. There are many effects which you do not like, but you possess a unique kind of consciousness, in which each individual has a hand in the overall formation of a world reality, and you are participating at a level of existence in which you are learning how to transform the imaginative realm of probabilities into a more or less specific, physically experienced world.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……你是自然的一部分，学会了做出选择；你是自然的一部分，自然且自动地产生梦境与信念，并据此组织你的实相。有许多你不喜欢的效果，但你拥有一种独特的意识，在其中每个个体都参与世界实相的整体形成，而你正参与一个存在层面，学习如何将可能性的想象领域转化为一个或多或少具体的、被物理体验的世界。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Now: These stages of consciousness are all a part of your own reality. A knowledge of them can be most useful. You can learn to "shift gears," stand aside from your own experience, and examine it with much better perspective. You can prepare questions or problems, suggesting that they be solved for you in the sleep state. You can suggest that you will speak with distant friends, or convey important messages that you cannot convey verbally, perhaps. You can bring about reconciliations, for example, at another layer of reality though you cannot do so in this one.
quotes.translation: 现在：这些意识阶段都是你自身实相的一部分。了解它们会非常有用。你可以学会“换挡”，从自身经验中抽身，以更佳视角审视它。你可以准备问题或难题，暗示它们在睡眠状态中为你解决。你可以暗示自己将与远方的朋友交谈，或传达你或许无法用言语传达的重要信息。例如，你可以在实相的另一层面达成和解，尽管在此层面你无法做到。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This is creativity of a most specialized nature and allows him to probe, if he will, into the nature of consciousness, the psyche, and creativity in a way that few can. Now he himself set up the conditions that would make such results possible. A certain part of my reality is a portion of a certain part of his reality, and here the creation of what I seem to be takes place.
quotes.translation: 这是一种极为特殊的创造力，若他愿意，便能以少数人能做到的方式，深入探究意识、心灵与创造力的本质。现在，他自身设定了使这类结果成为可能的条件。我实相的某一部分是他实相某一部分的片段，而在此处，我表面所是的创造发生了。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Now at times I will be using the term "camouflage," referring to the physical world to which the outer ego relates, for physical form is one of the camouflages that reality adopts. The camouflage is real, and yet there is a much greater reality within it -- the vitality that gave it form. Your physical senses then allow you to perceive this camouflage, for they are attuned to it in a highly specialized manner. But to sense the reality within the form requires a different sort of attention, and more delicate manipulations than the physical senses provide.
quotes.translation: 现在，我有时会使用“伪装”一词，指涉外在自我关联的物质世界，因为物质形态是实相所采用的伪装之一。这伪装是真实的，然而其中存在一个更伟大的实相——赋予它形态的生命力。你的物质感官让你能感知这伪装，因为它们以高度专门化的方式与之调谐。但要感知形态内的实相，则需要一种不同的注意力，以及比物质感官所提供的更精微的操作。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You cannot treat thoughts and imagination in such a literal manner, nor in a large respect should you try to "guard your thoughts" as if they were herds of animals that you wanted to keep purely bred. Your thoughts do form your reality. If you do not fear them, however, they create their own balances. The psyche dwells in a reality so different from the world you usually recognize that there good and evil, as you think of them, are also seen to be as operationally or relatively true as the difference between the perceiver and the object perceived.
quotes.translation: 你不能以如此字面的方式对待思想和想象，大体上也不应试图“守护你的思想”，仿佛它们是你要保持纯种的动物群。你的思想确实形成你的实相。然而，若你不恐惧它们，它们会创造自身的平衡。心灵栖居的实相与你通常认知的世界如此不同，以至于在你看来，善恶也如同感知者与被感知对象之间的差异一样，在运作上或相对上是真实的。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now, putting these two statements together, you see that an individual will perceive basic reality, in the main, only from his own reference point, and through his outer sense apparatus. His perception of basic reality in one way does not change the nature of that reality or of that action, as it exists independently of his field of reference. However, the very distortions that occur in his attempt to perceive this reality results in a new reality. What he perceives then is legitimate, for his very perception of it is the basis for its existence.
quotes.translation: 现在，将这两句话放在一起，你会发现，个体主要只能从自己的参考点出发，透过外在感官装置来感知基本实相。他对基本实相的单向感知并不会改变该实相或该行动的本质——这些本质独立于他的参考场域而存在。然而，他在试图感知此实相时所产生的扭曲本身，却造就了一个新的实相。他所感知到的事物因此具有正当性，因为正是他的感知构成了其存在的基础。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: All of you have abilities and potentials but you are held in bond by preconceived ideas of reality. I tell you no. Reality is the table that moved, the table that shattered! Reality is the energy you have. You cannot afford complacency. To be alive is to learn and to grow. How can you close yourselves off in rooms of limited reality. You close your eyes and do not look.
quotes.translation: 你们所有人都拥有能力与潜能，却被对实相的成见所束缚。我告诉你们：绝非如此！实相是移动的桌子、碎裂的桌子！实相是你们所拥有的能量。你们不能安于自满。活着就是要学习与成长。你们怎能将自己封闭在受限实相的小房间里？你们闭上眼睛，不去观看。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: If you have a physical symptom, do not run away from it. Feel its reality in your body. Let the emotions follow freely. These will lead you, if you allow them to flow, to the beliefs that cause the difficulty. They will take you through many aspects of your own reality that you must face and explore. These methods release your withheld natural aggressiveness. You may feel that you are swamped by emotion, but trust it -- again, it is the motion of your being, and it arouses your own creativity. Followed, it will seek the answers to your problems.
quotes.translation: 若出现身体症状，不要逃避。在身体中感受它的实相。让情绪自由流动。若允许它们流动，这些情绪会引导你们找到造成困扰的信念。它们将带领你们穿越自身实相的诸多面向——那些你们必须面对与探索的面向。这些方法会释放你们被压抑的自然攻击性。你或许会感到被情绪淹没，但请信任它——再次强调，这是你存在的动能，它能激发你自身的创造力。跟随它，它将为你寻求问题的答案。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The decision is your own according to your understanding. You cannot be hounded from one level of reality to another by a fear that you do not understand. You cannot be threatened in this life by fears from your early childhood, or by so-called past existences, unless you so thoroughly believe in the nature of fear that you allow yourselves to be conquered by it. Each of your personalities are free to accept and develop, from the miraculous banks of reality, those experiences and emotions that you want, and to reject those you do not want.
quotes.translation: 决定权在于你们自己，依据你们的理解而定。你们不会因无法理解的恐惧而被从一个实相层面驱赶到另一个层面。在此生中，你们不会受到童年早期恐惧或所谓前世存在的威胁——除非你们如此彻底地相信恐惧的本质，以至于允许自己被它征服。你们每个人格都自由地从实相的神奇宝库中，选择并发展你们想要的经验与情感，同时拒绝那些你们不想要的。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: (11:16.) In dream travel it is quite possible to journey to other civilizations -- those in your past or future, or even to worlds whose reality exists in other probable systems. There is even a kind of "cross-breeding," for you affect any system of reality with which you have experience. There are no closed realities, only apparent boundaries that seem to separate them.6 The more parochial your own world view, however, the less you will recall of their dreams or their activities, or the more distorted your "dream snapshots" will be.
quotes.translation: （11:16）在梦中旅行中，完全有可能前往其他文明——那些存在于你们过去或未来的文明，甚至前往那些实相存在于其他可能性系统中的世界。甚至存在某种"跨种交融"，因为你们会影响任何你们所经验过的实相系统。没有封闭的实相，只有看似分隔它们的表面界限。然而，你们的世界观越是狭隘，你们对梦境或活动的记忆就越少，你们的"梦境快照"也会越扭曲。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 719
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: On some relatively few occasions, for example, Ruburt has been able to contact what he calls "Seth Two." That level of reality, however, is even further divorced from your own. It represents an even greater extension of the psyche, in your terms. (Long pause.) There is a much closer relationship, in that I recognize my own identity as a distinct portion of Seth Two's existence, where Ruburt feels little correspondence. In a manner of speaking, Seth Two's reality includes my own, yet I am aware of my contribution to "his" experience.
quotes.translation: 举例来说，在少数情况下，鲁柏曾能接触到他所称的"赛斯第二"。然而，那个实相层面与你们的实相更为疏离。用你们的说法，它代表着心灵更进一步的延伸。（长停顿）存在一种更密切的关系——我认知到自己的身份是赛斯第二存在中的一个独特部分，而鲁柏却几乎感受不到对应。从某种角度说，赛斯第二的实相包含了我自己的实相，然而我清楚自己对"他"的经验所做的贡献。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: With splendid innocence and exuberant pride, you imagine that the evolutionary system as you know it is the only one, that physically there can be no more. Now within the physical reality that you know, there are hints and clues as to the nature of other physical realities. There are, latent, within your own physical forms other senses, unused, that could have come to the front but in your probability did not. Now I have been speaking of earthly developments, realities therefore clustered about earthly aspects as you know them.
quotes.translation: 你以极度的天真和自豪，想象你所知的演化系统是唯一的，认为物质层面不可能再有其他。然而，在你所知的物质实相中，存在着其他物质实相本质的暗示与线索。在你自身物质形态中潜伏着其他未被使用的感官，它们本可发展为主流，但在你的概率中并未如此。此刻我谈论的是地球层面的发展，因此这些实相都围绕着你所熟知的地球面向聚集。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Until you are honest with yourself and become consciously aware of yourself, you cannot honestly relate with others; you will project upon them your own fears and prejudices. You cannot afford to help them because you have too many insecurities within yourself. Now, you form the physical reality that you know, individually and en masse. To change your world you must change your thoughts. You must become consciously aware of what you tell yourself is true every moment of the day, for that is the reality that you project outward.
quotes.translation: 除非你对自己诚实并清醒地觉察自我，否则你无法真诚地与他人相处；你会将自己的恐惧与偏见投射到他们身上。你无法帮助他们，因为内心有太多不安全感。现在，你以个体和集体的方式塑造着你所知的物质实相。要改变你的世界，你必须改变你的思想。你必须时刻清醒地觉察自己告诉自己什么是真实的，因为那正是你向外投射的实相。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: To understand that you create your own reality requires that same kind of "awakening" from the normal awake state -- at least for many people. Some of course have this knack more than others. The realization itself does indeed change "the rules of the game" as far as you are concerned (louder) to a rather considerable degree. There are reasons why I am mentioning this now rather than in earlier books. Indeed, our books follow their own rhythms, and this one is in a way a further elaboration upon The Nature of Personal Reality.1
quotes.translation: 理解你创造自己的实相，需要从正常清醒状态中经历同样的"觉醒"——至少对多数人而言如此。当然有些人天生更擅长此道。这种领悟本身确实会（提高音量）相当程度地改变"游戏规则"。我选择现在而非早期著作中提及此事自有原因。事实上，我们的书籍遵循自身韵律，这本《个人实相的本质》在某种程度上正是对前作的进一步阐述。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Five weeks ago [in the notes leading off Session 821], I wrote that Jane could resume work on The Further Education of Oversoul Seven -- or Seven Two -- at any time. She finished her first novel about Seven in July 1972, and within a month, long before it was published, she wrote the first five chapters for Seven Two. Then we became involved in so many other projects that she laid it aside until August 1976, when she wrote two more chapters. The book has hovered in the back of her consciousness ever since, waiting until she focused her attention upon it once again. "Seven's got all the time in the world," she laughed more than once. But now that her editor, Tam Mossman, has scheduled a visit to us at the hill house in a couple of weeks [on April 10, to be exact], Jane feels that she wants to study what she's done on Seven Two, go over it with Tam, and perhaps take up work on it again.)
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Not only are you part of other independent selves, each one focused in its own reality, but there is a sympathetic relationship that exists. For example, because of this relationship, your experience need not be limited by the physical perceptive mechanisms. You can draw upon knowledge that belongs to these other independent selves. You can learn to focus your attention away from physical reality, to learn new methods of perception that will enable you to enlarge your concept of reality and greatly expand your own experience.
quotes.translation: 你不仅属于其他独立自我的一部分——每个自我都聚焦于自身实相——而且存在一种共鸣关系。例如，由于这种关系，你的体验不必受限于物质感知机制。你可以汲取属于这些其他独立自我的知识。你能学会将注意力从物质实相转移，学习新的感知方法，从而扩展你对实相的概念，极大丰富自身经验。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: When you reach adulthood, following our analogy, then you will learn to be successful in manipulating dream reality as you now manipulate objective reality. For if you create your dreams, you also create your objective environment. The dream reality is as real and actual as physical reality, but it can be changed you see by you, as physical reality can be changed.
quotes.translation: 按照我们的比喻，当你达到成年阶段，就会学会像操控客观实相那样成功操控梦境实相。因为如果你创造了自己的梦境，你也同样创造了客观环境。梦境实相与物质实相同样真实有效，但你看，它如同物质实相一样可以被你改变。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: Now, I project a portion of my reality as I dictate this book to an undifferentiated level between systems that is relatively clear of camouflage. It is an inactive area, comparatively speaking. If you were thinking in terms of physical reality, then this area could be likened to one immediately above the atmosphere of your earth. However I am speaking of psychological and psychic atmospheres, and this area is sufficiently distant from Ruburt's physically oriented self so that the communications can be relatively understood.
quotes.translation: 此刻，在我口述此书时，我将自身实相的一部分投射到系统间一个相对清晰的未分化层面。这是个相对而言不活跃的区域。若以物质实相思维考量，此区域可比拟为地球大气层正上方。然而我指的是心理与心灵氛围，这个区域距离鲁柏的物质导向自我足够遥远，因此这些通讯能被相对清晰地理解。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Nor do such projections involve necessarily journeys through space as you know it. There are systems, extremely vivid in intensity, that have no existence in physical reality. It is now thought, I believe, that time and space are basically one, but they are both a part of something else. They are merely the camouflage patterns by which you perceive reality. Space, as you experience it in the dream state, comes much closer to reality.
quotes.translation: 这些投射也未必涉及你所认知的空间旅行。有些体系极为生动鲜明，却不存在于物质实相中。我相信如今人们认为时空本质上是统一的，但它们都只是某种更根本存在的一部分。它们不过是你们感知实相所采用的伪装模式。你在梦境状态中体验到的空间，反而更接近实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: The physical senses usually blind you to these open channels, for they perceive reality only in their own image. To some extent, then, I enter your reality through a psychological warp in your space and time. In a manner of speaking, such an open channel serves much as a pathway between Ruburt's personality and my own, so that communication is possible between. Such psychological and psychic warps between dimensions of existence are not infrequent. They are merely recognized as such infrequently and utilized even less so.
quotes.translation: 物质感官通常会使你们对这些开放通道视而不见，因为它们只按自身形象感知实相。从某种程度上说，我正是通过你们时空中的心理扭曲进入你们的实相。打个比方，这样的开放通道就像鲁柏人格与我之间的通路，使沟通成为可能。存在维度间的这种心理与心灵扭曲并不罕见。只是它们很少被如此认知，更少被利用。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You always know what you are doing, even when you do not realize it. Your eye knows it sees, though it cannot see itself except through the use of reflection. In the same way the world as you see it is a reflection of what you are, a reflection not in glass but in three-dimensional reality. You project your thoughts, feelings, and expectations outward, then you perceive them as the outside reality. When it seems to you that others are observing you, you are observing yourself from the standpoint of your own projections.
quotes.translation: 你始终知道自己在做什么，即使你并未意识到。你的眼睛知道它在看，尽管它只能通过反射才能看见自身。同样，你们所见的世界正是你们自身的反射——不是镜中倒影，而是三维实相中的投射。你们将思想、情感与期待向外投射，然后将它们感知为外在实相。当你们觉得别人在观察你时，其实你正从自身投射的角度观察自己。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Many such people are highly creative and imaginative. Often, however, they have less of a solid foundation than others in dealing with a mass-shared reality, and so they attempt to impose their own private symbols upon the world, or to form a completely private world. I am speaking in general terms now, and in those terms such people are leery of human relationships. Each person forms his or her own reality, and yet that personal reality must also be shared with others, and must be affected by the reality of others ...
quotes.translation: 许多这样的人极具创造力和想象力。然而在处理大众共享实相时，他们往往比他人缺乏坚实基础，因此试图将个人象征强加于世界，或构建完全私密的世界。我现在泛泛而谈，这类人对人际关系通常保持警惕。每个人都在构建自己的实相，但这份个人实相也必须与他人共享，并受他人实相的影响……
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: hen he originally gave us the 812th session some 16 months ago, Seth told us it would "be part of a chapter later in the book...." [See my opening notes for Session 814.] The 812th session was held following an encounter Jane and I had with an unexpected visitor, although these excerpts aren't personal at all. They fit in well with material in Mass Events, however -- indeed, as soon as Seth confirmed Jane's headings for Part 3 and Chapter 6, we knew that we'd found the place to present this material. We made our decision without consulting Seth, by the way.
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: I have lived in physical terms within your three-dimensional universe, and I am aware of that universe. My main existence however is in a dimension of which your third-dimensional reality is only a part. I can therefore perceive, for example, portions of your future, simply because the future is only an illusion that exists in three-dimensional reality. (Pause.)
quotes.translation: 我曾以物质形态存在于你们的三维宇宙中，也了解那个宇宙。但我的主要存在维度，你们的第三维实相只是其中一部分。因此我能感知你们未来的片段，仅仅因为未来只是三维实相中的幻象。（停顿）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: These portraits obviously have a biological reality. In a manner of speaking, now, each person dips into the same supplies of paint, and so forth -- which are the elements out of which your likenesses emerge. There must be great creative leeway allowed for such portraits. Each one interacting with each other one helps form the psychological and physical reality of the species, so you are somehow involved in the formation of a multitudinous number of portraits. I simply want you to keep that analogy in the background.
quotes.translation: 这些画像显然具有生物实相。打个比方，每个人都在取用相同的颜料——这些元素构成了你们的相似性。这样的画像必须留有巨大的创作空间。每幅画像相互影响，共同塑造了该物种的心理与物质实相，因此你们在某种程度上参与了无数画像的构建。我只希望你们将这个比喻铭记于心。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 867, JULY 23, 1979
9:28 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: In this abundance nothing is meaningless nor wasted. There is interrelationship, intertwining realities, and connections that cannot be denied. I told you that dream reality consisted of more than you knew, and that the dream universe continued whether or not you perceived it. Within that context those inhabitants dream in turn their own dreams and form electromagnetic realities. You are not at the top nor at the bottom of the heap of consciousness, so to speak. You are not at the center. You are not at the rim.
quotes.translation: 在这丰沛之中，无一物是无意义或被浪费的。存在着相互关联、交织重叠的实相，以及无法否认的连结。我曾告诉过你，梦的实相远比你认知的更为广阔，而梦的宇宙无论你是否感知到它都持续存在。在那个脉络中，那些居民反过来编织他们自己的梦，并形成电磁实相。可以说，你既不在意识堆叠的顶端也不在底层。你不在中心。你也不在边缘。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: These "period pieces," overall, have a particular purpose. By the very nature of consciousness, it seeks to materialize itself in as many dimensions as possible -- to create from itself new levels of awareness, new offshoots. In doing so it creates all reality. Reality, therefore, is always in a state of becoming. The thoughts that you think, for example, in your actor's roles, are still completely unique and lead to new creativity. Certain aspects of your own consciousness could be fulfilled in no other way.
quotes.translation: 这些"时代剧"整体而言具有特定目的。意识的本性使其力求在尽可能多的维度中物质化自身——从自身创造出新的觉知层次、新的分支。在此过程中，它创造了所有实相。因此，实相始终处于"生成"状态。例如，你在演员角色中所思的念头，依然完全独特并导向新的创造力。你意识的某些面向无法通过其他方式得以实现。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: He first saw this library from the inside last Wednesday. He was simultaneously himself here in this living room, watching the image of himself in a library room, and he was the self in the library. Period. Before him he saw a wall of books, and the self in the living room suddenly knew that his purpose here in this reality was to re-create some of those books. He knew that he was working at both levels. The unknown and the known realities merged, clicked in, and were seen as the opposite sides of each other.
quotes.translation: 上周三他首次从内部看见这座图书馆。他同时存在于此处客厅中，观看着图书馆房间里的自己影像，同时他也是图书馆中的那个自己。句号。他面前是一整墙的书籍，而客厅中的那个自己突然明白，他在这实相中的目的是重新创作其中某些书籍。他知道自己同时在两个层面运作。未知与已知的实相融合、契合，并被视为彼此的对立面。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 715
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: The physical with its multitudinous and astonishing complexity, merely hints at the full nature of inner reality however, for all of inner reality cannot be translated in physical terms. Your own physical environment is constantly tended by you automatically. It constantly reflects in its own way the nature of your inner environment. Again, this is far from symbolic. Every least alteration of psychic and Emotional existence immediately alters the nature of the physical environment through instant interaction.
quotes.translation: 物质实相以其繁多惊人的复杂性，仅暗示了内在实相的全部本质，因为所有内在实相无法以物质术语完全转译。你自身的物质环境不断被你自动照料着。它持续以其自身方式反映你内在环境的本质。再次强调，这远非象征性的。心灵与情绪存在的每一次最细微改变，都会通过即时互动立即改变物质环境的本质。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: Suffice it to say, you are surrounded by other influences and events. Certain of these you perceive in your three-dimensional reality. You accept them as real without realizing that they are only portions of other events. Where your vision fails, you think reality ceases, so again you must train yourselves to look between events, between objects, within yourself when you do not seem to be doing anything. Watch out for events that appear to make no sense, for they are often clues to larger invisible events.
quotes.translation: 只需说，你被其他影响与事件所环绕。其中某些你以三维实相感知。你视其为真实，却未意识到它们仅是其他事件的部分片段。当你的视野失效时，你便认为实相终止了，因此你必须训练自己在看似无所事事时，去观察事件之间、物体之间、以及自身之内。留意那些看似毫无意义的事件，因为它们往往是更宏大无形事件的线索。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Experience is the product of the mind, the spirit, conscious thoughts and feelings, and unconscious thoughts and feelings. These together form the reality that you know. You are hardly at the mercy of a reality, therefore, that exists apart from yourself, or is thrust upon you. You are so intimately connected with the physical events composing your life experience that often you cannot distinguish between the seemingly material occurrences and the thoughts, expectations and desires that gave them birth.
quotes.translation: 经验是心智、灵性、意识思想与感受，以及无意识思想与感受的产物。这些共同构成了你所知的实相。因此，你绝非受制于一个独立于你之外或强加于你的实相。你与构成人生经验的物质事件如此紧密相连，以至于常常无法区分看似物质性的发生，与赋予它们生命的念头、期望和渴望。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: They represent, however, deep unities that you do not understand. Your conception of good and evil results in large part from the kind of consciousness you have presently adopted. You do not perceive wholes, but portions. The conscious mind focuses with a quick, limited, but intense light, perceiving from a given field of reality only certain "stimuli." It then puts these stimuli together, forming the liaison of similarity. Anything that it does not accept as a portion of reality, it does not perceive.
quotes.translation: 然而，它们代表的是你们尚未理解的深层统一性。你们对善恶的概念很大程度上源于目前所采用的意识类型。你们无法感知整体，只能感知片段。意识心以快速、有限但强烈的光芒聚焦，从特定现实场域中只感知某些"刺激"，然后将这些刺激组合起来，形成相似性的联结。任何它不承认为现实一部分的事物，它便无法感知。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Basic reality is not uncontrolled, savage, undisciplined or chaotic, but if you were suddenly confronted with it at your present state of development you would be like a straw in a hurricane. But because of your weakness, the various levels of existence merely prepare you and equip you for the experience of true reality, of which you have but a glimmering.
quotes.translation: 基本现实并非不受控制、野蛮、无纪律或混乱的，但若以你们目前的发展状态突然面对它，你们就会像飓风中的稻草一般。然而，正因你们的脆弱，存在的各个层次只是在为你们做准备，使你们装备好去体验真正的现实——对此你们仅有一丝微光。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Again, in this distortion we see the creation, however minute, of a new reality. The universe is cohesive, but it is also more various than you know even now. The nature of our object, our automobile for example, is indeed largely determined by those who perceive it, for it is different things in reality, and not one thing. Electrically it has an identity, and would be perceived as an entirely different phenomena from within an electrical system, where there would be no perceptors of physical data.
quotes.translation: 再次强调，在这种扭曲中，我们看到了新现实的创造——无论多么微小。宇宙是凝聚的，但它比你们目前所知的更为多样。我们物体的本质——例如我们的汽车——确实在很大程度上由感知者所决定，因为它在现实中是不同的事物，而非单一事物。在电性层面它有一个身份，若从电性系统内部感知，它将被视为完全不同的现象，那里没有物理数据的感知者。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: New paragraph. Basically, however, consciousness is freewheeling. Such realities therefore always exist -- in your own psyche -- outside of your "home station," and some portion of your own consciousness is always involved in them. Period. There are bleed-throughs, so to speak, in the form of unofficial perceptions that often occur, or "impossible" events that are seemingly beyond explanation. (Pause.) For now think of your own psyche, which is a consciousized identity, as a kind of "supernatural radio." All of the stations exist at once within the psyche. These do not come through with sound alone, but with all the living paraphernalia of the world. The "you" that you recognize is but one signal on one such station, tuned in to a certain frequency, experiencing that station's overall reality from your own viewpoint -- one that is unique and like no other, and yet contributing to the whole life of the station.
quotes.translation: 新段落。然而，从根本上看，意识是自由流动的。因此，这类现实始终存在于你们自己的心灵中——在你们的"本台"之外——而你们意识的某部分始终参与其中。句号。可以说，存在着以非官方感知形式出现的渗透现象，这些现象经常发生，或是看似无法解释的"不可能"事件。（停顿。）现在，请将你们的心灵——一个意识化的身份——视为一种"超自然收音机"。所有电台同时存在于心灵之内。这些电台不仅通过声音传递，还带着世间所有活生生的附属物。你们所认知的"你"只是其中一个电台上的一个信号，调谐到特定频率，从你们自己的视角体验该电台的整体现实——这个视角独一无二、无可替代，同时又为该电台的整体生命做出贡献。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 711
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: For that reason, science--after its first great adventurous era--had its own flaws built in, and so it must expand its definitions of reality or become a tin-can caricature of itself, a prostituted handmaiden to an outworn technology, and quite give up its early claims of investigating the nature of truth or reality. It could become as secondary to life as, say, the Roman Catholic Church is now, losing its hold upon world dominance, losing its claim of being the one official arbiter of reality.
quotes.translation: 正因如此，科学——在其第一个伟大冒险时代之后——本身就带有内在缺陷，因此它必须扩展对现实的定义，否则就会沦为自身的铁罐漫画，成为过时技术的娼妓婢女，彻底放弃早期探索真理或现实本质的主张。它可能变得像罗马天主教会现在这样次要于生活，失去对世界的主导权，失去作为现实唯一官方仲裁者的地位。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You may have become a part of the drama of a natural disaster, or avoided it as a result of other seemingly chance occurrences. What appears to you as chance or coincidence, however, is actually the result of the amazing organizations and communications active in the psychological reality of Framework 2. Again, you form your reality -- but how? And how do private existences touch each other, resulting in world events? Before we go any further, then, we must look into the nature of Framework 2.
quotes.translation: 你可能已成为某场自然灾害戏剧的一部分，或因其他看似偶然的事件而避开了它。然而，在你们看来是机缘巧合的事物，实际上是框架二心理现实中活跃的惊人组织与沟通的结果。再次强调，你们形成自己的现实——但如何形成？私人存在又如何相互接触，导致世界事件的发生？因此，在我们进一步探讨之前，必须审视框架二的本质。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 823, FEBRUARY 27, 1978
9:43 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: During our next session we will go more deeply into those pyramid gestalts of which we have spoken in the past, and perhaps consider from another viewpoint entirely the scope and nature that make up the idea of gods. Within certain frameworks these pyramid gestalts do have an electrical reality, but their existence goes beyond not the range but the nature of electrical reality. And because of the strength of their intensity, they are projected within every other field of actuality that exists.
quotes.translation: 在下一次课程中，我们将更深入地探讨过去提及的那些金字塔形完形，或许会从完全不同的视角来审视构成"神"这一概念的范畴与本质。在特定框架内，这些金字塔形完形确实具有电性实相，但其存在超越的并非电性实相的范围，而是其本质。由于它们强度的力量，它们被投射到所有存在的其他实相场域中。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Much of "Unknown" Reality is involved with the breaking up of theories that have been long accepted, but that prevent you from perceiving the powerful nature of those absent portions of the self. As you focus upon certain details from a larger field of physical reality, so then you focus upon only the small portion of yourself that you consider "real."
quotes.translation: "未知的"实相大部分涉及对长期被接受的理论的破除，这些理论阻碍你感知自我那些缺失部分的强大本质。正如你从更大的物质实相场域中聚焦于某些细节，你也只聚焦于你认为"真实"的那一小部分自我。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 731
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: A strong part of your personalities is therefore a product of the physical system in which you have physical existence. All ideas of reality must be glimpsed through the physical net which is the materialized form of these assumptions. You are, in other words, physically able to observe reality in a highly specialized fashion. You must, physically speaking, interpret existence in terms of these very definite assumptions.
quotes.translation: 因此，你们人格中强而有力的部分是你们拥有物质存在的物质系统的产物。所有关于实相的观念都必须透过物质之网——即这些假设的物质化形式——来瞥见。换句话说，你们在物质上能够以高度专门化的方式观察实相。从物质角度来说，你们必须根据这些非常明确的假设来诠释存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Consider this analogy. For one instant your consciousness is "alive," focused in physical reality. Now for the next instant it is focused somewhere else entirely, in a different system of reality. It is unalive, or "dead" to your way of thinking. The next instant it is "alive" again, focused in your reality, but you are not aware of the intervening instant of unaliveness. Your sense of continuity therefore is built up entirely on every other pulsation of consciousness. Is that clear to you?
quotes.translation: 考虑这个类比。一瞬间，你的意识"活着"，聚焦在物质实相中。下一瞬间，它完全聚焦在别处，在另一个不同的实相系统中。以你的思维方式，它是无生命的，或"死的"。再下一瞬间，它又"活着"，聚焦在你的实相中，但你并未意识到中间那个无生命的瞬间。因此，你的连续感完全建立在意识的每一次脉动之上。这对你来说清楚吗？
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: (Slowly:) Only because these units have their source outside of space and time is the present corporal reality a triumph of probabilities. Period. Your present image, for example, seems to be the only one possible for you, permanently yours for your lifetime at least; and what happens to it appears almost inevitable. If you become ill, say, you may wonder why, and yet once illness has happened it becomes part of the body's reality, and seems almost like an inevitable part of its experience.
quotes.translation: （缓慢地：）仅仅因为这些单元在时空之外有其源头，当前的肉体实相才是概率的胜利。句号。例如，你当前的影像似乎是你唯一可能的影像，至少在你一生中永久属于你；发生在它身上的事似乎几乎是不可避免的。如果你生病了，比如说，你可能会想知道为什么，然而一旦疾病发生，它就变成了身体实相的一部分，并且几乎像是其经验中不可避免的一部分。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 684
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: I am speaking now of the basic nature of reality, and not of any particular action, for particular actions can indeed be altered by other actions, and no given action occurs in a solitary manner. This last is extremely important. Actions are perceived as realities according to the nature, not of a given action, but according to the nature of the perceiver. His viewpoint and his field of reference will at all times color to some extent or another the nature of the reality which he perceives.
quotes.translation: 我现在谈论的是实相的基本本质，而非任何特定行动，因为特定行动确实可以被其他行动改变，而且没有任何行动是孤立发生的。最后这一点极其重要。行动被感知为实相，并非根据特定行动的本质，而是根据感知者的本质。他的观点和参考场域将始终以某种程度影响他所感知的实相的本质。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Now. As I said earlier, sense data does have a reality, but this reality does not reside in an object. The object represents your interpretation of the basic reality. The energy belongs to the idea. In other words, the prime energy within physical reality resides precisely in those intangibles which do not, because of their nature, appear within physical frameworks. They give life and reality to the physical framework.
quotes.translation: 现在。正如我之前所说，感官资料确实具有实相，但这种实相并不存在于物体之中。物体代表你对基本实相的解释。能量属于意念。换言之，物质实相中的主要能量恰恰存在于那些因其本质而无法在物质架构中显现的无形之物中。它们赋予物质架构生命与实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: Many people, however, remembering a dark dream, become frightened. You even structure your dreams, of course. For that matter, your dream world is as varied as the physical one. Each physical photographer has an idea of what he wants to capture on film, and so to that extent he structures his picture and his view. The same applies to the dream state. You have all kinds of dreams. You can take what you want, so to speak, from dream reality, as basically you take what you want from waking life. For that reason, your dream snapshots will show you the kind of experience that you are choosing from inner reality.
quotes.translation: 然而许多人因记得一个黑暗的梦而心生恐惧。你当然也在构建自己的梦境。就此而言，你的梦境世界与物质世界一样丰富多彩。每位物质摄影师对想要捕捉的画面都有构想，因此在一定程度上他构建了自己的照片与视角。梦境状态也是如此。你拥有各式各样的梦。可以说，你能从梦境实相中取用你所需之物，正如你从根本上从清醒生活中取用所需之物。正因如此，你的梦境快照将向你展示你从内在实相中选择的体验类型。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 719
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Any individual reacts to a reality as he perceives it to be, and he perceives it to be since he has himself created it from basic reality. The very distortions therefore form many of the characteristic differences which for him gives his reality its peculiar nature. Those whose actions set them apart drastically from others within your system, and who seem in one way or another mentally unbalanced, are often told that they must relate themselves more clearly with reality as it exists.
quotes.translation: 任何个体都依据自己所感知的实相做出反应，而他之所以如此感知，是因为他自身已从基本实相中创造了它。因此，正是这些扭曲构成了诸多特征差异，赋予其个人实相独特的性质。那些行为与你们系统内其他人截然不同、在某种程度上显得精神失衡的人，常被告知必须更清晰地与"存在"的实相建立关联。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: This is not hedging the matter, if you will excuse a pun, and the subject will take much further study on your parts. You must act as if there were an objective universe. The world, or field, that you presently inhabit is real, definite on your level. The fact that its reality is only limited to your level, and does not extend to other fields, must not tempt you to discount it; and yet while you must behave in a large manner as if your universe were inherently and basically objective, you must still retain the knowledge that this apparent objectivity has great limits, even practically speaking; and a too-great dependence in a world of objectivity can lead to a psychic imprisonment which is unnecessary.
quotes.translation: 请原谅我使用双关语，这并非回避问题，而你们需要对此课题进行更深入的研究。你必须表现得仿佛存在一个客观宇宙。你们当前栖居的世界或场域，在你们的层面上是真实而确定的。其实相仅限于你们层面、不延伸至其他场域这一事实，不应诱使你们轻视它；然而，当你们大体上必须表现得仿佛宇宙本质上是客观的时，仍需保留这样的认知：即便从实际角度而言，这种表面客观性也有极大局限；过度依赖客观世界可能导致不必要的灵性囚禁。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: The scientific framework is basically, now, just as senseless, though within it the facts often seem to prove themselves out, also. There are viruses, for example. Your beliefs become self-evident realities. It would be impossible to discuss human suffering without taking that into consideration. Ideas are transmitted from generation to generation--and those ideas are the carriers of all of your reality, its joys and its agonies. Science, however, is all in all (underlined) a poor healer. The church's concepts at least gave suffering a kind of dignity: It did (underlined) come from God--an unwelcome gift, perhaps--but after all it was punishment handed out from a firm father for a child's own good.
quotes.translation: 科学框架从根本上说同样毫无意义，尽管其内部的事实往往也能自我印证。例如病毒的存在。你们的信念成为不言自明的实相。若不考虑这一点，便无法讨论人类的苦难。观念代代相传——而这些观念承载着你们全部实相中的喜悦与痛苦。然而科学终究（着重号）是个蹩脚的疗愈者。教会的概念至少赋予苦难某种尊严：它确实（着重号）来自上帝——或许是不受欢迎的礼物——但终究是严厉父亲为子女好而施予的惩罚。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In the same way, each of my readers has a connection with the same level of psychic reality. In greater terms, all of this is happening at once. Ruburt is contributing and forming a certain portion of my experience, even as I am contributing to his. Your identities are not something already completed. Your most minute action, thought, and dream adds to the reality of your psyche, no matter how grand or austere the psyche may appear to you when you think of it as a hypothetical term.
quotes.translation: 同样地，我的每位读者都与同一层次的灵性实相有所联结。从更宏大的角度而言，这一切同时发生。鲁柏正在贡献并塑造我经验的特定部分，正如我贡献于他的经验。你们的身份并非已然完成之物。你们最微小的行动、思想与梦境都在增添你们心灵的实相——无论当你们将心灵视为假设性术语时，它显得多么庄严或纯粹。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The inner portions of your own identity and reality are not known to you, for you cannot objectify them and therefore you do not perceive them. So much of your energy is used in these productions that you cannot afford to perceive any reality except your own. Like children playing with blocks your focus of attention is upon physical blocks.
quotes.translation: 你自身身份与实相的内在部分并不为你所知，因为你无法将它们客观化，因此你感知不到它们。你的大量能量都用于这些创造，以至于除了你自己的实相之外，你无法感知任何其他实相。就像玩积木的孩子一样，你的注意力焦点集中在物质积木上。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: He has had many experiences in which he glimpsed momentarily the rich otherness within physical reality. He has known heightened perceptions of a unique nature. Never before, however, has he stepped firmly, while awake, into another level of reality, where he allowed himself to sense the continual vivid connection between worlds. He hid his own purpose from himself, as many of you do. At the same time he was pursuing it, of course, as all of you are working toward your own goals.
quotes.translation: 他曾有过许多经历，在其中他短暂地瞥见了物质实相中丰富的他者性。他体验过独特性质的高度感知。然而，他从未在清醒时坚定地踏入另一个实相层次，让自己感受到世界之间持续生动的联系。他像你们许多人一样，向自己隐藏了自己的目的。当然，与此同时他也在追求它，就像你们所有人都在朝着自己的目标努力一样。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 715
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: The most negative projection or prophecy seems to be the most practical one; when you are reading of the world's ills, you say in all honesty, and with no humor: "How can I ignore the reality, the destructive reality, of the present?" In the most practical, immediate, mundane terms, however, you and your world are in that moment naturally and physically safe, as your bodily senses immediately perceive. In the most basic of bodily terms you are not reacting to present conditions.
quotes.translation: 最负面的投射或预言似乎是最实际的；当你读到世界的弊病时，你会诚实地、毫无幽默地说："我怎能忽视当下的实相，那毁灭性的实相？"然而，从最实际、最直接、最世俗的角度来看，你和你的世界在那一刻自然且物质上是安全的，正如你的身体感官立即感知到的那样。在最基本的身体层面上，你并没有对当下的状况做出反应。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You understand the basic reality of subjective life. You will then still organize experience (underline organize), but you will not need artificial aids such as time to lean upon. You will not need to see thoughts materialized in physical matter, for you will have long since learned that the thoughts and not the matter are the basic reality. You will be able therefore to dispense with many seemingly permanent mass images, but when you form them you will realize why and how.
quotes.translation: 你理解主观生命的基本实相。那么你仍会组织经验（强调组织），但不再需要时间这样的人为辅助来依靠。你不再需要看到思想在物质中具体化，因为你早已学会思想而非物质才是基本实相。因此，你将能够摒弃许多看似永恒的大众形象，但当你形成它们时，你会明白为何以及如何。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Action, you may say, is carried away by itself. Reality possibilities are endless. You are familiar with very small portions of reality. Your perception characteristics at this time dictate and limit the aspects of action that you can perceive. You can, however, focus very clearly on other aspects. And particular types of consciousness and identities are merely the result of action's formation into perception patterns with which it can focus upon certain aspects of itself.
quotes.translation: 行动，你可能会说，被自身所带走。实相的可能性是无限的。你只熟悉实相的极小部分。你此时的感知特性决定并限制了你所能感知的行动面向。然而，你可以非常清晰地聚焦于其他面向。而特定类型的意识与身份，仅仅是行动形成感知模式的结果，通过这些模式，行动可以聚焦于自身的某些面向。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: In the predream state you directly encounter a reality in which those probabilities exist all at once to your perception. In a dazzling display you are aware of such events from infinite perspectives. Consciously you could not grasp such information, much less act upon it, nor could you maintain your particular, unique, psychological stance. You still take advantage of that level of being, however, using that immeasurable data as a basis to form the reality that you know.
quotes.translation: 在预梦状态中，你直接遭遇一个实相，其中那些可能性同时存在于你的感知中。在令人目眩的展示中，你从无限视角意识到这些事件。有意识地，你无法把握这样的信息，更不用说据此行动，也无法维持你特定、独特的心理立场。然而，你仍利用那个存在层次，以那不可估量的数据为基础，形成你所知的实相。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This reality and those attributes will appear within various systems of actuality in keeping with the camouflage data of any given system. The inner experience with the multidimensional God can come in two main areas. One is through the realization that this prime moving force is within everything that you can perceive with your senses. The other method is to realize that this primary motive force has a reality independent of its connection with the world of appearances.
quotes.translation: 这个实相及其属性会出现在各种不同的实际系统中，以符合任何特定系统的伪装资料。与多维神的内在体验可来自两个主要领域。一是领悟到这股原动力存在于你感官所能感知的一切事物之中。另一方法是认识到这股原动力具有独立于表象世界之外的实相。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This may sound contradictory. We have stated that a reality remains as it is, unchanged even though the perceiver, because of his sense apparatus, may perceive it in a limited or distorted fashion. While this is to some extent true, we can now delve into the matter somewhat more fully. In our later sessions for example, we have mentioned that the desire for duplication must always result in a distortion, but this distortion is also the basis therefore for a new reality.
quotes.translation: 这听起来可能矛盾。我们曾说过，即使感知者因其感官机制可能以有限或扭曲的方式感知，实相仍保持其本来面目不变。虽然这在某种程度上属实，但现在我们可以更深入地探讨这个问题。例如在后续课程中，我们提到复制的欲望必然导致扭曲，但这种扭曲本身也是新实相的基础。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Then also, I remember what Seth said about being reckless in the pursuit of the ideal. (See the Introductory Notes.) I don't know that I was that daring, but I was persistent despite the hesitations and misgivings. So along with Seth's work, we tried to share our reality with the reader, and to provide a platform in time for knowledge that must basically straddle our ideas of time and reality alike.
quotes.translation: 此外，我记得赛斯关于追求理想时要无所畏惧的说法（见引言注释）。我不确定自己是否真有那般胆识，但尽管犹豫不决、心存疑虑，我始终坚持不懈。因此，在赛斯著作的伴随下，我们试图与读者分享我们的实相，并为本质上必须超越我们时间与实相观念的知识，及时提供一个平台。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 7. In Session 692 for Volume 1 of "Unknown" Reality see the material on Sue's double dreams in the opening notes and in Note 2. Personally, at least, I see strong connections between the idea of double dreams and the kind of conscious reincarnational memory -- or knowledge -- detailed by Sue in this appendix.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Now this is my outline for the book, but it contains merely a sketch of my intentions. I am not giving a fuller outline, for I do not want Ruburt to anticipate me. The difficulties involved in such communications will be given thoroughly. It will be made clear that so-called paranormal communications come from various levels of reality, and that those communications describe the reality in which they exist. So I will describe mine, and others of which I have knowledge.
quotes.translation: 这就是我对本书的规划，但仅勾勒了我的意图轮廓。我不给出更详尽的纲要，因为我不想让鲁柏预知我的想法。这类沟通所涉及的困难将得到全面阐述。我们将明确说明，所谓超自然沟通来自不同层次的实相，而这些沟通所描述的正是它们自身存在的实相。因此，我将描述我的实相，以及我所知晓的其他实相。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Here is a copy of that outline, which was given to us in Session 510, January 19, 1970. As you'll see here, Seth calls me Ruburt, and Rob, Joseph. These names represent our entire personalities as distinguished from our present physically oriented selves.

quotes.text: Such electrical realities then, through the strength of their intensities and their particular range, are projected into some fields, but not projected into others. Any so-called travel through time involves then a traveling through such intensities. This is obviously impossible for the physical camouflaged self, but it is not impossible for the inner self, which as you know has its own electrical reality, which is again composed of particular impulses and intensities.
quotes.translation: 这类电性实相，凭借其强度的力量与特定范围，被投射到某些场域，而非其他场域。任何所谓的时间旅行，都涉及穿越这类强度。这对物质伪装自我而言显然不可能，但对内在自我却非不可能——如你所知，内在自我拥有自身的电性实相，同样由特定脉冲与强度构成。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: You visit with travelers from other systems. You plant thought forms in other systems, and you tend them. You hold congregation with the many segments of your own reality. Your world is like a thought form in my experience. Your physical system exists in many intensities, and can be perceived. Probable selves can be viewed by myself and others like me, for we see your reality in all of its dimensions, and you view it from the small present forms that you know. (Pause.)
quotes.translation: 你与来自其他系统的旅者会面。你在其他系统中植入思想形态，并照料它们。你与自己实相的众多片段举行集会。你的世界在我体验中如同一个思想形态。你的物质系统存在于多种强度中，且能被感知。可能自我可以被我和像我这样的存在所观照，因为我们看到你实相的所有维度，而你则从你所知的微小当下形态中观看它。（停顿）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: I must explain this before I can clearly give you an idea of my environment, or of those other systems of reality in which I operate. There is no space between my environment and yours, for example, no physical boundaries that separate us. In a very real way of speaking, your concept of reality as seen through your physical senses, scientific instruments, or arrived at through deduction, bears little resemblance to the facts -- and the facts are difficult to explain.
quotes.translation: 我必须先解释这一点，才能清楚地让你了解我的环境，或我所运作的其他实相系统。举例来说，我的环境与你的环境之间并无空间，也没有任何物理界限将我们分隔。从非常真实的意义上说，你们通过肉体感官、科学仪器所看到的实相概念，或通过推理得出的结论，与事实几乎毫无相似之处——而事实本身很难解释。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You see physical bodies and suppose that any personality must appear in physical terms. None of you are physical; that which is enduring is not physical. In that respect at least you are no different than I. Consciousness is the force behind matter, and it forms many other realities beside the physical one. It is only because your own viewpoint is presently so limited that it seems to you that physical reality is the rule and the mode and the purpose of existence.
quotes.translation: 你们看到物质身体，便以为任何人格都必须以物质形式呈现。但你们中没有一个人是物质的；那永恒不变的并非物质。至少在这方面，你们与我并无不同。意识是物质背后的力量，它除了物质实相之外，还形成了许多其他实相。仅仅因为你们目前的视角如此受限，才使你们觉得物质实相是存在的规则、模式与目的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: I have told you that dream reality is more cohesive than you may have supposed. Such characteristics as shared dreams go a long way to stabilize dream reality. When you dream of others they know it. When they dream of you, you know this. There would be nothing to be gained however in conscious awareness of these conditions.
quotes.translation: 我曾告诉你们，梦的实相比你们可能以为的更具凝聚力。诸如共享梦境之类的特性，大大有助于稳定梦的实相。当你们梦见他人时，他们知道；当他们梦见你们时，你们也知道。然而，有意识地觉察这些状况并无益处。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: At a conscious level, of course, neither of you realized, or wanted to realize, the kind of complete repeal and overhaul that was implied by our sessions, and for some years you managed to hold many official views of reality along with the newer concepts, not ready to understand that an entire new way of thinking was involved, a new relationship of the individual with reality. So you tried out some new methods piecemeal, here and there, with good-enough results.
quotes.translation: 当然，在意识层面上，你们两人都没有意识到，或不想意识到，我们的课程所暗示的那种彻底的废除与重整。多年来，你们设法将许多关于实相的官方观点与新概念并存，尚未准备好理解这涉及一整套全新的思维方式，即个体与实相的新关系。于是你们零零碎碎地尝试了一些新方法，结果还算不错。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth speaks in Session 826, held for Mass Events on March 8, 1978:

quotes.text: They must be interpreted instead through the use of those inner senses of which I have spoken, for these are the basic tools of the inner self. I realize that it is frowned upon to speak in terms of a limited intellect, which cannot understand a whole reality because of a built-in deficiency; nevertheless while it is true that the intellect by itself cannot grasp or comprehend inner reality, this should not be thought of as a deficiency inherent in intellect.
quotes.translation: 相反，它们必须通过我所说的那些内在感官来解读，因为这些是内在自我的基本工具。我明白，谈论有限智力因其固有缺陷而无法理解整体实相，是令人不悦的；然而，尽管智力本身确实无法把握或理解内在实相，但这不应被视为智力固有的缺陷。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: You can intellectually understand what I am telling you, but the brain （shaking head） cannot experience reality directly. This experience must come from the mind, through use of the inner senses. I want it understood that camouflage physical reality is indeed a reality, even while it is a distortion of something else.
quotes.translation: 你们可以在理智上理解我所说的话，但大脑（摇头）无法直接体验实相。这种体验必须来自心灵，通过使用内在感官。我希望你们明白，伪装物质实相确实是一种实相，即使它是对其他事物的扭曲。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: You must indeed for practical reasons pretend as if the automobile had no reality except the reality with which you are familiar, but this is not the case. I mentioned feeling sound because this is a capability that lies latent within your own physical system, but this same sort of a juggling of perspective data is what happens, generally speaking, when inhabitants of a different system perceive realities that also have an existence within your own system.
quotes.translation: 为了实际考量，你确实必须假装汽车除了你所熟悉的实相外别无实相，但事实并非如此。我提到"感受声音"，是因为这种能力潜藏于你们自身的物理系统之内，但一般而言，当不同系统的居民感知到也存在于你们系统内的实相时，正是这种视角数据的交错重组在发生。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The flame feeds the fire and the fire feeds the flame. Therefore, while this time one of continuous moments is no longer experienced after death, it is still a reality within basic time itself, a reality toward which the personality simply is no longer focused. Because the individual is focused within time one now, you still realize, or should, that the time one is only a small portion of time, and that other kinds of time exist of which you are not aware.
quotes.translation: 火焰滋养火势，火势也滋养火焰。因此，虽然这种连续时刻的时间在死后不再被体验，但它仍是基本时间本身内的一个实相——只是人格不再聚焦于它。由于个体此刻聚焦于时间一，你仍应认识到时间一仅是时间的一小部分，还有其它你未曾察觉的时间存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: It may outrage your intellect, and the evidence of your physical senses may shout that it is untrue, yet a belief in good without a belief in evil is actually highly realistic, since in physical life it will keep your body healthier, keep you psychologically free of many fears and mental difficulties, and bring you a feeling of ease and spontaneity in which the development of your abilities can be better fulfilled. After death it will release you from the belief in demons and hell, and enforced punishment. You will be better prepared to understand the nature of reality as it is. I understand that the concept does indeed offend your intellect, and that your senses seem to deny it. Yet you should already realize that your senses tell you many things, which are not true; and I tell you that your physical senses perceive a reality that is a result of your beliefs.
quotes.translation: 这可能冒犯你的理智，你肉体的感官证据也可能大声宣称这是虚假的，然而相信善而不信恶实际上高度务实——因为在物质生活中，这能让你的身体更健康，让你心理上免于许多恐惧和精神困扰，带给你轻松与自发性，使你的能力得以更好发展。死后，它将使你摆脱对恶魔、地狱和强制惩罚的信念。你将能更好地理解实相的本质。我明白这个概念确实冒犯你的理智，你的感官似乎也在否认它。但你应该已经意识到，你的感官告诉你许多不真实的事；我告诉你，你的肉体感官所感知的实相，正是你信念的结果。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Unknowingly, the sick often give up their power to act in a healthy manner to the physicians. The doctors accept this mandate since they share the same framework of belief, so the medical profession obviously needs patients as badly as the ill need the hospitals. Society as you know it, not understanding the nature of normal aggression, considers it violent. The prisons and law enforcement agencies need criminals in the same way that criminals need them, for they operate within the same system of belief. Each accepts violence as a method of behavior and survival. (Pause.) If you do not understand that you create your own reality, then you may assign all good results to a personified god, and need the existence of a devil to explain the undesirable reality. So churches as they now exist in Western society need a devil as well as a god.
quotes.translation: 病人往往在不知不觉中将健康行动的能力让渡给医生。医生接受这个委托，因为他们共享相同的信念架构，因此医疗行业显然像病人需要医院一样需要病人。你们所知的社会，因不理解正常攻击性的本质，将其视为暴力。监狱和执法机构需要罪犯，正如罪犯需要它们——因为它们在相同的信念系统内运作。双方都将暴力视为行为与生存的方式。（停顿）若你不明白自己创造了自己的实相，就可能将所有好的结果归于人格化的神，并需要恶魔的存在来解释不理想的实相。因此，西方社会现存的教会既需要神，也需要恶魔。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The point of all this is that these units are unpredictable, and fulfill all probabilities of consciousness. Any concepts of gods or other beings that are based upon limited ideas of personhood will ultimately be futile. You view the fantastic variety of physical life -- its animals, insects, birds, fish, man and all his works -- with hardly a qualm; yet you must understand that the nature of consciousness itself is far more varied, and you must learn to think of an inner reality that is as infinite as the exterior one. These concepts alone do alter your present consciousness, and change it in degree. The present idea of the soul, you see, is a "primitive" idea that can scarcely begin to explain the creativity or reality from which mankind's being comes. You are multipersons (intently). You exist in many times and places at once. You exist as one person, simultaneously. This does not deny the independence of the persons, but your inner reality straddles their reality, while it also serves as a psychic world in which they can grow.3
quotes.translation: 这一切的关键在于：这些单位是不可预测的，它们实现了意识的所有可能性。任何基于有限人格观念的神或其它存有概念，最终都将徒劳无功。你目睹物质生命惊人的多样性——动物、昆虫、鸟类、鱼类、人类及其所有作品——几乎毫无疑虑；但你必须明白，意识本身的本质远为多样，你必须学会思考一个与外在实相同样无限的内在实相。仅这些概念就能改变你当前的意识，并在程度上改变它。你看，当前关于灵魂的观念是一种"原始"观念，几乎无法开始解释人类存在所源自的创造力或实相。你是多重人格（专注地）。你同时存在于许多时间与地点。你同时作为一个人存在。这并不否定各人格的独立性，但你的内在实相跨越了它们的实相，同时作为它们可以成长的心灵世界。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 683
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: The picture is a relatively simple one, all in all -- one in which each consciousness is assumed to be directed toward a particular focus, is ensconced in one body, with its existence bounded by birth at one end and by death at the other. (Pause.) Unfortunately, that picture is as limited as any one of your photographs. You are used to examining your dream state from the viewpoint of your "waking" condition, but some time in the dream state try to examine your normal waking reality. Simply give yourself the instructions to do so. You may be quite surprised with the results. Speaking as simply as I can, and using concepts that you can understand, let me put it this way: From the other side -- within what is loosely called the dream state -- there is an existence quite as valid as your own, and from that viewpoint you can be considered as the dreamer. "You" are the part of you concentrating in this reality. You form it through information and through energy that on the one hand has its source outside this system, and that on the other constantly flows into this system -- and so in that respect the systems are united.
quotes.translation: 总而言之，这幅图景相对简单——其中每个意识都被假定指向特定焦点，安居于一个身体内，其存在以出生为起点、死亡为终点。（停顿）不幸的是，这幅图景就像你任何一张照片那样有限。你习惯于从"清醒"状态检视梦境状态，但请在梦境状态中尝试检视你正常的清醒实相。只需给自己下达这样的指令。你可能会对结果感到相当惊讶。尽可能简单地说，用你能理解的概念，让我这样表述：从另一边——在所谓的梦境状态内——存在着与你们实相同等有效的存在，从那个视角看，你可以被视为做梦者。"你"是专注于这个实相的那部分你。你通过信息与能量形成它——这些信息与能量一方面源自这个系统之外，另一方面不断流入这个系统——因此在这个意义上，这些系统是统一的。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 699
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: You may, for example, have read books numbering the "inner realms," and telling you what you can expect to encounter in each. Many of these speak of lords or gods of the realm, or of demons. In a strange way these books do provide a service, for at certain levels you will find your own ideas materialized; and if you believe in demons then in those terms you will encounter them. The authors, however, suppose that the devils have a reality outside of your belief in them, and such is not the case. The demons simply represent a state of your own mind that is seemingly out there, objectified. Therefore, whatever methods the authors used to triumph over these demons is often given as proof not only of the demons' reality, but of each method's effectiveness.
quotes.translation: 例如，你可能读过一些书籍，它们列举了"内在领域"，并告诉你每个领域会遇到什么。其中许多书谈到领域之主或神祇，或恶魔。奇怪的是，这些书确实提供了一种服务，因为在某些层次上，你会发现自己的想法被具体化；如果你相信恶魔，那么在这些条件下你就会遇到它们。然而，作者们假设这些恶魔在你相信它们之外具有实在性，但事实并非如此。恶魔仅仅代表你自己心智的一种状态，看似外在，被客观化了。因此，无论作者们用什么方法战胜这些恶魔，这些方法往往不仅被当作恶魔实在性的证明，也被当作每种方法有效性的证据。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 710
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: You are social creatures, as the animals are. Despite many of your cherished, erroneous beliefs, your nations exist as the result of cooperation, not competition, as do all social groupings. To be ostracized is no laughing matter. The comfort of social discourse represents one of the great building blocks of families and civilizations. The set criteria of reality, therefore, operate as organizing, psychic, and physical frameworks. There is still more flexibility within those frameworks, however, than is recognized.
