You still try to carry your own cultural versions of reality into the dream state, for example, but the natural heritage of both body and mind escapes such repression -- and despite yourselves, in your dreams you come in touch with a greater picture of reality that will not be shunted aside.
quotes.translation: 你们是社会性生物，就像动物一样。尽管你们有许多珍视的错误信念，你们的国家是合作而非竞争的结果而存在，所有社会群体也是如此。被排斥绝非小事。社会交流的舒适感代表着家庭和文明的伟大基石之一。因此，实在的设定标准作为组织性的、心灵的和物理的框架运作。然而，这些框架内仍有比人们所认识的更多的灵活性。例如，你们仍然试图将自己文化版本的实在带入梦境状态，但身体和心灵的自然遗产却逃脱了这种压制——尽管你们不情愿，在梦中你们接触到一种更广阔的实在图景，它不会被搁置一旁。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Session 757 was devoted to other matters that Jane and I were interested in.)

quotes.text: They cast their light over historical events because they are responsible for those events. They mix and merge the inner, unseen but felt, eternal psychic experience of man with the temporal events of his physical days, and form a combination that structures thoughts and beliefs from civilization to civilization. In Framework 2 the interior power of nature is ever-changing. The dreams, hopes, aspirations and fears of man interact in a constant motion that then forms the events of your world. That interaction includes not only man, of course, but the emotional reality of all earthly consciousnesses as well, from a microbe to a scholar, from a frog to a star. You interpret the phenomena of your world according to the mythic characteristics that you have accepted. You organize physical reality, then, through ideas. You use only those perceptions that serve to give those ideas validity. The physical body itself is quite capable of putting the world together in different fashions than the one that is familiar to you.
quotes.translation: 它们将光芒投射到历史事件上，因为它们对这些事件负责。它们将人类内在的、看不见但可感受到的永恒心灵体验与他物质日子的短暂事件混合融合，形成一种组合，从文明到文明地构建思想和信念。在框架二中，自然的内在力量不断变化。人类的梦想、希望、渴望和恐惧在持续的运动中相互作用，然后形成你们世界的事件。当然，这种相互作用不仅包括人类，还包括从微生物到学者、从青蛙到星星的所有地球意识的情感实在。你们根据所接受的神话特征来解释你们世界的现象。因此，你们通过观念来组织物理实在。你们只使用那些能赋予这些观念有效性的感知。物质身体本身完全有能力以不同于你们熟悉的方式组合世界。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: eth's statement at the end of dictation for the 815th session, "I will try to begin work on our book in a more predictable fashion," reflected his good intent, but things didn't turn out that way. Jane and I let the holiday season intervene to some extent, and Session 816, which came through the day after Christmas, didn't concern book work at all. Then, starting in early January, we held eight sessions having to do with questions we'd put off dealing with for a long time; some were personal and some were not. We stayed with our Monday-Saturday evening session routine, though, and kept busy with all of our other projects.1
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Much of this is difficult to explain, again, for information and knowledge is constantly transformed -- almost completely reborn, so to speak, through characteristics that are inherently a part of thought itself. Knowledge is changed automatically through the auspices of each consciousness who perceives it. It is magnified and yet refined. It is a constant language, yet one that transforms itself. When I "attend these sessions," or "speak," then I exchange with others a more complicated system of reality than any computer could handle. You do not understand or perceive the ways in which your reality contributes to the foundation of the mass-world reality that you experience. Unconsciously, each individual participates in forming that world. In my case, however, I am aware of the same kind of activities, only in regard to many realities rather than one.2
quotes.translation: 这其中的许多内容难以解释，再次强调，因为信息和知识不断被转化——几乎可以说是完全重生，通过思想本身固有的特性。知识通过感知它的每个意识的赞助而自动改变。它被放大，又被精炼。它是一种恒定的语言，却又自我转化。当我"参加这些课程"或"说话"时，我与他人交换的实在系统比任何计算机能处理的都要复杂。你们不理解或感知不到你们的实在如何为你们所经历的大众世界实在的基础做出贡献。无意识地，每个个体都参与形成那个世界。然而，就我而言，我意识到同样类型的活动，只是涉及许多实在而非一个。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 818, FEBRUARY 6, 1978
10:19 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Man does have an instinct and a desire to live, and he has an instinct and a desire to die. The same applies to other creatures. In his life [each] man is embarked upon a cooperative venture with his own species, and with the other species, and dying he also in that regard acts in a cooperative manner, returning his physical substance to the earth. (Pause.) Physically speaking, man's "purpose" is to help enrich the quality of existence in all of its dimensions. Spiritually speaking, his "purpose" is to understand the qualities of love and creativity, to intellectually and psychically understand the sources of his being, and to lovingly create other dimensions of reality of which he is presently unaware. (Pause.) In his thinking, in the quality of his thoughts, in their motion, he is indeed experimenting with a unique and a new kind of reality, forming other subjective worlds which will in their turn grow into consciousness and song, which will in their turn flower from a dream dimension into other ones. Man is learning to create new worlds. In order to do so he has taken on many challenges.
quotes.translation: 人类确实有生存的本能和欲望，也有死亡的本能和欲望。这同样适用于其他生物。在他的生命中，每个人都与自己的物种以及其他物种进行合作冒险，而在死亡时，他也以合作的方式行动，将他的物质实体归还给地球。（停顿。）从物理角度来说，人类的"目的"是帮助丰富所有维度的存在品质。从灵性角度来说，他的"目的"是理解爱与创造力的品质，在理智上和心灵上理解他存在的源头，并充满爱地创造他目前尚未意识到的其他实在维度。（停顿。）在他的思考中，在他思想的品质中，在它们的运动中，他确实在实验一种独特而新颖的实在，形成其他主观世界，这些世界反过来将成长为意识和歌声，将从梦境维度绽放成其他维度。人类正在学习创造新世界。为了做到这一点，他承担了许多挑战。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: There are countless relationships between species that go unrecognized. The generations of all species interact. The genetic cues are not triggered on the proposition, obviously, that a species exists alone on the planet, but also in response to genetic sequences that operate in all of the species combined. The genetic system, again, is not closed nearly as much as supposed. That is, again, because the basic units of consciousness that build up matter--that form matter--are themselves endowed with a subjective acuteness. This also accounts for my earlier statement, that in usually understood terms the environment and its creatures "evolve" together. (Long pause.) Your position on the scale of awareness inclines you to categorize consciousnesses so that only your own familiar brand seems to fit the definition--so again here I remind you that consciousness is everywhere in the deepest terms, because All That Is disperses itself throughout physical reality. All portions of that reality have their own rights to existence, and purposes within it. So of course do all peoples, and the races.
quotes.translation: 物种之间存在无数未被认识的关系。所有物种的世代相互影响。基因线索显然不是基于一个物种单独存在于行星上的假设而被触发，而是也对所有物种组合中运作的基因序列做出反应。基因系统再次不像人们假设的那样封闭。这是因为构成物质——形成物质——的基本意识单位本身具有主观的敏锐性。这也解释了我之前的陈述，即通常理解中，环境及其生物"共同进化"。（长时间停顿。）你们在意识尺度上的位置使你们倾向于对意识进行分类，以至于只有你们自己熟悉的类型似乎符合定义——所以再次提醒你们，在最深层的意义上，意识无处不在，因为一切万有将自己分散到整个物理实在中。该实在的所有部分都有其存在的权利和其中的目的。当然，所有民族和种族也是如此。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The intellect is extremely important in the manipulation of camouflage patterns once they are created. You have made your world, and your intellect should help you deal with what you have created. It has other vital functions which I shall enumerate at a later date. However, I cannot say this too often: You are more than your conscious mind, much more, and the self which you do not admit happens to be the portion of yourself which not only insures your own survival in the physical universe which it has made, but which is also the connective portion of yourself with inner reality. Which is, when all is said and done, the only basic reality; and which also continually enables you to create these camouflage patterns, and which contains knowledge and intuitions and memories which you need in a most desperate manner if you are ever to understand yourselves, and if the race of mankind is ever to evolve to its fullest.
quotes.translation: 智力在伪装模式被创造后的操控中极为重要。你们创造了你们的世界，而你们的智力应帮助你们处理自己所创造的一切。智力还有其他重要功能，我将在日后详述。然而，我再怎么强调也不为过：你们远不止是你们的意识心智，远不止于此，而你们所不承认的那个自己，恰好是你们自身的一部分——它不仅确保你们在它所创造的物理宇宙中的生存，也是你们与内在实相相连的部分。而内在实相，说到底，是唯一的基本实相；它也不断使你们能够创造这些伪装模式，并包含你们最迫切需要了解的知识、直觉和记忆——如果你们要理解自己，如果人类种族要进化到其极致的话。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Nevertheless it also partakes of the strange unity that unites all personalities beneath the camouflage pattern, while at the same time it is only partially connected to the camouflage pattern. In some respects art creations are a meeting of the dream world and the world of camouflage patterns, but in a deeper way art creations represent the appearance or materialization in the actual element of physical time of inner realities. That is, the inner individualistic self forces its vision and knowledge into the world of camouflage pattern, giving its dreams a physical reality denied to the usual dream. And here the use of energy for this purpose is conscious, that is the strong hidden self actually makes use of the camouflage conscious self and molds the two into a reality that combines two planes. This I hope will become clear as you read it over.
quotes.translation: 然而，它也参与了那种奇特的统一性，这种统一性在伪装模式之下将所有人格联结在一起，同时它又只与伪装模式部分相连。在某些方面，艺术创作是梦境世界与伪装模式世界的交汇，但更深入地说，艺术创作代表了内在实相在物理时间的实际元素中的显现或物质化。也就是说，内在的个体化自我将其愿景和知识强行注入伪装模式的世界，赋予其梦境一种通常梦境所不具备的物理实相。而在此，能量为此目的的使用是有意识的，即强大的隐藏自我实际上利用了伪装的意识自我，并将两者塑造成一种结合了两个层面的实相。我希望你们反复阅读后能明白这一点。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Beneath this, pure and simple, undistorted, there for the searching, absolutely free for the asking, is the knowledge inherent in the inner self pertaining to the inner universe as a whole, its laws and principles, its composition. Here you will find, undistorted, uncamouflaged, the innate knowledge of the creation of the camouflage universe, the mechanics involved, much of the material that I have given you, the method and ways by which the inner self as a basic inhabitant of the inner universe, existing in the climate of psychological reality, helps create the various planes of existence, constructs outer senses to project and perceive the various apparent realities or camouflages, how the inner self reincarnates on the various planes. Here you will find your answers as to how the inner self transforms energy for his own purposes, changes his form, adopts other apparent realities, and all this free for the investigation.
quotes.translation: 在此之下，纯粹而简单，未经扭曲，唾手可得，完全免费，是内在自我所固有的知识，关乎整个内在宇宙及其法则、原理和构成。在这里，你们会发现，未经扭曲、未经伪装的，是创造伪装宇宙的固有知识、所涉及的机制、我给你们的大量资料、内在自我作为内在宇宙的基本居民（存在于心理实相的气候中）帮助创造各种存在层面、构建外在感官以投射和感知各种表面实相或伪装、以及内在自我如何在各个层面转世的方法和途径。在这里，你们会找到答案：内在自我如何为自己的目的转化能量、改变形态、采用其他表面实相，而这一切都可供探究。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: And good evening to our friend. I do have some remarks for you and I hope that they will be helpful. You must remember to hold your head up. You should know better than to cower when you come here. I have no whip; and I would not use one if I had one. You are not sure enough of your own abilities or of your own worth. You cannot drive through physical life in the same way that you drive your car down the highway. You shall indeed, get traffic tickets and of a different kind, only you yourself give yourself tickets. You cannot force reality to give you what you want. You cannot manipulate events for egotistical purposes. You can manipulate events and you can manipulate them for your own egotistical purposes; but when you do so, you give yourself a traffic ticket. You must want what is best for your own development and the development of others rather than specifically determining what you think consciously is better for you and then trying to force or coerce fate to get this for you. If you want what is best for your own development and what is best for the development of others, then you shall attain it. It shall come to you effortlessly. I am leading up to certain issues here. You are not always aware of what is best for you on a conscious level. Often the person that you think you want or need is not the person you want or need on other levels. When you drive your car you often attempt to speed through reality as quickly as you can, and you are pleased with yourself as the driver of the vehicle. You like driving because you feel that it gets you where you want to go and quickly, and you do not mind breaking a few small rules of the road in the process. Now the small rules that you break, are indeed, minute ones. It is not that you break a specific rule; it is the attitude that allows you to break the rule; and this applies to other roads beside the physical highway. You want to get to your destination too quickly. The destination is within you. You do not have to go any place to get that destination; and it is only when you think that that destination lies elsewhere that you allow yourself to go astray. Your identity is within you and do not look for it in others. This is perhaps the strongest point of my message to you this evening, the one I would have you take to heart. When you realize that your own identity is within you will not spend energy seeking to find yourself in others. Others cannot give you a sense of worth; this is your own. Any lack is your own lack. I may perhaps deal with more specific issues this evening. But I will not discuss them until these points have been made.
quotes.translation: 晚上好，我们的朋友。我确实有些话要对你说，希望它们能有所帮助。你必须记住要抬起头来。你来这里时，应该知道不该畏缩。我没有鞭子；就算有，我也不会用。你对自己的能力或价值不够确信。你不能像在高速公路上开车那样，以同样的方式穿越物质生活。你确实会收到交通罚单，而且是另一种类型的，只是这些罚单是你自己开给自己的。你不能强迫实相给你想要的东西。你不能为了自我目的而操控事件。你可以操控事件，也可以为了自我目的而操控它们；但当你这样做时，你就给自己开了一张交通罚单。你必须想要对你自身发展及他人发展最有利的东西，而不是具体决定你意识上认为什么对你更好，然后试图强迫或胁迫命运为你得到它。如果你想要对你自身发展最有利、对他人发展最有利的东西，那么你就会得到它。它会毫不费力地来到你身边。我在这里要引出某些问题。你在意识层面并不总是知道什么对你最有利。通常，你认为你想要或需要的那个人，在其他层面上并非你想要或需要的那个人。当你开车时，你常常试图尽可能快地穿越实相，并为自己作为车辆的驾驶者而沾沾自喜。你喜欢开车，因为你感觉它能让你快速到达想去的地方，而且你不介意在这个过程中违反一些小的交通规则。现在，你违反的那些小规则，确实微不足道。问题不在于你违反了一条具体规则；而在于允许你违反规则的态度；而这适用于物理高速公路以外的其他道路。你太急于到达你的目的地。目的地就在你之内。你不需要去任何地方就能到达那个目的地；只有当你认为那个目的地存在于别处时，你才会让自己误入歧途。你的身份就在你之内，不要到别人那里去寻找它。这也许是我今晚对你传达的信息中最有力的一点，也是我希望你铭记于心的一点。当你意识到你自己的身份就在你之内时，你就不会花费精力去别人那里寻找自己。别人不能给你一种价值感；这是你自己的。任何缺乏都是你自己的缺乏。我今晚可能会讨论更具体的问题。但在这些要点被阐明之前，我不会讨论它们。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

source_raw.title: (I thought it a good idea that this be the end of the session, however, Seth came through again at 10:30 as the three of us sat talking about various things. A humorous repartee followed, with Seth answering our questions on a variety of subjects. Two serious notes: Alice Butts, who died last week, has already visited my father. And Dr. Instream, who would say "There is no Seth, " has not much longer to live. Also -- Bill Gallagher will not see certain truths about himself etc. Alice Butts not helping Ruth Butts ' son, etc. See Session 398.)

quotes.text: I realize that when you discuss him that you say--I know he has failings. This is to assure yourself that, after all, the male is not so all powerful. But you do not see this man's good points or failings clearly. Some of the qualities that you imagine in him as virtues are not and some of the qualities that you imagine to be failings are not failings. You will never have any relationship with the Dick Reed that you have projected upon a living human being. You may have a relationship with that human being, but there is a world of difference between that human being and the imagined image of him to which you react. And it is that image that you see when you look at him and when think of him. That imagined image is real in your mind, it is reality. But you cannot project that image upon another human being and deny him his own reality. You have no chance in a thousand lives of having a relationship with the man you think of as being Dick Reed, because you cannot have a two-way relationship with an image that is one-sided and has no flesh. Now give us a moment. While we are beginning a job, we may as well do a good one.
quotes.translation: 我意识到，当你谈论他时，你会说——我知道他有缺点。这是为了让自己确信，毕竟，男性并非那么全能。但你并没有清楚地看到这个人的优点或缺点。你想象中他的一些品质是美德，其实并非如此；而你想象中他的一些品质是缺点，其实也不是缺点。你永远无法与你投射到一个活生生的人身上的那个迪克·里德建立任何关系。你可以与那个人建立关系，但那个人与你所反应的那个想象形象之间有天壤之别。而当你看着他、想着他时，你看到的就是那个形象。那个想象的形象在你的心智中是真实的，它是实相。但你不能把那个形象投射到另一个人身上，并否认他自身的实相。你在一千次生命中都没有机会与你认为的那个迪克·里德建立关系，因为你无法与一个单方面的、没有血肉的形象建立双向关系。现在给我们一点时间。既然我们开始做一项工作，我们不妨把它做好。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

source_raw.title: (I thought it a good idea that this be the end of the session, however, Seth came through again at 10:30 as the three of us sat talking about various things. A humorous repartee followed, with Seth answering our questions on a variety of subjects. Two serious notes: Alice Butts, who died last week, has already visited my father. And Dr. Instream, who would say "There is no Seth, " has not much longer to live. Also -- Bill Gallagher will not see certain truths about himself etc. Alice Butts not helping Ruth Butts ' son, etc. See Session 398.)

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... Individually and en masse, you form the world that you know, yet it has an overall individual and mass basis so that some things are agreed upon. You view those things through your own unique vision. You form the reality. It is a valid one. It is experience. It is not therefore unreal, but one of the appearances that reality takes. It has a valid basis -- an environment that you all accept, in which certain experiences are possible.
quotes.translation: 给我们一点时间……个体地、集体地，你们形成了你们所知道的世界，然而它有一个整体的个体和集体基础，以至于某些事情是共同认可的。你们通过自己独特的视角来看待那些事情。你们形成了实相。它是一个有效的实相。它是经验。因此它并非不真实，而是实相所呈现的一种面貌。它有一个有效的基础——一个你们都接受的环境，在其中某些经验是可能的。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (There were clear-cut connections between her creative performance today and her reception of the outline for The Way Toward Health last March; see the notes at 10:45, as well as Note 8, for Session 713; and in Volume 1, see Appendix 7. But there was even more creative expression to come through Jane this evening, not only in the session itself of course, but after it, as I try to explain in the concluding notes.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: You seem to think that there is an expanse of time between reincarnational existences, that one follows the other as one moment seems to follow another. Because you perceive a reality of cause and effect, you hypothesize a reality in which one life affects the next one. With your theories of guilt and punishment you often imagine that you are hampered in this existence by guilts collected in the last life -- or worse, accumulated through the centuries.
quotes.translation: 你似乎认为转世之间存在一段漫长的时间间隔，一世接续另一世，如同一个瞬间接续另一个瞬间。因为你感知到因果实相，便假设存在一个前世影响来世的实相。凭借你的罪与罚理论，你常想象此生被前世累积的罪疚所束缚——更糟的是，历经数世纪不断累积的罪疚。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: They were responsible for the birth of agriculture, as well as industry, the rise and fall of nations, the "glory" that was Rome, and Rome's destruction. (Pause.) Your present technological advances can almost be dated from the [invention of] the printing press, to Edison's inventions, which were flashes of intuition, dream-inspired. But if what I am telling you is true, then it is obvious that when I say that your physical world originated in the world of dreams, I must mean something far different from the usual definition of dream reality. Again, I could choose another term, but I want to emphasize each person's intimate contact with that other reality that does occur in what you think of as the state of dreaming (all very intently).
quotes.translation: 他们促成了农业与工业的诞生、国家的兴衰、罗马的"荣光"及其毁灭。（停顿。）你们当前的技术进步几乎可以追溯到印刷术的发明，再到爱迪生的发明——那些直觉的闪现、梦境的启迪。但若我所说属实，那么显然当我宣称你们的物质世界源于梦境世界时，我所指的含义必然与通常定义的梦境实相截然不同。我本可另择术语，但我想强调每个人与那另一种实相的亲密接触——这种接触确实发生在你们所谓的梦境状态中（语气极为专注）。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Basic reality is not a chaos. It is a raw material. All diversity implies a whole, and if you theorize a whole then you must also imply diversity. These personality structures are like you a portion of basic reality, or All That Is. They are simply able to use and express and act within a larger framework of it.
quotes.translation: 基本实相并非混沌。它是原材料。所有多样性都暗示着整体，若你推论出整体，则必然也暗示多样性。这些人格结构如同你一样，是基本实相或"一切万有"的一部分。它们只是能在其更广阔的框架内运用、表达和行动。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: It also unites experience. The unifying and the limiting aspects of consecutive moments are absent in inner reality. Time cannot be counted upon to unify action. The series you see does not exist basically. The unifying elements will be those of your own understanding, your own abilities. You are not forced to perceive action as a series of moments within inner reality, therefore.
quotes.translation: 它也统合经验。内在实相中不存在连续瞬间的统合性与限制性。时间无法被依赖来统合行动。你所见的序列在基本层面上并不存在。统合要素将是你自己的理解力与能力。因此，你无需被迫将行动感知为内在实相中的一系列瞬间。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: When you receive more material on the inner senses, you can begin using them to a much higher degree than you do now. For those interested in inner reality the inner senses can be utilized, of course, to explore and perceive portions of this inner reality; and the inner reality is after all what you are after.
quotes.translation: 当你获得更多关于内在感官的资料时，便能开始以远高于当下的程度运用它们。对于关注内在实相者而言，当然可以运用内在感官来探索与感知内在实相的各个部分——毕竟内在实相正是你所追寻的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Here, however, I want to describe these conditions somewhat more thoroughly. Now, in physical life you see what you want to see. You perceive from the available field of reality certain data -- data selected carefully by you in accordance with your ideas of what reality is. You create the data to begin with.
quotes.translation: 然而在此，我想更详尽地描述这些状态。在物质生命中，你只看见自己想看见的。你从可得的实相场域中感知特定数据——这些数据是你根据自己对实相的看法精心筛选的。你从一开始就在创造这些数据。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: And that self can see through physical reality. And to that self, physical reality is like a breath of smoke in air... and that self does not need the characteristics that you know and find so endearing. And yet it is not an unemotional self; it is a self that has condensed emotions; and, it is not distant.
quotes.translation: 那个自己能看穿物质实相。对那个自己而言，物质实相如同空气中的一缕轻烟……那个自己并不需要你所知且觉得如此可爱的那些特性。然而它并非无情的自己，而是一个凝聚了情感的自己，并且它并不疏离。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The outer senses do not deal with direct perception of reality. The outer senses themselves are camouflage patterns, part of the necessary and essential physical body camouflage. They, the outer senses, are perceptron of camouflage patterns. They were developed on your level, to deal with your world. They are useless outside of your world. They are useless in themselves as far as enabling you to perceive inner reality. This is not their function.
quotes.translation: 外在感官并不处理对实相的直接感知。外在感官本身就是伪装模式，是必要且本质的物质身体伪装的一部分。它们，外在感官，是伪装模式的感知器。它们是在你们的层面上发展出来的，用以处理你们的世界。它们在你们的世界之外毫无用处。就它们本身而言，它们无法让你感知内在实相。这不是它们的功能。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: For sometime he literally perceives many levels of reality at once, and part of what seems to be disorientation is simply the result of early confusion with so much data. According to the situation and the individual, the fetus may still be receiving messages from those he has known in the past. This adds to the confusion, and it is a matter of physical survival that he largely ignores these messages while he learns to focus in physical reality.
quotes.translation: 有一段时间，他确实同时感知到许多层次的实相，而部分看似迷失方向的现象，不过是早期面对大量数据时的困惑结果。根据情况和个体的不同，胎儿可能仍在接收来自过去所认识之人的讯息。这加剧了混乱，而为了物质生存，他在学习聚焦于物质实相时，必须大体上忽略这些讯息。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... This book itself, because of the method of its production, is an excellent example of the unknown reality becoming, if not "known," then recognized. Do not look for neat answers or tidy solutions, for when you do your explanations and theories will always be too small. There is always an unknown reality to some extent, for the miracle of your being works outside of the kind of explanations that you so often seem to require.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……这本书本身，由于其制作方式，是未知实相变得——即使不是“已知”——至少被认出的绝佳范例。不要寻找整齐的答案或干净的解决方案，因为当你这样做时，你的解释和理论永远会显得过于狭隘。在某种程度上，总有一个未知的实相存在，因为你存在的奇迹运作在你似乎经常要求的那种解释之外。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 743
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Hypnosis is one method of examining dreams. Our method is perhaps the best one. The inner self constantly changes its focus. I have said that consciousness is merely the direction in which the inner self focuses at any particular time. In order to examine the reality of dreams it is necessary to change the focus of the inner self to those directions in which the inner self moves, when the ego does not limit its scope to camouflage reality only.
quotes.translation: 催眠是检视梦境的一种方法。我们的方法或许是最好的。内在自己不断改变其焦点。我曾说过，意识只是内在自己在任何特定时间聚焦的方向。为了检视梦的实相，有必要将内在自己的焦点转向那些内在自己移动的方向，当自我不将其范围局限于伪装实相时。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: As there are portions of reality that you do not consciously perceive, and other systems of probability of which you are not consciously aware, so also are there aspects of primary godhood that you cannot at this moment comprehend. There are, therefore, probable gods, each one reflecting in its way the multidimensional aspects of a prime identity so great and dazzling that no one reality form or particular kind of existence could contain it.
quotes.translation: 正如有部分实相你并未有意识地感知，有其他概率系统你并未有意识地觉察，同样地，也有你此刻无法理解的原初神性的面向。因此，存在概率性的神，每一个都以其方式反映一个如此伟大而耀眼的多维原初本体面向，以至于没有任何一种实相形式或特定种类的存在能够容纳它。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: There is a constant translation of inner reality into objects in the waking state, and a constant translation of ideas into pseudoobjects in the dreaming state. Within a certain portion of dream reality, ideas or thoughts can be translated into pseudoobjects, and transported. This can only happen within certain ranges of intensities. This is what happens when you adopt a pseudoform in projections, though I am simplifying this considerably.
quotes.translation: 在清醒状态下，内在实相不断被转化为物体；而在梦境中，意念则不断被转化为伪物体。在梦境实相的特定范围内，意念或思想可被转化为伪物体并加以传送。这仅能在特定强度范围内发生。当你在投射中采用伪形时，便会出现这种情况——尽管我对此做了极大简化。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: All Ruburt could remember of his dream was the knowledge that many of mankinds most cherished conceptions about reality are completely false. He was shown a multidimensional diagram of reality as it exists in terms of the spacious present, and in terms of qualities that represent dimensional points.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏能记住的梦境，仅是人类对实相最珍视的许多观念完全错误这一认知。他被展示了一个多维实相图解，该图解以"广袤当下"的维度呈现，并以代表维度点的特质加以诠释。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

source_raw.title: （Once before, in the first and 18th envelope experiments, an object was used that was similar but not identical by any means. In the first experiment in the 179th session, Seth came through with one accurate impression. In the 18th experiment, held during the 212th session, a few more impressions were obtained; but overall the results were interfered with by other factors in a way that doesn't seem to happen these days. See Session 179 in Volume 4, and Session 212 in Volume 5.

quotes.text: Now: When you properly understand how to use psychological time, then to some extent you can learn to alter the nature and focus of your consciousness. You can turn it in many directions. You can focus it in other ways, away from physical reality. This does not mean that you will be left high and dry here. It does mean that you will begin to explore the reality of yourselves, and of those other dimensions in which you have your existence.
quotes.translation: 现在：当你正确理解如何运用心理时间时，便能在某种程度上学会改变意识的本质与焦点。你可以将意识转向诸多方向，以其他方式聚焦，远离物质实相。这并不意味着你会在此陷入孤立无援的境地，而是意味着你将开始探索自身实相，以及你存于其中的其他维度。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This however is not their problem, for like all other individuals they perceive a reality that they have created. Their problem therefore is a distortive one. It is not related to their attitude toward reality as a whole, but it is intimately connected with the reality which they have created. They are indeed relating to that reality very well. The reality, however, is much more distorted than usual, and this is one of the main problems.
quotes.translation: 然而这并非他们的问题，因为与其他个体一样，他们感知到的实相正是自己所创造的。因此，他们的问题在于扭曲性。这与他们对整体实相的态度无关，却与他们所创造的实相紧密相连。他们确实与那实相产生了良好关联，但该实相比寻常情况扭曲得更严重——这正是主要问题之一。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The connections between dream images and the physical body are always maintained. There is a difference in mass, and a difference in thickness of molecules. Nevertheless the dream location does exist in its own legitimate reality, and its reality is to some extent dependent upon physical reality.
quotes.translation: 梦境意象与物质身体之间的联结始终存在。两者在质量与分子密度上存在差异。然而，梦境地点确实存在于其自身合法的实相中，且其实相在某种程度上依赖于物质实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Philosophy should allow you to deal with them and not to avoid them. In one case of course there is nothing to avoid. Moment points, as I have discussed them, will clear up many of these points for you. There is every reason to explore inner reality, but this exploration should lead you to understand the nature of physical reality also. Perhaps your physical existence could be compared with an excellent book, given to you by a teacher.
quotes.translation: 哲学应使你直面它们而非逃避。当然，在某种情况下根本无需逃避。如我所讨论的"瞬间点"，将为你厘清许多此类问题。探索内在实相的理由充分，但这种探索也应引导你理解物质实相的本质。或许你的物质存在可比作老师赠予你的一本卓越之书。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: An individual inner self, then, through constant massive effort of great creative intensity, cooperates with all other inner selves to form and maintain the physical reality that you know, so that physical reality is an offshoot or by-product of (Pause), the highly conscious creative inner self.
quotes.translation: 那么，一个内在自体通过持续而庞大的创造性强度努力，与所有其他内在自体合作，形成并维持你所知的物质实相，因此物质实相是（停顿）高度自觉的创造性内在自体的一个分支或副产品。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: These are the ground rules, so to speak. Your physical mechanisms are equipped to function in such a way that reality is perceived through the lens of particular root assumptions. Using the physical senses alone, it is almost literally impossible for you to perceive reality in any other manner.
quotes.translation: 可以说，这些是基本规则。你的身体机制被设计成以特定方式运作，使得实相通过特定根本假设的透镜被感知。仅凭物质感官，你几乎不可能以任何其他方式感知实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: It is energy concentrated to a degree quite unbelievable to you. It contains potentials unlimited, but it must work out its own identity and form its own worlds. It carries within it the burden of all being. Within it are personality potentials beyond your comprehension. Remember, this is your own soul or entity I am speaking of, as well as soul or entity in general. You are one manifestation of your own soul. How many of you would want to limit your reality, your entire reality, to the experience you now know? You do this when you imagine that your present self is your entire personality, or insist that your identity be maintained unchanged through an endless eternity.
quotes.translation: 它是能量，其集中程度令你难以置信。它包含无限潜能，但必须发展出自己的身份并形成自己的世界。它承载着所有存在的重担。其中包含你无法理解的人格潜能。记住，我所说的是你自己的灵魂或存有，以及一般的灵魂或存有。你是自己灵魂的一种显现。你们中有多少人愿意将自己的实相——你全部的实相——限制在你现在所知的体验中？当你想象现在的自我就是你的全部人格，或坚持你的身份必须在一个无尽的永恒中保持不变时，你就在这样做。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: (Humorously again:) you do not consider a dream as a success or a failure. If the dream ends in a moment of destruction you do not consider the dream a failure. In the overall then, when I told you that the planet was dispensable, I meant it somewhat in those terms, for while in your reality you are vulnerable, and agony is real, still it is not the whole reality, and success and failure have no meaning in those (underlined) terms.
quotes.translation: （再次幽默地：）你不会把一个梦视为成功或失败。如果梦在毁灭的瞬间结束，你不会认为这个梦是失败的。那么，总体而言，当我告诉你这个星球是可以舍弃的时，我在某种程度上是就这些术语而言的，因为虽然在你的实相中你是脆弱的，痛苦是真实的，但它仍然不是全部实相，而成功与失败在这些（强调的）术语中没有意义。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: You cannot react physically in the same way to projected or imagined dangers. There seems to be no possible reaction. You are frustrated. You are meant to deal with your immediate, primary experience, and in so doing you take care of your responsibility. You are able to take action in your own experience, and therefore affect others. You do not have to be ignorant of wars in other corners of the world, or close your eyes. But if you allow those experiences to overcloud your present, valid intersection with reality, then you speak and act from a position not your own, and deny the world whatever benefits your own present version of reality might allow you to give.
quotes.translation: 你无法以同样的方式对投射或想象的危险做出身体反应。似乎没有可能的反应。你感到沮丧。你被期望处理你直接的、主要的经验，这样做时你就承担了你的责任。你能够在自己的经验中采取行动，从而影响他人。你不必对世界其他角落的战争无知，或闭上眼睛。但如果你让那些经验遮蔽你当下与实相的有效交汇点，那么你就从一个不属于你自己的立场说话和行动，并否定了世界可能从你当前版本的实相中获益的任何可能性。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In your journeys into inner reality you cannot proceed with the same root agreements, you see. Reality, per se, changes completely according to the basic root agreements from which you proceed. One of the root agreements upon which physical existence is based is that physical objects have a reality that is entirely independent of any subjective cause; and that these objects, within definitely specified limitations, are permanent.
quotes.translation: 在你进入内在实相的旅程中，你不能带着同样的根本假设前进，你看。实相本身根据你前进的基本根本假设而完全改变。物质存在所基于的根本假设之一是：物质对象具有完全独立于任何主观原因的现实；并且这些对象，在明确规定的限制内，是永久的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Its beliefs about the nature of reality are then given to inner portions of the self. These rely mainly upon the conscious mind's interpretation of temporal reality. The conscious mind sets the goals and the inner self brings them about, using all its facilities and inexhaustible energy.
quotes.translation: 它对实相本质的信念随后被传递给自我的内在部分。这些信念主要依赖意识心对时间实相的解释。意识心设定目标，内在自我则运用其所有能力与无穷能量来实现这些目标。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: This vastvoid, this infinite mind, came out of another that was greater than itself. (Pause, smile.) The possibilities that have come to reality within this universal system have each given birth to other systems and other realities, as one tree bears a thousand seeds. (Pause.) you yourselves through your own mental actions create realities of which you yourselves are unaware, and you give birth to more than physical children.
quotes.translation: 这浩瀚的虚空，这无限的心智，源自另一个比它自身更伟大的存在。（停顿，微笑。）在这个宇宙系统内成为实相的可能性，每一个都孕育出其他系统与其他实相，正如一棵树结出千粒种子。（停顿。）你们自己通过自身的心智活动，创造出连你们自己都未曾察觉的实相，你们所孕育的远不止是肉身子女。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Within him there are methods of perception that allow him to see through the camouflage settings, to see beyond the stage. He uses these inner senses constantly, though the actor part of himself is so intent upon the play that this escapes him. In a large manner, the physical senses actually form the physical reality they seem to only perceive. They are themselves part of the camouflage, but they are like lenses over your natural inner perceptions that force you to "see" an available field of activity as physical matter; and so they can be relied upon only to tell you what is happening in a superficial manner. You can tell the position of the other actors for example, or time by clock, but these physical senses will not tell you that time is itself a camouflage, or that consciousness forms the other actors, or that realities that you cannot see exist over and beyond the physical matter that is so apparent.
quotes.translation: 在他之内存在着感知方法，能让他看穿伪装设定，看见舞台之外。他持续运用这些内在感官，尽管他作为演员的那部分如此专注于戏剧而忽略了这一点。从广义上说，肉体感官实际上形成了它们似乎仅仅感知到的物质实相。它们本身就是伪装的一部分，但就像覆盖在你自然内在感知上的透镜，迫使你将可用的活动场域"看作"物质；因此它们只能告诉你表面发生的事情。例如，你能知道其他演员的位置，或时钟显示的时间，但这些肉体感官不会告诉你时间本身就是一种伪装，不会告诉你意识形成了其他演员，也不会告诉你那些你看不见的实相存在于如此明显的物质之外。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: When you are determined to manipulate your environment, then you separate yourself from it. Since you are part of it, this also leads you to try to place yourself apart from your own subjective reality. It is quite possible to take your normally conscious "I" into the dream state, to your advantage. When you do this you will see that the dreaming "I" and the waking "I" are one, but operating in entirely different environments. Therefore, you become familiar with depths of experience and knowledge unknown to you before. You acquire a true flexibility and expanded awareness of your own being, and open channels of communication between your waking and dreaming realities. This means that you are far better able to utilize unconscious knowledge, and also to acquaint the unconscious with your present physical situation.
quotes.translation: 当你决心操控环境时，你便将自己与环境分离。既然你是环境的一部分，这也会导致你试图将自己与自身的主观实相分开。将你通常清醒的"我"带入梦境状态是完全可以做到的，这对你有利。当你这样做时，你会看到做梦的"我"与清醒的"我"是同一个，但运作在完全不同的环境中。因此，你熟悉了以前未知的经验与知识深度。你获得了真正的灵活性，扩展了对自身存在的觉知，并打开了清醒实相与梦境实相之间的沟通渠道。这意味着你能更好地运用无意识知识，同时让无意识了解你当前的物质状况。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Your scientists spend many long years in training. If the same amount of time were spent to learn a different kind of science, you could indeed discover far more about the known and unknown realities. There are some individuals embarked upon a study of dreams, working in the "dream laboratories"; but here again there is prejudiced perception, with scientists on the outside studying the dreams of others, or emphasizing the physical changes that occur in the dream state. The trouble is that many in the sciences do not comprehend that there is an inner reality. (Intently:) It is not only as valid as the exterior one, but it is the origin for it. It is that world that offers you answers, solutions, and would reveal many of the blueprints that exist behind the world of your experience.
quotes.translation: 你们的科学家花费许多漫长岁月进行训练。如果花同样时间学习另一种科学，你们确实能对已知与未知实相有更多发现。有些人投身于梦境研究，在"梦境实验室"工作；但这里同样存在偏见感知，科学家们从外部研究他人的梦，或强调梦境状态中发生的生理变化。问题在于许多科学界人士不理解存在内在实相。（专注地：）它不仅与外在实相同样真实，而且是外在实相的源头。正是那个世界为你提供答案、解决方案，并揭示存在于你经验世界背后的许多蓝图。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 700
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: In some of their own private dreams, many of my readers will have discovered a reality quite as vivid as the normal one, and sometimes more so. These experiences can give you some vague hint of the kind of existence I am speaking of.11 There are also physical apparatuses connected with the hibernation abilities of some animals that can give further clues as to the possible relationships of consciousness to the body. Under certain conditions, for example, consciousness can leave the corporal mechanism while it remains intact -- functioning, but at a maintenance level. When optimum conditions return, then the consciousness reactivates the body. Such behavior is possible not only with the animals. In systems different from your own, there are realities in which physical organisms are activated after what would seem to you to be centuries of inactivity12 -- again, when the conditions are right. To some extent your own life-and-death cycles are simply another aspect of the hibernation principle as you understand it. Your own consciousness leaves the body almost in the same way that messages leap the nerve ends.13 The consciousness is not destroyed in the meantime.
quotes.translation: 在我许多读者的私人梦境中，他们会发现一种与正常实相同样生动、有时甚至更生动的实相。这些经验能给你一些模糊的暗示，关于我所谈论的那种存在。还有一些与某些动物冬眠能力相关的生理机制，能进一步提示意识与身体之间可能的关系。例如，在某些条件下，意识可以离开肉体机制，而肉体保持完整——运作着，但处于维持水平。当最佳条件恢复时，意识便重新激活身体。这种行为不仅动物可能做到。在与你们不同的系统中，存在这样的实相：生理有机体在你们看来似乎经过数个世纪的不活动后被激活——同样，当条件合适时。在某种程度上，你们自己的生死循环只是你们所理解的冬眠原则的另一个面向。你们的意识离开身体，几乎就像信息跃过神经末梢一样。在此期间，意识并未被摧毁。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 708
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: What he was sensing, however, was an entirely different kind of reality. He was beginning to recognize another synapse [neuronal] pattern not "native"; he was familiarizing himself with perceptions at a different set of coordinate points. Such activity automatically alters the nature of time in your experience, and is indicative of intersections of your consciousness with another kind of consciousness. That particular type of consciousness operates "at different speeds" than your own. Biologically, your own physical structures are quite able to operate at those same speeds, though as a species you have disciplined yourselves to a different kind of neurological reaction. By altering such neurological prejudice,10 however, you can indeed learn to become aware of other realities that coincide with yours. Period.
quotes.translation: 然而，他感知到的是一种完全不同的实相。他开始辨识出另一种非“原生”的突触（神经元）模式；他正在熟悉不同坐标点上的感知。这类活动会自动改变你经验中的时间本质，并标志着你的意识与另一种意识的交汇点。那种特定类型的意识以与你不同的“速度”运作。从生物学角度看，你的生理结构完全能够以相同速度运作，尽管作为一个物种，你们已自我训练出另一种神经反应模式。然而，通过改变这种神经偏见，你确实能学会觉察与你的实相并存的其他实相。句号。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 712
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: In a probable reality, a Ruburt and a Joseph now live there. In the world that you recognize as official, however, they moved into the hill house. To some extent both of them are aware of the inner processes involved in the final decision. I do not mean that they are simply familiar with the exterior thought processes involved, such as: "The hill house is better constructed," or "It has a fine view." I am speaking of deeper mechanisms of consideration (pause), in which correlations are made between interior and exterior realities. (Pause.) It is obvious that when you move from one place to another you make an alteration in space -- but you alter time as well, and you set into motion a certain psychological impetus that reaches out to affect everyone you know. (Long pause.) When a house is vacant all of the people in the neighborhood send out their own messages. To a certain extent any given inhabited area forms its own "entity." This applies to the smallest neighborhood4 and to the greatest nation. Such messages are often encountered in the dream state. Empty houses are psychic vacancies that yearn to be filled. When you move, you move into other portions of your own selfhood.5
quotes.translation: 在某个可能的实相中，鲁柏和约瑟现在正居住在那里。然而，在你们所认定的官方世界里，他们搬进了山丘上的房子。某种程度上，两人都意识到最终决定所涉及的内在过程。我并非指他们仅仅熟悉外在的思维过程，例如：“山丘上的房子建造得更好”或“它有绝佳的视野”。我指的是更深层的考量机制（停顿），其中内在实相与外在实相产生关联。（停顿。）显然，当你从一个地方搬到另一个地方时，你改变了空间——但你也改变了时间，并启动了一种特定的心理动力，这种动力会延伸出去影响你认识的每一个人。（长停顿。）当一栋房子空置时，邻里所有人都会发出自己的讯息。某种程度上，任何特定居住区域都会形成自己的“存有”。这适用于最小的社区和最大的国家。这类讯息常在梦境状态中被遇到。空房子是渴望被填满的心灵空缺。当你搬家时，你搬进了自我本体的其他部分。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 742
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Your species shares with the other species a feeling of kinship for its kind. There is a great give-and-take of ideas. You end up, then, with a consensus, generally speaking, as to what a reasonable picture of agreed-upon reality is. Your system has frowned upon many experiences, considering them eccentric behavior in an adverse fashion, since your belief systems have so regimented behavior, and so narrowly defined sanity. (Long pause.) The intellect, I want to stress, is socially oriented. It is peculiarly suited, of course, to react to cultural information. (Pause.) It wants to see the world as it is seen by the minds of others. Through that kind of action it helps form your cultural environment, the civilization of which you are justly proud.
quotes.translation: 你们物种与其他物种共享着对其同类的归属感。思想之间存在大量的给予与接收。因此，总体而言，你们最终会达成一种共识，即关于公认实相的合理图景是什么。你们的系统曾不赞同许多经验，将其视为反常的古怪行为，因为你们的信念系统如此严格地规范行为，并如此狭隘地定义神智健全。（长停顿。）我要强调，智力是社会导向的。它当然特别适合对文化信息做出反应。（停顿。）它希望看到的世界与他人心智所见相同。通过这类行动，它帮助塑造了你们的文化环境，即你们引以为傲的文明。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: For much more Seth material on the counterpart concept, see Session 732 and Appendix 25 in Volume II of The "Unknown" Reality (Published by Amber-Allen Publishing, Inc., San Rafael, CA).

quotes.text: Emotions are also, then, actualities within the overall electric system, existing not as representations of feeling, but as definite charged action. I want if possible to get the idea over that this system involves realities that are directly experienced and vivid, and also that in some ways emotions and thoughts are more directly perceived in that field than your physical field. They are more readily apparent, with more force behind them acting directly upon the electric environment, without any intermediary steps; that is, they, emotions and feelings and thoughts, exist immediately within the environment as electrical forces within that system. In your system they need to be translated and given reality by a method of interrelationships. Your field attains its actuality only as a result of the cooperating merger of many other independent systems.
quotes.translation: 因此，情绪也是整体电性系统内的实相，它们并非作为感受的表征存在，而是作为明确的带电行动。如果可能，我希望传达这样的概念：这个系统涉及直接体验且生动的实相，并且在某些方面，情绪和思想在那个场域中比在你们的物质场域中更直接被感知。它们更显而易见，背后有更强的力量直接作用于电性环境，无需任何中介步骤；也就是说，情绪、感受和思想作为该系统中的电力，直接存在于环境中。在你们的系统中，它们需要通过相互关系的方法被转化并赋予实相。你们的场域只有作为许多其他独立系统合作融合的结果，才能获得其现实性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: And when you realize that you form the events of your life in the same way, you will learn to take hold of your entire consciousness in whatever aspect it shows itself in this life. Through all of this you must realize that you are not powerless. Remember, also, that this life is a dimension of experience and reality even if it is, in contrast, a dream in a higher level of reality in which you have your larger consciousness.
quotes.translation: 当你意识到你以同样的方式形成你生活中的事件时，你将学会把握你的整个意识，无论它在此生中以何种面貌显现。通过这一切，你必须认识到你并非无能为力。也请记住，此生是一个经验与实相的维度，即使相比之下，它只是你拥有更大意识的更高实相层面中的一个梦。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The essay form gave us chances for at least a minimal study of the various forms our creative learning experiences have taken to date. We quickly agreed that we'd been setting up the illness syndrome for years, yet the deep emotional shocks accompanying its physical developments seemed to come at us like attacking dark birds zooming in from another probable reality. We learned. We adjusted in ways that a few weeks previously would have seemed unbelievable to us--and, ironically, as must often happen in such situations, once we'd moved into our new joint reality, it appeared that those particular challenges had always been incipient for us.
quotes.translation: 论文形式至少让我们有机会初步研究至今为止我们的创造性学习经验所采取的各种形式。我们很快达成共识，多年来我们一直在建立疾病综合征，然而伴随其身体发展而来的深层情感冲击，却像从另一个可能实相中猛冲而来的黑暗飞鸟般袭击我们。我们学习了。我们以几周前对我们而言似乎难以置信的方式进行了调整——讽刺的是，正如这类情况中常发生的那样，一旦我们进入新的共同实相，那些特定的挑战似乎从一开始就对我们而言是萌芽状态的。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: --FROM SESSION 929, NOVEMBER 26, 1980
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 6: Genetic Heritage and Reincarnational Predilections

quotes.text: In your terms, the person at birth is affected by multidimensional conditions, and the collective position of the planets is but one very minute indication of the other realities involved. Ruburt is correct: Even in conventional terms a true horoscope would have to involve the time of death in your temporal reality, as well [as that of birth]. Your focus of attention forms boundaries that predispose you to believe in a point at which your consciousness emerges, as you understand it, and a point when it is no longer effective, or dies. Your beliefs in such concepts limit your perception, for by altering the focus of your attention you can to some extent become aware of perception before and after the recognized points of birth and death.
quotes.translation: 就你们的说法而言，出生时的人受到多维条件的影响，而行星的集体位置只是所涉其他实相的一个极微小指示。鲁柏是对的：即使按传统说法，真正的星盘也必须包含你们时间实相中的死亡时刻，如同出生时刻一样。你们注意力的焦点形成了界限，使你们倾向于相信有一个你们所理解的意识出现的点，以及一个它不再有效或死亡的时刻。你们对这些概念的信念限制了你们的感知，因为通过改变注意力的焦点，你们可以在某种程度上意识到公认的出生与死亡点之前和之后的感知。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 730
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: The location does not exist within your physical system, but the dream location is a reality. It is a superimposed value which you have created, and which is valid and vivid. A dream unfolds. Miles may appear before your inner vision. Are then these miles contained within your head? Are these miles contained within the small skull? Obviously not. But here we are closer to that reality which is beyond space as you know it.
quotes.translation: 这个位置并不存在于你们的物质系统内，但梦境位置是一个实相。它是一个你们创造出来的叠加价值，有效且生动。一个梦境展开。数英里可能出现在你们的内在视野前。那么这些英里是否包含在你们的头颅内？这些英里是否包含在小小的头骨内？显然不是。但在这里，我们更接近那个超越你们所知空间的实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Your dreams affect your cellular reality, even as that reality is also largely responsible for the fact that you dream, in your terms, at all. Dreams are a natural "product" of cellularly tuned consciousness. As fire gives off light, cellularly tuned consciousness gives off dreams.
quotes.translation: 你们的梦境影响你们的细胞实相，即使那个实相也很大程度上负责你们做梦这一事实——按你们的说法。梦境是细胞调谐意识的一种自然"产物"。如同火发出光，细胞调谐意识发出梦境。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now. The portion of the image that came closest to any semblance of reality was the region about the eyes, and the eyes themselves. There is much to be said here. For this was not imaginary, in that it did have a certain physical reality that existed within your field of reference.
quotes.translation: 现在。这个形象中最接近任何实相样子的部分是眼睛周围的区域，以及眼睛本身。这里有很多可说的。因为这并非想象，因为它确实具有某种存在于你们参考场域内的物质实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Many psychological intangibles, including dreams, have such an electric reality, and do exist as electric actions. They are to be reckoned with. The connections between the emotional climate and physical health have never been clearly understood, because emotions were not known to have such electrical reality. They were not known to directly affect the system. More is understood as far as the chemical relationships here.
quotes.translation: 许多心理上的无形之物，包括梦境，都具有这样的电力实相，并确实作为电力活动存在。它们必须被认真对待。情绪氛围与身体健康之间的联系从未被清楚理解，因为人们不知道情绪具有这样的电力实相。它们不被知道会直接影响系统。就这里的化学关系而言，人们了解得更多。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: It is possible (underlined) that the psychedelic experience for these individuals would involve consciousness expansion directed toward (underlined) usual physical reality, and bring about a more unified personality structure from society's standpoint. Insanity as a term means nothing. All mental life is characterized by divergent perceptions of any given set of Sense data that is recognized as Reality, with a capital R.
quotes.translation: 这些个体的迷幻体验可能（强调）涉及朝向（强调）通常物质实相的意识扩展，并从社会立场带来更统一的人格结构。疯狂这个词毫无意义。所有精神生活的特征是对任何一组被承认为实相（大写R）的感官资料的不同感知。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: The words: "May peace be with you," will get him through any difficulty in other layers of reality, for as he formed that image others also form images, and he could encounter them on occasion. To wish them peace will be to give them some comfort, for they do have a kind of reality. To fear them is to put yourself into their realm of reality, and then you are forced to fight on those terms, and there is no need for this.
quotes.translation: “愿你平安”这句话，能助他穿越其他实相层级的任何困境，因为当他形成那个意象时，他人也在形成意象，他有时会与他们相遇。祝愿他们平安，便是给予他们慰藉，因为他们确实拥有某种实相。恐惧他们，则是将自己投入他们的实相领域，届时你被迫按他们的规则作战，而这毫无必要。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: To one extent or another, therefore, all of the potentials of the species are now latent within each individual. Often these spring to the surface through events that may seem bizarre. The "unknown" reality is unknown only because you have not looked for its aspects in yourself. You have been taught to pay almost exclusive attention to your exterior behavior. Privately, then, much of your inner life escapes you. You often structure your life according to that exterior pattern of events. These, while important, are the result of your own inner world of activity. That inner world is your only real connection with the exterior events, and the objective details make sense only because of the subjectivity that gave them birth.6
quotes.translation: 因此，在某种程度上，物种的所有潜能如今都潜伏在每个个体之内。这些潜能常通过看似怪异的事件浮现表面。“未知”的实相之所以未知，只因你未曾在自己身上寻找它的面向。你被教导几乎只关注外在行为。于是，你的大量内在生活悄然流逝。你常根据外在事件模式构建人生。这些事件固然重要，却是你内在活动世界的结果。那个内在世界是你与外在事件唯一的真实连结，客观细节之所以有意义，全因赋予它们生命的主观性。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 709
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: It is, therefore, as if your thoughts appear within other realities as objects -- alive and vital in themselves, growing into other systems as flowers or trees grow up seemingly from nothing within physical reality. These can then be used as the raw material, so to speak, in certain other systems. They are the given "natural data," the raw material of creativity in the realities that you help seed but do not perceive.
quotes.translation: 因此，仿佛你的思想以物体形态出现在其他实相中——它们自身鲜活而充满生机，如同花朵或树木在物质实相中看似凭空生长般，蔓延至其他系统。这些思想可被用作某些其他系统的原材料，可谓“天然数据”，是那些你协助播种却未察觉的实相中的创造原料。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It is not as if that vaster reality were utterly closed to your perception, for it is not. To some extent it is everywhere apparent in each person's private experience, and it is obviously stated in the very existence of your world itself. The religions, in one way or another, have always perceived it, although the attempt to interpret that reality in terms of the recognized facts of the world is bound to distort it.
quotes.translation: 并非那个更广大的实相对你的感知完全封闭——事实并非如此。在某种程度上，它处处显现在每个人的私人经验中，且明确昭示于你所在世界的存在本身。宗教总以各种方式感知到它，尽管试图以世界公认事实诠释那个实相，必然导致扭曲。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

source_raw.title: (Session 819, which was held last Saturday evening, had nothing to do with Mass Events. In our opinion tonight's session did, however, and for a number of reasons that will become obvious as it's read. Yet Seth didn't call it dictation.

quotes.text: In such interrelationships, the steps or identities are each immeasurably enriched by the added perception of the others. As mentioned earlier, thoughts, containing their own electromagnetic reality, have form whether or not you perceive it. With each thought, then, you send out from yourself shapes and images that can be quite legitimate realities to those within the system of reality into which they are propelled.
quotes.translation: 在此类相互关系中，每个步骤或身份都因其他身份的附加感知而获得不可估量的丰富。如前所述，思想携带着自身的电磁实相，无论你是否感知，它们都有形态。因此，每个思想都从你自身发出形状与意象，这些形状与意象对它们被推入的实相系统中的存在而言，可能是完全合法的实相。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The creative abilities have full reign within the dream state, and it is here that the personality first tests its creative intensities and methods. The personality's physical environment therefore is greatly colored and formed by his dream existence. There is some leakage from waking-life experience to dream experience, but this leakage usually represents the material needed, or the problem to be solved. Mental events and dream events are primary. The individual first manipulates situations within the dream reality, and then transposes his characteristic method of handling them upon the physical reality.
quotes.translation: 创造能力在梦境状态中完全驰骋，人格正是在此处首次测试其创造强度与方法。因此，人格的物质环境深受其梦境存在的影响与塑造。清醒生活经验会渗入梦境经验，但这种渗漏通常代表所需材料或待解问题。心灵事件与梦境事件是首要的。个体先在梦境实相中操控情境，然后将处理情境的独特方法转置于物质实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Returning to a previous discussion, I would like to make it plain once more that dream images are not pseudo realities. They exist as fact, though in a different dimension from physical reality. Again, no one would deny the reality of psychological experience when it is felt by the waking personality. Yet such experience also does not have a physical reality, to the same degree that a definite physical object has.
quotes.translation: 回到先前的讨论，我想再次明确说明：梦境意象并非虚假实相。它们作为事实存在，只是处于与物质实相不同的维度。同样，当清醒人格感受到心理经验时，无人会否认其真实性。然而这种经验同样不具备物质实相——至少不像具体实物那样具有物质性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Life goes on, then, only it's different. Of course my feelings, while being unique in that they're my own, are intimately bound up with those of everyone else. How could it be otherwise, since no one can live in true isolation? Each one of us is an immortal portion, a spark of endless perception and beauty and feeling -- and yes, of conflict and denial at times -- within this probable reality that we're all creating together, even when we don't know we're doing it! I watch with awe as within this reality each one of us expresses as best we can our creative understanding of this wonderful mystery.
quotes.translation: 生命仍在延续，只是形态不同。当然，我的感受虽因属于自己而独特，却与所有人的感受紧密相连。若非如此又怎么可能？毕竟无人能真正孤立生存。我们每个人都是不朽的片段，是无限感知、美与情感的火花——有时也包含冲突与否定——在这共同创造的或然实相中，即便我们浑然不觉！我满怀敬畏地注视着：在这实相中，每个人都在以最佳方式表达对这场奇妙奥秘的创造性理解。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Many of your misconceptions about the nature of reality are directly related to the division you place between your sleeping and waking experience, your conscious and unconscious activity. Opposites seem to occur that do not exist in actuality. Myths, symbols and rationalizations all become necessary to explain the seeming divergences, the seeming contradictions between realities that appear to be so different.
quotes.translation: 你们对实相本质的诸多误解，直接源于将睡眠与清醒经验、意识与无意识活动强行割裂。看似存在的对立面，在真实中并不存在。神话、象征与合理化解释都成为必要，用以解释那些看似迥异的实相之间存在的表面分歧与矛盾。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Simple questions. It is because the man thinks differently that he has the ulcer, for the ulcer is the result of his characteristic method of thought. It is the result of his characteristic manner of viewing reality. This causes him to add to his self-image, in reality, that disability. The ulcer is formed intimately from the electrical reality that composes his thoughts. Do you find that a satisfactory answer?
quotes.translation: 简单的问题。正因此人思维方式不同才患上溃疡——溃疡正是其特有思维模式的结果，是其看待实相特有方式的产物。这种模式在实相中为他的自我形象增添了这项残疾。溃疡由构成其思想的电性实相精密形成。您觉得这个答案令人满意吗？
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The legs run, and leap over areas of ground. They cannot themselves interpret the reality beneath them. The feet are not aware of the ants they crush. They may feel the grass or sidewalk or the road, but the peculiar individual sensate life of the grass itself, or of the ant, escapes the feet, which are involved in their own reality and concerned with these other things only in their relationship to feethood.
quotes.translation: 双腿奔跑跳跃，跨越地面区域。它们自身无法诠释脚下的实相。双脚不知晓被碾碎的蚂蚁。它们能感知草地、人行道或道路，但草叶或蚂蚁独特的个体感知生命，却逃过了专注于自身实相的双脚——这些事物唯有在与"脚性"的关系中才被关注。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Seth began dictating The "Unknown" Reality: A Seth Book, in the 679th session for February 4, 1974, and finished it with the 744th session for April 23, 1975. In the beginning we anticipated another intriguing Seth book, the successor to Seth Speaks and The Nature of Personal Reality. We thought the new work would probably be a long one, but we hardly expected that it would require publication in two volumes.
quotes.translation: 赛斯于1974年2月4日第679节课开始口述《"未知"实相：赛斯书》，并于1975年4月23日第744节课完成。起初我们期待另一本引人入胜的赛斯著作，作为《赛斯说》与《个人实相的本质》的续篇。我们预料新作篇幅可能较长，却未曾想到需要分两卷出版。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Session 704:* More on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: It seems evident to you that one person has one mind. You identify with the mind you use. If you had another, then it would seem as if you must be someone else. A mind is a psychic pattern through which you interpret and form reality. You have physical limbs that you can see. You have minds that are invisible. Each one can organize reality in a different fashion. Each one deals with its own kind of knowledge.
quotes.translation: 对你而言，一个人只有一个心智似乎是显而易见的。你认同你所使用的心智。如果你拥有另一个心智，那么你似乎必定是另一个人。心智是一种心灵模式，你通过它来诠释并形成实相。你有看得见的身体四肢，也有看不见的心智。每个心智都能以不同的方式组织实相，每个心智都处理它自己种类的知识。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: These conditions, however, only exist at the conscious level of your perception. The larger psyche deals with the greater dimension of events, and the dream state itself is like a laboratory in which your waking reality is constructed. The physical earth is bombarded by cosmic rays, and by other phenomena that you do not perceive, yet they are highly important to your survival. The psyche is bombarded in the same way by phenomena important to your survival. In the laboratory of dreams this information is processed, collected, and finally formed into the dreams that you may or may not remember; dreams that are already translations of other events, shaped into forms that you recognize.
quotes.translation: 然而，这些条件只存在于你感知的意识层面。较大的心灵处理事件的更广大维度，而梦境状态本身就像一个实验室，在其中构建你醒时的实相。物质地球受到宇宙射线和其他你未感知的现象轰击，但它们对你的生存极为重要。心灵也以同样的方式受到对你生存重要的现象轰击。在梦的实验室中，这些信息被处理、收集，最终形成你可能记得或不记得的梦；这些梦已经是其他事件的翻译，塑造成你认识的形式。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In certain areas of mass shared dreams, collective mankind deals with the problems of his political and social objective structure. The solutions which he makes within his dream reality are often, however, not the same solutions that he accepts within physical reality.
quotes.translation: 在集体共享梦的某些领域，集体人类处理其政治和社会客观结构的问题。然而，他在梦的实相中所做的解决方案，往往与他接受于物质实相中的解决方案不同。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: Such a consciousness is at a state of being in which its reality generates more energy and power than it can physically express in its brilliant intersection with physical reality. The "sparks" generated by each instant of its existence cause additional experiences, perceptions, that will not fit in the known moment of the present -- for by then in your terms that present has already vanished into the past.
quotes.translation: 这样的意识处于一种存在状态，其产生的实相能量与力量，多于它在其与物质实相灿烂交汇时所能物质性地表达。其存在的每一瞬间所产生的“火花”，引发额外的经验与感知，这些无法容纳于已知的当下时刻——因为以你们的说法，那当下已消逝于过去。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Many of these dimensions cannot be discussed at this time. But you will see, indeed, that the nature of action, or the nature of any reality, is greatly colored by the viewpoint or dimension from which it is examined. It is my purpose here to examine for you reality from as many different aspects as possible, lifting you from the limitations of your own dimensions, and allowing you the advantage of others.
quotes.translation: 这些维度中有许多目前无法讨论。但你确实会看到，行动的本质或任何实相的本质，会因检视它的观点或维度而大为着色。我在此的目的是尽可能从许多不同面向为你检视实相，将你从自身维度的限制中提升，并让你享有其他维度的优势。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: A thought is an action. A dream is as much an action as a breath is an action. Although we speak in terms of separation, all reality is a part of action. When we divide action in order to discuss it, we in no way change the reality of action, nor alter its nature.
quotes.translation: 思想是一种行动。梦是一种行动，正如呼吸是一种行动。尽管我们以分离的术语来讨论，但所有实相都是行动的一部分。当我们为了讨论而划分行动时，我们绝未改变行动的真实性，也未改变其本质。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Entities or souls, in other words, send out portions of themselves to open up avenues of reality that would not exist otherwise. (Long pause at 9:39.) The three-dimensional selves, in existing within these realities, must focus their attention there completely. An inner awareness gives them a source of energy and strength. They must, however, come to understand their roles as actors, "finally" from their roles, and through another act of comprehension, return to the entity.
quotes.translation: 换言之，存有或灵魂会派出自身的一部分，去开辟原本不存在的实相途径。（9:39处长时间停顿。）三维自我在存在于这些实相中时，必须将注意力完全集中于此。内在觉知为他们提供了能量与力量的源泉。然而，他们必须理解自己作为演员的角色，最终从角色中脱离，并通过另一层领悟，回归到存有。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: The same applies to dream reality, for the dreams that you recall are indeed like quick pictures snapped under varying conditions. No one picture alone tells the entire story. You should write down your description of each dream picture, therefore, and keep a continuing record, for each one provides more knowledge about the nature of your own psyche and the unknown reality in which it has its existence.
quotes.translation: 这同样适用于梦境实相，因为你所记得的梦境，确实如同在不同条件下快速拍摄的快照。没有一张照片能单独讲述完整的故事。因此，你应该写下每张梦境照片的描述，并持续记录，因为每一张都提供了更多关于你自身心灵本质及其所存在的未知实相的知识。

quotes.text: If there were discrepancies among the snapshots, however, people worried. While you expect pictures of your own reality to be diverse, those who journeyed into the unknown reality became concerned if their snapshots did not agree, so they tried desperately to make all of the pictures look alike. They touched them up, in other words.
quotes.translation: 然而，如果快照之间存在差异，人们就会担忧。当你期待自己实相的照片是多样化的时，那些探索未知实相的人却因快照不一致而焦虑，于是他们拼命让所有照片看起来相似。换句话说，他们进行了修饰。

quotes.text: This does not, however, mean that this is the only reality. It is simply the only reality that you perceive with the physical senses. In order to perceive other realities, you must therefore switch from your outer senses to the inner senses, for the inner senses are clearer, and are equipped to perceive action and reality as it exists independently of the distortions given to it by the physical senses.
quotes.translation: 但这并不意味着这是唯一的实相。它仅仅是你用肉体感官感知到的唯一实相。因此，要感知其他实相，你必须从外在感官切换到内在感官，因为内在感官更清晰，能够感知行动与实相，即独立于肉体感官所赋予的扭曲而存在的实相。

quotes.text: It is therefore some force to be reckoned with. If it were a primary reality, you would not escape from it even in the sleeping state. Those realities which are primary and basic you can never escape from. This should give you an idea, for those realities which run through all systems, and which are primaries, are those which exist for you in all conditions of consciousness and under any circumstances.
quotes.translation: 因此，这是一种不可忽视的力量。如果它是首要实相，你即使在睡眠状态也无法逃脱。那些首要且基本的实相，你永远无法逃离。这应该给你一个概念，因为那些贯穿所有系统、属于首要的实相，是在所有意识状态和任何情况下都为你存在的实相。
