quotes.text: Because of its very nature, however, and even though it comprises enough "evidence" in favor of a generalized principle that explains the workings of certain phenomena, a theory inevitably contains errors, since it's based upon incomplete data to begin with. It's therefore vulnerable to later theories through which investigators attempt to reduce or eliminate those errors. A continuous refining of detail takes place in the search for a final truth that can become "fact." (I also note that that truth being sought may end up as so abstract a quality that it loses its emotional and intellectual meanings for us, and moves out of our generalized perception. I'm noting, then, that we can analyze something right out of our own reality by ultimately declaring it to be impossible--when actually it, and other versions of it, continue to exist in related probable realities.)
quotes.translation: 然而因其本质使然，即便理论包含足够"证据"支持解释特定现象运作的普遍原则，它仍不可避免地存在谬误——毕竟基于不完整数据。因此它易被后续理论推翻，研究者试图借此减少或消除谬误。在追寻可成为"事实"的终极真理时，细节不断被精炼。（我亦注意到：被追寻的真理最终可能抽象到失去情感与智识意义，脱离我们的普遍认知。于是我们通过最终宣称其不可能性，将某些事物分析出自身实相——而实际上，它及其变体仍在相关可能性实相中持续存在。）
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: I don't really think we can conceive of anything to be truly "alien," though, so I use the word here only to lead into the next of Seth's larger concepts that I want to mention: that of probable realities, or probabilities, as Jane and I usually say. Not only does Seth stress the constant psychic motion of reincarnational and counterpart selves upon this earth we know--but he also tells us that each of those selves can move into other or parallel realities. I quote him from Session 681 in Volume 1 of "Unknown" Reality:
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 6: Genetic Heritage and Reincarnational Predilections

quotes.text: "They were these: that the entire world with its organization was kept together by certain stories, like those of the Roman Catholic Church; that it was dangerous beyond all knowing to look through the stories or examine them for the truth, and that all kinds of taboos existed to keep us from doing this, since ... on the other side, so to speak, there was an incomprehensible frightening chaotic dimension, malevolent; powers beyond our imagining; and that to question the stories was to threaten not just personal survival but the fabric of reality as we know it. So excommunication was the punishment, or damnation ... which meant more than mere ostracism, but the complete isolation of a person from those belief systems, with nothing between him or her and those frightening realities ... without a framework in which to even organize meaning. This was what damnation really meant. To seek truth was the most dangerous of well-intentioned behavior, then ... and retribution had to be swift and sure.
quotes.translation: "这些故事包括：整个世界及其秩序由特定叙事维系（如罗马天主教会的故事）；审视这些故事或探求其真相的危险超乎想象；存在各种禁忌阻止我们如此行事，因为……在故事的另一面（姑且如此说），存在着不可理解、令人恐惧的混乱维度，充满恶意；存在着超乎想象的恐怖力量；质疑这些故事不仅威胁个人存续，更威胁我们所知的实相结构。因此惩罚是逐出教会或永罚……这不仅是放逐，而是将个体完全剥离信仰体系，使其直面那些恐怖实相——连组织意义的框架都不复存在。这才是永罚的真义。追寻真理成为最危险的善意行为……报应必须迅速而确定。"
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth's comments of February 17 also reflect upon Jane's own ideas as she described them just 11 days ago; see the excerpts in Note 4. Then in this Chapter 9 of Dreams, see Note 8 for Session 920.

quotes.text: As far as these drugs are concerned there are a few points I would like to make. One of your purposes as I have said is to learn to organize energy units and to focus your own energy along those lines. That is, you are the energy that you use. The self-consciousness is not to be blotted out but taken along. It is so to speak to walk side by side with the inner self. The use of these drugs sweeps the personality off its feet. Often the personality does receive a rewarding glimpse of the inner reality, but more often the ego is merely sent tumbling through frightening images of chaotic phantom realities formed by a suddenly-released subconscious, and with no guide. The experience is often vivid and long remembered, but so unorganized and undisciplined that no inner order is arrived at, no other organization glimpsed, and the ordinary but necessary camouflage footing suddenly dispensed with.
quotes.translation: 关于这些药物，我想强调几点：如我所言，你们的目的之一是学习组织能量单元，并沿特定路径聚焦自身能量。换言之，你们就是所运用的能量本身。自我意识不应被抹除，而应被携带前行——它应与内在自我并肩而行。使用这些药物会令整个人格失去平衡。人格偶尔确实能瞥见内在实相，但更多时候，自我只是被抛入由突然释放的潜意识形成的混乱幻象中，在恐惧意象里翻滚，且无人指引。这种体验往往生动难忘，却因缺乏组织与纪律，既无法达成内在秩序，也未能瞥见其他组织形态，反而突然丧失了寻常却必要的伪装基础。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: I am very glad that this is not test night. Shall I give you all gold stars? Nevertheless, at least you do remember to some degree. It is not remembering with which we are concerned, however. The inner senses, and this should help Philip, the inner senses deal with direct perception of reality, of inner reality.
quotes.translation: 我很高兴今晚不是测验之夜。要不要给你们每人一颗金星？不过，你们至少还记得一些。但我们关心的并非记忆。内在感官——这应该能帮到菲利普——内在感官处理的是对实相的直接感知，对内在实相的感知。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: When you are born, you understand that you have a new consciousness. You explore its ramifications. It is your primary evidence that you exist in flesh. Basically, each person must confront the experience of reality through a direct encounter with it. This encounter takes place through the use of the physical senses, of course, as they are used to perceive and interpret physical data. The very utilization of those senses, however, is dependent upon the nature of your consciousness itself, and that consciousness is aware of its power and action through the exercise of its own properties.
quotes.translation: 当你出生时，你明白自己拥有一个新的意识。你探索它的衍生分支。这是你存在于肉身中的首要证据。从根本上说，每个人必须通过直接遭遇来面对实相的经验。这种遭遇当然是通过使用物质感官发生的，因为它们被用来感知和解读物质资料。然而，这些感官的运用本身取决于你意识自身的本质，而那个意识通过运用自身的属性来觉察自己的力量和行动。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The inner senses belong to you as inhibitors of a spontaneous inner reality universe. They, the inner senses, are your regardless of the particular camouflage plane you might inhabit at any given instance. Only by using the inner senses can you perceive while on your plane the inner reality of which it is part.
quotes.translation: 内在感官属于你，作为自发内在实相宇宙的居住者。无论你此刻可能栖身于何种特定的伪装层，这些内在感官都是你的。只有通过运用内在感官，你才能在你的层面上感知到它所属的内在实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: It is not that your being exists in a lesser reality. It is that (eyes open occasionally, slitted again) you have not learned to recognize the extent of the reality in which you do exist. So our information must somehow appear within the small scope of what you recognize as reality, or you will not perceive it.
quotes.translation: 并非你的存在存在于一个较次等的实相中。而是（眼睛偶尔睁开，又眯起）你尚未学会认出你确实存在于其中的那个实相的范围。所以我们的信息必须以某种方式出现在你所认出的实相的小范围之内，否则你将无法感知到它。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Complications are far too intricate to explain to you. You form your image and the physical matter of your environment, and these, being action, are perceived as reality in other systems. They may not be perceived as physical objects, but as reality that conforms to the particular camouflage structure within.
quotes.translation: 复杂之处太过精微，难以向你解释。你形成你的形象和你环境的物质，而这些，作为行动，在其他系统中被感知为实相。它们可能不被感知为物质客体，而是作为符合其内在特定伪装结构的实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

source_raw.title: (We received a letter from Pat Norelli today in which she substantiates some clairvoyant material concerning her, given by Seth in Session 340. Notes are included in that session.)

quotes.text: Some of the material in the last chapter should help to explain the reasons for frameworks in which violence is built-in, so to speak, and indeed becomes a challenging context through which reality is perceived. The situation is one of danger, yet is chosen by those involved, and is not inflicted upon them. In somewhat the same way, entire life contexts are selected that might appear to be incomprehensible, foolhardy, or even insane to an observer.
quotes.translation: 最后一章中的一些材料应该有助于解释暴力被内置其中的框架的原因，可以说，它确实成为一个具有挑战性的背景，实相通过它被感知。这种情况是危险的，然而却是由那些参与者选择的，并非强加于他们。在某种程度上，整个生命背景也是被选择出来的，这些背景在观察者看来可能难以理解、鲁莽甚至疯狂。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: It goes without saying that as you create physical reality you also form the other planes of existence in which you operate. You do operate simultaneously within all levels of reality and if you become familiar with various coordinates, the self that you know could become aware of your own other existences.
quotes.translation: 不言而喻，当你创造物质实相时，你也形成了你运作其中的其他存在层面。你确实同时存在于所有实相层面中，如果你熟悉各种坐标，你所认知的自我就能意识到你自己的其他存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: The nature of basic reality is known according to the degree to which it is directly experienced, and it can only be directly experienced through the use of the inner senses. The inner senses are of course utilized on your plane, as on any other, constantly. Without any such use no existence would be possible. And without the unconscious and constant use of the inner senses you could not even construct your precious camouflage patterned universe.
quotes.translation: 基本实相的性质根据其被直接体验的程度而得以认知，而它只能通过内在感官的使用来直接体验。内在感官当然在你的层面以及其他任何层面上被持续运用。没有这种运用，任何存在都不可能。没有内在感官的无意识且持续的运用，你甚至无法构建你那珍贵的伪装模式宇宙。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: You can see now how a subjective experience can have an electrical reality, and through this reality directly affects the human physical structure. Dreams, also being electric actualities, depend upon an electric system of patterns to communicate their data to the various levels or areas of the inner self.
quotes.translation: 你现在可以看到，主观体验如何具有电性实相，并通过这种实相直接影响人类的身体结构。梦境作为电性实相，也依赖电性模式系统将其数据传递给内在自我的各个层面或领域。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Through all your lives you will interpret the reality that you see in your own way, and that way will have its effect upon you, and in turn upon others. The man who literally hates, immediately sets himself up in this fashion: He prejudges the nature of reality according to his own limited understanding.
quotes.translation: 在所有的生命历程中，你都会以自己的方式诠释你所见的实相，而这种方式会影响你，进而影响他人。真正心怀憎恨的人，会立即以这种方式自设局限：他根据自己有限的理解预判实相的性质。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: By now it should be obvious to you that you perceive only a small portion of reality; indeed, that your idea of reality is formed and limited by your perceptions.
quotes.translation: 到现在你应该已经清楚，你只感知到实相的一小部分；事实上，你对实相的观念是由你的感知所形成并限制的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: These probable realities exist within what may be generally termed your own sphere of activity, your own plane. There are many other realities that are far divorced from your own however. Every thought, as I have told you, is an action and must seek to fulfill itself. It is a fragment, therefore. You are literally surrounded by other realities that you do not perceive consciously. Some of these are perceived by the inner self, but all are not.
quotes.translation: 这些可能的实相存在于通常可称为你自身活动范围、你自身层面之内。然而，还有许多其他实相与你的实相相去甚远。正如我曾告诉你的，每一个思想都是一个行动，必须寻求自我实现。因此，它是一个片段。你确实被其他你并未有意识地感知到的实相所包围。其中一些被内在自我所感知，但并非全部。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: "In your terms -- the phrase is necessary -- the moment point, the present, is the point of interaction between all existences and reality. All probabilities flow through it, though one of your moment points may be experienced as centuries, or as a breath, in other probable realities of which you are a part." Thus, just as a moment point can be explored indefinitely, so each newly arising question results in an ever-widening pool of inquiry.
quotes.translation: “用你们的话来说——这个措辞是必要的——瞬间点，即当下，是所有存在与实相交互的焦点。所有可能性都流经此处，尽管你们的一个瞬间点可能被体验为几个世纪，或一次呼吸，在你们所参与的其他可能实相中。”因此，正如一个瞬间点可以被无限探索，每个新出现的问题都会导致一个不断扩大的探究之池。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: To me, some of the most important material in Volume 1 is Jane's information on her sensing of other neurological pulses as they're connected with probable events, and how she picked up those pulses by bypassing her direct, or ordinary neurological impact. See her work in appendixes 4 and 5. Seth also discussed such neurological changeovers in Session 685, among others. I think this kind of material offers a rich source for future scientific investigation.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: When the ego is of such a rigid nature that it distorts physical reality out of all context, then however the personality had better rely upon the subconscious even in this respect, for the subconscious will at least perceive those elements of physical reality that immediately threaten the whole self.
quotes.translation: 当自我具有如此僵化的性质，以至于完全脱离背景扭曲物质实相时，那么人格最好在这方面也依赖潜意识，因为潜意识至少会感知到那些直接威胁整个自我的物质实相元素。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: These methods themselves will vary. The systems vary, the systems being the manifestation of reality as it shows itself in various forms. The methods by which it manifests itself also vary. Certain characteristics however belong to reality regardless of the very methods and the various manifestations.
quotes.translation: 这些方法本身会有所不同。系统各不相同，系统是实相以各种形式显现自身的表现。它显现自身的方法也各不相同。然而，无论方法和各种显现如何，某些特性始终属于实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: The ego however is quite secure. It would exist regardless, now, of the particular theories of time which might be held, for it is strongly anchored as a specific manipulator of physical reality. And physical reality, believe it or not, is not dependent upon the theory of time as a series of moments.
quotes.translation: 然而，自我是相当安全的。无论持有何种特定的时间理论，它都会存在，因为它作为物质实相的特定操控者被牢牢锚定。而物质实相，信不信由你，并不依赖于将时间视为一系列瞬间的理论。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: In the beginning, then, these units operated both as identities or particles, and as waves. The main concentration was not yet physical in your terms. What you now think of as the dream state was the waking one, for it was still the recognized form of purposeful activity, creativity, and power. The dream state continues to be a connective between the two realities, and as a species you literally learned to walk by first being sleepwalkers. You walked in your sleep. You dreamed your languages. You spoke in your dreams and later wrote down the alphabets--and your knowledge and your intellect have always been fired, sharpened, propelled by the great inner reality from which your minds emerged.
quotes.translation: 因此，最初这些单位既作为身份或粒子运作，也作为波运作。主要焦点在你们看来还不是物质的。你们现在所谓的梦境状态曾是清醒状态，因为它仍然是公认的有目的活动、创造力和力量的形式。梦境状态继续作为两种实相之间的连接，作为一个物种，你们实际上是通过先成为梦游者才学会行走的。你们在睡梦中行走。你们梦见了你们的语言。你们在梦中说话，后来写下了字母表——而你们的知识和智力一直由你们心智所源自的伟大内在实相所激发、磨砺和推动。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Man tried to form a new realm, attain a different kind of focus and awareness. His consciousness turned a corner outside of itself. To do this he concentrated less and less upon inner reality, and therefore began the process of inner reality only as it was projected outward into the physical world.
quotes.translation: 人类试图形成一个新领域，获得一种不同的焦点和觉知。他的意识在自身之外转了一个弯。为了做到这一点，他越来越不关注内在实相，因此只将内在实相视为投射到物质世界中的过程。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now: Dictation. (With many pauses:) When I speak of the dream world, I am not referring to some imaginary realm, but to the kind of world of ideas, of thoughts, of mental actions, out of which all form as you think of it emerges. In actuality this is an inner universe rather than an inner world. Your physical reality is but one materialization of that inner organization. All possible civilizations exist first in that realm of inner mind.
quotes.translation: 现在：口授。（多次停顿：）当我谈到梦境世界时，我指的不是某个虚幻领域，而是指观念、思想、心灵行动所构成的世界——你们所认为的一切形态皆由此涌现。实际上，这是一个内在宇宙，而非内在世界。你们的物质实相仅是那内在组织的一种物质化呈现。所有可能的文明首先存在于那内在心灵的领域。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: As long as you believe this so will your expectations of reality become, in truth, reality. It goes without saying that if all of your energy goes into money making little will be left for painting, but this is a long stretch between this equation and the one that says that an artist must be poor.
quotes.translation: 只要你们相信这一点，你们对实相的期待就会真正成为实相。不言而喻，若你们将所有精力投入赚钱，留给绘画的精力便会所剩无几——但这与"艺术家必须贫穷"的等式之间，尚隔着漫长的距离。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Now. In certain levels of dream reality forms that you do not as yet perceive as physical, dfl exist. They can be seen and perceived. In certain dreams you do perceive them. Within dream reality you can come in contact with many other kinds of reality with which you do not ordinarily have to deal.
quotes.translation: 现在。在梦境实相的某些层次中，存在着你们尚未感知为物质形态的形体。它们能被看见和感知。在某些梦境中，你们确实能感知到它们。在梦境实相中，你们能与许多其他通常无需应对的实相类型产生接触。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: Hitler preached on the great value of social action as opposed to individual action. He turned children into informers against their own parents. He behaved nationalistically, as any minor cult leader does in a smaller context. The Jews believed in martyrdom. (Pause.) Germany became the new Egypt, in which their people were set upon. I do not want to oversimplify here, and certainly I am nowhere justifying the cruelties the Jews encountered in Germany. You do each create your own reality, however (intently), and en masse you create the realities of your nationalities and your countries -- so at that time the Germans saw themselves as victors, and the Jews saw themselves as victims.
quotes.translation: 希特勒鼓吹社会行动相对于个人行动的伟大价值。他让儿童成为告发自己父母的告密者。他的民族主义行径，与任何小规模邪教领袖在较小范围内的所作所为如出一辙。犹太人则信奉殉道精神。（停顿。）德国成了新的埃及，在那里他们的人民遭受迫害。我不想在此过度简化，当然也绝非为犹太人在德国遭遇的残酷行径辩护。然而，你们确实各自创造自己的实相（专注地），并且集体创造你们民族和国家的实相——因此在那个时期，德国人视自己为胜利者，而犹太人视自己为受害者。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 852, MAY 9, 1979
9:39 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: I speak to those who believe in a god, and those who do not, to those who believe that science will find all answers as to the nature of reality, and to those who do not. I hope to give you clues that will enable you to study the nature of reality for yourself as you have never studied it before.
quotes.translation: 我对信仰神的人说话，也对不信仰神的人说话；对相信科学能解答实相本质所有问题的人说话，也对不相信的人说话。我希望给予你们线索，使你们能以前所未有的方式自行研究实相的本质。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: There is great cooperation behind such momentous productions, and in playing his role, each actor first actualizes himself within three-dimensional reality. The multidimensional self cannot act within three-dimensional reality until it materializes a portion of itself within it. Do you follow me?
quotes.translation: 在如此重大的创作背后存在着伟大的合作，每位演员在扮演角色时，首先在三维实相中实现自己。多维度自性必须将其部分物质化于三维实相中，才能在其中运作。你们明白我的意思吗？
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Each inner self is a portion of the basic inner reality. It cannot know itself however, except through experience, and it must create in order to experience. The experience constantly deepens the value fulfillment of basic reality itself. There is no alternative between diversity and nothingness.
quotes.translation: 每个内在自体都是基本内在实相的一部分。然而，它无法通过自身认识自己，除非通过经验，而它必须创造才能经验。经验不断深化基本实相本身的价值完成。在多样性与虚无之间别无选择。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Consciousness therefore continually creates and maintains itself, and this includes the physical materiaizationl, the properties of the dimension, and yet basically there is no difference between the creator in these terms and the created. Nor between inner reality, which forms physical matter, and physical objects themselves, for the atoms which are manipulated to form objects are themselves a portion of consciousness, and alive in those terms. They respond to Emotional and psychic directives as the physical body responds to light.
quotes.translation: 因此，意识持续创造并维持自身，这包括物质的具体化、维度的属性，然而在这些方面，创造者与被创造者之间并无根本差异。形成物质的内在实相与物质客体本身之间也是如此，因为被操纵以形成物体的原子本身就是意识的一部分，并且在这些方面是活生生的。它们对情绪和心灵指令的反应，正如肉体对光的反应。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: Obviously, physical reality only happens to be the portion of reality you recognize. The paintings that you will paint exist now. It is possible for you to project yourself into one of your own future landscapes. This would not be an imaginative projection. This is what I am trying to tell you.
quotes.translation: 显然，物质实相恰好只是你所认知的实相的一部分。你将要绘制的画作现在就已存在。你有可能将自己投射到未来的某个风景中。这并非想象力的投射。这正是我试图告诉你的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: I mentioned that objects are symbols to express a basic reality, the reality of direct experience. On one level dreams deal with objects and dream images. Here they are still being used as symbols. At deeper levels however in the dream state there is direct experience, and objects are not used.
quotes.translation: 我曾提到，物体是表达基本实相——直接经验的实相——的象征。在某一层面，梦境处理的是物体和梦意象。在这里，它们仍被用作象征。然而，在梦境状态的更深层次，存在直接经验，而不使用物体。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: There are also training centers. In these the nature of reality is explained in accordance with an individual's ability to understand and perceive it. The familiar parables, for some, will still be used at least initially, and then these individuals will be gradually weaned away from them. In these centers there are certain classes in which instruction is given for the benefit of those who chose to return to the physical environment.
quotes.translation: 还有训练中心。在这些中心里，实相的本质会按照个人理解和感知的能力加以解释。对某些人来说，熟悉的寓言至少在一开始仍会被使用，然后这些人会逐渐脱离它们。在这些中心里，有特定的课程，为那些选择返回物质环境的人提供指导。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Every nerve and fiber within the physical image has an inner purpose that is not seen, and that serves to connect the inner self with physical reality--in other words, that allows the inner self to create physical reality.
quotes.translation: 物质形象中的每条神经和纤维都有看不见的内在目的，这个目的用于连接内在自体与物质实相——换句话说，它允许内在自体创造物质实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Therefore, you have greater use of symbolism in the dream state, for you are aware of past and future symbols. These vary in intensity; often they cluster together. Such multidimensional symbols will appear then in many ways, not simply visually. They will affect not only your own physical reality, but all realities in which you are involved. In a manner of speaking the symbols that you know are but the tail end of greater symbols.
quotes.translation: 因此，你在梦境状态中对象征符号的运用更为广泛，因为你意识到过去与未来的象征符号。这些符号强度各异，常常聚集在一起。这种多维度的象征符号会以多种方式显现，而不仅限于视觉形式。它们不仅会影响你自己的物质实相，还会影响你所涉足的所有实相。可以说，你所知的象征符号不过是更宏大象征符号的末端。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: We have discussed the ways in which telepathy operates in this respect, and we have to some extent discussed the ways in which the appearance of cohesiveness is maintained. In many cases the individual who is called mentally unbalanced is simply one whose individual reality is so composed that it is impossible for others to find in his expression of it any similarity with their own. The error is one of inadequate idea construction.
quotes.translation: 我们已讨论过心灵感应在此方面运作的方式，并在某种程度上探讨了连贯性表象得以维持的途径。在许多情况下，被称为精神失衡的个体，只是其个人实相的构成方式，使得他人无法从其表达中找到与自身经验的相似之处。这种错误源于观念构建的不当。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: You must understand that each mental act is a reality for which you are responsible. That is what you are in this particular system of reality for. As long as you believe in a devil, for example, you will create one that is real enough for you, and for the others who continue to create him.
quotes.translation: 你必须明白，每一个心智活动都是你需为之负责的实相。这正是你存在于这个特定实相体系中的目的。例如，只要你相信魔鬼的存在，你就会创造出一个对你足够真实的魔鬼，也会为那些持续创造它的他人而存在。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I affect reality, physical reality, because men's minds are changed according to their ideas, and we shall change these ideas for the better. The ideas, working through men, will affect physical reality for the better. I am here more often than you think. Never, however, in a prying manner.
quotes.translation: 我影响实相，影响物质实相，因为人类的心智会随其观念而改变，而我们将把这些观念向更美好的方向转变。这些观念通过人类运作，将改善物质实相。我在此处的频率远超你的想象，但从不以窥探的方式出现。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: There would indeed be a mass transference of consciousness, but not necessarily at all to one particular reality. The individual consciousness involved would not for example necessarily choose to start anew, in the same kind of reality, agreeing to form more or less identical conditions.
quotes.translation: 确实会存在大规模的意识转移，但未必会转向某个特定的实相。例如，所涉的个体意识未必会选择在同类实相中重新开始，并同意形成大致相同的条件。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: So while you operate in a one reality field psychological structure, you do not on a conscious level even perceive its entire reality, nor your place in it. The word progression is a poor one, and again however I must use it. (Pause; smile.) Seth's material on action will help you here.
quotes.translation: 因此，当你在单一实相场的心理结构中运作时，你甚至无法在意识层面感知其完整实相，也无法感知你在此中的位置。"进展"一词并不恰当，但我仍必须使用它。（停顿；微笑。）赛斯关于"行动"的资料将在此处助你一臂之力。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: The dreaming self as you conceive of it, however, is but a shadow of its own reality, for the dreaming self is a psychological point of reference and, in your terms, [of] continuity, that brings together all portions of your identity. Of its deeper nature, only the most developed are aware. It represents, in other words, one strong uniting facet of your entire identity. Its experiences are as vivid and its "personality" as rich -- in fact richer -- in context as the physical personality you know.
quotes.translation: 你所认为的做梦自己，不过是其自身实相的一个影子，因为做梦自己是心理上的一个参考点，用你们的话说，也是连续性的一个参考点，它将你身份的所有部分汇聚在一起。只有最进化者才意识到它更深层的本质。换句话说，它代表了你整个身份中一个强大的统一面向。它的体验同样生动，其"人格"在脉络中与你们所知的物质人格一样丰富——事实上更为丰富。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The electromagnetic reality within the human organism has considerable mass, but the entire physical weight amounts to 3 to 6 ounces at the very most. Again, the mass is composed of electrical intensities. I have told you that all experience is basically psychological, and that it is held in coded form within the cells. One electrical pulsation can represent an emotional experience. The importance of the experience to the individual will be responsible for the intensity with which it is recorded.
quotes.translation: 人类有机体内的电磁实相具有相当的质量，但整个物理重量最多只有3到6盎司。再次说明，这个质量是由电性强度组成的。我曾告诉过你们，所有经验基本上都是心理性的，并且以编码形式存在于细胞内。一次电性脉冲可以代表一种情感体验。该经验对个体的重要性将决定其被记录的强度。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Your own physical environment appears as it does to you because of your own psychological structure. If you gained your sense of personal continuity through associative processes primarily, rather than as a result of the familiarity of self moving through time, then you would experience physical reality in an entirely different fashion. Objects from past and present could be perceived at once, their presence justified through associative connections. Say that your father throughout his lifetime has eight favorite chairs. If your perceptive mechanisms were primarily set up as a result of intuitive association rather than time sequence, then you would perceive all of these chairs at one time; or seeing one, you would be aware of the others. So environment is not a separate thing in itself, but the result of perceptive patterns, and these are determined by psychological structure.
quotes.translation: 你们自己的物质环境之所以呈现为你们所见的样子，是因为你们自身的心理结构。如果你们主要通过联想过程而非通过自我在时间中移动的熟悉感来获得个人连续性感，那么你们将以完全不同的方式体验物质实相。过去和现在的物体可能同时被感知，它们的存在通过联想连接而得到合理化。假设你父亲一生中有八把最喜欢的椅子。如果你的感知机制主要是由直觉联想而非时间顺序建立起来的，那么你会同时感知所有这些椅子；或者看到其中一把，你就会意识到其他椅子。因此，环境本身并非独立存在的事物，而是感知模式的结果，而这些模式又由心理结构决定。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Dictation then: Probabilities are an ever-present portion of your invisible psychological environment. You exist in the middle of the probable system of reality. It is not something apart from you. To some extent it is like a sea in which you have your present being. You are in it, and it is in you. Occasionally at surface levels of consciousness, you might wonder what might have happened had you made other decisions than those you have; chosen different mates, for example, or taken up residence in other portions of the country. You might wonder what would have happened had you mailed an important letter that you subsequently decided not to mail; and in such small wonderings only, have you ever questioned the nature of probabilities. But there are deep connections between yourself and all those individuals with whom you have had relationships, and with whom you were involved in deep decisions.
quotes.translation: 口述开始：可能性是你们无形心理环境中始终存在的一部分。你们存在于可能实相系统的中间。它并非与你们分离之物。在某种程度上，它就像一片海洋，你们在其中拥有当下的存在。你们在它之中，它也在你们之中。偶尔在意识的表层，你们可能会好奇，如果做了与现在不同的决定会发生什么；例如，选择了不同的伴侣，或在国家的其他地方定居。你们可能会好奇，如果寄出了一封后来决定不寄的重要信件会发生什么；只有在这样小小的好奇中，你们才曾质疑过可能性的本质。但你们自身与所有那些曾与你们有关系、曾与你们共同卷入重大决定的人之间，存在着深刻的联系。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In our next chapter I will speak of these in some detail. For now it is sufficient to realize that specific steps, definite alterations, occur as consciousness is shifted from the exterior to the interior reality, and that these changes are not random; that consciousness leaves through a very predictable route to its many destinations. Through the ages the Speakers have taught dreamers how to manipulate in these other environments. They have taught them how to bring back information that could be used for the good of the present personality. According to the intent, present purpose, and development, an individual may be aware of these travels to varying degrees. Some have excellent recall, for example, but often misinterpret their experience because of conscious ideas.
quotes.translation: 在下一章中，我将详细讨论这些。目前，只需认识到当意识从外在实相转向内在实相时，会发生特定的步骤和明确的改变，并且这些变化并非随机；意识会通过一条非常可预测的路径离开，前往其众多目的地。历代以来，传讯者教导做梦者如何在这些其他环境中运作。他们教导他们如何带回可用于当前人格福祉的信息。根据意图、当前目的和发展程度，个体可能在不同程度上意识到这些旅程。例如，有些人有极好的回忆，但常常因意识观念而误解他们的经验。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: "You" presently have a once-centered consciousness, in that "you" close off from your experience these other stages of consciousness in which other portions of your entire identity are intimately involved. These other stages of consciousness create their own realities as you create your own. The realities are, therefore, byproducts of consciousness itself. If you could become aware of these, they might appear to be other places to you, rather than realms or fields of different kinds of activities. If you probe into these realms you will be forced to perceive them with the root assumptions of your own system, translating feelings of warmth and comfort, for example, into images of warm shelters or buildings, or feelings of fear into images of demons.
quotes.translation: "你"目前拥有一个单点聚焦的意识，因为"你"从你的经验中关闭了这些其他意识阶段，而你的整个身份的其他部分正密切参与其中。这些其他意识阶段创造它们自己的实相，就像你创造你自己的实相一样。因此，实相是意识本身的副产品。如果你能意识到它们，它们对你而言可能看起来像是其他地方，而非不同种类活动的领域或场域。如果你深入探究这些领域，你将被迫用你自己系统的根本假设来感知它们，例如，将温暖和舒适的感觉转化为温暖庇护所或建筑物的意象，或将恐惧的感觉转化为恶魔的意象。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I am not saying that, nor implying it in the case just mentioned. In your system you have a direct hand in the formation of physical reality. Your natural given data is the result of individual, mass, and collective thoughts, feelings, and emotions, materialized. Your system in this respect is more creative than the systems just mentioned. On the other hand, within these other systems there is a strong innovative group consciousness developing, in which identity is retained but greater inner play allowed between individuals, a large creative interchange of symbol-pools, a drawing upon mental and psychic symbols with greater facility. Because of this, these individuals recognize more clearly the connection between creative images and given sense data. They purposely alter and change their given sense data, and experiment with it.
quotes.translation: 我并非在说，也并非暗示刚才提到的情况。在你的系统中，你直接参与了物质实相的形成。你自然给定的感官资料，是个体、群体和集体思想、感受与情感物质化的结果。你的系统在这方面比刚才提到的那些系统更具创造力。另一方面，在这些其他系统中，一种强大的创新性群体意识正在发展，其中身份得以保留，但个体之间允许更大的内在互动，符号库之间进行大规模创造性交流，并能更自如地运用心智与心灵符号。正因如此，这些个体更清晰地认识到创造性意象与给定感官资料之间的联系。他们有意改变和调整自己的给定感官资料，并对其进行实验。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The man called Christ was not crucified. In the overall drama however it made little difference what was fact, in your terms, and what was not -- for the greater reality transcends facts and creates them. You have free will. You could interpret the drama as you wished. It was given to you. Its great creative power still exists and you use it in your own way, even changing your own symbolism as your beliefs change. But the main idea is the affirmation that the physical being, the self that you know, is not annihilated with death. This comes through even in the distortions. The whole concept of God the Father, as given by Christ, was indeed a "new testament." The male image of God was used because of the sex orientation of the times, but beyond this the Christ personality said, "... the kingdom of God is within (among) you" (Luke 17:21).
quotes.translation: 那个被称为基督的人并未被钉死在十字架上。然而，从整体戏剧来看，用你们的说法，什么是事实、什么不是事实，其实并无多大区别——因为更大的实相超越事实，并创造事实。你们拥有自由意志。你们可以按自己的意愿诠释这出戏剧。它被赐予你们。它伟大的创造力依然存在，你们以自己的方式运用它，甚至随着信念的改变而改变自己的象征体系。但核心思想是：肯定物质存在——你所认知的自我——不会随死亡而湮灭。这一点即使在扭曲的诠释中也能体现出来。基督所传达的"天父"这一整体概念，确实是一部"新约"。上帝以男性形象呈现，是因为当时社会以性别为导向，但基督人格在更深层面上说："……神的国就在你们心里（或：在你们中间）"（路加福音17:21）。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: According to the intensity of the situation, now, another also desirable solution may be worked out in a probable reality. On an unconscious level you are aware of your probable selves, and they of you. You share the same psychic roots, and your joint yet separate dreams are available to "all of you." This does not mean that you are dreaming someone else's dream, any more than it means that twins, for example, do. It does mean that your probable selves and you share in a body of symbolism, background, and ability. The multistructured nature of the dream state allows for dream dramas in which probable selves do appear. They may appear as symbolically representing strong characteristics upon which they have focused, though you have ignored them.
quotes.translation: 现在，根据情境的强度，另一个同样理想的解决方案可能会在某个可能的实相中实现。在无意识层面，你意识到你的可能自我，它们也意识到你。你们共享相同的心灵根源，而你们共同却又各自独立的梦境，对"你们所有人"都是可及的。这并不意味着你在做别人的梦，就像双胞胎并非在做对方的梦一样。它确实意味着你的可能自我与你共享一套象征体系、背景和能力。梦境状态的多重结构允许出现可能自我参与其中的梦剧。它们可能以象征形式出现，代表它们所专注的强烈特质——尽管你忽略了这些特质。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 687
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: (Intently:) I am saying that the individual self must become consciously aware of far more reality; that it must allow its recognition of identity to expand so that it includes previously unconscious knowledge. To do this you must understand, again, that man must move beyond the concepts of one god, one self, one body, one world, as these ideas are currently understood.5 You are now poised, in your terms, upon a threshold from which the race can go many ways. There are species of consciousness. Your species is in a time of change. There are potentials within the body's mechanisms, in your terms, not as yet used. Developed, they can immeasurably enrich the race, and bring it to levels of spiritual and psychic and physical fulfillment. If some changes are not made, the race as such will not endure.
quotes.translation: （专注地：）我是说，个体自我必须有意识地意识到远为更多的实相；它必须允许自己对身份的认知扩展，以包含先前无意识的知识。要做到这一点，你必须再次理解：人类必须超越当前所理解的"一位神、一个自我、一个身体、一个世界"这些概念。用你们的说法，你们现在正站在一个门槛上，人类种族可以从这里走向许多方向。存在多种意识物种。你们的物种正处于变革时期。用你们的说法，身体机制内存在着尚未被利用的潜能。一旦发展起来，它们将无可估量地丰富人类种族，并将其带到灵性、心灵与物质成就的新高度。如果不做出某些改变，这个种族将无法存续。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: In your terms, until now your consciousness has specialized in neurological patterning. As mentioned (in Session 682), this was extremely important while it learned the art of specialized focus. Now, however, it must begin to recognize that it can indeed expand, and bring into its awareness other quite legitimate realities. The nature of probabilities must be understood, for the time has come in the world as you experience it where the greatest wisdom and discrimination are needed. Your consciousness and neurological prejudice blind you to the full dimension of physical activity. The true implications of physical action are not as yet apparent to you.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: The spiritual and the biological cannot be separated. Their purposes and reality merge. Give us a moment ... (Long pause at 10:19.) I will have much more to say concerning this later in the book. For now simply let me mention that any gods appearing among you must always be of your time, while expressing ideas and concepts that must shoot beyond your time into the future, and serve as psychic stimuli strong enough to effect future changes. When, in historic terms, the race was in the process of adopting a necessary artificial separation of itself from the rest of nature; when it needed to be assured of its abilities to do so; when it took upon itself the task of a particular kind of specialization and individual focus, it needed a religion that would assure it of its abilities.
quotes.translation: 灵性与生物性不可分割。它们的目的与实相相互融合。请稍等片刻……（10:19处长时间停顿。）关于这一点，我将在本书后面详细阐述。现在，我只想提一点：任何出现在你们之中的神祇，都必须属于你们的时代，同时表达那些必须超越时代、射向未来的理念与概念，并作为足够强大的心灵刺激，以促成未来的改变。从历史角度看，当人类种族正在经历一种必要的、与自然其余部分人为分离的过程时；当它需要确信自己有能力这样做时；当它承担起特定专门化与个体聚焦的任务时，它需要一种能确保其能力的宗教。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 690
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: The many directions possible for the species exist now. Joseph reacted on a cellular level in one respect. The cells recognized the probable reality involved,6 and he, Joseph, felt that he was "at home" (in the Markle place), and yet consciously could not explain the feeling. In certain terms his mother will feel vindicated if Joseph buys that house, but the choice is still his and Ruburt's. If you pay more attention to what you think of as coincidences, you will discover another kind of order that underlies the recognized order you follow. This has all kinds of implications biologically as far as the species is concerned; you can perhaps understand, then, that there are also probable histories beneath your lives, individually and en masse.
quotes.translation: 这个物种可能走向的许多方向现在都存在。约瑟在某个层面上做出了细胞层面的反应。细胞识别了其中涉及的可能实相，而他——约瑟——感到自己"如在家般自在"（在马克尔庄园），但意识层面却无法解释这种感觉。从某种角度看，如果约瑟买下那栋房子，他的母亲会感到自己的正确性得到证实，但选择权仍在他和鲁柏手中。如果你更多地关注你所认为的"巧合"，你会发现一种新的秩序，它潜藏在你所遵循的公认秩序之下。就物种而言，这在生物学上具有各种含义；那么，你或许可以理解：在你的个体与集体生活之下，也存在着可能的历史。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 693
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Because you have in the past convinced yourselves that the conscious mind must of necessity be cut off from inner reality, you think that it must be alienated from the dream state. Following such beliefs, you find yourselves thinking of dreaming as chaotic, unreasonable, and as completely divorced from normal conscious direction, purpose, or function. It often seems that sleep is almost a small death, and psychologists have compared dreaming with controlled insanity.5 You have so divorced your waking and dreaming experience that it seems you have separate "lives," and that there is little connection between your waking and dreaming hours. The rich tapestry of probable actions from which you choose your official life becomes just as invisible. This is quite needless.
quotes.translation: 由于你们过去曾说服自己，认为意识心必然与内在实相隔绝，因此你们认为它必然与梦境状态疏离。遵循这样的信念，你们发现自己将梦境视为混乱、不合理，且完全脱离正常的意识方向、目的或功能。睡眠常常几乎像是一场小死亡，心理学家甚至将做梦比作可控的疯狂。你们如此割裂清醒与做梦的经验，以至于它们仿佛成了各自独立的"生命"，清醒时刻与梦境之间几乎毫无关联。你们从中选择官方实相的那个充满可能行动之丰富织锦，也因此变得不可见。这完全是没有必要的。

quotes.text: In usual circumstances you may remember the emotions that you felt at the time a picture of yourself was taken, and to some extent those emotions may show themselves in gestures or facial expression. But the greater subjective reality of that moment does not appear physically in such a photograph. It completely escapes insofar as its physical appearance within that structure is concerned. In the same way the past or the future is closed out. The particular focus necessary to produce such a picture then necessitates the exclusion of other data. That certainly is obvious. Because you must manipulate within specific time periods, you do the same kind of thing in daily life, and on a conscious level ignore or exclude much information that is otherwise available.
quotes.translation: 在通常情况下，你们或许记得自己被拍照那一刻所感受到的情绪，而这些情绪在一定程度上可能通过姿态或面部表情显现出来。但那一刻更伟大的主观实相，并不会以物理形式出现在那张照片中。就其在那个结构内的物理显现而言，它完全逃逸了。同样地，过去或未来也被排除在外。为了产生这样一张照片所需的特定聚焦，必然要排除其他数据。这当然是显而易见的。由于你们必须在特定的时间段内运作，你们在日常生活中也做着同样的事，在意识层面上忽略或排除了许多原本可用的信息。

quotes.text: In many cases your scientists seem to have the strange idea that you can understand a reality by destroying it; that you can perceive the life mechanism of an animal by killing it; or that you can examine a phenomenon best by separating yourself from it. So, often, you attempt to examine the nature of the brain in man by destroying the brains of animals, by separating portions of the animal brain from its components, isolating them, and tampering with the overall integrity of both the animal in question and of your own spiritual processes. By this I mean that each such attempt puts you more out of context, so to speak, with yourself and your environment, and other species. Period. While you may "learn" certain so-called facts, you are driven still further away from any great knowledge, because the so-called facts stand in your way. You do not as yet understand the uniqueness of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 在许多情况下，你们的科学家似乎持有一种奇怪的想法：认为可以通过摧毁实相来理解它；认为可以通过杀死动物来感知其生命机制；或者认为可以通过与现象分离来最好地检视它。因此，你们常常试图通过摧毁动物的大脑、将动物大脑的某些部分与其组成部分分离、孤立它们，并干预该动物以及你们自身灵性过程的整体完整性，来检视人类大脑的本质。我的意思是，每一次这样的尝试，可以说都让你们更加脱离自身、环境以及其他物种的脉络。句号。虽然你们可能"学到"某些所谓的事实，但你们却离任何伟大的知识越来越远，因为这些所谓的事实挡在了你们面前。你们至今尚未理解意识的独特性。

quotes.text: On many occasions then you set yourself a problem -- "Shall I do this or that?" -- and form a dream in which you follow through the probable futures that would "result" from the courses available. While you are sleeping and dreaming, your chemical and hormonal activity faithfully follows the courses of the dreams. Even in your accepted reality, then, to that extent in such a dream you react to probable events as well as to the events chosen for waking physical experience. Your daily life is affected, because in such a dream you deal with probable predictabilities. You are hardly alone, however, so each individual alive also has his and her private dreams, and these help form the accepted probability sequence of the following day, and of "time to come." The personal decisions all add up to the global happenings on any given day.
quotes.translation: 因此，在许多情况下，你们会给自己设定一个问题——"我该做这个还是那个？"——并形成一个梦境，在其中你们会跟随那些从可用路径中"产生"的可能未来。当你们在睡眠和做梦时，你们的化学和荷尔蒙活动会忠实地跟随梦境的路径。即使在你们所接受的实相中，在这样一个梦境里，你们也会对可能事件以及为清醒物理经验所选择的事件做出反应，其程度便是如此。你们的日常生活会受到影响，因为在这种梦境中，你们处理的是可能的可预测性。然而，你们绝非孤立的，因此每个活着的个体也拥有他或她自己的私人梦境，而这些梦境有助于形成第二天以及"未来时间"中被接受的可能性序列。个人的决定最终汇聚成任何特定日子里的全球事件。

quotes.text: (With many pauses:) Give us a moment ... Such a performance actually means that physical reality clicks off and on.11 In your terms, it exists only in your waking hours. The inner work that makes it possible is largely done in the sleep state. The meeting of body consciousness and your main consciousness requires an intense focus, in which the greatest manipulations are necessary. Perceptions must be precise in physical terms. To some extent, however, that exquisite concentration means that certain limitations occur. Cellular comprehension is not tuned into by the normally conscious self, which is equally unaware of its own free-wheeling nature at "higher" levels. So a disengagement process must happen that allows each to regenerate. The consciousness then leaves the body. The body consciousness stays with it.
quotes.translation: （多次停顿：）给我们一点时间……这样的表现实际上意味着物理实相在开启与关闭。在你们的说法中，它只存在于你们的清醒时刻。而使其成为可能的内在工作，大部分是在睡眠状态中完成的。身体意识与你们主要意识的会合需要一种强烈的聚焦，其中必须进行最重大的操作。感知必须在物理层面上精确。然而，在某种程度上，这种精妙的专注意味着某些限制会发生。细胞的理解力不会被通常的意识自我所调谐，而后者同样对自己在"更高"层面上的自由驰骋本性毫无察觉。因此，必须发生一个脱离过程，让两者都能再生。意识随后离开身体。身体意识则留在其中。

quotes.text: In the dream state and in certain other levels of reality, ideas and their symbols are immediately experienced. There is no time lag, then, between a feeling and its "exteriorized" condition. It is automatically experienced in whatever form is familiar and natural to the one who holds it. The psyche is presented with its own concepts, which are instantly reflected in dream situations and other events that will be explained shortly. If you dream of or yearn for a new house in physical life, for instance, it may take some time before that ideal is realized, even though such a strong intent will most certainly bring about its physical fulfillment. The same desire in the dreaming state, however, may lead to the instant creation of such a house as far as your dream experience is concerned. Again, there is no time lag there between desire and its materialization.
quotes.translation: 在梦境状态以及实相的其他某些层面上，观念及其象征会被立即体验。因此，在感觉与其"外化"状态之间没有时间延迟。它会自动以持有者所熟悉和自然的任何形式被体验。心灵会呈现其自身概念，这些概念会立即反映在梦境情境以及其他即将解释的事件中。例如，如果在物理生活中你梦见或渴望一栋新房子，即使如此强烈的意图必定会带来其物理实现，这个理想也可能需要一些时间才能实现。然而，就你的梦境经验而言，同样的渴望在梦境状态中可能会导致这样一栋房子的即刻创造。再次强调，在那里，渴望与其物质化之间没有时间延迟。

quotes.text: In certain terms, then, this involves in a very small way the creation and colonization of a different kind of reality -- consciously accepted, however, from your perspective. On an unconscious level, the world as you know it expands in just such a fashion.10 Several students have had dreams involving their participation in such a project. Ruburt found himself in an out-of-body state, looking at a jacket. It had four rectangular pockets. It was giant-sized. As he looked at it the front flap was open. In the dream he flew through this flap literally into another dimension, where the point of the flap was a hill upon which he landed. From that second perspective, the pockets of the jacket in the first perspective became the windows of a building that existed in a still-further, third dimension beyond the hill. Standing on the hill, he knew that in Perspective One the windows of the building in Perspective Three were jacket pockets, but he could no longer perceive them as such. Looking out from the hill in Perspective Two, Perspective One was invisibly behind him, and Perspective Three was still "ahead" of him, separated from him by a gulf he did not understand.
quotes.translation: 从某种角度来看，这涉及一种不同实相的创造与殖民——尽管从你们的视角来看，这是被有意识地接受的。在无意识层面上，你们所知的世界正是以这种方式扩展的。10 几位学生曾做过涉及参与此类计划的梦。鲁柏发现自己处于出体状态，看着一件夹克。它有四个长方形口袋，尺寸巨大。当他看着它时，前襟是敞开的。在梦中，他 literally 穿过这片前襟进入另一个维度，在那里前襟的尖端变成了一座山丘，他降落在上面。从第二个视角来看，第一个视角中夹克的口袋变成了存在于山丘之外更远的第三个维度中的一栋建筑的窗户。站在山丘上，他知道在视角一中，视角三中建筑的窗户是夹克口袋，但他再也无法如此感知它们。从视角二的山丘向外看，视角一在他身后不可见，而视角三仍然"在他前方"，被一道他不理解的鸿沟隔开。

quotes.text: (At a slower pace:) He is consciously entering into another room of the psyche, and also entering into the reality that corresponds to it. This brings the two experiences together so that they coincide. They are held, however, both separately and in joint focus. As a rule you use one particular level of awareness, and this correlates all of your conscious activities. I told you that the physical body itself was able to pick up other neurological messages beside those to which you usually react.8 Now let me add that when a certain proficiency is reached in alterations of consciousness, this allows you to become practically familiar with some of these other neurological messages. In such a way Ruburt is able to physically perceive what he is doing in his "library."
quotes.translation: （放慢语速：）他正在有意识地进入心灵的另一间房间，同时也进入与之对应的实相。这使两种体验汇聚在一起，使它们重合。然而，它们既被分开持有，又被共同聚焦。通常，你使用一个特定的意识层次，这协调了你所有的有意识活动。我曾告诉过你，物质身体本身能够接收除了你通常反应的那些之外的其他神经信息。8 现在让我补充一点：当在意识转换中达到一定熟练程度时，这使你能够实际熟悉其中一些其他神经信息。通过这种方式，鲁伯能够物质性地感知他在"图书馆"中所做的事情。

quotes.text: In your sleep, however, your consciousness slips out of your body and returns to it frequently. You dream when you are out of your body, even as you dream inside it. You may therefore form dream stories about your own out-of-body travel, while your physical image rests soundly in bed. The unknown reality, you see, is not really that mysterious to you. You only pretend that it is. Sometimes you have quite clear perceptions of your journeys, but the actual native territories that you visit are so different from your own world that you try to interpret them as best you can in the light of usual conditions. If you remember such an episode at all it may well seem very confusing, for you will have superimposed your own world view where it does not belong.
quotes.translation: 然而，在睡眠中，你的意识会滑出你的身体并频繁返回。当你离开身体时你会做梦，就像你在身体内做梦一样。因此，当你的物质形象安然躺在床上时，你可能会形成关于自己出体旅行的梦境故事。你看，未知的实相对你来说其实并不那么神秘。你只是假装它是神秘的。有时你对你的旅程有相当清晰的感知，但你所访问的实际原生领域与你的世界如此不同，以至于你尽力根据通常条件来诠释它们。如果你完全记得这样一段经历，它可能看起来非常混乱，因为你将你的世界观叠加在了它不属于的地方。

quotes.text: In greater terms, the past is definitely created from the present. In your system of reality this does not seem to be the case at all, since your senses project a forward kind of motion outward upon events. "Subatomic particles," however, appear in your present, rippling into your system's dimensions, creating their own "tracks," which scientists then try to observe. In some cases, unknowingly, your scientists are close to observing the birth of time effects within your system. (Pause.) Since your brains are composed of cells with their atoms and molecules, and since these are themselves made of certain invisible particles,2 then your memories are already structured by the biological mechanisms that make them possible in your terms. (In parentheses: After death, for example, you still possess a memory, though it does not operate through the physical organism as you understand it.)
quotes.translation: 从更宏大的角度来看，过去绝对是由现在创造的。在你们的实相系统中，这看起来完全不是这样，因为你们的感官将一种向前的运动投射到事件上。然而，"亚原子粒子"出现在你们的现在，涟漪般进入你们系统的维度，创造它们自己的"轨迹"，科学家们随后试图观察这些轨迹。在某些情况下，你们的科学家在不知不觉中接近观察你们系统内时间效应的诞生。（停顿。）由于你们的大脑由细胞及其原子和分子组成，而这些本身又由某些不可见粒子构成，2 那么你们的记忆已经由使它们在你们术语中成为可能的生物机制所构建。（括号内：例如，死后你仍然拥有记忆，尽管它并不通过你所理解的物质有机体运作。）

quotes.text: People have written here asking about soul mates.3 In certain circles this is the latest vogue. The idea is an old one; it is based upon the reality of counterparts, and presents another version of the theory. But, again, it is treated with an almost pompous seriousness. (Pause.) Many of those who use the term do it to hide rather than to release their own joyful abilities. They spend time searching for their soul mates -- but the search involves them in a pilgrimage for a kind of impossible communication with another, in which all division is lost, with the two then trying to join in a cementing oneness, suffocating all sense of play or creativity. You are not one part, or one half, of another soul,4 searching through the annals of time for your partner, undone until you are completed by your soul mate.
quotes.translation: 人们曾在此写信询问关于灵魂伴侣的事。3 在某些圈子里，这是最新的风尚。这个想法很古老；它基于对应体的实相，并呈现了该理论的另一个版本。但同样，它被以一种近乎浮夸的严肃态度对待。（停顿。）许多使用这个术语的人用它来隐藏而非释放他们自己喜悦的能力。他们花费时间寻找他们的灵魂伴侣——但这种寻找使他们卷入一场朝圣之旅，寻求与另一个人某种不可能的沟通，在其中所有分裂都消失，两人试图融合成一种粘合的一体，窒息了所有游戏或创造的感觉。你不是另一个灵魂的一部分或一半，4 在时间的编年史中寻找你的伴侣，直到被你的灵魂伴侣补全才得以完整。

quotes.text: The categories [healing, teaching, or whatever] are general descriptions of the families of consciousness. You can split them up also and make further distinctions, if you choose. You can cut those divisions down. They merely represent interpretations that you can understand in your reality. In the most mundane of terms, some families are travelers, and some prefer to stay at home. But generally speaking, I have simply given you an outline which follows the characteristics of consciousness as it is embarked in physical form. I am not giving you these groups to set up divisions, but to help you understand that consciousness is diversified -- that usually each of you falls, because you want to, into a certain family. And there you acquire friends, alliances, and counterparts.
quotes.translation: 这些类别（疗愈、教学或其他）只是对意识家族的一般性描述。如果你愿意，也可以进一步细分或缩减这些分类。它们仅仅代表你能够在自身实相中理解的诠释。用最通俗的话来说，有些家族是旅者，有些则偏好安居。但大体上，我只是给出了一个遵循意识在物质形态中显现特征的纲要。我并非要借这些群体制造分裂，而是帮助你理解意识的多样性——通常你们每个人都会因自身意愿而归属某个特定家族，并在其中获得朋友、联盟与对应体。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (FOR SESSION 734)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: If the artist paints a doorway, all of the sensed perspectives within it open, and add further dimensions of reality. Since this is our analogy, we can stretch it as far as we like -- far further than any artist could stretch his canvas (leaning forward humorously). Therefore, there is no need to limit ourselves. The canvas itself can change size and shape as the artist works. The people in the artist's painting are not simple representations either -- to stare back at him with forever-fixed glassy eyes, or ostentatious smiles (again humorously), dressed in their best Sunday clothes. Instead, they can confront the artist and talk back. They can turn sideways in the painting and look at their companions, observe their environment, and even look out of the dimensions of the painting itself and question the artist.
quotes.translation: 若画家描绘一扇门，门内所有感知到的视角都会敞开，为实相增添更多维度。既然这是我们的比喻，我们大可尽情延伸——远超任何画家所能拉伸的画布（幽默地前倾）。因此，无需自我设限。画布本身可在画家创作时改变尺寸与形状。画中人物也绝非简单的再现——不会永远以呆滞的目光或浮夸的笑容（再次幽默地）回望画家，穿着最好的礼拜日服装。相反，他们能与画家对峙并反驳，能在画中侧身注视同伴，观察自身环境，甚至能跳出画作的维度质疑画家。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Alter your time orientation in other such exercises. This will automatically allow you to break away from too narrow a focus. It will to some extent break apart the rigid interlocking of your perception into reality as you have learned how to perceive it. Children can play so vividly that they might, for example, imagine themselves parched under a desert sun, though they are in the middle of the coolest air-conditioned living room. They are on the one hand completely involved in their activity, yet on the other hand they are quite aware of their "normal" environment. Yet the adult often fears that any such playful unofficial alteration of consciousness is dangerous, and becomes worried that the imagined situation will supersede the real one.
quotes.translation: 在其他类似练习中调整你的时间取向。这将自动让你摆脱过于狭隘的焦点，在一定程度上打破你习以为常的感知与实相之间僵硬的连锁。例如，儿童能如此生动地玩耍，即便身处最凉爽的空调客厅，他们仍可想象自己正被沙漠烈日炙烤。他们一方面完全沉浸于活动，另一方面却清醒知晓"正常"环境。然而成人常恐惧任何此类非正式的意识嬉戏性改变，担忧想象情境会取代真实情境。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: If you do not buy headache potions, your uncle or your neighbor may be out of business and not able to support his family, and therefore lack the means to buy your wares. You cannot disconnect one area of life from another. En masse, your private beliefs form your cultural reality. Your society is not a thing in itself apart from you, but the result of the individual beliefs of each person in it. There is no stratum of society that you do not in one way or another affect. Your religions stress sin. Your medical profession stresses disease. Your orderly sciences stress the chaotic and accidental theories of creation. Your psychologies stress men as victims of their backgrounds. Your most advanced thinkers emphasize man's rape of the planet, or focus upon the future disaster that will overtake the world, or see men once again as victims of the stars.
quotes.translation: 若你不购买头痛药水，你的叔父或邻居可能因此失业而无法养家，进而无力购买你的商品。你无法将生活的某个领域与其他领域割裂。集体而言，你们的私人信念构成了文化实相。社会并非独立于你而存在的实体，而是其中每个个体信念的集合。没有哪个社会阶层不受你以某种方式影响。你们的宗教强调罪孽，医疗行业强调疾病，有序的科学强调混沌偶然的创世理论，心理学强调人是背景的受害者，最先进的思想家强调人类对地球的掠夺，或聚焦于即将降临世界的未来灾难，或将人再次视为星辰的牺牲品。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Your private beliefs merge with those of others, and form your cultural reality. The distorted ideas of the medical profession or the scientists, or of any other group, are not thrust upon you, therefore. They are the result of your mass beliefs -- isolated in the form of separate disciplines. Medical men, for example, are often extremely unhealthy because they are so saddled with those specific health beliefs that their attention is concentrated in that area more than others not so involved. The idea of prevention is always based upon fear -- for you do not want to prevent something that is joyful. Often, therefore, preventative medicine causes what it hopes to avoid. Not only does the idea [of prevention] continually promote the entire system of fear, but specific steps taken to prevent a disease in a body not already stricken, again, often set up reactions that bring about side effects that would occur if the disease had in fact been suffered.
quotes.translation: 你们的私人信念与他人信念融合，形成文化实相。因此，医疗行业、科学家或其他群体的扭曲观念并非强加于你，而是你们集体信念的产物——以独立学科的形式被隔离。例如，医疗从业者常极度不健康，正因他们背负着特定的健康信念，其注意力比未涉足该领域者更集中于此。预防的概念始终基于恐惧——因为你不会想预防令人愉悦之事。因此，预防医学常引发它希望避免的后果。不仅（预防的）观念持续强化整个恐惧体系，而且针对未患病身体采取的特定预防措施，也常引发类似实际患病时才会出现的副作用。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

I want to use the material in connection with a passage I'm putting together for Volume 2 of "Unknown" Reality. [See the excerpts from Session 211, in Appendix 18 of that work.] However, Seth didn't answer my question.)
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Now give us a moment ... As creatures dwelling in time and space, your senses provide you with highly specific data, and with a cohesive-enough physical reality. Each person may react to the seasons in a very personalized manner, and yet you all share those natural events. They provide a framework for experience. It is up to the conscious mind to interpret sense events as clearly and concisely as possible. This allows for the necessary freedom of action for psychological and physical mobility. You are an imaginative species, and so the physical world is colored, charged, by your own imaginative projections, and powered by the great sweep of the emotions. But when you are confused or upset, it is an excellent idea to return your attention to the natural world as it appears at any given moment -- to sense its effect upon you as separate from your own projections.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……作为栖居于时空中的生物，你们的感官提供高度具体的数据，以及足够连贯的物理实相。每个人可能以高度个性化的方式回应季节，但你们共享这些自然事件。它们为经验提供框架。有意识心智需尽可能清晰简洁地解读感官事件，这为心理与身体的流动性保留了必要的行动自由。你们是富有想象力的物种，因此物质世界被你们自身的想象投射所着色、充能，并被情感的磅礴浪潮驱动。但当你困惑或不安时，将注意力回归到任一时刻呈现的自然世界——感受它对你产生的影响，将其与自身投射区分开来——这是绝佳的主意。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Our session is late this evening because Ruburt and Joseph watched the beginning of a (television) movie in which a young woman I will call Sarah appeared as an actress. Sarah wrote Ruburt a letter, telling him of the movie. Sarah has abilities, and she is banking on them, developing them in a practical way. She believes that she forms her own reality. She quenched doubts that she was not good enough to succeed, or that it was too difficult to get ahead in show business. The satisfaction of performance leads [her] to more expansive creativity, and to her natural sense of personal power. Through developing those abilities personally, she will contribute to the enjoyment of others. She is an idealist. She will try to bring a greater sense of values to the screen, for example, and she is willing to do the work necessary.
quotes.translation: 今晚我们的课迟了，因为鲁柏和约瑟看了一部（电视）电影的开头，片中一位我称为莎拉的年轻女子担任演员。莎拉写信给鲁柏，告诉他这部电影的事。莎拉拥有能力，她正依赖这些能力，并以务实的方式发展它们。她相信她形成自己的实相。她消除了自己不够好而无法成功、或在演艺界出头太难的疑虑。表演的满足感引领她走向更广阔的创造力，以及她自然的个人力量感。通过发展这些个人能力，她将为他人的愉悦做出贡献。她是个理想主义者。例如，她会努力将更大的价值感带到银幕上，并且她愿意做必要的工作。

quotes.text: You dreamed you spoke languages before their physical invention, of course. It was the nature of your dreams, and your dreams' creativity, that made you what you are, for otherwise you would have developed a mechanical-like language -- had you developed one at all -- that named designations, locations, and dealt with the most simple, objective reality: "I walked there. He walks there. The sun is hot." You would not have had that kind of bare statement of physical fact. You would not have had (pause) any way (pause) of conceiving of objects that did not already exist. You would not have had any way of imagining yourselves in novel situations. You would not have had any overall picture of the seasons, for dreaming educated the memory and lengthened man's attention span. It reinforced the lessons of daily life, and was highly important in man's progress.
quotes.translation: 你当然梦见过在语言被物理发明之前就说它们。正是你梦的本质，以及你梦的创造力，造就了你的现状，因为否则你会发展出一种机械式的语言——如果你真能发展出语言的话——那只会命名指示、地点，并处理最单纯、客观的实相：“我走到那里。他走到那里。太阳很热。”你不会有那种赤裸裸的物理事实陈述。你不会有（停顿）任何方式（停顿）去构想尚未存在的物体。你不会有任何方式想象自己处于新奇的情境。你不会有任何对季节的整体画面，因为梦教育了记忆，延长了人类的注意力跨度。它强化了日常生活的教训，在人类的进步中极为重要。

quotes.text: The reasoning mind, as you have used it thus far, roughly (underlined) since the birth of Christianity, has used--instead of used, confined--has confined its reasoning abilities to a very narrow spectrum of reality. It has seen the value of life largely only as that life conforms to its own standards. (Pause.) That is, the reasoning mind, as you have used it, considers that only reasoning creatures are capable of understanding life's values. Other forms of life have almost seemed beside the point, their value considered only insofar as they were of service to man. But man's life is obviously dependent upon the existence of life's other species, and with him those species share certain values. Life is sacred--all life--and again, all life seeks value fulfillment, not simply physical survival.
quotes.translation: 推理心智，如你迄今所使用的那样，大致上（划线）自基督教诞生以来，它已经使用——不如说限制——将它的推理能力限制在实相的一个非常狭窄的光谱上。它主要只在生命符合其自身标准时，才看到生命的价值。（停顿。）也就是说，推理心智，如你所使用的那样，认为只有推理的生物才能理解生命的价值。其他形式的生命几乎显得无关紧要，它们的价值只在于它们对人类有用时才被考虑。但人类的生命显然依赖于其他物种生命的存在，而与他一起，这些物种共享某些价值。生命是神圣的——所有生命——而且，再说一次，所有生命寻求价值完成，而不仅仅是物理生存。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Important misunderstandings involving time have been in a large measure responsible for many of Ruburt's difficulties, and also of your own, though they have been of a lesser nature. All of this involves relating to reality in a more natural, and therefore magical, fashion. There is certainly a kind of natural physical time in your experience, and in the experience of any creature. It involves the rhythm of the seasons -- the days and nights and tides and so forth. In the light of that kind of physical time, which is involved within earthly biology, there is no (pause) basic cultural time. That is, to this natural rhythm you have culturally added the idea of clocks, moments and hours and so forth, which you have transposed over nature's rhythms.
quotes.translation: 涉及时间的重要误解在很大程度上是鲁柏许多困难的根源，也是你自己的困难，尽管程度较轻。这一切都涉及以更自然、因而更神奇的方式与实相相连。在你的经验中，以及在任何生物的经验中，当然有一种自然的物理时间。它涉及季节的节奏——日夜、潮汐等等。在这种涉及地球生物学的物理时间的光照下，没有（停顿）基本的文化时间。也就是说，在这种自然节奏之上，你在文化上添加了时钟、时刻和小时等概念，你将这些概念叠加在自然的节奏之上。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: To study the human body from only the physical standpoint, or to consider it as exclusively a physical phenomenon, is to severely limit your perception of it, and of reality as a whole. To study psychology exclusively in terms of the brain's effect upon the physical body is likewise hampering and limiting, for the brain is merely that portion of, that very small portion, of the mind which is apparent within matter. As such within your physical field the brain is subject to the laws of your field. The mind, having its existence within the scope of the physical field but independent of it, is a much more fruitful subject for study; and not of course study through means of physical instruments or of operations performed. In any case the mind cannot be found by such procedures.
quotes.translation: 仅从物理角度研究人体，或将其视为纯粹的物理现象，会严重限制你对它以及整体实相的看法。仅从大脑对物理身体的影响角度研究心理学，同样是有阻碍和限制的，因为大脑仅仅是心灵在物质中显现的那一部分，非常小的一部分。因此，在你的物理场中，大脑受制于你场的法则。心灵，其存在在物理场的范围内却独立于它，是一个更富有成果的研究主题；当然不是通过物理仪器或手术操作来研究。无论如何，心灵无法通过这类程序找到。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The thought, as an electrical reality, is not subject to the physical laws that bind the physical body. The durability of the thought as an electrical act is determined by its initial electric charge. This electric charge can propel the thought, as an electrical action, through the apparent dimensions of your physical time in such a manner that it can exist simultaneously within your past and your present. Because a thought, as electric action, may exist both within your past and your present, this is not to say that it becomes two electric actions, one existing in the past and one existing in the future. Rather the one electric action or thought is simultaneously projected, through a peculiarity of its axis, so that it appears within your field, not in two places at once, but in two times at once.
quotes.translation: 思想，作为一种电性实相，不受束缚物理身体的物理法则所支配。思想作为电性行为的持久性，由其初始电荷决定。这个电荷可以将思想作为电性行为，推进穿过你物理时间的明显维度，以这样一种方式，它可以在你的过去和现在同时存在。因为一个思想，作为电性行为，可能同时存在于你的过去和现在，这并不意味着它变成两个电性行为，一个存在于过去，一个存在于未来。相反，这个单一的电性行为或思想，通过其轴心的一个特性被同时投射，以至于它出现在你的场中，不是同时在两个地方，而是同时在两个时间。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: And yet a portion of you knows the answer, and a portion of you knows who you are, and the memories of your previous lives are not in your genes or in your chromosomes, but in the psychic reality that forms the genes and the chromosomes. For the identity is a different identity than the name that you bear. The physical mechanism has chromosomes indeed, but the physical chromosomes have a psychic counterpart, and the psychic counterpart is the original; and within you is the codified information containing all your past lives and all your knowledge, and it is hidden so deep within you that the subconscious as you know it does not realize the truth, for the subconscious as you know it is, indeed, a very shallow affair containing only those hidden memories from this life.
quotes.translation: 然而，你们有一部分知道答案，有一部分知道你们是谁，你们前世的记忆不在基因或染色体中，而在形成基因与染色体的心灵实相里。因为身份认同不同于你们所背负的名字。肉体机制确实有染色体，但肉体染色体有其心灵对应物，而心灵对应物才是本源；在你们之内，编码信息包含了你们所有前世和所有知识，它深藏于你们之内，以至于你们所知的潜意识并未意识到这个真相，因为你们所知的潜意识实际上非常浅薄，只包含此生隐藏的记忆。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Now, you see what you are willing to see, and it is stupidity to consider suggestion as the result or the cause of what you see. It is stupidity in class to worry that suggestion would cause a given result... for suggestion causes whatever you see. You form your physical reality through suggestion and expectation. You experience what you expect to experience at a subconscious and a conscious level. And therefore as Ruburt is very careful that suggestion is not involved, so he has also had you be overly cautious, and there have been many opportunities in class that you have missed for this reason... and these are the bets that I have spoken about earlier this evening. You have the ability to see more than you saw, and you have the ability. You enjoyed your passivity (to CW) to the point of a joyous giving-up; and instead, you see, there is a point within passivity where you are passively alert. And you went beyond the point and lost what you might have seen. As our friend here went beyond the point (referring to TM), looked at the portraits and consciously... for you did not make an original intuitive judgment... but consciously looked at those portraits in terms of nationality, age, and all the requirements that you thought of.
quotes.translation: 现在，你们只看到你们愿意看到的，将暗示视为所见结果或原因是愚蠢的。在课堂上担心暗示会导致特定结果是愚蠢的……因为暗示导致你们所见的一切。你们通过暗示和期望形成你们的物质实相。你们在潜意识和意识层面体验你们期望体验的。因此，正如鲁柏非常小心不让暗示介入，他也让你们过于谨慎，为此你们错过了课堂上许多机会……这就是我今晚早些时候提到的赌注。你们有能力看到比你们所见更多的东西，你们有这个能力。你们（对CW）享受被动到了愉快放弃的地步；然而，你看，被动中有一个点，在那里你是被动警觉的。你越过了那个点，失去了你可能看到的东西。就像我们这位朋友（指TM）越过了那个点，看着那些肖像，有意识地……因为你没有做出最初的直觉判断……而是有意识地根据国籍、年龄以及你想到的所有要求来看那些肖像。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: However, efforts were being made then to map inner reality in ways that have not been attempted since. It is true that in the dream state and in some other levels of existence close to your own, there is strong individual play in the creation of images, and a magnificent use of symbolism, but all of this takes place, again, in an "objective" definite environment, an environment whose characteristics make such phenomena possible -- a field of activity, then, with its own rules. Now the Speakers are familiar with those rules, and often serve as guides. They have at times worked within organizations as in Egypt, where they worked through the temples and became involved with the power structures. As a rule, however, they are far more solitary.
quotes.translation: 然而，当时正努力以此后未曾尝试过的方式绘制内在实相的地图。确实，在梦境状态和某些接近你们自己的存在层次中，图像创造中有强烈的个体游戏，以及象征主义的壮丽运用，但这一切再次发生在一个“客观”确定的环境中，一个其特性使此类现象成为可能的环境——一个活动场域，有其自身的规则。现在，发言者熟悉这些规则，并常担任向导。他们有时在组织内工作，如在埃及，他们通过寺庙运作并卷入权力结构。但通常，他们远更孤独。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: As you read this book, you are also immersed in such intimate physical experiences. Do not consider them as separated from the greater reality of your being, but as a part of it. You do not exist outside of your psyche's being, but within it. Some of you may have just put children to bed as you read these lines. Some of you may be sitting at a table. Some of you may have just gone to the bathroom. These mundane activities may seem quite divorced from what I am telling you, yet in each simple gesture, and in the most necessary of physical acts, there is the great magical unknowing elegance in which you reside -- and in the most ordinary of your motions, there are clues and hints as to the nature of the psyche and its human expression.
quotes.translation: 当你阅读这本书时，你也沉浸在这种亲密的肉体体验中。不要将它们视为与你存在的更大实相分离，而是视为它的一部分。你并非存在于你心灵的存在之外，而是在它之内。你们中有些人可能在读这些文字时刚把孩子哄上床。有些人可能正坐在桌旁。有些人可能刚去过洗手间。这些平凡的活动似乎与我告诉你的内容相去甚远，然而在每个简单的手势中，在最必要的肉体行为中，都有你栖居其中的伟大神奇的无知优雅——在你最普通的动作中，有关于心灵本质及其人类表达的线索和暗示。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Jung's collective unconscious was an attempt to give your world its psychological roots, but Jung1 could not perceive the clarity, organization, and deeper context in which that collective unconscious has its own existence. Reality as Framework 2 is organized in a different fashion than it is in the Framework 1 world, and the processes of reasoning are far quicker. In Framework 1 the reasoning processes work largely by deduction, and they must constantly check their own results against the seemingly concrete experience of physical events. The reasoning of the inner ego is involved with the creative invention of those experiences. It is involved with events in a context of a different kind, for it deals intimately with probabilities.
quotes.translation: 荣格的集体潜意识试图为你们的世界提供其心理根源，但荣格无法看清那个集体潜意识自身存在其中的清晰度、组织性和更深层背景。作为框架2的实相以不同于框架1世界的方式组织，推理过程远为迅速。在框架1中，推理过程主要通过演绎运作，它们必须不断对照肉体事件的看似具体经验来检查自身结果。内在自我的推理涉及对这些经验的创造性发明。它涉及不同背景中的事件，因为它与概率密切打交道。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json
