quotes.text: It is a portion that has been physical. It is a portion that can understand your (TM) being overwhelmed with work; it is a portion that can understand your being full with child (SW); it is a portion that can understand the times when you wanted to leave physical reality (CW); it is a portion that can understand the part of you (SB) that wanted to be a star; it is a portion that can understand the part of you that wanted to conquer, and is afraid to conquer (WLJ); it is a portion that can understand the guilt you (RC) feel for no reason; it is a portion that can understand the aspirations that you (LD) were unable to fulfill... and if you had fulfilled them, you would not be looking into the subjects that you are looking into now.
quotes.translation: 它是曾为肉体的一部分。它是能理解你（TM）被工作压垮的一部分；它是能理解你（SW）身怀六甲的一部分；它是能理解你（CW）曾想离开肉体实相的时刻的一部分；它是能理解你（SB）想成为明星的那部分的一部分；它是能理解你（WLJ）想征服又害怕征服的那部分的一部分；它是能理解你（RC）无缘无故感到内疚的那部分的一部分；它是能理解你（LD）未能实现的抱负的一部分……如果你实现了它们，你就不会研究你现在研究的课题了。

quotes.text: It is no coincidence that Ruburt does not possess a scientific vocabulary, though he does possess a scientific as well as intuitive mind. The very attempt to describe reality in scientific terms, as they are currently understood, pays, my dear friend, undue tribute to a vocabulary that automatically scales down greater concepts to fit its rigors. In other words, such attempts further compound the problem of considering a seemingly objective universe, and describing it in an objective fashion.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏虽具备科学思维与直觉，却未掌握科学术语，这绝非偶然。亲爱的朋友，以当前理解的科学术语描述实相，无异于对词汇体系过度推崇——这套体系会自动将更宏大的概念压缩至符合其严谨框架。换言之，这类尝试只会加剧问题：人们既将宇宙视为客观存在，又试图以客观方式描述它。

quotes.text: You think in terms of linear time, and the best you can do to imagine your deeper reality is to consider reincarnation in time. It is a matter of focus. You usually identify with the outside of yourself, and with the outside of the world. You do not, for example, usually identify with the inside of your body, with its organs, much less its cells or atoms -- yet in that direction lies a certain kind of infinity (intently).
quotes.translation: 你以线性时间思考，所能想象最深层的实相，不过是时间中的转世轮回。这关乎焦点问题。你通常认同于自身外在及世界表象，例如鲜少认同身体内部——器官、细胞乃至原子——然而那个方向正蕴含着某种无限性（专注地）。

quotes.text: Many concepts, huge advancements and practical inventions, simply wait in abeyance in the world of dreams until some man accepts them as possibilities within his frame of reality. Why man so often mistrusts the dream reality would escape me, if I did not know the nature of the ego.

quotes.text: Now. There are indeed portions of dream reality that you yourself may not have constructed, but that are constructed by others. You recall that in physical reality you can only perceive your own constructions, as a rule. Now, my clear friends, this does not apply to dream reality.
quotes.translation: 现在。梦境实相中确有你未构建的部分，它们由他人构建。须知在物质实相中，你通常只能感知自己的构建。但亲爱的朋友们，这并不适用于梦境实相。

quotes.text: He is not responsible for other people's realities, but he is responsible for his own. Give us a moment ... .(Pause, eyes closed). The ill woman's reality does not threaten his own in any way. The situation, however, shows that he sometimes still thinks he should be able to solve all problems, and to know all the reasons for any given sorrow or tragedy. The intellect cannot handle that kind of information at that level.
quotes.translation: 他无需为他人实相负责，但需为自己的实相负责。请稍候……（停顿，闭目）。那位病妇的实相丝毫不威胁他的实相。然而此情境表明，他仍有时认为自己应能解决所有问题，并洞悉任何悲伤或悲剧的全部缘由。理智无法处理那个层面的信息。

quotes.text: A person may choose a great talent instead, through which he or she will perceive reality and concentrate all experience. This will serve as a formidable focus, yet by its nature it may often preclude other experiences that many individuals find quite normal. Some artists with great ability may shut out intellectual maturity, utilizing native emotional qualities to such an extent and with such intensity that the mental reasoning faculties are largely shunted aside. (Pause.) Without rational illumination, the emotional elements may be so unwieldy that the artist, for all of his spontaneous expression, cannot relate in any kind of permanent situation of an intimate nature. For reason and emotion are natural counterparts.
quotes.translation: 有人可能选择卓越天赋，借此感知实相并凝聚所有体验。这将成为强大的焦点，但其本质常会排斥许多常人视为正常的体验。某些天赋异禀的艺术家可能屏蔽心智成熟，过度且强烈地运用先天情感特质，以致理性思辨能力被大幅搁置。（停顿。）缺乏理性之光的照耀，情感元素可能变得难以驾驭——纵使艺术家能自发表达，却无法维系任何持久的亲密关系。因理性与情感本是天然的双生子。

quotes.text: Some dead friends and relatives do visit you, projecting from their own level of reality into yours, but you cannot as a rule perceive their forms. They are not more ghostly, or "dead," however, than you are when you project into their reality -- as you do, from the sleep state.
quotes.translation: 一些已故的亲友确实会拜访你，从他们自己的实相层面投射进入你的层面，但通常你无法感知他们的形态。他们并不比你更"幽灵"或"死亡"——正如你从睡眠状态投射进入他们的实相时那样。

quotes.text: The church's view of reality was the accepted one. I cannot stress too thoroughly the fact that the beliefs of those times structured individual human living, so that the most private events of personal lives were interpreted to mean thus and so, as were of course the events of nations, plants, and animals. The world's view was a religious one, specified by the church, and its word was truth and fact at the same time.
quotes.translation: 教会的实相观曾是公认的真理。我无论如何强调都不为过：那个时代的信念塑造了人类的个体生活，以至于个人生命中最私密的事件都被解释为某种特定含义，国家、植物和动物的命运自然也是如此。当时的世界观是宗教性的，由教会具体规定，其话语同时兼具真理与事实的双重身份。

quotes.text: They appear to lack the apparent physical durability of, say, a table or a chair, the table and chair being examples of physical realities. And yet their effect is much more durable, and they impress and to some extent manipulate physical realities, in their strong and sometimes explosive emergence into your universe. Such realities do not bow to the artificial time measurements which so often limit you in daily life.
quotes.translation: 它们似乎缺乏桌子或椅子那种明显的物理持久性——桌子和椅子正是物质实相的范例。然而它们的影响却持久得多，当它们以强烈甚至爆炸性的方式进入你的宇宙时，会深刻影响并在某种程度上操控物质实相。这类实相不会屈服于日常生活中常限制你的人为时间测量标准。

quotes.translation: 《个人实相的本质：解决日常问题与丰富已知生活的具体实用技巧》。在这本经久不衰的畅销书中，赛斯挑战了我们对实相本质的假设，并强调个体进行有意识行动的能力。

quotes.text: Now the physical organism as such is capable of that kind of reality system. It is not better or worse than your own. It is simply alternate behavior, biologically and spiritually possible. No complicated physical transportation systems were set up. In the physical state, in what you would call the waking state, these individuals slept. To you, comparatively speaking, their waking activities would seem dreamlike, and yet they behaved with great natural physical grace, allowing the body to function to capacity. They did not saddle it with negative beliefs of disease or limitation. Such bodies did not age to the extent now, that yours do, and enjoyed the greatest ease and sense of belonging with the environment.
quotes.translation: 物理有机体本身能够适应那种实相系统。它并不比你的实相系统更好或更差，只是另一种在生物学和灵性上皆可行的替代行为模式。当时并未建立复杂的物理运输系统。在你们所谓的清醒状态下，这些个体处于睡眠中。相对而言，他们的清醒活动对你们来说会显得如梦似幻，然而他们以极其自然的身体优雅行事，让身体发挥最大潜能。他们不会用疾病或限制的负面信念来束缚身体。这样的身体不会像你们现在那样衰老，并且与环境保持着最轻松自在的归属感。

quotes.text: (With much animation:) As an example, it appears to you that animals do not reflect upon their own reality. Certainly it seems that a cell has no "objective" knowledge of its own being, colon: as if it is without knowing what it is, or without appreciation of its own isness. You are quite wrong in such deductions. Nor are there necessarily gradations in which one kind of consciousness progresses in rigid terms from a lower to a higher state. Any cell has practical use of precognitive abilities,4 for example, that quite escape you, yet many of you assign such abilities to "higher" souls. Each kind of life has its own qualities that cannot be compared with those of others, and that often cannot be communicated.
quotes.translation: （充满活力地）举例来说，你们认为动物不会反思自身的实相。当然，细胞似乎对其自身存在没有"客观"认知——仿佛它既不知自己为何物，也不懂得欣赏自身的存在。你们这样的推论大错特错。意识也未必必须严格遵循从低级到高级的阶梯式进化。例如，任何细胞都具备预知能力的实际运用，这完全超出你们的理解，而你们许多人却将这种能力归于"高等"灵魂。每种生命形态都有其无法与其他生命比较的特质，这些特质往往无法被传达。

quotes.text: The more prosaic elements of the personality then take whatever measures seem necessary at the time, while new orientation is tried out. These methods may seem to lead to great distortions, particularly in contrast with the sensed possibilities of development. In one way or another, however, they still provide a framework in which the personality feels itself free to pursue its goals. The built-in impetuses provide clue points. When the new sensed reality is strong enough to provide not only greater comprehensions but also to construct a new framework, then the old framework is seen as limiting, and discarded.
quotes.translation: 人格中较为平淡的部分随后会采取当时看来必要的任何措施，同时尝试新的定位。这些方法似乎会导致巨大的扭曲，尤其与感知到的发展可能性相比时更是如此。然而，它们无论如何仍提供了一个框架，让人格在其中感到自由地追求其目标。内在的冲力提供了线索点。当新感知到的实相强大到不仅提供更深刻的理解，还能构建一个新框架时，旧的框架便被视为限制而被抛弃。

quotes.text: He projected a meaninglessness upon reality, and then fell prey to it. Here the senses themselves can help revive him. (Long pause.) He seems now to be choosing between identities, but there is one waiting for him, and it is a probable ego that he did not adopt as a child. He has been plunged back into a physical universe that he would not accept even as a valid mirage. This time he will be able to find himself within it. (Long pause.) In his case he must accept himself as a human being before he can hope to discover the inner self. The ego got no help from him, and it could not carry on alone. There was a definite splitting of personality elements, and a complete abandonment by the inner self of the ego.
quotes.translation: 他将无意义投射到实相上，然后成为其牺牲品。在这里，感官本身可以帮助他复苏。（长停顿。）他现在似乎在选择身份之间徘徊，但有一个身份在等待他，那是他童年时未采纳的一个可能自我。他被抛回一个他甚至不愿接受为有效幻象的物质宇宙。这次他将能够在这个宇宙中找到自己。（长停顿。）就他而言，他必须先接受自己作为人类，才能希望发现内在自我。自我没有得到他的帮助，无法独自支撑。人格元素出现了明确的分裂，内在自我完全抛弃了自我。

quotes.text: Since these are far more lively than ordinary shadows, and are definitely more colorful, they may be more difficult to distinguish at first. You must remember that you are wandering through a mental or psychic landscape. You can stand before the shadow of a friend in the afternoon, in waking reality, and snap your fingers all you want to, but your friend's shadow will not move one whit. It will certainly not disappear because you tell it to. In the dream world, however, any hallucination will vanish immediately as soon as you recognize it as such, and tell it to go away. It was cast originally by your own thought or feeling, and when you withdraw that source, then its "shadow" is automatically gone.
quotes.translation: 由于这些比普通阴影生动得多，且绝对色彩更丰富，起初可能更难区分。你必须记住，你正在穿越一个心智或心灵的景观。在清醒实相中，你可以在午后站在朋友的影子前，随意打响指，但朋友的影子不会移动分毫。它当然不会因为你命令它消失就消失。然而，在梦境世界中，任何幻觉一旦你认出它并命令它离开，就会立即消失。它最初是由你自己的思想或情感投射的，当你撤回那个源头时，它的"影子"便自动消失了。

This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Consciousness's experiences following such therapy may be those of elation, but it feels that any of its adventures rest on issues that it cannot understand, and its capacity to deal with physical reality is less secure than before. This is not the case with natural inner treatments that are carried on in individual behavior. These are the ones that should be understood and encouraged, say, by the psychologists.
quotes.translation: 经过此类治疗后的意识体验可能是兴高采烈的，但它觉得自己的任何冒险都建立在无法理解的问题上，其处理物质实相的能力不如以前稳固。在个体行为中进行的自然内在治疗则并非如此。这些才是应该被理解并鼓励的，比如说，由心理学家来做。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Frameworks 1 and 2 obviously represent not only different kinds of reality in normal terms, but two different kinds of consciousness. To make this discussion as simple as possible for now, at least, think of these two frameworks or states of consciousness as being connected by "undifferentiated areas" in which sleep, dreaming, and certain trance states have their activity. Those undifferentiated areas are involved in the constant translation of one kind of consciousness into the other, and with energy transferences. You constantly process those data that come to you in your private life, and that information includes bulletins from all over the world, through your news broadcasts and so forth.
quotes.translation: 架构一与架构二显然不仅代表常态下不同种类的实相，也代表两种不同的意识。为了尽可能简化当前讨论，至少请将这两种架构或意识状态视为由"未分化区域"相连，睡眠、做梦及某些出神状态的活动便在其中进行。这些未分化区域参与着一种意识向另一种意识的持续转化，以及能量转换。你不断处理着私人生活中接收到的数据，这些信息包括通过新闻广播等渠道来自世界各地的简报。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 823, FEBRUARY 27, 1978
9:43 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: We have gone into this before, and I have no doubt that we will on endless occasions. And if I succeed in convincing you of my reality as a personality, I will have done extremely well ... Ruburt's subconscious has enough camouflage pattern to enable me to make contact, but not so much as to distort me out of all recognition. I am not his subconscious, though I speak through it. It is the atmosphere through which I can come to you, as the air is the atmosphere through which the bird flies. A certain reassembly of myself is necessary when I enter your plane, and this is done partially by myself, and by the combined subconscious efforts of you, Joseph, and Ruburt. Will this satisfy you for now?
quotes.translation: 我们之前探讨过这点，而且我确信我们还会无数次回到这个话题。若我能成功让你相信作为人格的我的实相，那将是我极大的成就……鲁柏的潜意识拥有足够的伪装模式让我得以接触，但又不至于扭曲到面目全非。我并非他的潜意识，尽管我通过它说话。它是我得以抵达你处的氛围，如同空气是鸟儿飞翔的介质。当我进入你们的层面时，必须对自身进行某种重组，这部分由我完成，部分由你约瑟夫和鲁柏的潜意识共同协作。这样解释暂时能让你满意吗？
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (However, from Session 14 [for January 8, 1964], there's other material that can be given here, as well as some that makes an interesting note at the end of this appendix.4 First, from my own note at 11:05: "Jane said that Seth was quite pleased with the new voice, and that she now knows what he's thinking sometimes, even though he doesn't relay it to or through her as part of a message."

This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: In your terms, probabilities are extensions and variations on the growth principle that is quite obvious in your daily reality. Such growth is a natural manifestation flourishing within your particular area of actuality, observable to your senses. Again, other entirely natural manifestations of that principle exist. Some can only be glimpsed in distorted form because of other "natural" conditions that you cannot perceive. Probabilities involve you with a rich psychological growth and development, present but not observable in your "home ground." Any kind of existence happens within the context of nature, and nature includes the soul. Your definition of nature has simply been too limiting.
quotes.translation: 用你们的术语来说，概率是成长原则的延伸与变奏，这在你们的日常实相中显而易见。这种成长是自然显化，在你们特定的现实领域蓬勃绽放，可为感官所察知。再次强调，该原则还存在其他完全自然的显化形式。有些只能以扭曲形态被瞥见，因为存在你们无法感知的其他"自然"条件。概率让你们参与丰富的心理成长与发展，这存在于你们的"主场"却不可见。任何存在都发生在自然的脉络中，而自然包含灵魂。你们对自然的定义实在太过局限。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: What often happens is that your conception of reality is so limited that you take fright whenever you perceive any experience that does not fit into your conception. Now I am not speaking merely of abilities loosely called "extrasensory perception." These experiences seem extraordinary to you only because you have for so long denied the existence of any perception that did not come through the physical senses.
quotes.translation: 常见的情况是：你们对实相的概念如此局限，以至于每当感知到任何不符合此概念的经验时便惊慌失措。我指的不只是那些被笼统称为"超感官知觉"的能力。这些经验对你们而言显得非凡，仅仅是因为你们长久以来否认任何非物理感官传来的感知存在。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Your present methods will simply bring you pat, manufactured results and answers. They will satisfy neither the intellect nor the soul. Since your universe springs from an inner one, and since that inner one pervades each nook and cranny of your own existence, you must look where you have not before -- into the reality of your own minds and emotions. You must look to the natural universe that you know. You must look with your intuitions and creative instincts at the creatures about you, seeing them not as other species with certain habits, not as inferior properties of the earth, to be dissected, but as living examples of the nature of the universe, in constant being and transformation.
quotes.translation: 你们当前的方法只会带来刻板造作的结果与答案，既无法满足理智也无法满足灵魂。既然你们的宇宙源自内在宇宙，而内在宇宙又渗透你们存在的每个角落，你们必须看向从未注视过的地方——进入自己心智与情感的实相。你们必须审视所知的自然宇宙。你们必须用直觉与创造本能观察周遭生物，不再视其为具有特定习性的异类物种，或可供解剖的地球低级财产，而是视其为宇宙本性的鲜活范例，处于持续存在与转化之中。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Alternate focus is merely a state in which you turn your consciousness in other than its habitual direction, in order to perceive quite legitimate realities that exist simultaneously with your own. You must alter your perception to perceive any reality that is not geared practically toward material form. This is something like looking out of the corner of your eye or mind, rather than straight ahead. (Pause.)
quotes.translation: 交替焦点不过是一种状态，你将意识转向非惯常方向，以感知与自身实相同存的合理实相。要感知任何未实际导向物质形态的实相，你必须改变感知方式。这有点像用眼角或心灵余光而非直视前方。（停顿）
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The past exists as a series of electromagnetic connections, held in the physical brain on the one hand, but it also consists of the same sort of realities retained in the nonphysical mind. These electromagnetic connections can be changed. The present exists as a series of electromagnetic connections in both the brain and the mind, and this is the only reality which you are justified in giving to your present.
quotes.translation: 过去作为一系列电磁连接而存在，一方面储存在物质大脑中，但也包含非物质的意识所保留的同类实相。这些电磁连接可以被改变。现在作为大脑和意识中一系列电磁连接而存在，这是你唯一有理由赋予当下的实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: If an inhabitant from another reality outside of your own physical system entirely were to visit it, and if "his" intelligence was roughly of the same degree as your own, he would still have to learn to focus his consciousness in the same way that you do, more or less, in order to perceive your world. He would have to alter his native focus and turn it in a direction that was foreign to him. In this way he could "pick up your station."1 There would be distortions, because even though he managed such manipulations he might not have the same kind of native physical structure as your own, of course, through which to receive and interpret those data his altered consciousness perceived.
quotes.translation: 如果来自你们物质系统之外的另一个实相中的居民造访它，并且"他"的智力大致与你们相当，他仍然需要学会以与你们大致相同的方式聚焦意识，才能感知你们的世界。他必须改变他本来的聚焦方式，转向一个对他而言陌生的方向。这样他才能"接收到你们的频道"。其中会有扭曲，因为即使他成功进行了这样的操作，他可能没有与你们相同的天然物质结构来接收和解读他改变后的意识所感知的数据。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You appear in astral form in realities that are comparatively more advanced than your own. You are usually recognized because of your disorientation. You do not know how to manipulate. (Humorously): You do not know the customs. But whether you have a physical form or not, if you have emotions or feelings, these will take form. They have a reality. If you think strongly of an object, somewhere it will appear.
quotes.translation: 你们以星光体的形态出现在相对更先进的实相中。你们通常因迷失方向而被认出。你们不知道如何操控。（幽默地）：你们不懂当地习俗。但无论你们是否有物质形态，只要你们有情绪或感受，这些都会具象化。它们具有实相。如果你强烈地想着某个物体，它就会在某个地方显现。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You form your history. You form your reality, and so no one is thrust into a position which first was not accepted as a challenge. So you work out your problems and challenges in whatever way you choose, historically. In your terms, again, you and the Roman are connected; and the Arab and the American; and the African and the Chinese; and so are your identities intermixed with others who may seem to be strangers, but others who speak with your own voice -- others who communicate with you in their dreams as you communicate with them. You have comrades, and you come to this earth at a given time and place of your choice, and so do you reap and form the great challenges of your age.
quotes.translation: 你们形成自己的历史。你们形成自己的实相，因此没有人会被推入一个最初未被接受为挑战的位置。所以你们以自己选择的方式，在历史中解决你们的问题和挑战。用你们的话说，你们与罗马人相连；阿拉伯人与美国人相连；非洲人与中国人相连；你们的身份同样与其他看似陌生的人交织——那些用你们自己的声音说话的人——那些在梦中与你们交流的人，正如你们与他们交流一样。你们有同志，你们在自己选择的时间和地点来到这个地球，你们同样收获并形成这个时代的伟大挑战。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (FOR SESSION 724)

quotes.text: ([Tom:] "He talked some more about physical matter... an elaboration on the things we've talked about during the evening. The reality on another plane or in another dimension is just as physical as what we experience as physical here... just as real, seems physical to them. But the freedoms are greater, so that when we think of something on another plane, it happens instantaneously. Whereas here, there is a time lag between the time we think of something and it results in a created action or object. In the time lag on this plane, there are other thoughts, projections on the same idea or object; Seth declined to go into their effects on the final idea or object until a later time.
quotes.translation: （汤姆："他进一步谈论了物质……是对我们晚间讨论内容的延伸。另一个层面或维度的实相与我们在此体验的物质实相同样物质……同样真实，对他们而言显得物质。但自由度更大，所以当我们在另一个层面想到某事时，它会瞬间发生。而在此处，从我们想到某事到它成为创造性的行动或物体之间存在时间差。在这个层面的时间差中，会有其他想法、对同一想法或物体的投射；赛斯拒绝在稍后时间之前讨论它们对最终想法或物体的影响。"）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Our ego must have its feet upon the solid earth, it is out of its element, naked and in an unfamiliar environment, outside of the normal characteristics of physical existence. Its basic distrust of dream experience is necessary for the overall balance of the personality. Physical reality is a rock to which the ego must cling, and from which it achieves its power, energy, position, and reason for existence.
quotes.translation: 我们的自我必须立足于坚实的土地，它脱离了自身元素，赤裸地处于陌生环境中，超出物质存在的正常特性。它对梦境体验的基本不信任对于人格的整体平衡是必要的。物质实相是自我必须紧抓的岩石，自我从中获得力量、能量、地位和存在的理由。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The emotions and the imagination, however, give you your closest contact with other portions of your own reality. They also liberate your intellect so that its powers are not limited by concepts it has been taught are true. Instead, such concepts are relatively true -- operationally true. For example, the physical laws that you are familiar with operate where you are. They are true, relatively speaking. In those terms you are one person physically objectified, staring upward in the scene just mentioned at an objectified sky. You weigh so many pounds, tilt your head at such-and-such an angle to peer upward at the skyscape, and physically speaking, you can be categorized.
quotes.translation: 然而，情感与想象力让你得以最亲密地接触自身实相的其他部分。它们也解放了你的智力，使其力量不受那些被教导为"真理"的概念所局限。相反，这些概念只是相对真实——在操作层面真实。例如，你所熟悉的物理定律在你所在之处运作。它们相对而言是真实的。就那些层面而言，你是客观化的物质个体，在上述场景中仰望客观化的天空。你体重若干磅，以特定角度仰头凝视天景，从物质层面来说，你是可以被归类定义的。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This does not mean that they are not advanced enough to understand the reality of your time. They do not share the illusion. Almost all animals, plants, birds, insects, rocks and trees perceive according to intensities. (Long pause.)The intensity of an experience is their present; but in many ways that I will not explain to you at this point, their present is of wider duration than yours. This does not necessarily mean that they perceive more of the past and future within their present than you do, for they do not in those (underlined) terms. But in terms of quality and value fulfillment there is greater duration and the "moment," in quotes, is more intensely perceived.
quotes.translation: 这并不意味着它们不够先进，无法理解你们时代的实相。它们并不共享这种幻相。几乎所有动物、植物、鸟类、昆虫、岩石和树木都根据强度来感知。（长停顿）经验的强度就是它们的当下；但此刻我不会向你们解释的许多方面中，它们的当下比你们的当下具有更长的持续期。这并不一定意味着它们比你们在当下感知到更多过去与未来，因为就那些（强调的）层面而言它们并不如此。但就品质与价值完成而言，存在着更长的持续期，而"当下"（引号内）被更强烈地感知。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The gods attain, of course, a psychic reality. I am not saying therefore that they are not real, but I am to some extent defining the nature of their reality. It is to some extent true to say: "Be careful of the gods you choose, for you will reinforce each other."
quotes.translation: 诸神当然达到了心灵实相。因此我并非说它们不真实，而是在某种程度上界定其实相的本质。某种程度上可以说："小心你选择的神祇，因为你们会相互强化。"
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You may be convinced that human nature is evil, or that no one is safe from another's aggression, or that people are motivated mainly by greed. Such beliefs attract their own reality. If you have anything worth losing, you are then automatically convinced that someone else will take it from you, or try their hardest to do so. In your own way you send out messages to just such a person. On basic levels your convictions will be quite similar, but one will see himself as the victim and one as the aggressor -- that is, each of you will react differently to the same set of beliefs. However the two of you are necessary if a crime of that nature is, or is to be, committed.
quotes.translation: 你或许深信人性本恶，或无人能免于他人的侵犯，或人们主要受贪婪驱使。这类信念会吸引其自身的实相。若你有任何值得失去之物，你便会自动确信他人会将其夺走，或竭力如此。你会以自己的方式向这样的人发出讯息。在基本层面上，你们的信念会相当相似，但一人视自己为受害者，另一人视自己为侵犯者——即你们每个人会对同一组信念做出不同反应。然而，若此类性质的罪行正在或将要发生，你们两者都是必要的。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Man thinks as naturally as the birds fly. He looks at physical reality for the rest of physical reality: He is earth coming alive to view itself through conscious eyes--but that consciousness is graced to be because it is so intimately a part of earth's framework.
quotes.translation: 人类思考如同鸟儿飞翔般自然。他透过物质实相观看其余物质实相：他是地球通过有意识的眼睛苏醒过来观看自身——但这份意识因与地球框架如此亲密相连而得以存在。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: While I am doing that I am also trying to introduce you, intuitively at least, to a larger framework, in which events straddle the reality that you know. Nevertheless, we will begin with issues in which it is very possible that contradictions may seem to occur, since your own definitions of an event are so simple that they ignore larger ramifications--ramifications that would reconcile any seeming contradictions in an overall greater unity of structure and action. Your imaginations will be of high value here, for they can often perceive unities that are not evident to the intellect--which you have trained to deal specifically with the evidence of the here and now.
quotes.translation: 当我这样做时，我也在试图至少凭直觉将你引入一个更大的框架，其中事件跨越你所知的实相。尽管如此，我们将从那些很可能看似矛盾的问题开始，因为你们对事件的定义如此简单，以至于忽略了更大的分支——这些分支会在结构与行动的整体更大统一中调和任何表面矛盾。在此你们的想象力将极具价值，因为它们常能感知到智力无法明见的统一性——而你们已训练智力专门处理此时此地的证据。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now. Ruburt is a teacher in this level of reality, as I am a teacher in another level of reality... (To Diane:) so if you come to his classes there will be more than pretty tales of reincarnation for you, for you must learn to work and develop your own abilities.
quotes.translation: 现在。鲁柏在这一层实相中是一位老师，正如我在另一层实相中是一位老师……（对黛安说：）所以如果你来上他的课，你会听到的不只是关于转世的美丽故事，因为你必须学会运用并发展自己的能力。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The artist does not identify with the colors he uses. He knows he chooses them, and applies them with a brush. So you paint your reality with your ideas in the same manner. You are not your ideas, nor even your thoughts. You are the self who experiences them. If a painter finds his hands stained with pigment at the end of a day, he can wash the stain off easily, knowing its nature. If you think that limiting thoughts are a portion of you, permanently attached therefore, you will not think of washing them off. You would behave instead like a mad artist who says, "My paints are a part of me. They have stained my fingers, and there is nothing I can do about it."
quotes.translation: 艺术家不会与他的颜料认同。他知道自己选择它们，并用画笔涂抹它们。同样地，你用你的观念描绘你的实相。你不是你的观念，甚至不是你的思想。你是体验它们的那个自己。如果画家在一天结束时发现手上沾满了颜料，他了解颜料的性质，可以轻易洗掉。如果你认为限制性的思想是你的一部分，因此永远附着在你身上，你就不会想到要洗掉它们。相反，你会像一个疯狂的艺术家那样说："我的颜料是我的一部分。它们染污了我的手指，我对此无能为力。"
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Dictation. (Long pause.) You form your own reality. That reality contributes to the experience of others, but each of you possesses a unique, original stance in space and time that is yours alone in quite practical terms, regardless of time's relative existence.
quotes.translation: 口述。（长停顿。）你形成你自己的实相。那个实相有助于他人的经验，但你们每个人都拥有一个独特、原创的时空立足点，在相当实际的层面上，那是你独有的，不论时间的相对存在如何。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The nature of reality can only be approached by an investigation of reality as it is directly experienced in all levels of awareness: reality as it appears under dream conditions, under other conditions of dissociation, and as it appears in the waking condition.
quotes.translation: 实相的本质只能通过直接体验所有意识层面的实相来探究：在梦境条件下显现的实相，在其他解离条件下显现的实相，以及在清醒状态下显现的实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: This does not involve however a change of focus in depth. A change to a different level entirely occurs when the individual switches focus from physical reality to other realities. In the dream state obviously we have an example of such change. One of the main reasons why this is possible within the dream state, rather than in the waking state, is that survival necessities have usually been satisfied.
quotes.translation: 然而，这并不涉及深度焦点的改变。当个体将焦点从物质实相转换到其他实相时，就会发生完全不同的层面转换。在梦境状态中，我们显然有一个这样的例子。这种转换之所以可能在梦境状态而非清醒状态下发生，主要原因之一是生存需求通常已经得到满足。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Ultimately your use of instruments, and your preoccupation with them as tools to study the greater nature of reality, will teach you one important lesson: The instruments are useful only in measuring the level of reality in which they themselves exist.1 Period.
quotes.translation: 最终，你使用仪器以及你专注于用它们作为工具来研究实相的更大本质，会教会你一个重要教训：仪器只对测量它们自身存在的那个实相层面有用。句号。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 702
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: ... But your thoughts also have a reality that you do not know. They have a reality that you do not perceive. Now I am going to give you a quickie course. The atoms and the molecules in this chair, you see, and in our friends camera, all have a consciousness...
quotes.translation: 但你的思想也拥有你所不知的现实。它们拥有你无法感知的现实。现在我要给你们上一堂速成课。这张椅子里的原子和分子，你们看，还有我们朋友相机里的原子和分子，全都拥有意识……
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: If you use psychological time as I have told you, you will get immediate firsthand experience of many facets of reality which take me pages to explain with the indirect use of words. All entities basically are self-aware portions of energy or vitality. They are self-generating and there is no possibility of thinking in terms of either beginning or end. Again, it is only your own camouflage- imprisoned data which makes you think that everything has a beginning and an end.
quotes.translation: 如果你们按照我教的方法运用心理时间，就能直接亲身体验到现实的许多层面，这些层面若用间接的文字解释，需要我写好几页篇幅。所有存有本质上都是能量或活力的自意识部分。它们是自我生成的，根本不可能用开始或结束的概念来思考。再说一次，正是你们自己那被伪装束缚的数据，才让你们认为万事万物都有开始和结束。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Now. In your system of reality you are learning what mental energy is, and how to use it. You do this by constantly transforming your thoughts and emotions into physical form. You are supposed to get a clear picture of your inner development by perceiving the exterior environment. What seems to be a perception, an objective concrete event, independent and apart from you the perceiver, is instead the physical materiaizationl of the perceiver's own inner emotions, energy and mental environment. As long as scientists insist upon considering the perceiver and the perceived event as entirely separate, then the true nature of perception will not be understood.
quotes.translation: 在你们的现实系统中，你们正在学习什么是心灵能量，以及如何运用它。你们通过不断将思想和情感转化为物质形态来做到这一点。你们本应通过感知外在环境来清晰了解自己的内在发展。那些看似独立于感知者之外的客观具体事件，实则是感知者自身内在情感、能量和心灵环境的物质化显现。只要科学家坚持认为感知者与被感知的事件完全分离，那么感知的真实本质就永远无法被理解。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Our books are in the regular trade department. This poses some problems for the legal department, which is given to the most literal translation of reality as interpreted through law. You have almost what you could call a schizophrenic relationship, existing, say, between Parker Books and Prentice's trade-book division. The textbook division represents the workings of the intellect in the usual terms of rational thought, and in those books the qualities of the imagination, of the psyche, of poetry, of creativity, are quite lacking. Such qualities are indeed considered threats, for they do not accept easy answers, and are not content with the status quo.
quotes.translation: 我们的书属于常规商业部门。这给法律部门带来了一些问题，他们习惯于对现实进行最字面的法律解释。你们几乎可以说在帕克图书公司与普伦蒂斯商业图书部之间存在一种精神分裂式的关系。教科书部门代表理性思维常规术语下的智力运作，那些书中完全缺乏想象力、心灵、诗歌和创造力的特质。这些特质确实被视为威胁，因为它们不接受简单的答案，也不满足于现状。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: For much more Seth material on the counterpart concept, see Session 732 and Appendix 25 in Volume II of The "Unknown" Reality (Published by Amber-Allen Publishing, Inc., San Rafael, CA).

quotes.text: Your spirit joined itself with flesh, and in flesh, to experience a world of incredible richness, to help create a dimension of reality of colors and of form. Your spirit was born in flesh to enrich a marvelous area of sense awareness, to feel energy made into corporeal form. You are here to use, enjoy, and express yourself through the body. You are here to aid in the great expansion of consciousness. You are not here to cry about the miseries of the human condition, but to change them when you find them not to your liking through the joy, strength and vitality that is within you; to create the spirit as faithfully and beautifully as you can in flesh.
quotes.translation: 你的灵与肉身结合，在肉身中体验一个无比丰富的世界，帮助创造一个充满色彩与形态的现实维度。你的灵诞生于肉身，是为了丰富这个奇妙的感官觉知领域，感受能量化为有形之躯。你在此是为了通过身体来运用、享受和表达自己。你在此是为了协助意识的伟大扩展。你在此不是为了哀叹人类处境的苦难，而是当你发现它们不合你意时，通过内在的喜悦、力量和活力去改变它们；尽可能忠实而美丽地在肉身中创造灵性。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: All such projections have an electromagnetic and chemical reality that has its origin within the field of the given personality. This projection of energy is one of the main characteristics of your kind. Dreams and dream images are then projected by the individual in sleep, and also in the waking state, although on a subconscious basis. So are thoughts constantly sent outward, and other projections which we have not yet discussed. Therefore as the individual sends out these projections, so does he receive the projections of others. As you know, telepathy operates constantly beneath the dictates of the ego, and so is your intellectual climate formed.
quotes.translation: 所有这类投射都具有电磁和化学的现实性，其源头在于特定人格的场域之内。这种能量投射是你们这类存有的主要特征之一。梦境和梦意象是由个体在睡眠中投射的，在清醒状态下也有，尽管是在潜意识层面。思想也是如此，不断向外发送，还有其他我们尚未讨论的投射。因此，当个体发出这些投射时，他也会接收他人的投射。如你们所知，心电感应持续在自我指令之下运作，你们的思想氛围就是这样形成的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Now some of those within your reality are having their first experience with an ego as you think of it. Others are returning to it, the system, in an effort to learn more. There are guardians, so to speak, within your system, reincarnated for the last time to help keep it in some kind of order while the others mature. There are also some, not physical, who keep an eye out over the whole proceedings.
quotes.translation: 你们实相中的某些存有正在经历你们所认为的第一次自我体验。其他存有则重返这个系统以求学习更多。可以说，你们系统内存在一些守护者，他们最后一次转世，以在其他人成熟期间维持某种秩序。此外还有一些非物质的存有，始终注视着整个进程。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: M-A-U-B-U-N-D-U, in Africa, 14 B.C. The Speakers, more than most, are highly active through all aspects of existence, whether physical or nonphysical, waking or sleeping, between lives or at other levels of reality. As certain physical data are carried along in the gene structure, so this inner information is codified within whatever psychological structures the Speakers may inhabit; but far more readily available than it is to other personalities. It needs, often, trigger points to release it, however. These can take place in either the waking or dream state, and they serve to open up the reservoirs of knowledge and make past training available.
quotes.translation: 公元前14年，非洲的M-A-U-B-U-N-D-U。传讯者比大多数存有更活跃地贯穿存在的一切面向——无论是物质或非物质、清醒或沉睡、转世间隙或其他实相层面。正如某些物质数据随基因结构传递，这种内在信息也被编码于传讯者可能栖居的任何心理结构中，但比其他人格更容易获取。不过，它通常需要触发点才能释放。这些触发点可能发生在清醒或梦境状态，用以开启知识宝库，使过往训练得以运用。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Physical reality is therefore glimpsed by other kinds of personalities in other systems, from their own unique viewpoint. Peering at it from this angle, so to speak, you would not recognize it as your own home system. From some of these viewpoints, your physical matter has little or no permanency, while to others your own thoughts have a shape and form, perceived by observers but not by yourselves.
quotes.translation: 因此，其他系统中的各类人格以其独特视角瞥见物质实相。若从这个角度窥视，你甚至认不出这是自己的家园系统。从某些视角来看，你们的物质几乎没有永恒性；而对另一些视角而言，你们的思想具有形状与形态，能被观察者感知，你们自己却浑然不觉。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: We find once again a basic reality, that of the personality, which is accepted and recognized within the physical field, even while it does not appear there as a definite physical unit. It can indeed be examined but the examination itself, being action, changes it. For the personality, true to the roles of action, will seize upon the new action and form of it new realities and unities with itself.
quotes.translation: 我们再次发现一个基本实相——人格的实相。它在物质领域中被接受和认可，即便它并未以明确的物质单位呈现。它确实可以被检视，但检视本身作为行动会改变它。因为人格遵循行动的本性，会抓住新行动，并以其构建新的实相与自身的统一体。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The field of probable events exists as a reality. From it the self chooses those actions which shall become a part of physical reality. Here indeed free will enters into the matter. This is a pun. I would be disappointed if it were missed. It is a good one.
quotes.translation: 可能事件的场域作为实相而存在。自我从中选择那些将成为物质实相一部分的行动。此处确实涉及自由意志。这是个双关语。若被忽略我会失望——这是个绝妙的双关。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: The Magical Approach is a perfect example of a scientifically-unacceptable method of working with reality. Yet, The Magical Approach works. I can state that with amazement from my own experience. At first it may not seem to be giving you the results you expect, but you are working within spacious time. Your schedule may have to change. You may start bringing in different experiences than you think you are seeking, then materialize your original requirements later on. Your wish-focus disappears into Frameworks 2, 3, and 4,1 and then reappears later. Natural magic: This process can bring you more complex ideas and proof than you conceived of.
quotes.translation: 魔法之道是运用实相时科学无法接受的方法的完美范例。然而，魔法之道确实有效。我可以基于自身经验惊叹地陈述这一点。起初它可能看似未带来预期结果，但你在广阔时间内运作。你的日程或许必须改变。你可能会开始吸引不同于自认为在追寻的体验，随后再让最初的愿望显化。你的愿望焦点会消失于框架二、三、四中，而后重新浮现。自然魔法：这个过程能带来比你构想更复杂的理念与实证。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: For much more Seth material on the counterpart concept, see Session 732 and Appendix 25 in Volume II of The "Unknown" Reality (Published by Amber-Allen Publishing, Inc., San Rafael, CA).

quotes.text: Your own attitudes toward the family have changed considerably, and have become more tolerant since our sessions. Such tolerance helps you as well as those otherwise involved. Ruburts own attitude toward his mother has also become less rigid, largely as a result of our sessions. When you are quiet and not condemning others you can see them more clearly, and you are not afraid of them for you become stronger. You will find yourself enjoying the Christmas season, even with your relatives, for you understand now that a basic reality is involved.
quotes.translation: 你对自己家庭的态度已大有改变，自我们的课程以来变得更为宽容。这种宽容不仅帮助了你自己，也惠及了其他相关的人。鲁柏对他母亲的态度也不再那么僵化，这主要归功于我们的课程。当你保持安静、不评判他人时，你就能更清晰地看见他们，也不再畏惧他们，因为你变得更强大了。你会发现自己在圣诞节期间也能享受乐趣，即便与亲戚们在一起，因为你现在明白其中涉及一个基本实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Like the entire American hostage affair (in Iran), any physical event serves as a focus that attracts all of its probable versions and outcomes. The hostage situation (now in day 53) is a materialized mass dream, meant to be important and vital on political and religious platforms of reality, meant to dramatize a conflict of beliefs, and to project that conflict outward into the realm of public knowledge. Everyone involved was consciously and unconsciously a willing participant at the most basic levels of human behavior, and it is of course no coincidence that 1980 is immediately foreshadowed by that event. What will the world do with it?
quotes.translation: 就像整个美国人质事件（在伊朗）一样，任何物理事件都充当着一个焦点，吸引着它所有可能的版本和结果。人质局势（现已进入第53天）是一个物质化的集体梦境，旨在在政治和宗教的实相平台上具有重要性和关键性，旨在戏剧化地呈现信念的冲突，并将这种冲突向外投射到公众认知的领域。每个参与者，无论有意识还是无意识，在人类行为的最基本层面上都是自愿的参与者，而1980年由这一事件直接预示，当然绝非巧合。世界将如何应对它？
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In modern times, then, the intellect was finally left with only one acceptable world view, with one set of assumptions, with only one main approach to reality and experience. The acceptable assumptions to a large extent ran directly contradictory to built-in biological, spiritual, and psychological assumptions that are a part of man's heritage. The intellect does try to order experience, to make sense out of perception. When it is enriched (pause) by having in its possession several world views, then it does an excellent job of merging those into meaningful patterns, of sorting information and sending it to the proper places, so to speak.
quotes.translation: 那么，在现代，理智最终只剩下一个可接受的世界观，一套假设，以及一种接近实相和体验的主要方式。这些可接受的假设在很大程度上与人类与生俱来的生物性、灵性和心理性假设直接相悖，而这些假设正是人类遗产的一部分。理智确实试图整理经验，从感知中找出意义。当它因拥有多种世界观而得到丰富（停顿）时，它就能出色地将这些世界观融合成有意义的模式，对信息进行分类并将其发送到适当的位置，可以这么说。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth speaks in Session 826, held for Mass Events on March 8, 1978:

quotes.text: The subconscious, and in fact all portions of the self with the exception of the ego, are capable of assimilating a wider area, so to speak, of action. Therefore to these other portions of the self, time has a much different essence than it has for the ego. The ego is indeed many things. It can be defined in relationship to many other aspects of reality. In relationship to action, and moment points, the ego is indeed that portion of the self which stands at the apex of the moment point, and is limited by the moment point. The ego is in this context the portion of the self which is utterly focused upon, and imprisoned by, the moment point.
quotes.translation: 潜意识，事实上除了自我之外的所有自我部分，都能吸收更广阔的行动领域，可以这么说。因此，对这些自我的其他部分而言，时间具有与自我截然不同的本质。自我确实是多面的。它可以相对于实相的许多其他方面来定义。相对于行动和片刻点，自我确实是那个站在片刻点顶端、并被片刻点所限制的自我部分。在这种语境下，自我是那个完全聚焦于片刻点、并被其囚禁的自我部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: I have a few matters to discuss here. The probable realities of which I have spoken are separated from your own, not in terms of space but in terms of energy focus. Survival personalities, as you term them, are not separated from you in space either, but in terms of energy focus.You do not perceive them physically. The direction of focus for them has changed. In the case of probable realities the focus of energy has always been different from your own. It is quite possible, as you should know, under certain circumstances, to contact survival personalities. It is highly difficult however for you to contact your own probable personalities.
quotes.translation: 我在这里有几件事要讨论。我所说的可能实相与你们自己的实相是分开的，不是以空间来划分，而是以能量焦点来划分。你们所谓的存续人格，也不是在空间上与你们分开，而是在能量焦点上。你们无法在物质上感知到他们。他们的焦点方向已经改变。就可能实相而言，能量焦点始终与你们自己的不同。如你所知，在某些情况下，接触存续人格是完全可能的。然而，要接触你自己的可能人格则极为困难。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: In each dimension the inner self begins to handle further aspects of reality. The primary personality must therefore deal with a much larger number of perceptions, while still maintaining its identity. It manipulates in a larger number of dimensions, until it is able you see to handle many, not just one, of its own egos at any given "time"--and you may put time in quotes--while still maintaining its own inner stability and individuality. It takes on more roles, you see, and the psychological structure becomes more complicated as the inner self becomes sure enough of itself to admit ever more stimuli, while still retaining its own core.
quotes.translation: 在每个维度中，内在自我开始处理实相的更多面向。因此，主要人格必须处理数量大得多的感知，同时仍保持其身份。它在更多维度中运作，直到它能够——你看——在任何一个给定的"时间"（你可以把时间加上引号）同时处理多个自我，而不仅仅是其中一个，同时仍保持其内在稳定性和个体性。它承担更多的角色，你看，随着内在自我对自己足够确信，能够接纳越来越多的刺激，同时仍保留其核心，心理结构也变得更加复杂。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

source_raw.title: （Once before, in the first and 18th envelope experiments, an object was used that was similar but not identical by any means. In the first experiment in the 179th session, Seth came through with one accurate impression. In the 18th experiment, held during the 212th session, a few more impressions were obtained; but overall the results were interfered with by other factors in a way that doesn't seem to happen these days. See Session 179 in Volume 4, and Session 212 in Volume 5.

quotes.text: "You" are not divorced from these other streams of consciousness in any basic way; only your focus of attention closes you off from them, and from the events in which they are involved. If you think of your stream of consciousness as transparent, however, then you can learn to look through and beneath it to others that lie in other beds of reality. You can also learn to rise above your present stream of consciousness and perceive others that run, for analogy's sake, parallel. The point is that you are only limited to the self you know if you think that you are, and if you do not realize that that self is far from your entire identity.
quotes.translation: “你”与这些其他意识流在根本层面上并非分离；只是你的注意力焦点将你与它们、以及它们所参与的事件隔绝开来。然而，如果你将自己的意识流视为透明的，那么你就能学会看穿并透过它，触及位于其他实相河床中的意识流。你也能学会超越你当前的意识流，感知那些——打个比方——平行流动的其他意识流。关键在于，你之所以只局限于你所知的自我，是因为你自认为如此，并且你没有意识到那个自我远非你的全部身份。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The experience of your species involves a certain kind of consciousness development, highly vital. (Pause.) This necessitated a certain kind of specialization, a certain "long-term" identification with form. Cellular structure maintains brilliant effectiveness in the body's present reality, but knows itself free of it. Man's particular kind of consciousness fiercely identified with the body. This was a necessity to focus energy toward physical manipulation. To some important extent the same applies to the animals. The cell might gladly "die," but the specifically oriented man-and-animal consciousness would not so willingly let go.
quotes.translation: 你们物种的经验涉及某种高度活跃的意识发展。（停顿。）这需要某种特定的专门化，一种对形态的“长期”认同。细胞结构在身体当前的实相中保持着辉煌的效能，但自知不受其束缚。人类特有的意识强烈地与身体认同。这是将能量聚焦于物理操控的必要条件。在某种程度上，动物也是如此。细胞或许会欣然“死去”，但特定导向的人类与动物意识却不会如此轻易地放手。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 688
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: (All emphatically and joyously:) In your terms, the world is intensely different from one moment to another, with each smallest portion of consciousness choosing its reality from a field of infinite probabilities.3 Immense calculations, far beyond your conscious decisions as you think of them, are possible only because of the unutterable freedom that resides within minute worlds inside your skull -- patterns of interrelationships, counterparts so cunningly woven that each is unique, freewheeling, and involved in an infinite cooperative venture so powerful that the atoms stay in certain forms, and the same stars shine in the sky.
quotes.translation: （全部强调且喜悦地：）用你们的话来说，世界在每一瞬间都截然不同，每一个最微小的意识部分都从无限可能性的场域中选择自己的实相。庞大的计算，远超你们所认为的有意识决定，之所以可能，只因为存在于你们头颅内微观世界中的难以言喻的自由——相互关系的模式，对应物如此巧妙地编织，以至于每一个都是独特、自由运转的，并参与到一个无限合作的冒险中，其力量如此强大，以至于原子保持特定形态，同一片星辰在天空中闪耀。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 733
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: It will refuse to assimilate certain experiences. This very refusal is in itself an action. It will attempt to construct itself on a physical level, since it is within the physical system of reality. The cooperation is broken down to some greater or lesser degree. This conflicts with the inner data in the case of illnesses, and the illnesses are actually the result of conflicts of data. It is obvious that the body is equipped to handle many such distorted actions, but a conflict is hereby brought into play, where constructive energies or actions are not given full reign, and patterns of distortions are set up within the system.
quotes.translation: 它会拒绝同化某些经验。这种拒绝本身就是一个行动。它会试图在物质层面上构建自己，因为它处于实相的物质系统之内。合作在或大或小的程度上被破坏。在疾病的情况下，这与内在资料相冲突，而疾病实际上是资料冲突的结果。显然，身体有能力处理许多这类扭曲的行动，但由此引发了一种冲突，其中建设性的能量或行动无法得到充分发挥，而扭曲的模式在系统内被建立起来。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Any word, simply by being thought, written or spoken, immediately implies a specification. In your daily reality it is very handy to distinguish one thing from another by giving each item a name. When you are dealing with subjective experience, however, definitions can often serve to limit rather than express a given experience. Obviously the psyche is not a thing. It does not have a beginning or ending. It cannot be seen or touched in normal terms. It is useless, therefore, to attempt any description of it through usual vocabulary, for your language primarily allows you to identify physical rather than nonphysical experience.
quotes.translation: 任何词语，仅仅通过被思考、书写或说出，就立即暗示了一种特定化。在你们的日常实相中，通过给每个事物命名来区分它们非常方便。然而，当你们处理主观经验时，定义往往起到限制而非表达特定经验的作用。显然，心灵不是一个“东西”。它没有开始或结束。它无法用常规方式被看见或触摸。因此，试图通过通常的词汇来描述它是无用的，因为你们的语言主要允许你们识别物质经验，而非非物质经验。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Because of my own writing experience, I'm also well aware of the process involved in translating unconscious material into conscious reality. It's particularly obvious when I'm working on poetry. Whatever else is involved in Seth's book, certainly some kind of unconscious activity is operating at high gear. It was only natural, then, that I found myself comparing my own conscious creative experience with the trance procedure involved in Seth's book. I wanted to discover why I felt that Seth's book was his, as divorced from mine. If both were coming from the same unconscious, then why the subjective differences in my feelings?
quotes.translation: 由于我自己的写作经验，我也很清楚将无意识材料转化为有意识实相的过程。当我在创作诗歌时，这一点尤其明显。无论赛斯书中还涉及了什么，某种无意识活动肯定在高速运转。因此，我自然而然地发现自己将我自己的有意识创作经验与赛斯书中涉及的出神过程进行比较。我想发现为什么我觉得赛斯的书是他的，而非我的。如果两者都来自同一个无意识，那么为什么我的感受会有主观差异？
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth began dictating the book in our next Session 511, January 21, 1970, and finished it in Session 591, August 11, 1971. The intervening sessions did not all involve book dictation, however. Some were devoted to personal matters, some given for specific people who needed help, and some were in answer to philosophical questions not connected with the book. I also took several "little vacations." Despite such layoffs, Seth always picked right up, precisely where he left off dictation.

quotes.text: And I hope that our Jesuit will also follow those instructions I have given him. We will here consider the dream reality in an isolated manner, as if it were a thing disconnected from normal consciousness, although in reality it is not so disconnected.
quotes.translation: 我希望我们的耶稣会修士也会遵循我给他的那些指示。在此我们将以孤立的方式探讨梦境实相，仿佛它是与正常意识脱节的事物，尽管实际上它并非如此脱节。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

source_raw.title: （Tracing of the photographic negative used in the 7th envelope test, in Session 187, September 13,1965.）

quotes.text: All you have to do is realize your own freedom. You form the reality that you know, not esoterically, not symbolically, not philosophically. Some great oversoul doesn't form it for you -- you cannot put the burden there, either. You have in the past, collectively and individually, blamed a god or a fate for the nature of your personal realities -- those aspects, indeed that you did not like.
quotes.translation: 你只需认识到自己的自由。你形成你所知的实相——并非以秘传方式、象征方式或哲学方式。没有伟大的超灵为你形成它——你也不能将责任推卸到那里。过去你们集体和个体都曾将个人实相的本质——那些你们不喜欢的面向——归咎于神或命运。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: They coalesce into the events with which you are so intimately familiar. I hope to teach you methods that will allow you to understand the nature of your own reality, and to point a way that will let you change that reality in whatever way you choose.
quotes.translation: 它们凝聚成你们如此熟悉的事件。我希望教导你们一些方法，让你们能理解自身实相的本质，并指出一条道路，使你们能按自己选择的任何方式改变那个实相。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Applied to old age, the color black denotes a returning to those unconscious forces. Now all of this so far is from the standpoint of American and Western belief. It is simply the reality in which many of my readers are involved. In other "underground" systems of belief, however, black is seen as a symbol of great knowledge, power and strength. When this is carried to an extreme you wind up with devil cults, in which the poorly understood powers of creativity and exuberance rush out in distorted form; the undersides of consciousness are then glorified at the expense of the other, white, "conscious and objective" values.
quotes.translation: 应用于老年时，黑色象征回归那些无意识力量。目前这一切都来自美国和西方信仰的立场。这仅仅是许多读者所涉入的实相。然而，在其他"地下"信仰体系中，黑色被视为伟大知识、力量和威能的象征。当这被推向极端时，你们最终会陷入魔鬼崇拜，其中那些未被充分理解的创造力和丰沛力量以扭曲形式爆发；意识的阴暗面随后被颂扬，而牺牲了另一面——白色、"有意识和客观"的价值。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: All of this happens through the window of your present. In terms of energy, again, the vitality of your entity impinging into three-dimensional reality forms a particle that is your present being. But this particle is also deflected away from the earth in a rhythmic pattern. The same happens to other portions of your self at other points in the space-time continuum, but at certain intervals you meet, so to speak. Each of your "presents" becomes charged, filled with potentiality; and your entity, itself conscious energy, is also enriched by your various experiences, by the combined and magnified power of its own "past."
quotes.translation: 这一切都通过你们当下的窗口发生。从能量角度而言，你们存有的生命力冲击三维实相，形成一个粒子——那就是你们当下的存在。但这个粒子也以有节奏的模式偏离地球。你们自我的其他部分在时空连续体的其他点上也发生同样情况，但可以说，在特定间隔你们会相遇。你们每个"当下"都变得充满电荷、充满潜能；而你们的存有——它本身就是有意识的能量——也因你们各种经验、因它自身"过去"的联合与放大的力量而得以丰富。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: On a conscious level certainly you are not all that God is, for that is the unstated, unmanifest portion of yourself. Your being rides upon that unstated reality, as a letter of the alphabet rides upon the inner organizations that are implied by its existence. In those terms your unstated portions "reach backwards to a Source called God," as various languages can be traced back to their source. Master languages can be compared to the historic gods. Each person alive is a part of the living God, supported in life by the magnificent power of nature, which is God translated into the elements of the earth and the universe.
quotes.translation: 在有意识层面上，你们当然不是神的一切，因为那是你们自身未言明、未显化的部分。你们的存在乘载于那未言明的实相之上，如同字母表中的字母乘载于其存在所隐含的内在组织之上。用那些术语来说，你们未言明的部分"向后追溯至一个称为神的源头"，如同各种语言可追溯至其源头。母语可比拟为历史上的诸神。每个活着的人都是活神的一部分，由自然的壮丽力量——即神转化为地球和宇宙的元素——所支持着生命。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: There were no schizophrenics in the time of the pagans, for the belief systems did not support that kind of interpretation. This does not mean that certain behavior did not occur that you would now call schizophrenic. It means that generally speaking such behavior fit within the psychological picture of reality. It [did so] because many of the behavior patterns associated, now, with schizophrenia, are "distorted and debased" remnants of behavior patterns that are part and parcel of man's heritage, and that harken back to activities and abilities that at one time had precise social meaning, and served definite purposes.
quotes.translation: 异教徒时代没有精神分裂症患者，因为当时的信仰体系不支持这种解读。这并非说如今你称为精神分裂的某些行为不曾发生，而是指一般而言，这类行为符合当时对实相的心理认知。之所以如此，是因为如今与精神分裂症相关的许多行为模式，实为人类与生俱来行为模式中"扭曲且退化"的残余——这些模式可追溯至曾具有明确社会意义并服务于特定目的的活动与能力。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Seth himself said nothing about publishing "Unknown" Reality in one volume, two volumes, or even more, while he was producing it. He referred to it as one unit until the very last session, the 744th, when he said in answer to a question I asked "The Seth material is endless. I organize it for your benefit. If you want to divide it into two volumes, that is fine. You will find several points where this can be done ..." In our final view, however, the obvious point of division is also the best one: three sections in each volume. I'll note a little more about this natural point of separation in the Epilogue of this book.
quotes.translation: 赛斯在创作《未知的实相》时，从未提及要将其出版为一卷、两卷或更多卷。他一直将其视为一个整体，直到第744节——我提问时他答道："赛斯资料无穷无尽。我为你们之故加以组织。若想分成两卷，也无不可。你们会发现几个可作分界之处……"但最终我们认为，最明显的分界点也是最恰当的：每卷各含三部分。关于这个自然分界点的更多细节，我将在本书后记中略作说明。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Session 704:* More on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: While the cells required future and past data, and used it to form from that invisible tension the body's present corporal reality, the same kind of information could be a threat then to the ego consciousness, which could be overwhelmed. Within the corporal structure, however, there are indeed messages that leap too quickly or too slowly2 from your viewpoint to allow for any physical response. In that way cellular comprehension is allowed its free flow; but the selectivity mentioned (in sessions 682-3) bypasses such information, so that it does not conflict with present sense data requiring physical action in time.
quotes.translation: 虽然细胞需要过去与未来的数据，并以此从无形张力中构建身体当下的物质实相，但这类信息对自我意识而言可能构成威胁——它可能被淹没。然而在物质结构内部，确实存在从你们视角看来跳跃过快或过慢的信息，以致无法引发任何身体反应。如此，细胞的理解得以自由流动；但（第682-3节提及的）选择性会绕过这类信息，使其不与需要及时身体行动的当下感官数据产生冲突。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 686
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: He does not realize his condition. When this momentary breakthrough does occur however, then someone with disciplined and developed abilities can explain the situation to him. He can then release himself. In such cases the break with physical reality was somehow incomplete at physical death. The stubborn spirit, usually impelled by still strong unfulfilled wishes, refuses to break contact.
quotes.translation: 他并未意识到自己的状况。然而当这种短暂突破发生时，具备训练有素能力的人便能向他解释情况。他因而得以释放自己。在此类案例中，与物质实相的断裂在肉体死亡时并未完全完成。那固执的灵体——通常受强烈未竟愿望驱使——拒绝中断联系。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: (Another one-minute pause.) Give us a moment ... The "unknown" reality sustains you and the web of life as you understand it. Your conscious concepts must enlarge so that the conscious self can understand its true nature. As you think of it, consciousness is barely -- barely -- half developed. It has learned to identify with one small group of neurologically accepted responses. Portions of the brain not used lie latent, waiting for the recognition that will trigger them into activity (intently). When this happens, the mind will become aware of the rich bed of probabilities upon which the ego now rides so blindly.
quotes.translation: （又停顿一分钟。）稍等片刻……"未知"的实相支撑着你，也支撑着你所理解的生命之网。你的意识概念必须扩展，使意识自我能理解其真实本质。就你目前认知而言，意识几乎——几乎——只开发了一半。它学会认同于一小群神经学认可的反应。未被使用的大脑区域处于潜伏状态，等待着能触发其活动的认知（专注地）。当此发生时，心智将意识到自我如今如此盲目驾驭的丰饶概率之床。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: In your terms, until now your consciousness has specialized in neurological patterning. As mentioned (in Session 682), this was extremely important while it learned the art of specialized focus. Now, however, it must begin to recognize that it can indeed expand, and bring into its awareness other quite legitimate realities. The nature of probabilities must be understood, for the time has come in the world as you experience it where the greatest wisdom and discrimination are needed. Your consciousness and neurological prejudice blind you to the full dimension of physical activity. The true implications of physical action are not as yet apparent to you.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: When you create a poem or a song or a painting you are in a state of play, of enjoyment, of freedom. You intend to make something different, to produce a new version of reality. You create out of love, for the sake of the experience. At one time or another almost everyone has that kind of experience, but children have it often. They compose songs and music and paintings in their heads. They alter the focus of their consciousnesses frequently. They do not stop to ask whether or not the play is real or pertinent. Physically, play develops their body mechanisms. It also flexes the great capabilities of their minds.
quotes.translation: 当你创作诗歌、歌曲或画作时，你处于游戏、愉悦与自由的状态。你意图创造不同之物，生产实相的新版本。你出于爱而创造，为体验本身而创造。几乎每个人在某个时刻都曾有过这种体验，但儿童常拥有它。他们在脑海中谱写歌曲、音乐与画作。他们频繁改变意识的焦点。他们不会停下来质问这游戏是否真实或恰当。在身体层面，游戏发展他们的身体机制，同时也锻炼他们心智的伟大能力。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json
