quotes.text: The cell's comprehension leaps its present form. The reality, the physical reality of a given cell, is the focused result of its existence before and after itself in time; and from its knowledge of past and future it receives its present structure.
quotes.translation: 细胞的领悟超越了它当前的形式。一个特定细胞的实相，其物质实相，是它在时间中前后存在的聚焦结果；从它对过去与未来的知识中，它获得了当前的结构。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 690
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: In the beginning there was instead, once more, a divine psychological gestalt--and by that I mean a being whose reality escapes the definition of the word "being," since it is the source from which all being emerges. That being exists in a psychological dimension (long pause), a spacious present, in which everything that was or is or will be (in your terms) is kept in immediate attention, poised in a divine context that is characterized (long pause, eyes closed) by such a brilliant concentration that the grandest and the lowliest, the largest and the smallest, are equally held in a multiloving constant focus.
quotes.translation: 起初，再次存在着一个神圣的心理完形——我指的是一个其真实本质超越了“存在”一词定义的存有，因为它是所有存在涌现的源头。那个存有存在于一个心理维度（长久的停顿），一个广袤的当下，在其中，一切曾经、正在或将要（以你们的说法）存在的事物，都被保持在即时的关注中，悬置于一个神圣的脉络里，其特征是（闭眼，长久的停顿）如此辉煌的专注，以至于最伟大与最卑微、最庞大与最微小的事物，都平等地被包含在一个多重关爱的恒定焦点中。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: When I speak of the subconscious in this manner, I speak of course of that larger portion which deals with the inner realities. I want to give you more detailed information about the inner realities themselves. Actually they do not parallel the outer senses, and this will sound appalling to you I'm afraid, simply because there is nothing to be seen, smelled, heard or touched in the manner in which you are accustomed. This is extremely hard to explain, since I do not want to give you the idea that existence without your particular set of camouflage patterns is bland and innocuous, because this is not the case.
quotes.translation: 当我以这种方式谈论潜意识时，我当然指的是那个处理内在实相的较大部分。我想给你们关于内在实相本身更详细的资讯。实际上，它们并不与外在感官平行，恐怕这听起来会让你们震惊，仅仅因为没有什么东西能以你们习惯的方式被看见、闻到、听到或触摸到。这极难解释，因为我不想给你们一种印象，即没有你们那套特定伪装模式的存在是平淡无奇的，因为事实并非如此。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Some people encounter war directly, however, in terms of hand-to-hand combat, or bombing. Others are only inconvenienced by it. Here the mass shared environment is encountered as physical reality according to individual belief, love, and intent. In the deepest meaning there is no such thing as a victim, either of war, poverty, or disease. This does not mean that those negative qualities should not be combatted, for in the terms of conventional understanding it certainly appears that men and women are victims in many such cases. Therefore they behave like victims, and their beliefs reinforce such experience.
quotes.translation: 然而，有些人直接遭遇战争，以肉搏或轰炸的形式。另一些人则只感到不便。在这里，共享的大众环境根据个人的信念、爱与意图，被体验为物质实相。在最深层的意义上，没有所谓战争的受害者、贫穷的受害者或疾病的受害者。这并不意味着不应对抗那些负面特质，因为按照常规理解，在许多此类情况下，男人和女人显然看起来是受害者。因此，他们表现得像受害者，而他们的信念强化了这类体验。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The personality structure can be studied from many viewpoints. We are now studying it in relation to its basic reality as action. While it may seem that the personality would be a result of a series of actions, this is not basically the case. The personality in actuality is simultaneous action, that is composed of actions within actions. Portions of it are conscious of its awareness as a part of action, and portions of it attempt to stand aside from action.
quotes.translation: 人格结构可以从许多观点来研究。我们现在正研究它与作为行动的基本实相的关系。虽然人格似乎是系列行动的结果，但基本上并非如此。人格实际上是同时性的行动，由行动中的行动组成。它的一部分意识到自己是行动的一部分，而另一部分则试图从行动中抽身。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The simple fact is that when you dream you are flying, you often are. In the dream state you operate under the same conditions, more or less, that are native to a consciousness not focused in physical reality. Many of your experiences, therefore, are precisely those you may meet after death. You may speak with dead friends or relatives, revisit the past, greet old classmates, walk down streets that existed fifty years earlier in physical time, travel through space without taking any physical time to do so, be met by guides, be instructed, teach others, perform meaningful work, solve problems, hallucinate.
quotes.translation: 简单的事实是，当你梦见自己在飞翔时，你常常确实在飞。在梦境状态中，你或多或少是在与不聚焦于物质实相的意识所固有的相同条件下运作。因此，你的许多体验正是你死后可能遇到的。你可以与已故的朋友或亲戚交谈，重访过去，问候老同学，走在物质时间中五十年前就已存在的街道上，不花任何物质时间就穿越空间，被向导迎接，接受教导，教导他人，执行有意义的工作，解决问题，产生幻觉。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: They, or the inhabitants of such realities, do not perceive your plane of existence for the same reason. This does not mean that any one field or plane is more valid than another. The closest field or plane is that one that you create, that you call the dream world, and that you imagine to be unsubstantial, impermanent, fleeting, having no reality except during your own contact with it.
quotes.translation: 他们，或那些实相中的居民，同样无法感知你们的存在层面。这并不意味着任何场域或层面比其他层面更真实。最接近的场域或层面，就是你们所创造、称为梦境世界的那一个——你们认为它虚幻无常、转瞬即逝，除了你们与之接触的片刻外毫无实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The "you" who is capable of such expansion must be a far more creative and multidimensional personality than you earlier imagined. Many of the suggested small exercises given earlier in the book will also help you become acquainted with your own reality, will give you direct experience with the nature of your own soul or entity, and will put you in contact with those portions of your being from which your own vitality springs. You may or may not have your own encounters with past reincarnational selves or probable selves. You may or may not catch yourselves in the act of changing levels of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 那个能够如此扩展的"你"，必然是一个远比先前所想象的更具创造力与多维性的人格。本书前文建议的许多小练习，也能帮助你认识自己的实相，让你直接体验自身灵魂或存有的本质，并让你接触那些生命力的源头。你或许会、或许不会遭遇前世的自己或可能的自己；你或许会、或许不会在意识层次转换的瞬间捕捉到自己的身影。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Ruburt advised Andrea to accept the validity of such feelings as feelings -- not to inhibit them, but to follow their flow with the understanding that they are feelings about reality. As themselves they are real. They express emotional reactions to beliefs. The next time Andrea feels inadequate, for example, she is to actively experience that feeling, realizing that even though she feels inferior this does not mean that she is inferior. She is to say, "I feel inferior," and at the same time to understand that the feeling is not a statement of fact but of emotion. A different kind of validity is involved.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏建议安德莉亚接受这些感受作为感受的有效性——不要压抑它们，而是跟随它们的流动，同时明白这些只是关于实相的感受。作为感受本身，它们是真实的。它们表达了对信念的情绪反应。例如，下次当安德莉亚感到不足时，她应主动体验那种感觉，意识到即使感到自卑，也不意味着她真的低人一等。她要说："我感到自卑"，同时明白这感受不是事实陈述，而是情绪表达。这涉及另一种有效性。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: His belief, illogical as it sounded when spoken, contradictory as it seemed when applied to daily life, stated that the individual somehow could perceive the nature of reality on his or her own by virtue of innate capacities that belonged to the individual by right -- capacities that were a part of man's heritage. In other words, Ruburt felt that there was a slim chance of opening doors of knowledge that had been closed, and he decided to take that chance.
quotes.translation: 他的信念——尽管说出来不合逻辑，应用于日常生活时显得矛盾——主张个体凭借与生俱来的天赋能力，本就可以自行感知实相的本质。这些能力是人类与生俱来的遗产。换言之，鲁柏觉得有一线希望可以打开那些被关闭的知识之门，于是他决定抓住这个机会。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 824, MARCH 1, 1978
9:40 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: (11:07.) You can learn to vastly enrich your own experience. Theoretically, you can even become aware of other existences to some degree. You can travel in the dream state into levels of reality separated from your own. You can learn to use and experience time in new fashions. You can obtain knowledge from other portions of your own being, and tap the psyche's resources. You can improve the world in which you live, and the quality of life. But while you are physical, you will still experience birth and death, dawn and dusk, and the privacy of the moments, for this is the experience you have chosen.
quotes.translation: （11点07分）你可以学会极大地丰富自己的经验。理论上，你甚至能在某种程度上觉察到其他存在。你可以在梦境状态中旅行，进入与自身实相分离的实相层次。你可以学会以全新方式运用和体验时间。你可以从自身存在的其他部分获取知识，并汲取心灵的资源。你可以改善你生活的世界，提升生命品质。但当你身处物质世界时，你仍会经历出生与死亡、黎明与黄昏，以及那些私密的时刻——因为这就是你选择体验的。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The feeling of unreality is not felt when the dream experience is being participated in by the dreamer. At that time the experience is felt to be real, and some dreams indeed are more vivid than waking experience. It is only when the personality passes out of the dream experience, or the dream universe, that the dream experience in retrospect may appear unreal. For now, again, the focus of attention and energy is in the physical universe. Reality then is a result of the focus of energy and attention.
quotes.translation: 当做梦者参与梦境体验时，并不会感到不真实。那时，体验被感知为真实，有些梦境甚至比清醒体验更生动。只有当人格离开梦境体验或梦境宇宙后，回顾时梦境才可能显得不真实。因为此时，注意力与能量的焦点再次回到了物质宇宙。因此，实相是能量与注意力聚焦的结果。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Your main point of contention is brought about by the emotional barriers that are caused by the difference in terms. It is as if you choose to work for a day in the slums. It would be ridiculous for you to choose to do this, and then say to yourself, "Why did I choose to work in the slums? I would prefer to work on Fifth Avenue." You know the reason, and your entire identity knows the reason. You hide it from the present self simply to insure the fact that the present reality is not a pretended one.
quotes.translation: 你主要的争执点源于术语差异所造成的情感障碍。这就像你选择在贫民窟工作一天，然后对自己说："我为何选择在贫民窟工作？我宁愿在第五大道工作。"你知道原因，你的整个身份也知道原因。你向当前自我隐瞒这一点，只是为了确保当前现实不是虚假的。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: All of your present experience was drawn from probable reality. During your life, any event must come through your creaturehood, with the built-in time recognition that is so largely a part of your neurological structure; so usually there is a lag, a lapse in time, during which your beliefs cause material actualization. When you try to change your convictions in order to change your experience, you also have to first stop the momentum that you have already built up, so to speak. You are changing the messages while the body is used to reacting smoothly, unquestioningly, to a certain set of beliefs.
quotes.translation: 你所有的当前体验都源自可能性的现实。在你的一生中，任何事件都必须通过你的生物性来实现，伴随着很大程度上构成你神经结构一部分的内在时间识别；因此通常存在一个时间滞后、一个间隔，在此期间你的信念导致物质实相化。当你试图改变信念以改变体验时，你首先也必须停止你已经建立起来的动量，可以这么说。当身体习惯于对某一套信念做出流畅、毫无疑问的反应时，你正在改变信息。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: There would be no psychological avenues to connect my world and yours. There would be no extensions of the self that would allow you to travel such a psychological distance to those thresholds of reality that form my mental environment. If the universe were structured as you have been told, the probability of my existence would be zero as far as you are concerned. There would have been no unofficial roads for Ruburt to follow, to lead him from the official beliefs of his time. He would never have acknowledged the original impulse to speak for me, and my voice would have been unheard in your world.
quotes.translation: 将没有心理途径连接我的世界和你的世界。将没有自我的延伸让你能够跨越如此心理距离，到达构成我心理环境的那些现实门槛。如果宇宙如你所被告知的那样构建，那么就你而言，我存在的概率将为零。将没有非官方道路供鲁柏遵循，引导他脱离他那个时代的官方信仰。他永远不会承认最初为我发声的冲动，而我的声音在你的世界中将无人听闻。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Our friend did not have cancer. See Note 2 for Session 805.)
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: I am as I told you an educator, and as such my main concern is with education, is with ideas. I want to tell you exactly what you want to know, and if you will hear me then to a large measure you will have to accept some of my terms, for I am quite willing to accept some of yours. Much of this has to do with your idea of the theory of suggestion. If you would read some of our material, it would then become obvious to you that mental suggestion is indeed the basis upon which all reality is founded.
quotes.translation: 正如我告诉你的，我是一位教育家，因此我的主要关注点是教育，是思想。我想告诉你你确切想知道的事情，如果你愿意听我说，那么在很大程度上你将不得不接受我的一些术语，因为我很愿意接受你的一些术语。这其中很大一部分与你们对暗示理论的看法有关。如果你阅读我们的一些资料，那么你就会明白，心理暗示确实是所有现实得以建立的基础。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Your planetary systems exist at once, simultaneously, both in time and in space. The universe that you seem to perceive, either visually or through instruments, appears to be composed of galaxies, stars, and planets, at various distances from you. Basically, however, this is an illusion. Your senses and your very existence as physical creatures program you to perceive the universe in such a way. The universe as you know it is your interpretation of events as they intrude upon your three-dimensional reality. The events are mental. This does not mean that you cannot travel to other planets, for example, within that physical universe, any more than it means that you cannot use tables to hold books, glasses, and oranges (as our coffee table did at that moment), although the table has no solid qualities of its own.
quotes.translation: 你们的行星系统同时存在于时间和空间中。你们似乎通过视觉或仪器感知到的宇宙，似乎由距离你们不同距离的星系、恒星和行星组成。然而，从根本上说，这是一种幻觉。你们的感官以及你们作为物质生物的存在本身，就编程让你们以这种方式感知宇宙。你们所知的宇宙是你们对事件闯入你们三维现实时的解读。这些事件是心理性的。这并不意味着你们不能在那个物质宇宙中旅行到其他行星，例如，就像它并不意味着你们不能用桌子来放书、杯子和橙子（就像我们的咖啡桌当时那样），尽管桌子本身没有固体性质。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: If its built-in instincts are left alone the body is basically self-regulating. It does not kill off all red blood cells if there are too many of them at a given time. It has better sense. But in your fear of negative thoughts you often attempt to deny all normal aggressiveness, and at the first glimpse of it bring up your mental antibodies prepared for action. In so doing you try to repudiate the validity of your own experience. If you do not feel your individual reality, then you can never realize that you form it, and so can change it. It is this denial of experience, and the energy blockages involved, that build up the accumulation of unnecessary "unnatural" guilt. The body itself cannot understand these blocked messages, and cries out to express its own corporeal knowledge of the moment as it experiences it. (Intently:) You mentally shout in such situations that you do not feel what you feel.
quotes.translation: 如果其内置本能不受干扰，身体基本上是自我调节的。它不会在特定时间红细胞过多时杀死所有红细胞。它更有智慧。但在你们对负面思想的恐惧中，你们常常试图否认所有正常的攻击性，并在其初现时立即调动你们准备好的心理抗体。这样做时，你们试图否认自己体验的有效性。如果你感受不到自己的个体现实，那么你就永远无法意识到你创造了它，因此可以改变它。正是这种对体验的否认，以及涉及的能量阻塞，累积了不必要的"非自然"内疚。身体本身无法理解这些阻塞的信息，并呼喊出它在其体验时刻所拥有的当下身体知识。（专注地：）你们在这种情境下在心理上大喊，你们没有感受到你们所感受到的。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: All of the probabilities practically possible in human development are therefore present to some extent or another in each individual. Any biological or spiritual advancement that you might imagine will of course not come from any outside agency, but from within the heritage of consciousness made flesh. Generally, those alive in this century chose a particular kind of orientation. The species chose to specialize in certain kinds of physical manipulation, to devote its energies in certain directions. Those directions have brought forth a reality unique in its own fashion. Man has not driven himself down a blind alley, in other words. He has been studying the nature of his consciousness -- using it as if it were apart from the rest of nature, and therefore seeing nature and the world in a particular light.6 That light has finally made him feel isolated, alone, and to some extent relatively powerless (intently).
quotes.translation: 因此，人类发展中所有实际可能的概率，都在某种程度上存在于每个个体之中。你所能想象的任何生物或灵性进步，当然不会来自任何外在力量，而是源于意识化为血肉的内在传承。总体而言，活在这个世纪的人们选择了某种特定的取向。这个物种选择专精于某些物理操控方式，将能量投入特定方向。这些方向催生了一种独具特色的实相。换言之，人类并未将自己逼入死胡同。他一直在研究自身意识的本质——仿佛将意识视为与自然其余部分分离的存在，因此以特定视角看待自然与世界。这种视角最终使他感到孤立、疏离，并在某种程度上相对无力（专注地）。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 708
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: I also think that if asked Seth would point out that since the concept of quantum mechanics is based upon the idea that everything we "know"--matter, energy, our sensual information--is made up of quanta, or the interactions of insubstantial fields that in turn, and quite paradoxically, produce very active subatomic packets or particles, then quantum mechanics is at least analogous with his statements that basically the universe is composed of consciousness itself. But I think that the continuum of consciousness, or All That Is, contains not only the phenomena of quantum mechanics, but also Seth's nonphysical EE (electromagnetic energy) units, and his CU's (or units of consciousness). In those terms, then, quantum mechanics is a theory that doesn't penetrate deeply enough into basic reality, even if physicists these days are basing their unified field theories upon quantum thinking. (These theories are themselves quite incomplete, since at this time they incorporate only three of the four basic interactions in nature: electromagnetism, and the strong and weak nuclear forces. So far, gravitation remains outside all attempts at integration.)
quotes.translation: 我也认为，若问及赛斯，他会指出：既然量子力学的概念基于这样的理念——我们所"知道"的一切（物质、能量、感官信息）都由量子构成，或由无形场域的相互作用构成，而这些场域又矛盾地产生极为活跃的亚原子包或粒子——那么量子力学至少与他的陈述相类似，即宇宙本质上由意识本身构成。但我认为，意识的连续体（或一切万有）不仅包含量子力学的现象，还包含赛斯非物质的EE（电磁能量）单位及其CU（意识单位）。按此术语，量子力学是一种未能深入触及基本实相的理论，即便当今物理学家正以量子思维为基础构建统一场论。（这些理论本身相当不完整，因为目前仅整合了自然界四种基本相互作用中的三种：电磁力、强核力和弱核力。迄今为止，引力仍未被纳入任何整合尝试。）
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: I don't really think we can conceive of anything to be truly "alien," though, so I use the word here only to lead into the next of Seth's larger concepts that I want to mention: that of probable realities, or probabilities, as Jane and I usually say. Not only does Seth stress the constant psychic motion of reincarnational and counterpart selves upon this earth we know--but he also tells us that each of those selves can move into other or parallel realities. I quote him from Session 681 in Volume 1 of "Unknown" Reality:
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 6: Genetic Heritage and Reincarnational Predilections

quotes.text: Once these physical pathways are opened, the way becomes easier. The pineal, pituitary, and thalamus are important here. The salt mentioned earlier has a connection with the thyroid. The physical brains alone, the unused portions, have it within their ability, for example, to hear color, to smell sound; in other words, these portions contain among other things functions, unused mainly, that would allow you to perceive physical reality in various other fashions. These undifferentiated areas existed first before the specialized senseapparatus was uniformly adopted. As a species you could just as easily have smelled color rather than viewed it, you see. These portions of the brain, once activated, then allow you to switch sense impressions from one sense mechanism to another, you see.
quotes.translation: 一旦这些物理通道被开启，道路便会变得更容易。松果体、脑垂体和丘脑在此处至关重要。先前提到的盐与甲状腺有关。仅就物理大脑而言，其未使用的部分具备这样的能力——例如，听见颜色、嗅到声音；换言之，这些区域包含多种功能（主要未被使用），能让你以其他方式感知物理实相。这些未分化的区域在专门感官器官被统一采用之前就已存在。作为一个物种，你本可以嗅到颜色而非看到它，你明白吗。大脑的这些部分一旦被激活，便能让你将感官印象从一个感官机制转换到另一个，你明白吗。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: In nature nothing is wasted, so the luxurious growth of man's dream landscapes are also utilized. Whether or not these are physically actualized, they have their own reality. Your own personalities are to some extent the result of your waking experience. But they are also formed equally by your dreaming experience, by the learning and knowledge and encounters that occur when many would tell you that you are beyond legitimate perception. Dreams, then, are deeply involved with the learning processes. Dreams of walking and running occur in infants long before they crawl, and serve as impetuses.
quotes.translation: 自然界中没有任何事物被浪费，因此人类梦境景观的繁茂生长也被充分利用。无论这些景观是否在物理上被实现，它们都拥有自身的实相。你的人格在某种程度上是清醒体验的结果，但同样由你的梦境体验、学习与知识、以及许多人在你被认为超出合法感知范围时发生的遭遇所塑造。因此，梦境与学习过程密切相关。婴儿在爬行之前很久就会出现行走和奔跑的梦境，这些梦境起着推动作用。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The investigation will allow you to probe into the realities of conscious wakefulness, extending your sensual perceptions so that you can probe further outward into physical reality. This is indeed an ambitious project. We shall proceed as we always do, slowly but steadily; and you shall change along the way, for the very actions involved will change you. I might add for the better.
quotes.translation: 这项探索将允许你深入探究清醒意识的实相，扩展你的感官知觉，以便你能进一步向外探索物理实相。这确实是一个雄心勃勃的计划。我们将一如既往地缓慢而稳定地推进；你将在过程中改变，因为所涉及的行为本身就会改变你。我可以补充说，是向好的方向改变。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: In the same way, if you are overly concerned about the nature of your own reality, and if you are looking to others to justify your existence, you will not be able to abandon your own world view successfully, for you will feel too threatened. Or, traveling in psychic exercises even slightly away from your own home station, you will still try to take your familiar paraphernalia with you, and interpret even entirely new situations of consciousness in the light of your own world view. You will transpose your own set of assumptions, then, into conditions in which they may not really fit at all.
quotes.translation: 同样地，如果你过度关注自身实相的本质，并寻求他人来证明你的存在，你将无法成功放弃自己的世界观，因为你会感到过于受威胁。或者，即使在心灵练习中稍微偏离自己的本营，你仍会试图携带熟悉的装备，并以自己的世界观来诠释甚至全新的意识情境。你会将自己的假设体系转置到可能完全不适合的条件中。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: I said (in sessions 711 and 716, for instance) that your normal focus of consciousness can be compared to your home station. So far, exercises have been described that will gently lead you away from concentration upon this home base, even while its structure is strengthened at the same time. You can also call this home station or local program your world view, since from it you perceive your reality. To some extent it represents your personal focus, through which you interpret most of your experience. As I mentioned (in Session 715, for instance), when you begin to move away from that particular organization, strange things may start to happen. You may be filled with wonder, excitement, or perplexity. You may be delighted or appalled, according to whether or not your new perceptions agree or disagree with your established world view.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Root assumptions are those laws upon which you agree in any system of reality. You agree, for example, upon what objects are physical -- it makes little difference whether they are or not, as long as you agree upon this. Your consciousness belongs in a body. You would not be caught DEAD with your consciousness outside of your body. It is taboo! Now the fact is that your consciousness is not imprisoned within your body; but as long as you believe that it is, again, you will not be caught dead outside of it. And when you are caught dead outside of it, there will be some amazement, indeed.
quotes.translation: 根本假设是你们在任何实相系统中都同意的那些法则。例如，你们同意物体是物质的——它们是否真的是物质的并不重要，只要你们同意这一点。你们的意识属于一个身体。你们绝不会让自己的意识离开身体，死也不干！这是禁忌！事实上，你们的意识并不被囚禁在身体内；但只要你们相信它被囚禁，同样，你们死也不会离开身体。而当你们真的发现意识离开身体时，确实会感到惊讶。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The deeply and strongly dimensioned sphere I used as an analogy for an action, if you recall, for any portion of action; you can now indeed further imagine one entity being composed of such an action, with egos like many faces looking outward in all directions, and each perceiving vastly different fields of reality; looking inward and outward, backward and forward as it were, through and beyond. And yet each action, or entity, is a part of another, and is both within and without another. And none of it is meaningless, and yet in a basic manner all of it has the meaning that you give it.
quotes.translation: 我曾用那个深度和强度都很大的球体来比喻一个行动，如果你还记得，它代表任何行动的一部分；现在你确实可以进一步想象，一个存有由这样一个行动组成，拥有许多面向外的自我，如同许多面孔，向各个方向观望，每个自我都感知到截然不同的实相领域；向内向外、向后向前，仿佛穿透并超越一切。然而，每个行动或存有都是另一个的一部分，既在另一个之内，又在另一个之外。这一切都不是无意义的，然而从根本上说，它们都具有你赋予的意义。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: It was necessary for the ego, in its origins, to objectify itself as much as possible. Now however the stability of the ego, generally speaking, as a part of the human personality is established. It can now afford to be much more elastic, to include, in other words, more and more of reality within its awareness. Such an inclusion would be most beneficial. It would of course however to some extent change the ego, and any change is resisted by the ego.
quotes.translation: 在自我起源时，它有必要尽可能地将自己客观化。然而现在，一般来说，自我作为人类人格的一部分，其稳定性已经建立。它现在可以变得更有弹性，换句话说，可以将越来越多实相纳入其觉知中。这样的纳入将非常有益。当然，这会在某种程度上改变自我，而任何改变都会遭到自我的抗拒。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Because of the brain's necessary specifications, large portions of your own greater reality cannot appear through its auspices. The brain might consider such extracurricular activity as background noise or clutter that it could not decipher. It is the mind, then, as the brain's nonphysical counterpart, that decides what data will activate the brain in that regard. The so-called ancient portions of the brain (among them the brainstem -- limbic system) contain "the mind's memories." Generally speaking, this means important data to which, however, no conscious attention need be given.
quotes.translation: 由于大脑必要的规格限制，你们自身更大实相的大部分无法通过它的中介显现。大脑可能将这种额外的活动视为无法解读的背景噪音或杂乱信息。因此，是心灵——作为大脑的非物质对应物——决定了哪些数据会在那个方面激活大脑。所谓的大脑古老部分（其中包括脑干——边缘系统）包含“心灵的记忆”。一般来说，这意味着重要的数据，然而这些数据无需有意识地关注。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Later, Jane suggested that I ask Seth to comment upon the ideas in a session. There would also be a number of other reasons for double dreams, of course. I briefly wrote about them in Volume I of "Unknown" Reality; see the notes for Session 692.)

quotes.text: This is not to say that such surface reality is not vital and important, or that some information about its own nature cannot be gleaned from its study; but only a small amount can be so obtained. The other fields of which I speak exert their influences upon the physical organism as it exists. These forces are dealt with and balanced by the subconscious, and not by the outer ego.
quotes.translation: 这并不是说这种表面实相不重要，或者说无法通过研究它获得关于其自身本质的一些信息；但只能获得少量信息。我所谈的其他场域，在物理有机体存在时，对其施加影响。这些力量由潜意识处理并平衡，而非由外在自我处理。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: You can only do so much in your painting. You can only create it as a reality in so many dimensions. You cannot appreciate, for that matter, all the systems of reality in which the painting does have reality. This is a very simple analogy:
quotes.translation: 你在绘画中只能做到这么多。你只能在这么多维度中将其创造为实相。就此而言，你无法欣赏那幅画确实具有实相的所有实相系统。这是一个非常简单的类比。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: It is now important that he relearn the objective nature of your reality, the simple sanity of an apple as an apple. After this then perhaps he can begin to wonder at the reality behind the objective universe. Now he must let it nurse him.
quotes.translation: 现在重要的是让他重新学习你们现实世界的客观本质，学习苹果作为苹果的简单合理性。在这之后，他或许才能开始探究客观宇宙背后的实相。现在他必须让这实相滋养他。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: "Some mountain climbers, when asked why they climb a certain peak, respond: 'Because the mountain is there to be climbed'--so the natural approach, the magical approach, is to be used because it exists, and because it represents an open doorway into a world of reality that is always present, always at the base of your cultures and experience. Theoretically at least, the magical approach should be used because it represents the most harmonious method of life (underlined). It is a way of living that automatically enhances all of your abilities and accelerates your comprehensions.
quotes.translation: "有些登山者被问及为何攀登某座山峰时会回答：'因为山就在那里等待攀登'——因此自然之道、魔法之道之所以被采用，是因为它存在，因为它代表着一扇通往实相世界的敞开之门，这世界始终存在，始终是你们文化与经验的基础。至少在理论上，魔法之道应当被采用，因为它代表着最和谐的生活方式（下划线强调）。这是一种能自动增强你所有能力、加速你领悟力的生活方式。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 5. It was inevitable that Jane's images would remind me of the note I'd written well over two months ago, on re-creating the past, or updating it, through nostalgia. In Chapter 11 of Dreams, see Note 8 for Session 936. Her images led me to search out the collection of battered black-and-white snapshots of her that somehow, some way, she'd managed to save from her early childhood. Along with scraps of her youthful poetry, the pictures are the only physical remnants she possesses of her first years, and studying them anew I realized just how valuable they really are. I talked of having them copied and enlarged by a professional photographer; I speculated about eventually having some of them reproduced in a book. That idea may have to wait, however: For some years Jane hasn't cared to be photographed--or have pictures of herself shown, no matter when they had been taken.

quotes.text: I am trying to stretch your imaginations, and to help you throw aside rigid concepts that literally blind you to the dimensions of your own reality. Again -- you are biologically equipped to perceive far more of that reality than you do.5
quotes.translation: 我正在试图拓展你们的想象力，帮助你们抛弃那些僵化的概念——这些概念实际上使你们对自身实相的维度视而不见。再次强调——你们在生物构造上本应能感知到比现在多得多的实相。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 740
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: This data must be interpreted in such a way that the conscious mind then can give a prediction, say, but also in such a way that its reality exits simultaneously with consciousness, but never so replacing consciousness that the ego grows alarmed. This is often done by the use of symbols which are not immediately apparent to consciousness, and thus are allowed through. Then it is too late for the ego to close its gates against them. But the symbols chosen must be of a nature so that they will become meaningful to consciousness, to as large an extent as possible after emergence.
quotes.translation: 这些数据必须被诠释成这样一种方式：既能让意识心做出预测，又能使其实相与意识同时存在，但绝不能取代意识以致让自我感到警觉。这通常通过使用对意识而言并非显而易见的符号来实现，因此这些符号得以通过。等到自我想要关闭心门时已经为时已晚。但所选符号必须具有这样的性质：在浮现之后，它们能尽可能多地对意识产生意义。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: (11:19.) This provides you with a certain kind of communication, but it also allows a molecular expression that is natural at that level, and then used by you for your own purposes. I am not saying that molecules speak. I am saying that they are expressed through your speech, however -- and that your speech represents an amplification of their existence. Through your words their reality is amplified, in the same way that man's emotions once found amplification through the physical elements.
quotes.translation: （11点19分）这为你们提供了一种特定的沟通方式，同时也允许一种在该层面自然存在的分子表达，然后被你们用于自身目的。我并非说分子会说话。我是说它们通过你们的言语被表达出来——而你们的言语代表了它们存在的放大。通过你们的词语，它们的实相被放大，正如人类的情感曾经通过物质元素得到放大一样。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: "Important misunderstandings involving time have been in a large measure responsible for many of Ruburt's difficulties, and also of your own, though of a lesser nature. All of this involves relating to reality in a more natural, and therefore magical, fashion. There is certainly a kind of natural physical time in your experience, and in the experience of any creature. It involves the rhythm of the seasons--the days and nights and tides and so forth. In the light of that kind of physical time, there is no basic cultural time ... which you have transposed upon nature's rhythms.
quotes.translation: "涉及时间的重要误解在很大程度上导致了鲁柏的许多困难，也导致了你们自己的困难——尽管程度较轻。这一切都涉及以更自然——因而也更魔法——的方式与实相相连。在你们的经验中，以及在任何生物的经验中，确实存在一种自然的物理时间。它涉及季节的节奏——昼夜、潮汐等等。在这种物理时间的背景下，并不存在你们强加于自然节奏之上的基本文化时间……"
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 4. In Chapter 2 for Volume 1 of Dreams, see the opening notes for Session 885.

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... Inner reality and private experience give birth to all mass events. Man cannot disentangle himself from the natural context of his physical life. His culture, his religion, his psychologies, and his psychological nature together form the context within which both private and mass events occur. (Loudly, then whispering so softly that I could barely hear:) This book will, then, be devoted to the nature of the great sweeping emotional, religious, or biological events that often seem to engulf the individual, or to lift him or her willy-nilly in their power.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……内在实相与私人经验孕育了所有群体事件。人无法脱离其物质生命的自然脉络。其文化、宗教、心理学及心理本质共同构成了私人事件与群体事件发生的场域。（先是大声道，随即转为几乎难以听闻的耳语：）因此，本书将致力于探讨那些常如洪流般席卷个体、或不由分说将其卷入其中的情感、宗教或生物学重大事件之本质。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 801, APRIL 18, 1977
9:31 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: You are familiar, all of you, with subconscious fabrications as they exist in the nightmare condition. Now on occasion when you are projecting from a dream point, you will meet such subconscious images. Your ordinary standards of reality mean absolutely nothing when you leave the physical system, therefore you will encounter, simultaneously perhaps, images that are subconsciously formed; quite valid images that belong in another dimension; constructions created by others within another system; and for any control at all, you must learn how to distinguish one from the other.
quotes.translation: 诸位都熟悉噩梦状态下潜意识虚构物的存在形态。当你们从梦境投射点时，偶尔会遇见此类潜意识影像。离开物质系统后，你们惯常的实相标准便毫无意义——因此你们可能同时遭遇：潜意识形成的影像、属于另一维度的有效实相、其他系统内他人创造的构造物。若要获得任何掌控力，就必须学会区分彼此。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: No electrical reality is composed of simply one impulse. Although we must speak in these terms for simplicity's sake, every electrical impulse is of itself composed of a truly endless variety and range of intensities. In other words, each electrical impulse contains within it its own infinity of variations.
quotes.translation: 没有电性实相是由单一脉冲构成的。尽管为简便起见我们需如此表述，但每个电性脉冲本身都包含着真正无穷尽的强度变化与范围。换言之，每个电性脉冲内部都蕴含其自身的无限变体。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: To a large extent the methods of communication may vary. A personality based within physical reality, between lives for example, would find entry in many ways easier. The information he would be able to give, however, would also be limited because of his experience. I do have a memory of physical existence however, and this automatically helps me in translating your mental data into physical form. I do perceive objects, for example. Using Ruburt's mechanism is of great help here also. At times I see the room and the people as he, or rather his perceptive mechanisms, do.
quotes.translation: 沟通方式在很大程度上可能千变万化。以物质实相为基础的人格（例如处于转世间隙者）会发现进入方式相对容易。然而因其经验所限，能提供的信息也有限。不过我确实保有物质存在的记忆，这自然有助于将你们的心智数据转化为物质形态。例如我确实能感知物体。借助鲁柏的机制在此也大有裨益。有时我透过他（更确切地说是他的感知机制）看见房间与人群。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This involves efficient, complete use of the outer senses in their perception of camouflage reality, and of joyful, effective behavior and manipulation within that field of camouflage in which you spend a certain level of your existence. When you operate within it you should indeed experience it completely, in as many phases as possible, and be it to a much greater degree than is usually achieved, the conscious mind using itself then in experience, and thus knowing itself. And then the switch to use of the inner senses. The great contrast then refreshes the whole self.
quotes.translation: 这涉及对外在感官的高效完整运用——在感知伪装实相时，以及在你们特定存在层次所处的伪装场域中，进行愉悦有效的行动与操控。当你们在此场域运作时，确实应当尽可能全面地体验它，以远超寻常的程度让意识心智在经验中运用自身，从而认知自我。随后转向内在感官的运用。这种强烈对比将使整体自我焕然一新。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The unknown reality and the psyche's greater existence cannot be separated from the intimate knowledge of the flesh, however, for the life of the flesh takes place within that framework. As earlier mentioned,4 the conscious self generally focuses in but one small dimension. Period. That dimension is experienced as fully as possible, its clear brilliance and exquisite focus possible only because you tune in to it and bring it to the forefront of your attention. In your terms, when you understand how to do this, then you can begin to tune in to other "stations" as well.
quotes.translation: 然而，未知实相与心灵更宏大的存在，始终与血肉之躯的亲密认知密不可分——因为血肉生命正是在此架构中展开。如前所述，意识自我通常只聚焦于一个微小维度。句号。这个维度被尽可能充分地体验，其清晰璀璨与精妙聚焦之所以可能，只因你们调准频率将其置于意识前沿。以你们的时间观而言，当理解如何做到这点时，你们就能开始调频至其他"频道"。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (There were clear-cut connections between her creative performance today and her reception of the outline for The Way Toward Health last March; see the notes at 10:45, as well as Note 8, for Session 713; and in Volume 1, see Appendix 7. But there was even more creative expression to come through Jane this evening, not only in the session itself of course, but after it, as I try to explain in the concluding notes.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Consciousness is a way of perceiving the various dimensions of reality. Consciousness as you know it is highly specialized. The physical senses allow you to perceive the three-dimensional world, and yet by their very nature they can inhibit the perception of other equally valid dimensions. Most of you identify with your daily physically oriented self. You would not think of identifying with one portion of your body and ignoring all other parts, and yet you are doing the same thing (smile) when you imagine that the egotistical self carries the burden of your identity.
quotes.translation: 意识是感知实相各个维度的一种方式。你们所知的意识是高度专门化的。肉体感官让你们能够感知三维世界，然而正是由于其本质特性，它们也会抑制对其他同样有效的维度的感知。你们大多数人认同于日常的、以物质为导向的自我。你不会想到只认同自己身体的一部分而忽略其他所有部分，但当你想象自我背负着你们身份的重担时，你正在做同样的事情（微笑）。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now: Your normal waking reality can be compared to a kind of television drama in which you participate directly in all of the dramas presented. You create them to begin with. You form your private and joint adventures, and bring them into experience by using your physical apparatus -- your body -- in a particular way, tuned in to a large programming area in which, however, there are many different stations. In your terms, these stations come alive. You are the drama that you experience, and all of your activities seem to revolve about you. You are also the perceiver.
quotes.translation: 现在：你们正常的清醒实相可以比作一种电视剧，在其中你们直接参与所有呈现的戏剧。你们首先创造了它们。你们形成自己私人和共同的冒险，并通过以特定方式使用你们的肉体装置——你们的身体——将它们带入经验，调谐到一个大型节目区域，然而其中有许多不同的频道。用你们的话说，这些频道变得活跃起来。你们就是你们所体验的戏剧，你们所有的活动似乎都围绕着你们。你们也是感知者。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In this new book, therefore, I will sometimes provide my own "scene setting." The psyche's production, in other words, has escaped practical, physical bounds, so that from my level of reality I can no longer expect Joseph to do more than record the sessions. I will ask you, my readers, to bear with me then. In my own way, I will try to provide suitable references so that you know what is going on physically in your time, as this book is written.
quotes.translation: 因此，在这本新书中，我有时会提供我自己的"场景设置"。换句话说，心灵的作品已经超越了实际的物质界限，所以从我的实相层面，我不能再期望约瑟夫做更多的事情，只能记录这些课。那么，我请求你们，我的读者们，请容忍我。以我自己的方式，我将尽力提供适当的参考，以便你们知道在你们的时间里，当这本书被写下时，物质层面正在发生什么。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The Nature of Personal Reality [our last book] is there for others to follow. Others, however, did not have a hand in producing it. They will try out the ideas, many of them, to the best of their ability, and learn and gain much -- all the time (much more forcefully.) hanging on safely to the banners of conventional beliefs. Ruburt has allowed himself no such comforts. He should remember that many people have far greater hassles ... with health, personal relationships, finances and vocations, and without any satisfying accomplishments to offset their misfortunes.
quotes.translation: 《个人实相的本质》[我们上一本书]在那里供他人遵循。然而，其他人并没有参与它的制作。他们会尽力尝试其中的许多理念，并学习和获得很多——始终（更强烈地）安全地紧握着传统信念的旗帜。鲁柏不允许自己有这样的安慰。他应该记住，许多人在健康、人际关系、财务和职业方面有更大的麻烦……而且没有任何令人满意的成就来抵消他们的不幸。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: As I have certainly hinted, the body is a miraculous organism, and you have barely learned the most simple of its structures.1 You do not understand the properties of soul or body, yet the body was given to you so that you could learn from it. The properties of the earth are meant to lead you into the nature of the soul. You create physical reality, yet without knowing how you do so, so that the wondrous structure of the earth itself is meant to lead you to question your own source. Nature as you understand it is meant to be your teacher. You are not its master.
quotes.translation: 正如我肯定暗示过的，身体是一个奇迹般的有机体，而你们几乎只学到了它最简单的结构。你们不了解灵魂或身体的特性，然而身体被赐予你们，以便你们能从中学习。地球的特性旨在引导你们进入灵魂的本质。你们创造物质实相，却不知道如何做到，因此地球本身奇妙的结构旨在引导你们质疑自己的源头。你们所理解的自然旨在成为你们的老师。你们不是它的主人。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: (11:12.) I gave him helpful information, but this could only be used by him as he felt it for himself and traveled through his own system of beliefs. When you understand the nature of reality and your part in forming it, then you can no longer look to others to solve your problems for you, and you realize that your own beliefs are the rich creative elements that you yourself must mix and match. If you think that certain foods will help you, then they will be effective in that system -- because of your beliefs. If you believe in doctors, then they will help you.
quotes.translation: （11:12）我给了他有益的信息，但这只能在他为自己感受并穿越自己的信念体系时才能被使用。当你理解实相的本质以及你在形成它中的角色时，你就不能再指望别人为你解决你的问题，并且你意识到你自己的信念就是你自己必须混合和搭配的丰富创造元素。如果你认为某些食物会对你有帮助，那么它们在那个体系中就会有效——因为你的信念。如果你相信医生，那么他们就会帮助你。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The mind can interpret the experiences that the legs and the feet have, however, and by imaginatively using that sensual data can perceive the ant's reality to some extent. Now when the mind races and runs, it sometimes has great difficulty interpreting its activities to the brain, which is usually concerned with other realities only to the extent that they impinge upon it.
quotes.translation: 然而，心智可以解读双腿和双脚所经历的经验，并通过想象性地运用那些感官数据，在一定程度上感知蚂蚁的现实。现在，当心智疾驰奔跑时，它有时很难向大脑解释自己的活动，而大脑通常只关心那些冲击到它的其他现实。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: That event resulted in a scribbled manuscript, unpublished, called The Physical Universe as Idea Construction. His desire and intense intent to understand more of the nature of reality triggered the production of that fragmentary automatic manuscript. He found himself as a young adult, at the time of the President Kennedy assassination, in a world that seemed to have no meaning. At the same time, while conditioned by the beliefs of his generation -- beliefs that still tinge your times -- he held on to one supporting belief never completely lost from childhood.
quotes.translation: 那个事件导致了一份潦草的手稿，未出版，名为《作为观念建构的物理宇宙》。他想要更深入理解现实本质的渴望和强烈意图，触发了那份零碎自动手稿的产生。在肯尼迪总统遇刺时，他发现自己作为一个年轻人，身处一个似乎毫无意义的世界。同时，在被他那一代人的信念所制约——这些信念仍影响着你们的时代——他紧紧抓住了一个从童年起就从未完全失去的支持性信念。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: The beliefs of course will be accepted by you not as beliefs, but as reality. Once you understand that you form your reality, then you must begin to examine these beliefs by letting the conscious mind freely examine its own contents.
quotes.translation: 当然，这些信念不会被你视为信念，而是被视为现实。一旦你明白你形成了自己的现实，那么你就必须开始审视这些信念，让意识心智自由地检视其自身的内容。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The most rejuvenating idea of all, and the greatest step to any true illumination, is the realization that your exterior life springs from the invisible world of your reality through your conscious thoughts and beliefs, for then you realize the power of your individuality and identity. You are immediately presented with choices. You can no longer see yourself as a victim of circumstances. Yet the conscious mind arose precisely to open up choices, to free you from a one-road experience, to let you use your creativity to form diversified, varied comprehensions.
quotes.translation: 所有想法中最具更新力量、通往任何真正启迪的最大一步，是认识到你的外在生活通过你有意识的念头和信念，从你现实的无形世界中涌现出来，因为那时你便意识到你个体性和身份的力量。你立刻面临选择。你再也无法将自己视为环境的受害者。然而，意识心智正是为了开启选择、将你从单一道路的经验中解放出来、让你运用创造力形成多样化、多变的领悟而出现的。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The physical universe serves then as a threshold for probabilities, and all possible species find their greatest fulfillment within that system, each of them neurologically tuned into their own reality and their own "time." So the body itself, as it presently exists, is innately equipped with other neurological responses that to you would seem to be biologically invisible. Nevertheless, your consciousness and your beliefs are what direct this neurological recognition. At birth, and before structured learning processes begin, you are far freer in that regard.
quotes.translation: 因此，物质宇宙充当了可能性的门槛，所有可能的物种都在那个系统中找到最大的圆满，它们每一个在神经上都调谐到自己的现实和自己的“时间”。所以，身体本身，以其目前的存在方式，天生就具备其他神经反应，这些反应对你而言在生物学上似乎是不可见的。然而，你的意识和你的信念正是引导这种神经认知的东西。在出生时，在结构化的学习过程开始之前，你在那方面要自由得多。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 688
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Then beyond those human-oriented parameters must lie a host of probable realities involving changes in psychic and physical form: nonhuman aspects of ourselves that in ordinary terms we'd have great difficulty relating to. This discussion could be carried further into such realms, but instead I'll note that even here I don't conceive of anything that would prohibit at least some exchanges between certain of those far probable realities and our own mundane universe. It all depends upon where you want to stop in your thinking, upon what you can conceive of....
quotes.translation: 然后，在那些以人类为导向的参数之外，必定存在大量涉及心理和物理形式变化的可能现实：我们自身的非人类面向，在通常条件下我们很难与之关联。这个讨论可以进一步延伸到这些领域，但相反，我要指出，即使在这里，我也无法想象有任何东西会阻止某些遥远的可能现实与我们平凡宇宙之间至少进行一些交流。这完全取决于你希望在哪里停止思考，取决于你能想象什么……
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: I don't really think we can conceive of anything to be truly "alien," though, so I use the word here only to lead into the next of Seth's larger concepts that I want to mention: that of probable realities, or probabilities, as Jane and I usually say. Not only does Seth stress the constant psychic motion of reincarnational and counterpart selves upon this earth we know--but he also tells us that each of those selves can move into other or parallel realities. I quote him from Session 681 in Volume 1 of "Unknown" Reality:
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 6: Genetic Heritage and Reincarnational Predilections

quotes.text: Such people, however, in their fashion refuse to accept standardized versions of reality. Even though they are so uncertain of themselves that their psychological patterns do follow those of culture, religion, science, or whatever, they try to use those patterns in their own individual ways. They are actually in the process of putting their own personalities together long after most people have settled upon one official version or another--and so their behavior gives glimpses of the ever-changing give-and-take among the various elements of human personality.
quotes.translation: 然而，这类人以自己的方式拒绝接受标准化的实相版本。尽管他们对自己如此不确定，以至于心理模式确实遵循文化、宗教、科学或其他框架，但他们仍试图以个人独特的方式运用这些模式。当大多数人早已选定某种官方版本时，他们实际上仍在拼凑自己的人格——因此他们的行为展现了人类人格各元素之间不断变化的互动与取舍。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: (To me:) Since your birth a probability has occurred that you could have followed, in which your wars did not happen. There is another probability in which the Second World War ended in nuclear destruction, and you did not enter that one either. You chose "this" probable reality in order to ask certain questions about the nature of man -- seeing him where he wavered equally between creativity and destruction, knowledge and ignorance; but a point that contained potentials for the most auspicious kinds of development, in your eyes. The same applies to Ruburt.
quotes.translation: （对我而言：）自你出生以来，就存在一个你可能追随的概率，其中你们的战争并未发生。另一个概率中，第二次世界大战以核毁灭告终，而你也未进入那个版本。你选择了"这个"概率实相，以便提出关于人类本质的特定问题——观察他在创造力与毁灭、知识与无知之间同样摇摆不定的状态；但这是一个在你眼中蕴含最吉祥发展潜能的节点。鲁柏的情况亦然。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Science has seen man as an accidental product of an uncaring universe, a creature literally without a center of meaning, where consciousness was the result of a physical mechanism that only happened to come into existence, and that had no reality outside of that structure. Science has at least been consistent in that respect. Christianity, however, officially asks children of sorrow to be joyful and sinners to find a childlike purity; it asks them to love a God who one day will destroy the world, and who will condemn them to hell if they do not adore him.
quotes.translation: 科学将人类视为冷漠宇宙的偶然产物，一个本质上缺乏意义中心的生物，意识仅是物理机制的结果——这种机制恰好存在，且在此结构之外毫无实相。科学至少在这方面保持了一致性。然而基督教正式要求悲伤之子欢欣、罪人重获孩童般的纯净；它要求他们爱一位终将毁灭世界的神，若不爱他，便会被判入地狱。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Finally: Just before tonight's session Jane said she thought Seth might do some work on Mass Events. She had a question about herself, and I asked for a few lines from Seth on whether she just might have been born left-handed. I want to use the material in connection with a passage I'm putting together for Volume 2 of "Unknown" Reality. [See the excerpts from Session 211, in Appendix 18 of that work.] However, Seth didn't answer my question.)
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: The inner and outer egos do not have a cementlike relationship, but can interrelate with each other in almost infinite fashions, still preserving the reality of physical experience, but varying the accents put upon it by the inner areas of subjective life. Even the bare-seeming facts of history are experienced far differently according to the symbolic content within which they are inevitably immersed. A war, in your terms, can be practically experienced as a murderous disaster, a triumph of savagery--or as a sublime victory of the human spirit over evil.
quotes.translation: 内在与外在自我并非水泥般固化的关系，而是能以近乎无限的方式相互关联，既保持物质经验的实相，又通过主观生活的内在领域调整其重点。即便是看似赤裸的历史事实，也会因其必然浸染的象征内容而被截然不同地体验。以你们的说法，一场战争既可被实际体验为血腥灾难、野蛮的胜利——也可被体验为人类精神战胜邪恶的崇高胜利。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I have mentioned that what I call the dream universe is indeed composed of molecular structure, and that it is a continuing reality, even though your own awareness of it is most usually limited, for quite necessary reasons, to the hours of your own sleep. There is a give and take here, for if you give the dream universe much of its continuing energy, much of your own energy is derived from it. There is, between the physical field and the dream universe, an interdependence that is not at all unusual, for all fields are indeed dependent one upon the other.
quotes.translation: 我曾提及，我所谓的梦境宇宙确实由分子结构构成，且是持续存在的实相——尽管出于必要原因，你们对它的觉知通常仅限于睡眠时段。这里存在能量交换：你们为梦境宇宙提供持续能量，而你们自身的许多能量也源自于此。物质场域与梦境宇宙之间存在一种毫不异常的相互依存关系，因为所有场域确实彼此依赖。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: All of your attention is focused in a highly specialized way upon one shining, bright point that you call reality. There are other realities all about you, but you ignore their existence, and you blot out all stimuli that come from them. There is a reason for such a trance, as you will discover, but little by little you must wake up. My purpose is to open your inner eyes.
quotes.translation: 你们全部注意力都以高度专门化的方式聚焦于一个称为"实相"的璀璨光点。周围存在其他实相，但你们忽视其存在，并屏蔽所有来自它们的刺激。这种出神状态自有其缘由——你们终将发现——但必须逐渐醒来。我的目的是开启你们的内在之眼。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You must still be able to experience any one of these illusions. Knowing they are illusory with full knowledge of their nature and still know that the basic reality is yourself. There is no place to go, because you are the place, in those terms, and all places. But the joke is relevant. The most important thing I have said to you this evening is that the joke is relevant.
quotes.translation: 你仍然必须能够体验这些幻象中的任何一个。在完全了解其本质的情况下知道它们是幻象，同时仍然知道基本实相就是你自己。无处可去，因为你就是那个地方，用那些术语来说，你就是所有的地方。但这个笑话是相关的。今晚我对你们说的最重要的事情就是，这个笑话是相关的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: [Each of] you will create the attributes of reality that interest you and work with them in your own way. If you want to study the nature of religion and do a good job of it, then you must be among other things a skeptic and a believer, and an Indian and a Jew, say. Otherwise you will not understand anything at all, and have a very lopsided picture. And (to a black student) you cannot know what it is like to be black in this culture -- you may not agree here -- unless you are also white in it.... Now I return you to yourselves and to your counterparts.
quotes.translation: 你们每个人都会创造自己感兴趣的实相属性，并以自己的方式与之合作。如果你想研究宗教的本质并做好这件事，那么你必须同时是一个怀疑论者和一个信徒，比如说，一个印度人和一个犹太人。否则你根本不会理解任何东西，只会有一个非常片面的画面。而且（对一个黑人学生说）你无法知道在这个文化中身为黑人是什么感觉——你可能不同意这一点——除非你同时也是其中的白人……现在我把你们交还给你们自己和你们的对应人物。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (The material in these recent excerpts rather prepared us for Seth's introduction of counterparts, then, in Session 721. In ESP class the next evening [on November 26], Seth began contending with some of the questions that instantly arose as a result of his new material. I'd just read aloud portions of the 721st session when one longtime student, whom I'll call Florence, commented that there "has to be a balance between each of us and our counterparts." Speaking strongly and humorously, Seth immediately took over the discussion.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: You have hypnotized yourselves so that it seems to you that there are great divisions between your waking and your sleeping experience. Yet each of you will fall sleep tonight, and you will have experiences that you forget only because you have been told that you cannot remember them. Many of the other dimensions of your own reality appear clearly when you are sleeping, however. When you sleep, you forget all definitions that you have placed upon yourself and your own existence through training. In sleep you use images and languages in their pure form.
quotes.translation: 你们已经催眠了自己，以至于在你们看来，清醒体验和睡眠体验之间存在巨大的鸿沟。然而，你们每个人今晚都会入睡，你们会有一些体验，你们之所以忘记它们，只是因为你们被告知无法记住它们。然而，当你们睡觉时，你们自己实相的许多其他维度会清晰地显现出来。当你们睡觉时，你们会忘记所有通过训练而加诸于自己和自身存在上的定义。在睡眠中，你们以纯粹的形式使用意象和语言。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Let us become more practical, and see how these issues merge in your reality. Some of this requires a great honesty on your own parts, as you try to recall some feelings and daydreams that you have tried to put away or forget or disown. Why are some people, then, born with conditions that are certainly experienced as genetically defective, granting even the overall value of such variances on the part of the species? For, again, I must stress the fact that in its way nature makes no such judgments, regardless of the beliefs of your science or religions.
quotes.translation: 让我们变得更实际一些，看看这些问题如何在你们的实相中融合。这其中的一部分需要你们自己极大的诚实，当你们试图回忆起那些你们试图搁置、忘记或否认的感受和白日梦时。那么，为什么有些人天生就带有肯定被体验为基因缺陷的状况，即使承认这种变异在物种层面的整体价值？因为，我再次必须强调一个事实：就其本身而言，自然并不做出这样的判断，无论你们的科学或宗教的信念如何。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You think that your own consciousness is the only logical culmination of your body's reality. You read yourself in a certain accepted fashion. In the "entire book of life," however, just physically speaking, there are interrelationships on adjacent levels that you do not perceive, as other portions of your own biological consciousness or biological language relate to the entire living fabric of the world. In physical terms you are alive because of substructures -- psychic, spiritual, and biological -- of which you have hardly any comprehension at all.
quotes.translation: 你们认为自己的意识是你们身体实相唯一合乎逻辑的顶点。你们以某种公认的方式解读自己。然而，在"生命全书"中，仅从物理角度来说，在你们没有察觉的相邻层面上存在着相互关联，就像你们自身生物意识或生物语言的其他部分与整个世界的生命结构相关联一样。从物理角度来说，你们之所以活着，是因为你们几乎完全不了解的亚结构——心灵、灵性和生物上的。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I have frequently said that beliefs cause reality, and that no symptom will simply fade away unless the "reason" is ascertained -- but such reasons go far beneath your current ideas of cause and effect. They involve intimate philosophical value judgments on the part of each individual. Beneath them, the apparent causes of limitations in personal life, there are other far-reaching beliefs, and each individual will use those elements in his private experience to back these up. This applies to any kind of lack or hindrance severe enough to be a problem.
quotes.translation: 我经常说信念导致实相，并且任何症状都不会简单地消失，除非"原因"被确定——但这些原因远远低于你们当前对因果关系的观念。它们涉及每个个体内在的、亲密的哲学价值判断。在它们之下，在个人生活中限制的明显原因之下，还有其他影响深远的信念，每个个体都会在自己的私人体验中使用这些元素来支持它们。这适用于任何严重到成为问题的匮乏或障碍。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The creative impulses are behind your languages, yet often you use the languages to silence rather than free inner communication. There have always been rhythms in consciousness that are not historically obvious. At certain times some behavior has been primarily expressed in the waking state, and sometimes in the dream state. The emphasis is never static, but ever-changing In some periods, then, the normal behavior was "more dreamlike," while more specific developments occurred in the dream state, which was then the more clear or specified of the two. Men went to sleep to do their work, in other words, and the realm of dreams was considered more real than waking reality. Now the opposite is true.
quotes.translation: 创造性的冲动是你们语言背后的驱动力，然而你们常常用语言来压制而非解放内在的沟通。意识中始终存在着历史上并不明显的节奏。在某些时期，某些行为主要显现在清醒状态，有时则显现在梦境状态。重点从来不是静止不变的，而是不断变化的。因此，在某些时期，"正常行为"更接近梦境，而更具体的发展则发生在梦境状态——那时梦境反而是两者中更清晰或更明确的状态。换句话说，人们入睡是为了工作，梦境领域被认为比清醒现实更真实。而现在的情况恰恰相反。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I never want any of my remarks to be construed in such a fashion that it seems I am in any way negating the fullness, validity, and magnificence of physical existence. I do want to point out, however, that a state you usually call dreaming is but a dim indication of an inner reality of events (intently), an inner order of events from which the physical world emerges. I hope to show how the nature of dreams has helped shape man's consciousness. I hope to show that consciousness forms the environment, and not the other way around (with many gestures).
quotes.translation: 我绝不愿我的任何言论被曲解为否定物质存在的圆满性、有效性与壮丽。然而我确实想指出，你们通常称为"做梦"的状态，不过是内在事件实相（热切地）的模糊暗示——那是物质世界从中涌现的内在事件秩序。我希望展示梦境本质如何塑造了人类的意识。我希望证明意识形成环境，而非环境形成意识（伴随许多手势）。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 881, SEPTEMBER 25, 1979
8:50 P.M. TUESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 6: Genetic Heritage and Reincarnational Predilections

quotes.text: I want it understood that we are indeed dealing with two entirely different approaches to reality and to solving problems -- methods we will here call the rational method and the magical one. The rational approach works quite well in certain situations, such as mass production of goods, or in certain kinds of scientific measurements -- but all in all the rational method, as it is understood and used, does not work as an overall approach to life, or in the solving of problems that involve subjective rather than objective measurements or calculations.
quotes.translation: 我希望你们理解，我们确实在处理两种截然不同的现实取向与问题解决方式——这里将称之为理性方法与魔法方法。理性方法在某些情境中运作良好，例如商品的大规模生产或特定科学测量——但总体而言，理性方法（按其被理解与使用的方式）并不能作为生活的整体取向，也无法解决涉及主观而非客观测量或计算的问题。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: (Slowly:) Since you are not only a physically focused creature, however, other issues also operate. You have within yourself the condensed knowledge of your entire being. This information cannot appear in any complete fashion within a consciousness connected with a physical brain. The multidimensional reality simply cannot be expressed. In the dreaming state, when consciousness relates opaquely to physical concerns, glimpses of the multidimensional self can appear in dream imagery and fantasies that will symbolically express your greater existence.
quotes.translation: （缓慢地：）然而，由于你们不仅是聚焦于物质世界的生物，其他议题也在运作。你们内在蕴含着整个存有的浓缩知识。这些信息无法以任何完整形式出现在与物质大脑相连的意识中。多维实相根本无法被表达。在梦境状态中，当意识与物质关注形成不透明关联时，多维自性的片段会以梦境意象与幻想的形式显现，象征性地表达你们更宏大的存在。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: You could not be consciously aware of those other realities all of the time, and deal with the world that you know. You have several time and space tracks in operation at once, then, but you acknowledge only certain neurological messages physically. Yet there is more to the body than you perceive of it, and this is difficult to explain to you ... If you can think of a multidimensional body existing at one time in various realities, and appearing differently within each one while still being whole, then you can get some glimpse of what is involved.3
quotes.translation: 你们不可能始终有意识地觉察那些其他实相，同时处理你们所认知的世界。因此，你们同时运作着多条时间与空间轨道，但只承认某些特定的神经信息。然而，身体远不止你们所感知到的部分——这很难向你们解释……如果你们能想象一个多维身体同时存在于各种实相中，在每个实相里呈现不同样貌却仍保持完整，那么你们就能略窥其中奥秘。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (FOR SESSION 721)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Christianity for many centuries served as an amazingly creative organizational framework, that expressed the vast complexity of the soul's reality. It also in its way managed to even focus some of man's less handsome attributes toward ends that were less reprehensible than in the past. Master events of that particular nature bring about a completely new interpretation of historic events. Their intensity, power, and seemingly impelling nature exist precisely because their origins are not physical, but are drawn from the psyche's deepest resources.
quotes.translation: 基督教在多个世纪中充当了极具创造力的组织框架，表达了灵魂实相的浩瀚复杂性。它甚至以自身方式成功将人类某些不那么光彩的特质导向比过去更少受谴责的结局。那种特定性质的主宰事件会带来对历史事件的彻底重新诠释。其强度、力量与看似不可抗拒的本质，恰恰源于它们的起源并非物质层面，而是取自心灵最深的资源。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now. I have told you that emotions also possess an electrical reality. Thoughts formed and sent out within the impulse range of emotion often succeed because of the peculiar nature of emotional electrical impulses themselves. They have a particularly strong electrical mass. They also usually fall within powerful intensities. For reasons that we will not discuss, this evening, thoughts formed under a strong emotional impetus will carry greater vividness, have a greater tendency toward duplication, and are apt to be interpreted with some success.
quotes.translation: 现在。我告诉过你，情绪也拥有一种电性实相。在情绪冲动范围内形成并发送出去的思想之所以常常成功，是因为情绪性电性冲动本身的特殊性质。它们具有特别强大的电性质量，也通常落在强烈的强度范围内。出于我们今晚不讨论的原因，在强烈情绪推动下形成的思想会携带更大的生动性，有更强的复制倾向，并且往往能被较为成功地解读。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Children understand the importance of symbols, and they use them constantly to protect themselves -- not from their own reality but from the adult world. They constantly pretend, and they quickly learn that persistent pretending in any one area will result in a physically-experienced version of the imagined activity. They also realize that they do not possess full freedom, either, for certain pretended situations will later happen in less faithful versions than the imagined ones. Others will seem almost entirely blocked, and never materialize.
quotes.translation: 孩子们理解象征的重要性，他们不断使用象征来保护自己——不是保护自己免受自身实相的影响，而是免受成人世界的影响。他们不断假装，并且很快发现，在任何领域持续假装，都会导致所想象的活动以身体经验到的版本呈现。他们也意识到自己并不拥有完全的自由，因为某些假装的情境后来会以不如想象中那么忠实的版本发生。另一些则似乎几乎完全被阻挡，从未具体化。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json
