quotes.text: I am well aware that the book raises many more questions than it presents answers for, and this has been my intent. The unknown reality will become known to the extent that you form new questions, and forget the old frameworks in which answers and myths were automatically given in response. If this book "works," then many old questions will be seen as relatively meaningless, formed not after any intimate encounter with basic issues, but in response to old dogmas.
quotes.translation: 我很清楚这本书提出的问题远多于它给出的答案，而这正是我的意图。未知的实相将变得可知，其程度取决于你提出新问题，并忘记那些自动给出答案和神话的旧框架。如果这本书“奏效”，那么许多旧问题将被视为相对无意义的，它们并非源于与基本议题的亲密接触，而是对旧教条的反应。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 743
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Ruburt instinctively likes your tree painting. It represents a certain state of consciousness -- an in-between threshold dimension of awareness, in which the imagination and the senses are almost caught in the act of putting an object together, or of bringing the world into a sensed reality, brand-new, from the realm of the inner mind: a very evocative state of consciousness, and one that as I believe Ruburt mentioned, you could also use in connection with faces.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏本能地喜欢你的树画。它代表了一种特定的意识状态——一种介于阈限之间的觉知维度，在这种状态中，想象力与感官几乎正处在将物体组合成形、或将世界从内在心灵的领域带入全新可感现实的过程之中：这是一种极具唤起力的意识状态，而且正如鲁柏提到过的那样，你也可以将其运用于面容的感知。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: For much more Seth material on the counterpart concept, see Session 732 and Appendix 25 in Volume II of The "Unknown" Reality (Published by Amber-Allen Publishing, Inc., San Rafael, CA).

quotes.text: As you know therefore we have differentiated between an outer ego, who manipulates within physical reality, and an inner ego who directs the activities of the inner self. Now then, the next step should indeed be clear. In the main, the ego deals with secondary realities, and in the main, the inner ego deals with primaries.
quotes.translation: 因此，如你所知，我们区分了在物质现实中运作的外在自我，与指挥内在自我活动的内在自我。那么，下一步确实应该很清楚了。大体上，自我处理的是次级现实，而大体上，内在自我处理的是原初现实。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: I am not speaking here of gods, but of psychological structures different from the ones you know. They cannot directly perceive or experience your reality in their "present" form of organization. In a manner of speaking they are in the background, from which the foreground of your experience emerges. They appear in your dreams in various ways. They are like the cloth from which you are cut. They are forces which have no need of names except for your convenience.
quotes.translation: 我在此并非谈论神祇，而是指与你们所知不同的心理结构。它们无法以其"当前"的组织形式直接感知或体验你们的现实。打个比方，它们处于背景之中，而你们经验的前景正是从这背景中浮现。它们以各种方式出现在你们的梦境里。它们就像裁剪出你们的布料。它们是无需名字的力量，除非为了你们的方便。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Matter, atoms and molecules, represent but one aspect and one single dimension of a far greater reality. You can manipulate only the portion of this reality which you can perceive.
quotes.translation: 物质、原子和分子，只代表一个远为宏大的现实的其中一个面向与单一维度。你们只能操控这个现实里你们所能感知的那一部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: The fly is intensely conscious, at every moment engrossed in itself and its environment, precisely tuned to elements of which you are "unconscious." There are simply different kinds of consciousness, and you cannot basically compare one to the other any more than you can compare, say, a toad to a star to an apple to a thought to a woman to a child to a native to a suburbanite to a spider to a cat. They are varieties of consciousness, each focused upon its own view of reality, each containing experience that others exclude.
quotes.translation: 苍蝇具有强烈的意识，每时每刻都沉浸于自身及其环境之中，精确地调谐于你们"无意识"的种种元素。只是存在着不同种类的意识，你基本上无法将一种与另一种相比较，就像你无法将一只蟾蜍与一颗星星、一个苹果与一个念头、一个女人与一个孩子、一个原住民与一个郊区居民、一只蜘蛛与一只猫相比较一样。它们是意识的变体，每一种都聚焦于自己对现实的看法，每一种都包含着其他意识所排除的经验。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: I said (in sessions 711 and 716, for instance) that your normal focus of consciousness can be compared to your home station. So far, exercises have been described that will gently lead you away from concentration upon this home base, even while its structure is strengthened at the same time. You can also call this home station or local program your world view, since from it you perceive your reality. To some extent it represents your personal focus, through which you interpret most of your experience. As I mentioned (in Session 715, for instance), when you begin to move away from that particular organization, strange things may start to happen. You may be filled with wonder, excitement, or perplexity. You may be delighted or appalled, according to whether or not your new perceptions agree or disagree with your established world view.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Now, probabilities exist in the same manner. They simply are not concrete for you, as dreams are not concrete for you. You may dream for example of holding an apple, then wake up and the apple is gone. This does not mean that the apple did not exist. It simply means that in the waking state you are not aware of its reality. You do not experience it, or perceive it. In the same manner you do not perceive or experience physically the actuality of probable events.
quotes.translation: 现在，可能性以同样的方式存在。它们只是对你们而言不具体，就像梦境对你们而言不具体一样。例如，你可能梦见自己拿着一个苹果，然后醒来，苹果就不见了。这并不意味着那个苹果不存在。它仅仅意味着在清醒状态下，你意识不到它的现实性。你没有体验它，也没有感知它。同样地，你也没有在物质上感知或体验可能事件的实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: The psychological frameworks behind physical reality are also composed of energy, and are the building blocks that I have mentioned earlier. Their expansion is not limited by any of your known, or misinterpreted, laws of space or time or thermodynamics, since they exist in a dimension where such laws simply do not apply.
quotes.translation: 物质实相背后的心理架构同样由能量构成，且是我先前提及的构建基石。它们的扩展不受任何你所知或误解的空间、时间或热力学定律限制，因为它们存在于这些定律根本不适用的维度中。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: (A one-minute pause.) The greatest scientific discoveries are always "accidents." They come from intuitional creativity, when suddenly a new kind of significance is seen that was not "earlier" predictable. You accept all data that fit your theories, and ignore clues to the contrary. Yet underneath it all you are significance-making creatures, pattern-formers, immersed in time but basically apart from it, and so new insights come into your awareness and literally change the quality of any given reality at any given time.
quotes.translation: （停顿一分钟。）最伟大的科学发现总是"意外"的。它们来自直觉创造力，当突然看到一种"先前"无法预测的新意义时。你接受所有符合你理论的数据，却忽略相反线索。然而在这一切之下，你们是创造意义的生物、模式构建者，沉浸于时间之中却又从根本上超越时间，因此新洞见进入你的意识，并在任何特定时刻切实改变任何特定实相的本质。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 694
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: I will tell you the book, in what Ruburt thinks of as its somewhat inane simplicity, is very profound. Nevertheless for others there are steps that must be followed through, and following these steps through brings the whole personality, including the intellect, to a more balanced understanding, which is my goal. The author, while basically correct, ignores for example the reality of reincarnation; and Ruburts protests to the contrary, reincarnation belongs both with metaphysics and psychology, and cannot be ignored.
quotes.translation: 我要告诉你，鲁柏认为那本书有些空洞的简单，实则非常深奥。但对其他人而言，必须遵循某些步骤，而遵循这些步骤能使整个人格（包括智力）达到更平衡的理解——这正是我的目标。那位作者虽然基本正确，却忽略了转世轮回的实相；而鲁柏虽持异议，但转世既属于形而上学也属于心理学，不可忽视。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Extension of energy must have contraction as an after effect, and contraction will lead to extension. Therefore, if you begin in an attempt to contract your consciousness and energy, in the hopes of attaining communication with inner reality, then indeed will your consciousness, closed and contracted like a tight fist, open and expand. And if you seek to communicate by expansion of consciousness, then will this expansion come crushing and closing upon itself.
quotes.translation: 能量的扩展必然伴随收缩作为后续效应，而收缩又将导向扩展。因此，若你试图收缩意识与能量以求与内在实相沟通，那么你紧闭收缩如紧握拳头的意识，终将张开并扩展。若你试图通过扩展意识来沟通，那么这种扩展将自行崩塌闭合。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The sound of your inner thoughts is the medium that you actually use. This is far more than an analogy, however, for in simple terms it explains quite clearly the way in which your beliefs form your reality. In quiet moments the word "O-O-O-O-O-M-M-M-M-M," said slowly, mentally or aloud, will be of benefit in toning up your general physical condition. The sounds contain within them a built-in impetus toward energy and well-being, as I will explain shortly.
quotes.translation: 你内在思想的声音才是你实际使用的媒介。这远非比喻，因为简单来说，它清晰解释了你的信念如何形成你的实相。在宁静时刻，缓慢地默念或出声念诵"O-O-O-O-O-M-M-M-M-M"，将有益于调谐你的整体身体状况。这些声音本身蕴含着朝向能量与福祉的内在动力，我稍后会解释。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Now, I would suggest that two main categories in particular be given special notice. These as you know will have to do with primary and secondary reality. And each dream should be scrutinized first of all with these main points in mind. You must look out for pitfalls, and use your critical faculties. Ruburt uses his critical faculties supremely well in scrutinizing my activities and nature, so I have no doubt that he can also apply them to the job at hand.
quotes.translation: 现在，我建议特别关注两个主要类别。如你所知，这涉及首要实相与次要实相。每个梦境都应首先以这些要点进行审视。你必须警惕陷阱，运用批判能力。鲁柏在审视我的活动与本质时，将批判能力运用得极为出色，因此我毫不怀疑他也能将其应用于手头的工作。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: They serve to mask other quite valid realities that exist at the same time, however, and actually from these other realities you gain the power and the knowledge to operate the material projections. You can "set the machine on idle," so to speak, stop the apparent motion, and turn your attention to these realities.
quotes.translation: 然而，它们也同时掩盖了其他同样有效的实相，而实际上，正是从这些其他实相中，你获得了运作物质投射的力量与知识。你可以说，让机器“空转”，停止表面的运动，将注意力转向这些实相。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: As you form your physical existence individually and collectively, so after the time of choosing, you join others who have decided upon the same general kind of experience. A strong cooperative venture is then begun as preparations are made. These will vary according to the type of existence chosen. There are general patterns, therefore. No individual's reality is identical with another's, and yet there are overall groupings.
quotes.translation: 当你个体和集体地形成你的物质存在时，在选择的时刻之后，你会加入那些已决定体验相同一般经验类型的其他人。随后，随着准备的进行，一个强大的合作事业便开始了。这些准备会根据所选择的存在类型而有所不同。因此，存在着一般的模式。没有一个个体的实相与另一个完全相同，然而却存在着总体性的分组。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: What happens to a thought when it leaves your conscious mind? It does not simply disappear. You can learn to follow it, but you are usually frightened of turning your attention away from its intense focus in three-dimensional existence. Therefore, it seems that the thought disappears. It seems also that your subjectivity has a mysterious unknown quality about it, and that even your mental life has a sort of insidious dropping-off point, a subjective cliff over which thoughts and memories fall, to disappear into nothingness. Therefore to protect yourself, to protect your subjectivity from drifting, you erect various psychological barriers at what you suppose to be the danger points. Instead, you see, you can follow these thoughts and emotions simply by realizing that your own reality continues in another direction, beside the one with which you mainly identify. For these thoughts and emotions that have left your conscious mind will lead you into other environments.
quotes.translation: 当一个念头离开你的意识心时，它会发生什么？它并不会简单地消失。你可以学会跟随它，但你通常害怕将注意力从其在三维存在中的强烈焦点上移开。因此，念头似乎消失了。你的主观性似乎也具有一种神秘未知的特质，甚至你的精神生活也有一种阴险的跌落点，一个主观的悬崖，思想和记忆从那里坠落，消失于虚无之中。因此，为了保护你自己，保护你的主观性免于漂泊，你在你认为是危险点的地方竖立起各种心理屏障。相反，你看，你可以通过认识到你自己的实相在你主要认同的那个方向之外，继续在另一个方向上存在，来跟随这些念头和情感。因为这些已经离开你意识心的念头和情感将引领你进入其他环境。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In other portions of the Seth material these processes will be made very clear for those of you who desire to pursue the question further, or those who may be interested from a scientific point of view. Here, we are discussing such issues only because they touch upon the multidimensional aspect of personality. They allow you to materialize certain subjective experiences into three-dimensional reality. Before I leave the subject, however, let me remind you that these emanations in varying degrees rise from all consciousness, not simply your own. This includes cellular consciousness as well, so that an invisible network of electromagnetic units pervades your entire atmosphere; and upon this webwork and from it, the particles of physical matter are then formed.
quotes.translation: 在赛斯资料的其他部分，这些过程对于那些希望进一步探讨此问题的人，或那些可能从科学角度感兴趣的人来说，将会变得非常清晰。在这里，我们讨论这些问题仅仅是因为它们触及了人格的多维面向。它们允许你将某些主观体验物质化为三维实相。然而，在我离开这个主题之前，让我提醒你，这些放射以不同程度源自所有意识，而不仅仅是你自己的。这还包括细胞意识，因此一个由电磁单元组成的无形网络弥漫于你的整个大气之中；而物质粒子的物理物质便是基于这个网络并从它形成。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It goes without saying then that the soul does not require a physical body for purposes of perception; that perception is not dependent upon physical senses; that experience continues whether or not you are in this life or another; and also that the soul's basic methods of perception are also operating within you now even as you read this book. It also follows that your experience within the physical system is dependent upon a physical form and physical senses -- again, because these interpret reality and translate it into physical data. It also follows that some hints of the soul's direct experience can be gained by momentarily switching the physical senses off -- by refusing to use them as perceptors, and falling back upon other methods. Now you do this to some extent in the dream state, but even then in many dreams you still tend to translate experience into hallucinatory physical terms. Most of the dreams that you recall are of this nature.
quotes.translation: 那么，不言而喻，灵魂并不需要物质身体来进行感知；感知并不依赖于物质感官；无论你是在此生还是另一生，体验都持续存在；并且，灵魂的基本感知方法此刻也在你之内运作，即使在你阅读这本书时也是如此。同样地，你在物质系统内的体验依赖于物质形态和物质感官——再次强调，因为这些将实相诠释并转化为物质数据。同样地，通过暂时关闭物质感官——拒绝将它们用作感知器，并退而依靠其他方法——可以获得一些关于灵魂直接体验的线索。现在，你在梦境状态中在一定程度上做到了这一点，但即使在许多梦中，你仍然倾向于将体验转化为幻觉性的物质术语。你所记得的大多数梦都具有这种性质。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now, no psychological structure is easy to describe in words. Simply to explain the nature of personality as it is generally known, all kinds of terms are used: id, subconscious, ego, superego; all of these to differentiate the interweaving actions that make up the physical personality. The dreaming self is just as complicated. So you can say that certain portions of it deal with physical reality, physical manipulation, and plans; some with deeper levels of creativity and achievement that insure physical survival; some with communication, with even more extensive elements of the personality now generally unknown; some with the continuing experience and existence of what you may call the soul or overall individual entity, the true multidimensional self.
quotes.translation: 现在，任何心理结构都难以用言语描述。仅仅为了解释通常所知的人格本质，就使用了各种术语：本我、潜意识、自我、超我；所有这些都是为了区分构成物质人格的交织行动。做梦的自己同样复杂。因此，你可以说，它的某些部分处理物质实相、物质操作和计划；某些部分处理确保物质生存的更深层次的创造力和成就；某些部分处理沟通，以及人格中通常未知的更广泛元素；某些部分处理你所谓的灵魂或整体个体实相，即真正的多维自体的持续体验和存在。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You may feel that you want to "relive" certain episodes of your life so that you can understand them better. Your life's experience, therefore, is your own. Such conditions certainly are not alien. In ordinary living, you often imagine yourself behaving in a different manner than you did, or in your mind reexperiencing events in order to gain greater understanding from them. Your life is your own personal experience- perspective, and when at death you take it out of the mass physical time context, then you can experience it in many ways. Events and objects are not absolute, remember, but plastic. Events can be changed both before and after their occurrence. They are never stable or permanent, even though within the context of three-dimensional reality they may appear so.
quotes.translation: 你或许觉得想要“重新体验”生命中的某些片段，以便更深入地理解它们。因此，你的人生经历是属于你自己的。这样的情境当然并不陌生。在日常生活中，你常常想象自己以不同于实际的方式行事，或在脑海中重新经历事件，以求从中获得更深刻的理解。你的人生是你个人的经验视角，当你在死亡时将其从大众的物理时间背景中抽离出来，你便能够以多种方式体验它。记住，事件和物体并非绝对，而是可塑的。事件在其发生前后都可以被改变。它们从不稳定或永恒，即便在三维实相的背景下它们可能显得如此。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Since consciousness forms matter, and not the other way around, then thought exists before the brain and after it. A child can think coherently before he learns vocabulary -- but he cannot impress the physical universe in its terms. So this inner knowledge has always been available, but is to become physically manifest -- literally made flesh. The Speakers were the first to impress this inner knowledge upon the physical system, to make it physically known. Sometimes only one or two Speakers were alive in several centuries. Sometimes there were many. They looked around them and knew that the world sprang from their interior reality. They told others. They knew (pause) that the seemingly solid natural objects about them were composed of many minute consciousnesses.
quotes.translation: 既然意识形成物质，而非相反，那么思想存在于大脑之前和之后。一个孩子在学会词汇之前就能连贯地思考——但他无法以其术语来影响物理宇宙。因此，这种内在知识一直可得，但需要变得物理上显化——literally made flesh。传讯者是最早将这种内在知识印刻在物理系统上、使其在物理层面被知晓的人。有时几个世纪里只有一两位传讯者活着。有时则有很多。他们环顾四周，知道世界源自他们的内在实相。他们告诉他人。他们知道（停顿）周围看似坚实的自然物体是由许多微小的意识组成的。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: After death, for example, an individual may continue to create these -- masses of individuals may -- until they realize that the frameworks are no longer necessary. The Speakers were not confined in their activities, therefore, to waking consciousness. In all periods of your time they went about their duties both in the waking and sleep state. Much of the most pertinent information, in fact, was memorized by trainees during the dream condition, and passed on in the same manner. These unwritten manuscripts therefore were also illustrated, so to speak, by dream journeys or field trips into other kinds of reality. Such training still goes on. The particular psychic or story framework may vary. For example, conventional images of the Christian God and the saints may be utilized by the Speakers, with all of this highly vivid. The dreamer may find himself then in a magnificent harem, or instead in a brilliantly illuminated field or sky. Some Speakers confine their abilities to the dream state; and, waking, are largely unconscious of their own abilities or experience.
quotes.translation: 例如，死后，个体可能会继续创造这些——大量个体可能会——直到他们意识到这些框架不再必要。因此，传讯者的活动并不局限于清醒意识。在你所有的时间段里，他们在清醒和睡眠状态中都履行着职责。事实上，许多最相关的信息是在梦境中被受训者记住，并以同样方式传递的。这些未写下的手稿，也可以说，通过梦境旅程或进入其他实相的实地考察而得以阐释。这种训练仍在继续。特定的心灵或故事框架可能有所不同。例如，基督教上帝和圣人的传统形象可能被传讯者利用，且这一切都极为生动。做梦者可能发现自己身处一个宏伟的后宫，或者在一片光芒四射的田野或天空中。有些传讯者将他们的能力局限于梦境状态；而在清醒时，他们大多对自己的能力或经验毫无意识。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Within these units there is, again, a propensity for growth and organization. Within a literally infinite field of activity, meaningful order arose out of the propensity for significance. Briefly, certain units would settle upon various kinds of organization, find these significant, then build upon them and attract others of the same nature. So were various systems of reality formed. (Pause.) The particular kind of significance settled upon would act both as a directive for experience and as a method of erecting effective boundaries, within which the selected kind of behavior would continue. The units can and do intermix, yet because of the propensity for selectivity and significance, whole groups of them will "repel" other whole groups, thus providing a protective inner system of interaction.
quotes.translation: 在这些单位内，再次存在着成长和组织的倾向。在一个literally无限的活动中，有意义的秩序从对意义的倾向中产生。简而言之，某些单位会定居于各种组织方式，发现这些方式有意义，然后在此基础上构建，并吸引其他相同性质的单位。于是形成了各种实相系统。（停顿。）被选定的特定意义既会作为经验的指引，也会作为建立有效边界的方法，在此边界内，选定的行为模式会持续。这些单位可以也确实会混合，但由于选择性和意义的倾向，整个群体将“排斥”其他整个群体，从而提供一个保护性的内在互动系统。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 683
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: You can understand what is meant by saying that your consciousness fluctuates -- for each individual is aware of various intensities and concentrations. You are more alert, or, in your terms more conscious on some occasions than others. Now the same applies to these units of consciousness -- and to atoms, molecules, electrons, and other such phenomena. The world literally blinks off and on. This reality of fluctuation in no way bothers your own feeling of consistency, however. The "holes (spelled) of nonexistence" are plugged up by the process of selectivity. This process chooses significances then, again, around which experience is built, and around which "life" is felt. The very sensations of one kind of life then automatically set up barriers against other such "world-schemes" (hyphen) that do not correlate with their own.
quotes.translation: 你可以理解所谓你的意识波动是什么意思——因为每个个体都意识到各种强度和集中度。在某些场合，你比在其他场合更警觉，或者用你的话说，更有意识。现在，同样的道理也适用于这些意识单位——以及原子、分子、电子和其他此类现象。世界literally闪烁明灭。然而，这种波动的实相丝毫不会影响你自己的一致性感觉。然而，“不存在的空洞”被选择过程所填补。这个过程再次选择意义，围绕这些意义构建经验，并围绕这些意义感受“生命”。一种生命的特定感觉会自动设置屏障，以对抗其他与其自身不相关的“世界方案”。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 684
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Your conscious mind tells you where you are in time and space, and directs your activity in a world of human action. That world has its own kind of rich complication, that is as unknown to the animals as is much of their acute realization unknown to you. Because you have a conscious mind, then, other portions of your being rely upon it to give them an adequate picture of your situation, and to give the conscious orders for action. These orders will then be carried out. To do this, you must use that mind as completely as possible. The picture of reality in time and space that you give to your cells must be accurate. They must act on a minute-to-minute, second-to-second, microsecond-to-microsecond basis, even though their own orientation is not familiar with your time concept.
quotes.translation: 你的意识心智告诉你你在时空中的位置，并在人类行动的世界中指导你的活动。那个世界有其自身的丰富复杂性，这种复杂性对动物来说是未知的，正如它们许多敏锐的认知对你来说是未知的一样。因为你拥有意识心智，所以你存在的其他部分依赖它来提供你处境的适当画面，并给出行动的意识指令。这些指令随后将被执行。要做到这一点，你必须尽可能充分地使用那个心智。你给予细胞的时空实相画面必须准确。它们必须在每分钟、每秒、微秒的基础上行动，即使它们自身的取向不熟悉你的时间概念。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 691
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: You dream, each of you, but there are few great dream artists. Many of the true purposes of dreams1 have been forgotten, even though those purposes are still being fulfilled. The conscious art of creating, understanding, and using dreams has been largely lost; and the intimate relationship between daily life, world events, and dreams almost completely ignored. The "future" of the species is being worked out in the private and mass dreams of its members, but this also is never considered. The members of some ancient civilizations, including the Egyptians, knew how to be the conscious directors of dream activity, how to delve into various levels of dream reality to the founts of creativity, and they were able to use that source material in their physical world.2
quotes.translation: 你们每个人都会做梦，但伟大的梦艺术家寥寥无几。梦的许多真实目的已被遗忘，尽管这些目的仍在实现。创造、理解并运用梦的有意识艺术已基本失传；日常生活、世界事件与梦之间的亲密关系也几乎被完全忽视。物种的"未来"正在其成员的私人梦境与集体梦境中酝酿成形，但这一点同样从未被考虑。包括埃及人在内的某些古代文明成员，曾知晓如何成为梦活动有意识的导演，如何深入梦实相的不同层次直达创造力的源泉，并能在物质世界中运用那些源头材料。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 698
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: I am simply suggesting that you become more natural. Because science has made an effective barrier to that method of approach, the power seems to reside in the gadgets rather than in man. Man no longer identifies with a storm, for example, and has lost his sense of relationship with it, and therefore his natural power over it. The same applies to storms of the psyche. The dream-art scientist, the true mental physicist, the complete physician -- such designations represent the kinds of training that could allow you to understand the unknown, and therefore the known reality, and so become aware of the blueprints that exist behind the physical universe. The proof is in the pudding, of course. Largely, it seems that your techniques work a good deal of the time. Let us look at medicine, for instance.
quotes.translation: 我只是建议你们变得更自然。由于科学已为这种接近方法设置了有效屏障，力量似乎存在于小装置中而非人类自身。例如，人类不再与风暴认同，失去了与它的关联感，因而也失去了对它的自然掌控力。这同样适用于心灵的风暴。梦艺术科学家、真正的精神物理学家、完整的医师——这些称谓代表着能让你们理解未知（因而也理解已知实相）的训练类型，从而意识到存在于物质宇宙背后的蓝图。当然，实践是检验真理的标准。大体而言，你们的技术似乎大部分时间都有效。让我们以医学为例。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 704
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Each individual is a universe in a small package. (A one-minute pause.) As the physical planets move in order while being individual, so there can be a social order that is based upon the integrity of the individual. But that order would recognize the inner validity that is within the self; and the inner order, unseen, that forms the integrity of the physical body, likewise would form the integrity of the social body. The self, the individual, being its fulfilled self, would automatically function for the good of itself and for the good of society. The individual's good, therefore, is the society's good, and represents spiritual and physical fulfillment. This presupposes, however, an understanding of the inner self and an exploration into the unknown reality of the individual psyche.
quotes.translation: 每个个体都是一个浓缩的宇宙。（停顿一分钟。）如同物质行星在保持个体性的同时有序运行，社会秩序也可以建立在个体完整性的基础上。但这种秩序会承认自我内在的有效性；那看不见的内在秩序，既构成物质身体的完整性，同样也会构成社会身体的完整性。自我、个体，当其成为圆满的自身时，会自动为自身利益与社会利益运作。因此，个体的善即是社会的善，代表着灵性与物质的圆满实现。然而，这预设了对内在自我的理解，以及对个体心灵未知实相的探索。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 704
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Your sexuality is a point of focus, and that is all. For those of you who need it said, I say it: A woman is as intellectual as a man. A man is as intuitive as a woman. You chose your sexual focus for a reason. The reason has more to do with the flexibility of consciousness than you presently understand. It has to do with the real nature of aggression and passivity,1 which you have allowed yourselves to forget ... Birth is an aggressive experience. Passivity is based upon the joyful recognition of natural aggression. To be carried along, each of you must be very sure of yourself. To allow yourself what is now, in your terms, a luxury of passivity, you must have confidence in the nature of your own reality and strength. Otherwise passivity frightens you to the core.
quotes.translation: 你们的性是一个焦点，仅此而已。对于那些需要听到这句话的人，我直言：女性与男性同样理智。男性与女性同样直觉。你们选择性的焦点自有其理由。这个理由与意识的灵活性有关，其关联程度远超你们当前的理解。它涉及攻击性与被动性的真实本质——你们已允许自己遗忘这一点……出生是一种攻击性体验。被动性建立在对自然攻击性的喜悦认知之上。要随波逐流，你们每个人都必须非常确信自己。要允许自己享有——用你们的话说——被动的奢侈，你们必须对自己实相与力量的本性有足够信心。否则被动性会让你们恐惧到骨子里。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (FOR SESSION 690)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Some individuals find themselves with memories of other lives, which are other days to the soul. Such persons then become aware of a greater consciousness reaching over those gaps, and realize that earthly experience can contain [among other things] a knowledge of existence in more than one body. Inherently then consciousness, affiliated with the flesh, can indeed carry such comprehensions. The mind of man as you know it shows at least the potential ability for handling a kind of memory with which you are usually not acquainted. This means that even biologically the species is equipped to deal with different sequences of time, while still manipulating within one particular time scheme. This also implies a far greater psychological richness -- quite possible, again, within corporal reality -- in which many levels of relationships can be handled. Such inner knowledge is inherent in the cells, and in ordinary terms of evolution is quite possible as a "future" development.
quotes.translation: 有些人发现自己拥有其他生命的记忆——那些对灵魂而言不过是其他日子。这样的人于是意识到一种更伟大的意识跨越那些间隙，并认识到尘世经验可以包含（除其他事物外）对存在于不止一个身体中的认知。因此，与血肉相连的意识，本质上确实能够承载这样的理解。你们所知的人类心智至少显示出处理某种你们通常不熟悉的记忆的潜在能力。这意味着即使在生物学层面，这个物种也装备了处理不同时间序列的能力，同时仍能在特定时间框架内运作。这也暗示着一种远为丰富的心理维度——再次强调，这在肉体实相中完全可能——其中可以处理多重层次的关系。这种内在知识存在于细胞中，用进化的普通术语来说，作为"未来"的发展是完全可能的。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. quotes.text: I have said (as at 9:48) that the body can indeed carry on, performing necessary maintenance activities while the main consciousness is detached from it. To some extent it can even perform simple chores. (Pause.) In sleep, in fact, it is not at all necessary that the main consciousness be alert in the body. Only in certain kinds of civilizations, for that matter, is such a close body-and-main-consciousness relationship necessary. There are other situations, therefore, in which consciousness ordinarily strays much further, returning to the body as a home station and basis of operation, relying upon it for certain kinds of perception only, but not depending upon it for the entire picture of reality. Physical life alone does not necessarily require the kind of identification of self with flesh that is your own.
quotes.translation: 我曾说过（如9:48所述），当主要意识脱离身体时，身体确实能够继续执行必要的维护活动。在某种程度上，它甚至能执行简单的杂务。（停顿。）事实上，在睡眠中，主要意识完全不必在身体中保持警觉。就此而言，只有在某些特定文明中，这种身体与主要意识的紧密关系才是必要的。因此，还存在其他情境，其中意识通常会游离得更远，将身体作为归航站和操作基地，仅依赖它获取特定类型的感知，但并不依赖它获得实相的全貌。仅仅物质生命本身，并不必然需要你们那种自我与肉身的认同。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (10:55: Jane's delivery had become somewhat faster and very absorbed as it progressed. Just as they had before our three-month layoff from book work [after Session 707], her trances for "Unknown" Reality were proving to be more "difficult" to initiate than those for the previous Seth books.9 She often had to wait for just the right moment to get back into dictation following a break, too; so tonight, after we'd shared an apple, she sat rather impatiently anticipating Seth's return. Resume, finally, at 11:25.)

quotes.text: Your experience of yourself marks the seeming boundaries of yourself. In a manner of speaking, I am one personality and one program or station. Ruburt is another. We have learned to be aware of each other* to communicate between stations, to affect each other's programs and to change each other's worlds. I do not speak alone to Ruburt and Joseph, for example, but my words go out to the world that you know. Still within your framework, Ruburt tunes in to another station, translates it and broadcasts the information. To do this, however, he has to alter his own consciousness, withdraw momentarily from the official station to bring in this one. That means tuning in to other portions of the psyche, as well as another kind of reality. The final translation of my material has to come through his organism, however, or it would be meaningless to you.
quotes.translation: 你对自己的体验标定了你自身看似存在的界限。可以说，我是一个人格、一个程序或一个电台。鲁柏是另一个。我们学会了彼此觉察，在电台之间沟通，影响彼此的程序，并改变彼此的世界。举例来说，我并非只对鲁柏和约瑟说话，而是我的话语会传向你所知的世界。然而，在你的架构内，鲁柏调频到另一个电台，进行翻译，并广播这些信息。但要做到这一点，他必须改变自己的意识，暂时脱离官方电台，以接入这个电台。这意味着他要调频到心灵的其他部分，以及另一种实相。不过，我的资料最终必须通过他的有机体来翻译，否则对你们而言将毫无意义。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The other [Saturday] evening while he was in bed Ruburt had a somewhat surprising experience. He was not dreaming. His body was asleep but his consciousness was drifting. He clearly heard my voice. It seemed to come literally from out of the sky, down into another room outside of (next-door to, actually) the one in which his body slept. For a moment the power frightened him, for it sounded like a radio turned up to an incredible degree -- louder than thunder. At the time words were clearly distinguishable, though later he forgot what they said. For an instant he was tempted to interpret the power as anger, for in your world when someone is shouting they are usually angry. He realized, however, that something else was involved. He did not sense my presence, but only heard the thunder of the voice. It shocked him because he is used to hearing my words from within his head -- he had never before been aware of my voice as existing apart from him. In the dream state he has heard me giving him information. In these instances, however, he was the channel through which my voice came. He has often wondered about the nature of my own independence, and the kind of reality in which I exist.
quotes.translation: 另一个（周六）晚上，鲁柏躺在床上时经历了一次颇为惊人的体验。他并非在做梦。他的身体睡着了，但他的意识在飘荡。他清晰地听到了我的声音。那声音仿佛真的从天而降，落入他身体所睡房间之外的另一个房间（实际上是隔壁）。那一刻，那力量让他害怕，因为它听起来像收音机被调到了难以置信的音量——比雷声还响。当时话语清晰可辨，尽管他后来忘记了它们说了什么。有一瞬间，他忍不住将那力量解释为愤怒，因为在你们的世界里，当有人大喊大叫时，他们通常是生气的。然而，他意识到其中另有玄机。他没有感知到我的临在，只听到了那声音的轰鸣。这让他震惊，因为他习惯于从自己脑袋里听到我的话语——他以前从未意识到我的声音是独立于他而存在的。在梦境状态中，他听到过我给他信息。但在那些情况下，他是我的声音得以传出的通道。他常常思考我自身独立性的本质，以及我所存在的实相类型。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: There are good reasons for conventions. Generally, they help organize experience. If they are lightly held to and accepted, they can serve well as guidelines. Applied with a heavy hand they become unnecessary dogma, rigidly limiting experience. This applies to inner and outer activity. Conventions are the results of stratified and rigid "spontaneity." At one time, in your terms, each custom had a meaning. Each represented a spontaneous gesture, an individual reaction. When these become a system of order, however, the original spontaneity is lost, and you project an artificial order that serves to stratify behavior rather than to express it. So there are psychic customs as there are physical ones, religious and psychic dogmas, guided tours of consciousness in which you are told to follow a certain line or a certain program. You become afraid of your private interpretation of whatever reality you find yourself experiencing.
quotes.translation: 惯例自有其存在的充分理由。一般来说，它们有助于组织经验。如果人们轻松地持有并接受它们，它们就能很好地充当指引。但如果运用得过于严苛，它们就会变成不必要的教条，严格地限制经验。这适用于内在和外在的活动。惯例是分层化且僵化的“自发性”的结果。用你们的话来说，在某个时期，每一种习俗都有其意义。每一种都代表着一个自发的姿态，一种个体的反应。然而，当这些变成一种秩序系统时，最初的自发性就丧失了，你们投射出一种人为的秩序，这种秩序的作用是固化行为，而非表达行为。因此，有心灵的习俗，正如有身体的习俗一样；有宗教和心灵的教条，有意识的有导游的旅行，在其中你被告知要遵循某条路线或某个程序。你开始害怕自己对所经历的任何实相的私人解读。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: These "other," reinterpreted world views form a matrix from which new creativity emerges. The same thing applies in more mundane endeavors in ordinary life. For example: You may be in a predicament that seems beyond solving. It may be highly individual, since it is yours. It is unique, and has happened in no other way before. No one else has viewed your particular dilemma through your eyes, yet others have been in similar situations, solved the challenges involved, and gone on to greater creativity and fulfillment. If you can momentarily abandon your private world view, that focus from which you experience reality, then you can allow the experience of others who have had similar challenges to color your perception. You can tune in to their solutions and apply them to your particular circumstances. You often do this unconsciously. I do not want you to think, then, that such occurrences work only in esoteric terms.
quotes.translation: 这些“其他的”、被重新诠释的世界观形成了一个矩阵，新的创造力从中涌现。同样的事情也适用于日常生活中更平凡的追求。例如：你可能陷入一个看似无法解决的困境。它可能极具个人色彩，因为它是你的。它是独特的，以前从未以其他方式发生过。没有其他人通过你的眼睛看待你的特定困境，然而其他人也曾处于类似情境，解决了所涉及的挑战，并走向了更大的创造力和成就。如果你能暂时放弃你的私人世界观，即你体验实相的那个焦点，那么你就能让那些经历过类似挑战之人的经验来影响你的感知。你可以调频到他们的解决方案，并将其应用于你的特定情况。你常常无意识地这样做。那么，我不希望你认为这类事情只发生在深奥的领域。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: I said (in sessions 711 and 716, for instance) that your normal focus of consciousness can be compared to your home station. So far, exercises have been described that will gently lead you away from concentration upon this home base, even while its structure is strengthened at the same time. You can also call this home station or local program your world view, since from it you perceive your reality. To some extent it represents your personal focus, through which you interpret most of your experience. As I mentioned (in Session 715, for instance), when you begin to move away from that particular organization, strange things may start to happen. You may be filled with wonder, excitement, or perplexity. You may be delighted or appalled, according to whether or not your new perceptions agree or disagree with your established world view.

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... Selfhood overspills with great luxurious outcroppings, yet you jealously guard against such creativity. To a certain extent you do carry the knowledge of your forefathers within your [cells'] chromosomes,1 which present a pattern that is not rigid but flexible -- one that in codified fashion endows you with the subjective living experience of those who, in your terms, have gone before. As Ruburt recently suspected, some very old cultures have been aware of this.2 Period. While being independent individuals their members also identified with their ancestors to some extent, accepting them as portions of their selfhoods. This does not mean that the individual self was less, but was more aware of its own reality. A completely different kind of focus was presented, in which the ancestors were understood to contribute to the "new" experience of the living; one in which the physically focused consciousness clearly saw itself as perceiving the world for itself, but also for all of those who had gone before -- (gradually louder for emphasis:) while realizing that in those terms he or she would contribute as well as the generations past.
quotes.translation: 给我们一点时间……自体性充满了大量丰富的涌现，然而你们却嫉妒地防范着这种创造力。在某种程度上，你们确实将祖先的知识携带在你们细胞的染色体中，这些染色体呈现出一个并非僵化而是灵活的模式——一个以编码方式赋予你们主观生活经验的模式，这些经验来自那些，用你们的话说，已经逝去的人。正如鲁柏最近所怀疑的，一些非常古老的文化已经意识到了这一点。句号。在作为独立个体的同时，他们的成员也在某种程度上认同他们的祖先，将祖先视为他们自体性的一部分。这并不意味着个体自体变得更少，而是对其自身实相有了更多觉知。一种完全不同的焦点被呈现出来，在这种焦点中，祖先被认为为生者的“新”经验做出了贡献；在这种焦点中，以身体为中心的觉知清晰地看到自己既为自己感知世界，也为所有那些已逝去的人感知世界——（逐渐提高音量以强调：）同时认识到，用那些术语来说，他或她也将像过去的世代一样做出贡献。

quotes.text: Now there are books programming out-of-body activity; millions of you are told that when you leave your body you will meet this demon or that demon, or this or that angry god. So, instead, we will form a free city to which those travelers can come, and where those who enter can read books about Buddhism if they prefer, or play at being Catholic. There will also be certain beloved traps set about the city, that will be of an enlightening nature ... Now listen: You think there is nothing intrinsically impossible about building a platform in [your] space ... I am suggesting, then, a platform in inner reality. It is as valid -- far more valid -- as an orbiting city in the sky, in physical terms, and it challenges your creative abilities much more. You need a good challenge -- it is fun! Not because you should do it, but because you desire it ... It is a great creative challenge that you can throw down to yourselves from your future selves.
quotes.translation: 现在有书籍规划着出体活动；你们中有数百万人被告知，当你离开身体时，你会遇到这个恶魔或那个恶魔，或这个或那个愤怒的神。所以，相反，我们将建立一个自由之城，那些旅行者可以来到那里，进入其中的人如果愿意可以阅读关于佛教的书籍，或者玩扮演天主教徒的游戏。城里还会设置一些可爱的陷阱，它们将具有启发性……现在听着：你们认为在你们的空间中建造一个平台并非本质上不可能……那么，我建议的是内在实相中的一个平台。它和物理意义上天空中一个轨道城市一样有效——甚至有效得多，并且它更能挑战你们的创造能力。你们需要一个好的挑战——这很有趣！不是因为你们应该去做，而是因为你们渴望它……这是一个伟大的创造性挑战，你们可以从未来的自己那里向自己抛出。

quotes.text: "Seth's data and my own on counterparts make sense to me. I feel (as Seth mentioned in the 721st session) that I wasn't Nebene, or two different Roman soldiers per se, but rather that my whole self chose to manifest such personalities together; that I, too, am such a manifestation at a "later" time, then, and that from my own vantage point I can tune in to those other lives. But I question, at least provisionally, any idea of past or counterpart lives that I lived one hundred percent. At this writing, I think that I am living my only one hundred percent life now, with the privilege of occasionally being able to focus upon scattered portions of those other existences emanating from my whole self, which has its basic reality outside of our space-time concepts.10
quotes.translation: 赛斯的资料和我自己关于对等体的看法，对我来说是合理的。我觉得（正如赛斯在第721节中所提到的）我并非尼本尼，也不是两个不同的罗马士兵本身，而是我的整个自己选择了共同显现这些人格；我也是在“后来”的一个时间点上的这样一种显现，并且从我自己的有利位置，我可以调频到那些其他的生命。但我至少暂时质疑任何我百分之百经历过的前世或对等体生命的想法。在写这篇文章时，我认为我正在经历我唯一百分之百的生命，同时有幸偶尔能够聚焦于那些从我的整个自己散发出的其他存在的零散部分，而我的整个自己在其基本实相中存在于我们的时空概念之外。

quotes.text: Events seem to happen to you. Often it seems that you have no more control over the drama of your own life than you have over the outcome of a television program. Sometimes, however, your own dreams or inspirations startle you by giving you information that is usually not available in the recognized order of events. It becomes very difficult to explain such occurrences in the light of the plots and scenes provided by your usual mental programming. You are so conditioned that even when you sleep you try to monitor your experiences, and to interpret dream events according to the habitual frequency that you have learned to accept as the only criterion of reality. Quite literally, however, when you are dreaming you are tuning in to different frequencies and biologically your body responds to those on many levels.
quotes.translation: 事件似乎发生在你身上。通常，你对自己人生戏剧的控制似乎并不比你对电视节目结局的控制更多。然而，有时你自己的梦境或灵感会令你惊讶，因为它们提供了在公认的事件秩序中通常无法获得的信息。根据你通常的心理程序所提供的剧情和场景，很难解释这类事件。你被如此制约，以至于即使在睡眠中，你也试图监控你的体验，并根据你学会接受为实相唯一标准的那种习惯性频率来解读梦境事件。然而，实际上，当你做梦时，你正在调频到不同的频率，而你的身体在多个层面上对这些频率做出反应。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Session 757 was devoted to other matters that Jane and I were interested in.)

quotes.text: The more able the child is to force such an artificial identification, the greater its feelings of inner rebellion. The lack of a "suitable" father or mother image has "saved" more children than it has hurt. The psyche, with its great gifts, always feels thwarted and attempts to take countering measures. Your schools further continue the process, however, so that the areas of curiosity and learning become separated for males and females. The "she" within the male does indeed represent portions of his personality that are being unexpressed -- not because of any natural predominance of mental or emotional characteristics over others, but because of artificial specializations. The same applies to the "male" within the female. You have accepted this version of personhood, again, in line with your ideas about the nature of consciousness. Those ideas are changing, and as they do the species must accept its true personhood. As this happens, your understanding will allow you to glimpse the nature of the reality of the gods you have recognized through the ages. You will no longer need to clothe them in limited sexual guises.
quotes.translation: 孩子越有能力强迫这种人为的认同，其内心的反叛感就越强烈。缺乏“合适的”父亲或母亲形象“拯救”的孩子比伤害的更多。具有伟大天赋的心灵总是感到受挫，并试图采取对抗措施。然而，你们的学校进一步延续了这一过程，使得好奇心和学习的领域在男性和女性之间被分割开来。男性内在的“她”确实代表了他个性中未被表达的部分——不是因为心智或情感特征相对于其他特征有任何自然的优势，而是因为人为的专业化。同样的情况也适用于女性内在的“男性”。你们接受了这种人格版本，再次与你们关于意识本质的观念相一致。这些观念正在改变，随着它们的改变，物种必须接受其真正的人格。当这发生时，你们的理解将使你们得以一窥你们历代以来所认识的神祇实相的本质。你们将不再需要用有限的性别伪装来包裹它们。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In the same way you form events, often without being aware that you do so. It seems that events happen as it seems words are spoken. You were taught how to construct sentences in school, and you learned how to speak from your elders. You were involved with event-making before the time of your birth, however. The psyche forms events in the same way that the ocean forms waves -- except that the ocean's waves are confined to its surface or to its basin, while the psyche's events are instantly translated, and splash out into mass psychological reality. In waking life you meet the completed event, so to speak. You encounter events in the arena of waking consciousness. In the dream state, and at other levels of consciousness, you deal more directly with the formation of events. You are usually as unaware of this process as you are in normal practice of the ways in which you form your sentences, which seem to flow from you so automatically.
quotes.translation: 同样地，你形成事件，通常却没有意识到你在这样做。事件似乎发生，就像话语似乎被说出一样。你在学校里被教导如何构建句子，你从长辈那里学会了如何说话。然而，在你出生之前，你就已经参与了事件的形成。心灵形成事件的方式，就像海洋形成波浪一样——只是海洋的波浪被限制在其表面或盆地内，而心灵的事件则立即被转化，并飞溅到大众心理实相中。可以这么说，在清醒生活中，你遇到的是已完成的事件。你在清醒意识的舞台上遭遇事件。在梦境状态和其他意识层面上，你更直接地处理事件的形成。你通常对这一过程毫无察觉，就像你在日常实践中对你形成句子的方式毫无察觉一样，那些句子似乎如此自动地从你口中流出。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In the dream state, with your body more or less safe and at rest, and without the necessity for precise action, these psychological intrusions become more apparent. Many of your dreams are like the tail end of a comet: Their real life is over, and you see the flash of their disappearance as they strike your own mental atmosphere and explode in a spark of dream images. They are transformed, therefore, as they travel through your own psychological atmosphere. You could not perceive them in your own state -- nor can they maintain their native state as they plunge through the far reaches of the psyche. They fall in patterns, forming themselves naturally into the dream contents that fit the contours of your own mind. The resulting structure of the dream suits your reality and no other: As this intrusive matter falls, plummets, or shifts through the levels of your own psychological atmosphere, it is transformed by the conditions it meets.
quotes.translation: 在梦境状态中，你的身体或多或少是安全和休息的，无需精确行动，这些心理上的侵入就变得更加明显。你的许多梦境就像彗星的尾巴：它们的真实生命已经结束，当它们撞击你的心理大气层并在梦境意象的火花中爆炸时，你看到它们消失的闪光。因此，当它们穿越你的心理大气层时，它们被转化了。在你自己的状态中，你无法感知它们——它们也无法在穿越心灵深处时保持其本然状态。它们以模式落下，自然形成适合你心灵轮廓的梦境内容。由此产生的梦境结构适合你的实相，而非其他：当这些侵入物质穿过你心理大气层的各个层面时，它会因所遇到的条件而被转化。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: To some extent, however, dream events are like physical ones a good deal of the time. Your dream perceptions seem physical -- you walk, run, eat, and perform other physical functions. There are actually many other conditions of existence, but for now we will speak of a predream state, which is actually composed of several conditions of actuality. Here the physical aspects of events largely vanish, comparatively speaking, the farther away you go from the dream state into inner reality. It would seem to you that experience becomes broader but less specific, but such is not the case. Experience does become broader, but it changes in quality so that, for example, one moment in your terms of such experience would provide the working material for five years of dreams.
quotes.translation: 然而，在某种程度上，梦境事件在很多时候就像物理事件一样。你的梦境感知似乎是物理的——你走路、跑步、进食，并执行其他物理功能。实际上还有许多其他存在状态，但现在我们将谈论一个前梦境状态，它实际上由几种实相状态组成。在这里，事件的物理方面在很大程度上消失了，相对而言，你离梦境状态越远，进入内在实相越深。对你来说，体验似乎变得更广阔但更不具体，但事实并非如此。体验确实变得更广阔，但其性质发生了变化，例如，在你看来，这种体验的一刻将为五年的梦境提供工作素材。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Actors visit casting agencies so that they know what plays need their services. In your dreams you visit "casting agencies." You are aware of the various plays being considered for physical production. In the dream state, then, often you familiarize yourself with dramas that are of a probable nature. If enough interest is shown, if enough actors apply, if enough resources are accumulated, the play will go on. When you are in other than your normally conscious state, you visit that creative inner agency in which all physical productions must have their beginning. You meet with others, who for their own reasons are interested in the same kind of drama. Following our analogy, the technicians, the actors, the writers all assemble -- only in this case the result will be a live event rather than a televised one. There are disaster films being planned, educational programs, religious dramas. All of these will be encountered in full-blown physical reality.
quotes.translation: 演员们去试镜机构，以便了解哪些戏剧需要他们的服务。在梦境中，你们也去"试镜机构"。你们会知晓那些正在考虑搬上物质舞台的各种戏剧。因此，在梦境状态中，你们常常熟悉那些具有或然性质的戏剧。如果表现出足够的兴趣，如果有足够的演员申请，如果积累了足够的资源，这出戏就会上演。当你们处于非正常意识状态时，你们会造访那个所有物质制作都必须起源的创意内在机构。你们与那些出于自身原因对同类戏剧感兴趣的人会面。按照我们的比喻，技术人员、演员、编剧都聚集在一起——只是在这种情况下，结果将是一个现场事件，而非电视转播。有灾难片在策划中，有教育节目，有宗教戏剧。所有这些都将以完整的物质实相呈现。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: All being is manifestation of energy -- an emotional manifestation of energy. Man can interpret the weather in terms of air pressure and wind currents. He can look to fault lines in an effort to understand earthquakes. All of this works at a certain level, to a certain degree. Man's psyche, however, is emotionally not only a part of his physical environment, but intimately connected with all of nature's manifestations. Using the terms begun in the last chapter, I will say then that man's emotional identification with nature is a strongly-felt reality in Framework 2. And there we must look for the answers regarding man's relationship with nature. There in Framework 2 the nature of the psyche appears quite clearly, so that its sweeps and rhythms can be understood. The manifestations of physical energy follow emotional rhythms that cannot be ascertained with gadgets or instruments, however fine.
quotes.translation: 一切存在都是能量的显化——一种情感性的能量显化。人类可以从气压和气流的角度解读天气。他可以寻找断层线来理解地震。这一切在某个层面、某种程度上是有效的。然而，人类的心灵不仅在情感上是其物质环境的一部分，而且与所有自然的显化紧密相连。使用上一章开始的术语，我要说，在架构二中，人类与自然的情感认同是一种强烈感受到的实相。我们必须在那里寻找关于人类与自然关系的答案。在架构二中，心灵的本质显现得相当清晰，因此其起伏和节奏可以被理解。物质能量的显化遵循着情感节奏，这些节奏无法用任何精巧的小工具或仪器来测定。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

source_raw.title: eth's statement at the end of dictation for the 815th session, "I will try to begin work on our book in a more predictable fashion," reflected his good intent, but things didn't turn out that way. Jane and I let the holiday season intervene to some extent, and Session 816, which came through the day after Christmas, didn't concern book work at all. Then, starting in early January, we held eight sessions having to do with questions we'd put off dealing with for a long time; some were personal and some were not. We stayed with our Monday-Saturday evening session routine, though, and kept busy with all of our other projects.1
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: In this part (2) of the book, we are more or less dealing with the events of nature as you understand it. It will seem obvious to some, again, that a natural disaster is caused by God's vengeance, or is at least a divine reminder to repent, while others will take it for granted that such a catastrophe is completely neutral in character, impersonal and [quite] divorced from man's own emotional reality. The Christian scientist is caught in between. Because you divorce yourselves from nature, you are not able to understand its manifestations. Often your myths get in the way. When myths become standardized, and too literal, when you begin to tie them too tightly to the world of facts, then you misread them entirely. When myths become most factual they are already becoming less real. Their power becomes constrained.
quotes.translation: 在本书的这一部分（第二部分），我们或多或少是在处理你们所理解的自然事件。对某些人来说，再次显而易见的是，自然灾害是由上帝的愤怒引起的，或者至少是神圣的提醒，要人忏悔；而另一些人则理所当然地认为，这样的灾难在性质上是完全中性的、非个人的，并且与人类自身的情感实相完全无关。基督教科学家则夹在中间。因为你们将自己与自然分离，所以无法理解自然的显化。你们的神话常常成为障碍。当神话变得标准化、过于字面化，当你们开始将它们过于紧密地与事实世界捆绑在一起时，你们就完全误读了它们。当神话变得最像事实时，它们已经变得不那么真实了。它们的力量受到了限制。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Obviously there are objects of all sizes, durability, and weight. There are private objects and public ones. There are also "vast psychological objects," then, sweeping mass events, for example, in which whole countries might be involved. There are also mass natural events of varying degrees, as say, the flooding of large areas. Such events involve psychological configurations on the part of all those involved, so that the inner individual patterns of those lives touched by each such event have in one way or another a common purpose that at the same time serves the overall reality on a natural planetary basis. In order to endure, the planet itself must be involved in constant change and instability. I know it is difficult to comprehend, but every object that you perceive -- grass or rock or stone -- even ocean waves or clouds -- any physical phenomenon -- has its own invisible consciousness, its own intent and emotional coloration. Each is also endowed with patterns toward growth and fulfillment -- not at the expense of the rest of nature, but to the contrary, so that every other element of nature may also be completed (all with much emphasis).
quotes.translation: 显然，存在着各种大小、耐久度和重量的物体。有私人物体和公共物体。那么，也存在"庞大的心理客体"，例如，席卷整个国家可能卷入其中的大规模群体事件。也存在不同程度的自然群体事件，比如说，大片区域的洪水。这类事件涉及所有相关者的心理构型，因此，被每个此类事件触及的那些生命的内在个体模式，都以某种方式拥有一个共同的目的，这个目的同时服务于行星层面的整体实相。为了持续存在，行星本身必须卷入持续的变化和不稳定之中。我知道这很难理解，但你们所感知的每一个物体——草、岩石或石头——甚至海浪或云朵——任何物理现象——都有其自身的无形意识、自身意图和情感色彩。每一个也都赋予了朝向成长和圆满的模式——不是以牺牲自然其余部分为代价，恰恰相反，是为了让自然的每一个其他元素也能得以完成（所有这些都着重强调）。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: 2. Seth mentioned a "correspondence" among my dreams, painting, and writing because just lately I've been doing small oil paintings of a few of my more vivid dream images. I've discovered that this is great fun -- and much more challenging than I'd anticipated, as I try to reduce the shifting, brilliant dream elements to the motionless painted surfaces we're so used to in waking reality. Each little painting becomes a unique adventure both technically and emotionally, and I hardly succeed in solving every attempt I make. Now, futilely, I wonder why I didn't try painting images from my dreams at a much earlier age; and why one so seldom hears about other artists doing the same thing. I don't personally know any other artist working with dreams this way.
quotes.translation: 2. 赛斯提到我的梦境、绘画和写作之间存在"对应关系"，因为最近我一直在用油画颜料画一些我比较生动的梦境意象的小画。我发现这非常有趣——而且比我预想的更具挑战性，因为我试图将那些变幻不定、光彩夺目的梦境元素，还原到我们在清醒实相中习以为常的静止画面上。每一幅小画在技巧和情感上都成为一次独特的冒险，我几乎无法成功解决我尝试的每一个问题。现在，我徒劳地想知道，为什么我没有更早地尝试描绘梦中的意象；以及为什么很少听说其他艺术家也做同样的事情。我个人不认识任何其他以这种方式处理梦境的艺术家。

quotes.text: This sense of division within the self forces you to think that there is a remote, spiritual, wise, intuitive inner self, and a bewildered, put-upon, spiritually ignorant, inferior physical self, which happens to be the one you identify with. Many of you believe, moreover, that the physical self's very nature is evil, that its impulses, left alone, will run in direct opposition to the good of the physical world and society, and fly in the face of the deeper spiritual truths of inner reality. The inner self then becomes so idealized and so remote that by contrast the physical self seems only the more ignorant and flawed. In the face of such beliefs the ideal of psychic development, or astral travel, or spiritual knowledge, or even of sane living, seems so remote as to be impossible. You must, therefore, begin to celebrate your own beings, to look to your own impulses as being the natural connectors between the physical and the nonphysical self. Children trusting their impulses learn to walk, and trusting your impulses, you can find yourselves again.
quotes.translation: 这种自我内部的割裂感迫使你们认为，存在一个遥远、灵性、智慧、直觉的内在自我，以及一个困惑、受欺、灵性无知、低劣的物质自我，而后者恰好是你们所认同的那个。此外，你们许多人相信，物质自我的本质就是邪恶的，它的冲动如果放任自流，会直接与物质世界和社会的福祉背道而驰，并公然违背内在实相的更深层灵性真理。于是，内在自我变得如此理想化、如此遥远，相比之下，物质自我似乎只是更加无知和有缺陷。面对这样的信念，心灵发展、星光体出游、灵性知识，甚至健全生活的理想，都显得如此遥不可及，以至于不可能实现。因此，你们必须开始庆祝你们自身的存在，将你们的冲动视为物质自我与非物质自我之间的自然连接器。信任自己冲动的孩子学会了走路，而信任你们的冲动，你们可以重新找到自己。
