quotes.text: We're not against reincarnation, then, only careful about our beliefs concerning it. Within the context of my discussion, reincarnation is Seth's historical version of his counterpart concept, which is that each of us is physically connected with certain other males and females who are living at the same time we are, and who are exploring physical life from a variety of viewpoints in ways that no one physical self could possibly match. This means that each reincarnational self has its own cluster of counterpart selves within its own time period, and that all are interconnected on nonphysical levels, joining together like magical gears meshing in constantly changing patterns across time and reality. And once one understands ideas of reincarnation and counterparts in these terms, it becomes difficult to think of one without the other, so inevitable do they appear to be.
quotes.translation: 我们并非反对转世，只是对其相关信念持谨慎态度。在我的讨论语境中，转世是赛斯对其"对应体"概念的历史版本诠释——即我们每个人与同时代生活的某些其他男性和女性存在物理层面的关联，他们正以单一物理自我无法企及的多重视角探索物质生命。这意味着每个转世自我在其所处时代都拥有自己的对应体集群，所有个体在非物质层面相互连接，如同魔法齿轮般在时空与实相中不断变换啮合模式。一旦以这种方式理解转世与对应体概念，便很难将两者割裂思考，它们显得如此密不可分。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: I referred to a "successful" progression because reaching into the future is evidently much more difficult. By its very nature a future life cannot be proven--records checked, and so forth. Anything goes. Jane and I have read of many systems designed to regress the individual to past lives. Often such "trips" are mediated by hypnosis. It can even happen spontaneously, and I had a most exhilarating glimpse of a past life of my own that way. (See Session 721 in Volume 2 of "Unknown" Reality.) However, neither of us have had such an outright encounter with a future self--that we know of. I'd say that under hypnosis the urge to fantasize the future lives must be a tempting one; but what's the explanation for achieving little more than a formless future state while "under," no matter how hard one tries? The failure to get there, to turn time around, could be taken as a sign of resistance on the part of the present self. (Or even a past self or selves, but that's too complicated a subject to go into here.)
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 6: Genetic Heritage and Reincarnational Predilections

quotes.text: But if the interactions between or among frameworks exist for everybody, in our terms, then as far as I'm concerned they exist for each thing as well--and I do mean the so-called "inanimate." (This isn't the place to go into it, but Seth maintains that for many reasons we arbitrarily decide what's living and nonliving.) Each reincarnational self, each counterpart self and probable self has its complement of frameworks. So does the most minute living or nonliving entity and the most gigantic. So, "probably," do most of the far-out probable realities one can imagine--for I won't go so far as to deny that some probable realities may exist without such framework structures. Strange one-dimensional "flatlands" indeed! But in each case where those framework interactions operate, they help each creation, each presence, each essence or vital principle fulfill "a larger picture that the conscious mind may not be able presently to perceive." In ways I can't even begin to describe here, all frameworks must ultimately be joined within the ineffable context of All That Is.
quotes.translation: 但若框架间的互动对所有人都存在（以我们的说法），那么在我看来，这对每个事物同样成立——我确实包括所谓的"无生命体"（此处不宜深究，但赛斯主张我们基于多重原因武断划分生命与非生命）。每个转世自我、每个对应体自我与可能自我都拥有其框架补集，最微小的生命/非生命实体与最宏大的存在亦然。因此，"很可能"连我们能想象的大多数遥远可能实相也是如此——我还不至于否认某些可能实相可能不存在此类框架结构。那真是奇异的一维"扁平世界"！但在每个框架互动运作的案例中，它们都协助每个造物、每个存在、每个本质或生命原则实现"当前意识可能无法感知的更大图景"。以我在此无法详述的方式，所有框架最终都必须在"一切万有"的不可言说语境中融为一体。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: I don't really think we can conceive of anything to be truly "alien," though, so I use the word here only to lead into the next of Seth's larger concepts that I want to mention: that of probable realities, or probabilities, as Jane and I usually say. Not only does Seth stress the constant psychic motion of reincarnational and counterpart selves upon this earth we know--but he also tells us that each of those selves can move into other or parallel realities. I quote him from Session 681 in Volume 1 of "Unknown" Reality:
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 6: Genetic Heritage and Reincarnational Predilections

quotes.text: But in your terms this was still largely a dream world, though it was fully fashioned. It had, generally speaking, all of the species that you now know. These all correlated with the multitudinous kinds of consciousnesses that had clamored for release, and those consciousnesses were spontaneously endowed by All That Is with those forms that fit their requirements. You had the birth of individualized consciousness as you think of it into physical context. Those consciousnesses were individualized before the beginning, but not manifest. But individualized consciousness was not quite all that bold. It did not attach itself completely to its earthly forms at the start, but rested often within its "ancient" divine heritage. In your terms, it is as if the earth and all of its creatures were partially dreaming, and not as focused within physical reality as they are now.
quotes.translation: 但从你们的角度看，这仍是个梦境世界，尽管已被充分塑造。大体而言，它拥有你们现今所知的所有物种。这些物种与那些渴望释放的万千意识形态相互关联，而"一切万有"自发赋予这些意识符合其需求的形态。你们所理解的个体化意识在物质语境中诞生了。那些意识在太初之前就已个体化，但未显化。不过个体化意识尚未完全勇敢，它起初并未完全依附于尘世形态，而是常驻于其"古老"的神圣传承中。以你们的说法，仿佛地球及其所有造物都处于半梦半醒之间，不像现在这般聚焦于物质实相。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: There is a part of man that Knows, with a capital K. That is the portion of him, of course, that is born and grows to maturity even while the lungs or digestive processes do not read learned treatises on the body's "machinery," 6 so in our book we will hope to arouse within the reader, of whatever persuasion, a kind of subjective evidence, a resonance between ideas and being. Many people write, saying that they feel as if somehow they have always been acquainted with our material--and of course they have, for it represents the inner knowing within each individual. (Pause.) In a fashion, creative play is your human version of far greater characteristics from which your universe itself was formed. There are all kinds of definite, even specific, subjective evidence for the nature of your own reality--evidence that is readily apparent once you really begin to look for it, particularly by comparing the world of your dreams with your daily life.
quotes.translation: 人类存在一个"知晓"（大写K）的部分。这当然是其与生俱来并臻于成熟的面向——即便肺腑或消化过程无需研读身体"机械装置"的学术论文。因此，在本书中，我们希望唤醒读者（无论其信仰为何）某种主观证据，即思想与存在之间的共鸣。许多人来信说，他们仿佛早已熟悉我们的资料——这当然合理，因为资料代表每个人内在的觉知。（停顿）从某种角度看，创造性游戏是你们人类对宇宙本身形成所依凭的更高特性的诠释。关于自身实相的本质，存在各种明确甚至具体的主观证据——一旦你真正开始寻找，尤其通过对比梦境世界与日常生活，这些证据便显而易见。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: There are as many kinds of suffering as there are kinds of joy, and there is no one simple answer that can be given. As human creatures you accept the conditions of life. You create from those conditions the experiences of your days. You are born into belief systems as you are born into physical centuries, and part of the entire picture is the freedom of interpreting the experiences of life in multitudinous fashions (all intently). The meaning, nature, dignity or shame of suffering will be interpreted according to your systems of belief. I hope to give you along the way a picture of reality that puts suffering in its proper perspective, but it is a most difficult subject to cover because it touches most deeply upon your hopes for yourselves and for mankind, and your fears for yourselves and for mankind.
quotes.translation: 痛苦与喜悦的种类同样繁多，没有单一答案可解。作为人类，你们接受生命条件，从这些条件中创造日常体验。你们生于信念体系，如同生于物理世纪。整个图景的一部分，便是以万千方式诠释生命体验的自由（全神贯注地）。痛苦的意义、本质、尊严或耻辱，将根据你们的信念体系被诠释。我愿沿途为你们呈现一幅将痛苦置于恰当视角的实相图景，但这主题极难涵盖，因为它最深切地触及你们对自身及人类的希望与恐惧。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now in a manner of speaking--though I see that little time has passed in this living room where I speak with Ruburt's permission--we have transcended time to some extent this evening, for in what I have said there are indeed hints and illusions--cadences--that can, if you are ready, give you a feeling for existence as it is outside of time's context. Even to try and verbally present such material necessitates alterations involving perception, for while that gridwork appears quite stable to your senses, giving you a reliable picture of reality, this is also because you have trained yourselves to pick up certain signals only. Others at other levels are (underlined) available. You can (underlined) tune into cellular consciousness, for example.
quotes.translation: 此刻，从某种意义上说——尽管我注意到在这间起居室里（经鲁柏允许我在此发言）时间几乎未流逝——今晚我们已在某种程度上超越时间，因为我所言中确实蕴含暗示与幻象——韵律——若你准备就绪，便能感受时间语境之外的存在感。即便试图以言语呈现此类资料，也需涉及感知的调整，因为那网格结构在你们感官中看似稳定，提供可靠的实相图景，但这恰因你们只训练自己接收特定信号。其他层面的信号（强调）同样可得。例如，你们（强调）可以调频至细胞意识。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: There is great traffic flow in a city: A body knows how to leap out of the way in a moment's time from an approaching car. In the interior physical environment there is far greater traffic flow. There are decisions made in periods of time so brief you cannot imagine them--reactions that are almost over before they begin, reactions so fast you cannot perceive them as the body responds to its inner reality, and to all the stimuli from the exterior environment. The body is an open system. As solid as it seems to you, there are constant chemical reactions between it and the world, electromagnetic adjustments, alterations of balance, changes of relationships--alterations that occur between the body and its relationship with every other physical event, from the position of the planets and moon and the sun, to the position of the smallest grain of sand, to the tiniest microbe in anyone's intestine (intently).
quotes.translation: 城市里车流如织：身体知道如何在千钧一发之际从疾驰的汽车前闪开。在内在物理环境里，有远为密集的交通流。决策在短得你无法想象的时间内做出——反应几乎在开始前就已结束，反应快到你无法察觉，因为身体在回应其内在实相，以及来自外在环境的所有刺激。身体是一个开放系统。尽管它在你看来如此坚实，但它与世界之间不断发生着化学反应、电磁调整、平衡改变、关系变化——这些变化发生在身体与所有其他物理事件的关系中，从行星、月亮和太阳的位置，到最小沙粒的位置，再到任何人肠道里最微小的微生物（专注地）。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Your overreliance upon physical norms, and your distorted concepts concerning survival of the fittest, help exaggerate the existence of any genetic defects, of course. Many religious dogmas consider such conditions, again, the result of a god's punishment. The survival of the species is far more dependent upon your subjective activities than your physical ones--for it is your subjective behavior that is responsible for your physical acts. Science of course looks at it the other way around, as if your physical acts are the result of a robot's mechanical, formalized behavior--a robot miraculously programmed by the blind elements of an accidental universe formed by chance. The robot is programmed only to survive at anyone's or anything's expense. It has no real consciousness of its own. Its thoughts are merely mental mirages, so if one of its parts is defective then obviously it is in deep trouble. But man is no robot, and each so-called genetic defect has an internal part to play in the entire picture of genetic reality. The principle of uncertainty must operate genetically, or you would have been locked into overspecializations as a species.2
quotes.translation: 你对物理规范的过度依赖，以及你对适者生存的扭曲观念，当然会夸大任何基因缺陷的存在。许多宗教教义再次将这些状况视为神惩罚的结果。物种的生存远更依赖你的主观活动而非物理活动——因为正是你的主观行为导致了你的物理行为。科学当然反其道而行之，仿佛你的物理行为是机器人机械、形式化行为的结果——一个被偶然宇宙的盲目元素奇迹般编程的机器人。这个机器人被编程为不惜任何代价只为生存。它没有自己的真实意识。它的思想只是心灵的幻象，所以如果它的某个部件有缺陷，显然它就陷入了大麻烦。但人不是机器人，每个所谓的基因缺陷在基因实相的整体图景中都有其内在作用。不确定性原理必须在基因层面运作，否则作为一个物种，你们早已陷入过度专业化的困境。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The next natural step would be to reassimilate those portions of the self, to acknowledge their ancient origins and abilities, to return them so that they form a new coating, as it were, or a new version of selfhood. It is as if (pause) man could not understand his own potentials unless he projected them outward into a godhead, where he could see them in a kind of isolated pure form, recognize them for what they are, and then accept them--the potentials--as a part of his own psychological reality (all very intently). As a species, however, you have not taken the last step. Your idea of the devil represents the same kind of process, except that it stands for your idea of evil or darkness, or abilities that you are afraid of. They also stand for elements of your own potential. I am not speaking of evil possibilities, but that man must realize that he is responsible for his acts, whether they are called good or evil.
quotes.translation: 下一步自然就是重新同化自我的那些部分，承认它们古老的起源和能力，让它们回归，从而形成一层新的外壳，或者说一种新的自我版本。就好像（停顿）除非人类将自己的潜能投射到神性中，在那里以某种孤立纯粹的形式看到它们，认出它们的本来面目，然后接受它们——这些潜能——作为自己心理实相的一部分，否则人类就无法理解自己的潜能（非常专注地）。然而，作为一个物种，你们还没有迈出最后一步。你们对魔鬼的观念代表了同样的过程，只不过它代表了你们对邪恶或黑暗的观念，或者你们害怕的能力。它们也代表了你们自身潜能的元素。我不是在谈论邪恶的可能性，而是说人必须意识到他要为自己的行为负责，无论这些行为被称为善还是恶。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: "The book was based on the idea that nature was against man; and that religion was man's attempt to operate within that unsafe context. The feelings I was getting went even further, that religion or science or whatever weren't attempts to discover truth--but to escape from doing so, to substitute some satisfying tale or story instead. And I suppose that if someone persisted long enough, he or she would find the holes in the stories ... and undo the whole works. The idea of the stories was to save each man from having to encounter reality in such a frightening fashion.... The characters in the stories did this for him in their own fashion, and if you kept [searching] ... you threatened the fine framework of organization that alone made life possible...."
quotes.translation: "这本书基于这样的观念：自然与人为敌；而宗教是人类试图在这个不安全的环境中运作的尝试。我感受到的感觉更进一步，即宗教、科学或其他任何东西都不是为了发现真理——而是为了逃避发现真理，用某种令人满意的故事或叙述来替代。而且我想，如果有人坚持得足够久，他或她就会找到故事中的漏洞……并瓦解整个体系。这些故事的目的是让每个人不必以如此可怕的方式面对实相……故事中的角色以他们自己的方式为他做到了这一点，如果你继续（探索）……你就威胁到了那个使生命成为可能的精妙组织框架……"
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth's comments of February 17 also reflect upon Jane's own ideas as she described them just 11 days ago; see the excerpts in Note 4. Then in this Chapter 9 of Dreams, see Note 8 for Session 920.

quotes.text: "The magical approach puts you in harmony with your own individual knowledge of the universe. It puts you in touch with the magical feeling of yourself that you had as a child, and that is familiar to you at levels usually beyond your physical knowledge of yourself. It is better, then, to use the approach because you recognize it for what it is than to use it specifically in order to get something that you want, however beneficial. (All very intently:) There is no doubt at my level that use of the approach can clear up Ruburt's difficulties naturally and easily. If it is used because you recognize its inherent rightness in yourself, its inherent 'superior stance,' then it automatically puts you in a position of greater trust and faith. It opens your options, enlarges your vista of comprehension, so that the difficulties themselves are simply no longer as important--and vanish from your experience in, again, a more natural manner." (9:37.) "In a fashion, all of the material that I have given you in the annals of our relationship was meant to lead you in one way or another to a place where the true nature of reality could at least be glimpsed. You are at that point now.
quotes.translation: "魔法方法让你与自己对宇宙的个体知识和谐一致。它让你接触到童年时那种对自己魔幻般的感觉，这种感觉在你通常超越物理自我认知的层面上是熟悉的。因此，使用这种方法更好，因为你认清了它的本质，而不是为了得到你想要的东西而特意使用它，无论那东西多么有益。（非常专注地：）在我的层面上，毫无疑问使用这种方法可以自然而轻松地解决鲁柏的困难。如果你使用它是因为你认识到它在你自身中的固有正确性，它固有的'优越立场'，那么它自动将你置于更大的信任和信心中。它打开你的选择，扩大你的理解视野，以至于困难本身不再那么重要——并以更自然的方式从你的经验中消失。"（9:37。）"从某种意义上说，在我们关系的历史中，我给你的所有资料都是为了以这样或那样的方式引导你到达一个至少能瞥见实相真实本质的地方。你现在就处于那个点。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 5. It was inevitable that Jane's images would remind me of the note I'd written well over two months ago, on re-creating the past, or updating it, through nostalgia. In Chapter 11 of Dreams, see Note 8 for Session 936. Her images led me to search out the collection of battered black-and-white snapshots of her that somehow, some way, she'd managed to save from her early childhood. Along with scraps of her youthful poetry, the pictures are the only physical remnants she possesses of her first years, and studying them anew I realized just how valuable they really are. I talked of having them copied and enlarged by a professional photographer; I speculated about eventually having some of them reproduced in a book. That idea may have to wait, however: For some years Jane hasn't cared to be photographed--or have pictures of herself shown, no matter when they had been taken.

quotes.text: The basic consciousness is never physical. Yet within your system it must collect experience within the physical system, hence the physical body. But experience itself is not physical, and cannot be contained within physical matter. Therefore this experience, collected within the physical field, is held in codified form by this inner self or astral identity. It is only by understanding the connection between the physical and nonphysical self, and the communication systems that operate here, that the true nature of human personality can be studied. While we are dealing now with your own species, it should be realized that all consciousness also possesses its own astral identity. The inner senses are part of this nonphysical self. They allow the personality to retain its relationship with nonphysical reality, permitting the material self to focus within its earthly environment.
quotes.translation: 基本意识从来不是物质的。然而在你的系统中，它必须在物理系统内收集经验，因此有了物质身体。但经验本身不是物质的，也不能被包含在物质中。因此，在物理领域收集的这些经验，以编码形式被这个内在自我或星光身份所持有。只有理解了物理自我与非物理自我之间的联系，以及在此运作的沟通系统，才能研究人类人格的真实本质。虽然我们现在讨论的是你们自己的物种，但应该认识到所有意识也拥有自己的星光身份。内在感官是非物理自我的一部分。它们允许人格保持与非物理实相的关系，使物质自我能够聚焦在其尘世环境中。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: I do not really waken however but continue dreaming. I'm downtown in the same town, with this group again. One girl asks me if Chuck stayed till nine last night and I say yes. Then one girl, not of the group, is crying. I say something about poetry and she says, well you d better talk fast because I've just about decided to stop writing poetry. She has light brown hair, rather dark circles under her eyes and is somewhat younger than I. I say that you can't just decide consciously to give up writing poetry, you d write it anyhow, though you could subconsciously make such a decision and never know about it at all. Then rather dramatically but beautifully I tell her that: "Poetry is your characteristic method of expression, your way of translating the data into physical reality and that even its rhythm is the rhythm of your heartbeat." Tell her that she can't give it up.
quotes.translation: 然而我并未真正醒来，而是继续做梦。我在同一个城镇的市中心，又和这群人在一起。一个女孩问我查克昨晚是否待到九点，我说是的。然后一个不属于这群人的女孩在哭泣。我提到诗歌，她说你最好快点说，因为我刚决定不再写诗了。她有一头浅棕色头发，眼下有相当深的黑眼圈，年纪比我小一些。我说你不能有意识地决定放弃写诗，无论如何你都会写，尽管你可以在潜意识里做出这样的决定而完全不自知。然后我相当戏剧化但优美地告诉她："诗歌是你特有的表达方式，是你将资料转化为物质实相的方式，就连它的节奏也是你心跳的节奏。"告诉她她无法放弃。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Every thought in one way or another is constructed by you in physical terms. You cannot escape the result of one thought. Every thought is an actuality. It affects every action and it forms your physical environment. As you sit listening to my voice, you are all subconsciously forming the physical environment of your classroom. You are forming the physical chairs. You are forming the blackboard. You are all forming reality, as you know it. Then with your physical senses, you are perceiving that which you have already so created. If you do not like what you see, then who is to blame? For these are your own constructions , formed in faithful replica to your own thoughts. We do not have the time to explain now how this is done, but in the material we have explained it. Certain telepathic connections exist which are what we term root assumptions, of which you are all telepathically aware. Using these you form a physical environment that is cohesive enough so that you can all agree with what you see and feel and smell and touch.
quotes.translation: 每一个念头都以某种方式被你用物质术语构建出来。你无法逃避任何一个念头的后果。每一个念头都是一个实相。它影响每一个行动，并形成你的物质环境。当你们坐着听我说话时，你们都在潜意识地形成你们教室的物质环境。你们在形成物质椅子。你们在形成黑板。你们都在形成你们所知的实相。然后你们用物质感官去感知你们已经创造出来的东西。如果你们不喜欢所看到的，那该怪谁呢？因为这些都是你们自己的建构，忠实地复制了你们自己的念头。我们现在没有时间解释这是如何做到的，但在资料中我们已经解释过了。存在某些心灵感应联系，我们称之为根本假设，你们所有人都能心灵感应地意识到这些。利用这些，你们形成一个足够连贯的物质环境，使你们都能对所看、所感、所闻、所触达成一致。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Now this also overshadows your relationship with the males to whom you have come in contact. For you have been, on the one hand, terrified of them, and on the other hand wanted a normal relationship. Give me a moment here --on the one hand you desire more from a relationship with a man than you have any right to expect. No human being could ever deliver what you expect a man to deliver in a relationship. This is because you see the male in terms inspired in you when you were a child. You were terrified of the male, your father. On the other hand, you felt that he did contain wisdom, truth, almost godlike qualities. These qualities you attempt to project into the male that you meet. At the same time you are also terrified because of this background. No man can possibly be as godlike as your inner conception. Therefore, each man is bound to disappoint you. At the same time, you hope and pray subconsciously that the man will disappoint you because this male in your mind has godlike qualities that attract you; on the other, you see him as all powerful and as one who gives out punishment and one who is unreasoning and cruel because you felt that your father was cruel. You are afraid, so to speak, to come under a man's thumb for this reason, to come under his domination. For to do so is to place yourself in a humble position and a frightening position underneath the male figure. Your terror as a child gave you an inner idea of reality and family group whereby you saw yourself completely powerless and helpless under the domination of this father figure. He was the source of all and yet he could at the same time take all away. And you felt, at the same time, that he would indeed do so. Because you were a male in past lives, you resented this all the more strongly. Give us a moment.
quotes.translation: 这也影响了你与所接触男性的关系。因为你一方面对他们感到恐惧，另一方面又想要一段正常的关系。给我一点时间——一方面，你渴望从与男性的关系中得到更多，超过你任何合理的期望。没有任何人类能够满足你期望男性在关系中给予的东西。这是因为你以童年时被激发的视角看待男性。你害怕男性，你的父亲。另一方面，你觉得他确实包含智慧、真理，几乎是神一般的品质。你试图将这些品质投射到你遇到的男性身上。同时，由于这个背景，你也很恐惧。没有男人可能像你内心概念中那样神一般。因此，每个男人都注定会让你失望。同时，你在潜意识中希望并祈祷男人会让你失望，因为你心中的这个男性具有吸引你的神一般品质；另一方面，你视他为全能的、施以惩罚的、不讲理且残忍的，因为你认为你的父亲是残忍的。所以，你害怕落入男人的掌控之下，害怕被他支配。因为这样做就是将自己置于男性形象之下的卑微和可怕位置。你童年的恐惧给了你一个关于实相和家庭群体的内在概念，你看到自己在父亲形象的支配下完全无能为力。他是一切之源，同时也能夺走一切。你同时觉得他确实会这样做。因为你在过去世曾是男性，你对此更加愤恨。给我们一点时间。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

source_raw.title: (I thought it a good idea that this be the end of the session, however, Seth came through again at 10:30 as the three of us sat talking about various things. A humorous repartee followed, with Seth answering our questions on a variety of subjects. Two serious notes: Alice Butts, who died last week, has already visited my father. And Dr. Instream, who would say "There is no Seth, " has not much longer to live. Also -- Bill Gallagher will not see certain truths about himself etc. Alice Butts not helping Ruth Butts ' son, etc. See Session 398.)

quotes.text: Now I am only speaking because many of you have known that I was here. And it ill behooves me not to bid you welcome when you felt my presence. But in many of your discussions, there has only been surface thought and surface feelings... and we do not walk on the surface in this class. I would push your heads collectively and individually underneath... for you still do not understand that you create your physical reality! you form your blocks and shove them up upon the surface of the earth. You cannot solve the problems of your world on a surface level. If you could, you would have done it centuries ago. You must step out of your present perspective in order to see your world clearly. Here you are not to hide within the closet of 3 dimensionality and cry, "It is dark and I cannot see!" And refuse to use the inner light that can alone aid your vision.
quotes.translation: 我现在说话只是因为你们许多人知道我在场。当你们感受到我的存在时，我不该不向你们表示欢迎。但在你们的许多讨论中，只有表面的思想和表面的感受……而在这个课堂上，我们不走在表面。我会把你们的头集体地、个别地按下去……因为你们仍然不明白你们创造了自己的物质实相！你们形成自己的障碍，把它们推到地球表面。你们无法在表面层面解决世界的问题。如果能，你们几个世纪前就做到了。你们必须跳出当前的视角才能看清自己的世界。在这里，你们不要躲在三次元的壁橱里哭喊："太黑了，我看不见！"而拒绝使用唯一能帮助你们视物的内在之光。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: You must become more aware of your inner selves.... They are not all that horrible. You still fear, as our Lady of Venice, that there is a cellar door... a cellar door to your mind--to your inner self. And, if you open it, all sorts of demons will emerge. And if there are any angels, the demons will gobble them up before you get to see them. Instead, I tell you, as I have told you before, you are more than you know. And it is up to you to find your own reality. I cannot give it to you. I can point you in the direction... but the experience is personal and the experience is subjective and the journey is one that you must make and that you must make alone. I cannot make it for you. I have my own journeys to make... and detours here. And any problems that you have I have had them... so look at me and know how indestructible you are!
quotes.translation: 你们必须更加意识到自己的内在自我……它们并没有那么可怕。你们仍然像我们的威尼斯女士一样，害怕有一扇地窖门……一扇通往你们心灵——通往你们内在自我的地窖门。如果打开它，各种恶魔就会涌现。如果有任何天使，恶魔会在你们见到它们之前就把它们吞掉。相反，我告诉你们，正如我以前告诉你们的，你们比自己所知的更伟大。找到自己的实相取决于你们自己。我不能把它给你们。我可以为你们指明方向……但体验是个人的，体验是主观的，旅程是你们必须独自进行的。我不能替你们走。我有自己的旅程要走……在这里也有绕路。你们有的任何问题我都经历过……所以看着我，知道你们是多么不可摧毁！
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The portrait is of Joseph and Ruburt. These are not portraits of yourselves as past personalities in your terms, or of particular re-incarnational selves. They are pictorial representatives of the whole selves that you are. In your terms the selves that are the sum of your re-incarnational personalities. These whole selves then are a part of your entity. Your probable selves are also a part of your entity, however. If you want to think in terms of guides, in quotes "angels," then you have the reality behind those terms. Ruburt and Joseph are the names I use for you, for these names themselves imply your greater goals. These personalities are aware of your present existence, and give you advice when you are in the sleep state. They are a source of strength. You can draw upon their knowledge, and you can also draw upon the knowledge of your own re-incarnational selves, past, in quotes, and future.
quotes.translation: 这幅肖像是约瑟夫和鲁柏。这些不是你们过去人格的肖像，按你们的说法，也不是特定的转世自我。它们是你们所是的整体自我的图像代表。按你们的说法，这些自我是你们转世人格的总和。这些整体自我是你们存有的一部分。然而，你们的可能自我也是存有的一部分。如果你们想用指导灵、引号"天使"的概念来思考，那么这些术语背后有实相。鲁柏和约瑟夫是我对你们使用的名字，因为这些名字本身就暗示了你们更大的目标。这些人格知道你们当前的存在，并在你们处于睡眠状态时给予建议。它们是力量的源泉。你们可以汲取它们的知识，也可以汲取你们自己转世自我——引号中的过去和未来——的知识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: An individual will therefore feel more at home operating within certain frequencies, and he will feel less at home with others. The original thought is used as a pattern, therefore, for the creation of a new electrical reality, which may or may not be directed at any given receiver. It is obvious that the attempt to duplicate is present; and were it not for this attempt to duplicate, then there would be little similarity between any separate identities.
quotes.translation: 因此，个体在特定频率范围内运作时会感到更自在，而在其他频率中则不那么自在。原始思想被用作模式，从而创造一种新的电性实相，这种实相可能被导向任何特定接收者，也可能不被导向。显然，复制的企图是存在的；若非这种复制的企图，那么任何独立身份之间几乎不会有什么相似之处。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The personality itself exists in many dimensions, as you know, and it has its reality within many other fields than the physical field, and is indeed basically not nearly as allied with the physical field as you may imagine. These unifying principles of which I spoke are themselves main, dominating groups of actions, about which the main energies of the personality group themselves.
quotes.translation: 如你所知，人格本身存在于许多维度中，它在物理场之外的其他许多场域中拥有其实相，而且本质上并不像你可能想象的那样与物理场紧密相连。我所说的这些统一原则本身就是主要的、主导性的行动群组，人格的主要能量围绕它们聚集。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: It is not that I am holding back so much as that, in your terms, what follows is dependent upon an understanding of concepts presented earlier. People who are still worrying about one soul, gods, and devils, must be helped to relate to greater realities from their own framework, and gently led away from it if possible. Probabilities have been mentioned in such a way that alternate realities are presented, showing such people that choices are available.
quotes.translation: 并非我有所保留，而是用你们的话说，接下来的内容取决于对先前提出的概念的理解。那些仍在担忧一个灵魂、神祇和魔鬼的人们，必须被帮助从他们自己的框架中关联到更大的实相，并尽可能温和地引导他们离开那个框架。概率已被以某种方式提及，从而呈现出交替实相，向这些人展示选择是存在的。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 681
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: He acts, speaks, walks, talks, makes certain deductions. He responds to interior stimuli. The personality also operates within the dream state in ways that would not be considered normal in the waking state. He travels through space in moments. He speaks with those who do not exist within physical reality. He sees parents who have died in physical ways, and in his dreams he knows, on many occasions, what other characters think within the dream context.
quotes.translation: 他行动、说话、行走、交谈，做出某些推论。他对内在刺激做出反应。人格也在梦境状态下以清醒状态下不被视为正常的方式运作。他在瞬间穿越空间。他与那些不存在于物理实相中的人交谈。他见到在物理意义上已故的父母，并且在梦中，他在许多场合知道其他角色在梦境背景中的想法。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

source_raw.title: （Tracing of the photographic negative used in the 7th envelope test, in Session 187, September 13,1965.）

quotes.text: Since these selves exist simultaneously, it is then possible for consciousness to enter, or really form, such an image, undergo experiences within the characteristic pattern of reality, and then project to another image. In all cases consciousness forms the image. When consciousness changes, or travels at certain frequencies, it automatically changes its form. It is indeed possible to be involved with more than one form at once as in usual projection.
quotes.translation: 由于这些自我同时存在，意识就有可能进入，或真正形成这样一个形象，在实相的特征模式中经历体验，然后投射到另一个形象。在所有情况下，意识形成形象。当意识改变或以特定频率旅行时，它会自动改变其形式。确实有可能像在通常的投射中那样同时涉及多个形式。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: When you look at the animal kingdom, you suppose that the male chooses blindly, led by "dumb" instinct, so that in overall terms one female will do as well as any other. When you discover that a certain chemical or scent will attract a certain male insect, for example, you take it for granted that that element is alone responsible for drawing the male to the female. You take it for granted, in other words, that individual differences do not apply in such cases so remote from your own reality.
quotes.translation: 当你观察动物王国时，你假设雄性盲目地选择，被“愚蠢的”本能引导，因此总体而言，一个雌性与任何其他雌性一样好。例如，当你发现某种化学物质或气味会吸引某种雄性昆虫时，你理所当然地认为该元素单独负责将雄性吸引到雌性。换句话说，你理所当然地认为，在离你自己实相如此遥远的案例中，个体差异并不适用。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: To this degree Dunne was correct. But the important point, if you will forgive a pun, is that these moment points are all intensities, electrical realities, and traveling through such dimensions involves a transformation of energy from one intensity to another. The whole self, or the entity of which I speak, is composed of all of these selves, but it must be realized that all divisions between these selves are illusions, basically speaking. For the sake of discussion we separate them, but in doing so we almost manage to change the very nature of that which we attempt to study.
quotes.translation: 邓恩在这点上是对的。但关键点在于——请原谅我玩个文字游戏——这些"瞬间点"都是强度、电性实相，穿越这类维度涉及能量从一种强度到另一种强度的转化。我所说的全我或存有，由所有这些自我组成，但必须认识到这些自我之间的所有划分本质上都是幻相。为便于讨论我们将其分开，但这样做时几乎改变了我们试图研究的对象的本质。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: There are no real barriers to separate the systems of which I speak. The only separation is brought about by the varying abilities of personalities to perceive and manipulate. You exist in the midst of many other systems of reality, for example, but you do not perceive them. And even when some event intrudes from these systems into your own three-dimensional existence, you are not able to interpret it, for it is distorted by the very fact of entry.
quotes.translation: 我所说的这些系统之间并无真正屏障。唯一的分离来自人格感知与操控能力的差异。例如你存在于许多其他实相系统之中，却无法感知它们。即便某些事件从这些系统侵入你的三维存在，你也无法解读，因为进入这个事实本身已使其扭曲。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: As I have said before, your thoughts are reality. They directly affect your body. It seems that you are highly civilized people because you put your ill into hospitals where they can be cared for. What you do, of course, is to isolate a group of people who are filled with negative beliefs about illness. The contagion of beliefs spreads. Patients are obviously in hospitals because they are ill. The sick and their doctors both work on that principle.
quotes.translation: 如我之前所说，你的思想就是实相。它们直接影响你的身体。你们自诩高度文明，因为将病人送进医院照料。但实际上你们做的，是将一群充满疾病负面信念的人隔离起来。信念的传染在蔓延。病人住院显然因为他们生病了。病人和医生都遵循这个原则运作。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Regardless of the field of reality from which the data is received, to a large extent the mechanisms are the same. It is only because so-called medium ship is more unusual than simple perception that it appears so striking. Ruburt, reading from a book, would still have to receive and translate that information without knowing the endless manipulations necessary. The same sort of inner calculations would have been involved by the author of the book.
quotes.translation: 无论数据来自哪个实相领域，其机制在很大程度上是相同的。所谓的通灵媒介之所以引人注目，只因它比普通感知更罕见。鲁柏从书本阅读时，同样需要接收并翻译信息，却不知其中所需的无数操作。书本作者也经历了同样的内在计算过程。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: I am not saying that words cannot be used to describe the psyche, but they cannot define it. It is futile to question: "What is the difference between my psyche and my soul, my entity and my greater being?" for all of these are terms used in an effort to express the greater portions of your own experience that you sense within yourself. Your use of language may make you impatient for definitions, however. Hopefully this book will allow you some intimate awareness, some definite experience, that will acquaint you with the nature of your own psyche, and then you will see that its reality escapes all definitions, defies all categorizing, and shoves aside with exuberant creativity all attempts to wrap it up in a neat package.
quotes.translation: 我并非说语言不能描述心灵，而是它无法定义心灵。追问"我的心灵与灵魂、存有与更高自我有何区别"是徒劳的——所有这些术语都试图表达你内在感知到的更大经验部分。但语言的使用可能让你急于寻求定义。希望本书能让你获得某种亲密觉知、某种明确体验，使你认识自身心灵的本质，届时你将发现它的实相超越所有定义，拒绝任何分类，以蓬勃的创造力将所有试图将其包装整齐的企图抛诸脑后。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: If you remember that beneath all, each unit of consciousness is aware of the position of each other unit, and that these units form all physical matter, then perhaps you can intuitively follow what I mean, for whatever knowledge man attains, whatever experience any one person accumulates, whatever arts or sciences you produce, all such information is instantly perceived at other levels of activity by each of the other units of consciousness that compose physical reality--whether those units form the shape of a rock, a raindrop, an apple, a cat, a frog or a shoe. Manufactured products are also composed of atoms and molecules that ride upon units of consciousness transformed into EE units, and hence into physical elements.
quotes.translation: 若你记得每个意识单元都知晓其他单元的位置，且这些单元构成所有物质，或许你能直觉理解我的意思——无论人类获得何种知识、积累何种经验、创造何种艺术或科学，所有这些信息都会立即被构成物质实相的其他意识单元在其他活动层面感知——无论这些单元形成岩石、雨滴、苹果、猫、青蛙还是鞋子的形态。人造产品同样由原子和分子组成，它们乘着转化为EE单元的意识单元，进而成为物理元素。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The gradations of intensities are so minute that it would be impossible to measure them, and yet each field contains in coded form the actual living reality of endless eons; contains therefore what you would call the past, present and future of unnumbered universes; contains the actual coded data of any and every consciousness that has been or will be, in any universe; those that have appeared to vanish, and those which seemingly do not yet exist.
quotes.translation: 强度的层级如此精微，以至于无法测量，然而每个场域都以编码形式包含着无尽纪元的真实鲜活实相；因此包含着你们所谓的无数宇宙的过去、现在与未来；包含着在任何宇宙中曾经存在或将要存在的每一个意识的实际编码数据；那些看似已消失的，以及那些看似尚未存在的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The inner self is acquainting itself with reality as it exists there. In many cases it is emerging into a new world. Since the learning process is involved, there are errors. Any faulty constructions however are used by the inner self in a feedback system and as a part of the learning process. Your system is not the most elementary, but it is one of the most elementary, and it is a way that the inner self acquaints itself with certain basic facts.
quotes.translation: 内在自我正在熟悉存在于那里的实相。在许多情况下，它正进入一个新世界。由于涉及学习过程，因此会有错误。然而，任何有缺陷的建构都被内在自我在反馈系统中用作学习过程的一部分。你们的系统并非最基础的，但却是最基础的之一，并且是内在自我熟悉某些基本事实的一种方式。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: All of these reality systems of which we have been speaking are closely connected. Every thought has this same electric potential. Thoughts are often translated into dream images by a simple transformation. There is little difference indeed, basically, between physical images and dream images, and apparitions, and very little difference between these and thoughts and emotions.
quotes.translation: 我们一直在谈论的所有这些实相系统都紧密相连。每个思想都具有相同的电位势能。思想常常通过简单的转化被转译成梦境影像。实际上，物质影像与梦境影像及幻象之间几乎没有基本差异，而这些与思想和情感之间也几乎没有区别。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: To me, consciousness or All That Is is an omnipresent, really indescribable awareness that to us human beings has no limits, "one" containing not only the attributes of time and space and of all feeling, thought, and objectivity, but numberless other properties, manifestations, and probabilities that lie outside our very limited interior and exterior perceptions. In terms of physics, then, reality is still unknowable.
quotes.translation: 对我而言，意识或一切万有是一种无所不在、真正难以描述的觉知，对我们人类来说没有界限，"一"不仅包含时间、空间以及所有感受、思想和客观性的属性，还包含无数其他特性、显现和可能性，这些都在我们极其有限的内外感知之外。因此，就物理学而言，实相仍然是不可知的。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: I don't really think we can conceive of anything to be truly "alien," though, so I use the word here only to lead into the next of Seth's larger concepts that I want to mention: that of probable realities, or probabilities, as Jane and I usually say. Not only does Seth stress the constant psychic motion of reincarnational and counterpart selves upon this earth we know--but he also tells us that each of those selves can move into other or parallel realities. I quote him from Session 681 in Volume 1 of "Unknown" Reality:
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 6: Genetic Heritage and Reincarnational Predilections

quotes.text: Many have seen that inner world as the source for the physical one, but imagined that man's purpose was merely to construct physically these perfect images to the best of his abilities. (Very forcefully:) In that picture man himself did not help create that inner world, or have any hand in its beauty. He could at best try to duplicate it physically -- never able, however, to match its perfection in those terms. In such a version of inner-outer reality the back-and-forth mobility, the give-and-take between inner and outer, is ignored. Man, being a part of that inner world by reason of the nature of his own psyche, automatically has a hand in the creation of those blueprints which at another level he uses as guides.
quotes.translation: 许多人将那个内在世界视为物质世界的源头，但想象人类的目的是仅仅尽其所能地在物质上构建这些完美影像。（非常有力地：）在那幅画面中，人类自身并未帮助创造那个内在世界，也未参与其美丽。他最多只能试图在物质上复制它——然而永远无法在这些方面达到其完美。在这种内外实相的版本中，内外之间的来回流动性、给予与索取被忽略了。人类，由于其自身心灵的本质而成为那个内在世界的一部分，自动参与了那些蓝图的创造，而在另一个层面上，他将这些蓝图用作指引。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 696
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: You [Joseph]] were determined to find the kind of relationship you have with Ruburt, the kind of bond your parents never had,12 and you applied the power of your will in that direction. At the same time you were determined to set yourself apart from the world to some extent, while still maintaining and developing an emotional contact with a mate that would be unlike any in your earlier experience. Creativity would have to be involved. You were also intrigued, determined to travel into the nature of reality, and at least glimpse a vague picture of what it could be. In this probability you provided yourself with a background that included sports and the love of the body, knowing [those qualities] would sustain you.
quotes.translation: 你（约瑟）决心找到你与鲁柏之间那种关系，那种你父母从未有过的纽带，并将你的意志力投向那个方向。同时，你决心在某种程度上将自己与世界隔离开来，同时仍维持并发展与伴侣的情感联系，这种联系将不同于你早期经历中的任何关系。创造力必须参与其中。你也被吸引，决心探索实相的本质，并至少瞥见它可能是什么的模糊画面。在这个概率中，你为自己提供了一个包括运动和热爱身体的背景，知道（这些特质）会支撑你。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 713
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: It is all in one respect hallucinatory, and yet it is your reality and you must deal with it. If you do not like the looks of the adult world, then you had better change your own expectations now. The world in which your parents live is a world which they created. It existed first in thought. It existed first in mental realms. It existed first as the stuff of dreams, and from this they spawned their universe, and from this they made their world.
quotes.translation: 从某个角度来看，这一切都是幻觉，但它却是你的实相，你必须面对它。如果你不喜欢成人世界的模样，那你最好现在就改变自己的期望。你父母所生活的世界，是他们自己创造的世界。它首先存在于思想中，首先存在于心灵领域，首先作为梦的素材存在，而他们由此孕育出他们的宇宙，由此构建出他们的世界。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: The imagination can vaguely perceive, of course, some probabilities, but the physical organism can directly experience but one of these within physical time, and in terms of continuity. The probable events however are precisely as real as that one event which is chosen from them to be a physical experience. And these events therefore become "real", in quotes, within other dimensions. As a sideline here, there are some interesting episodes, not at all understood, when a severe psychological shock, or even a deep sense of unendurable futility, will cause a short circuit, so to speak, so that one portion of the self becomes aware, and begins to experience reality as it exists for another portion of the self.
quotes.translation: 当然，想象力能模糊地感知某些可能性，但物质有机体在物理时间内，只能以连续性的方式直接体验其中一种可能性。然而，那些未发生的可能事件，与那个被选中成为物质体验的事件一样真实。因此，这些事件在其他维度中变成了"真实的"（加引号）。顺便提一句，有些有趣的情节至今未被理解：当遭受严重的心理冲击，甚至深陷难以忍受的徒劳感时，会产生一种所谓的短路现象，使得自我的某一部分开始觉察，并开始体验自我另一部分所存在的实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: While this may sound quite sacrilegious scientifically, it is possible to understand the electron's nature and greater reality by using certain focuses of consciousness: by probing the electron, for example, with a "laser" [beam] of consciousness finely focused and attuned -- and more will be said about this later in the book. So far in any of your investigations, you have been probing exterior conditions, searching for their interior nature.
quotes.translation: 虽然这在科学上听起来可能相当亵渎，但通过运用某些意识的焦点——例如，用高度聚焦且调谐的意识"激光束"探测电子——是可以理解电子的本质及其更宏大的实相的。本书后面还会对此有更多阐述。迄今为止，在你们的所有研究中，你们一直在探测外在条件，寻找它们的内在本质。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 703
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Now. I have said that any of you and all of you create a dream universe of validity, actuality, durability, and for Philip's benefit, self-determination, in the same manner that the entity projects the reality of its various personalities. As there is no contact between the entity and the ordinary conscious ego, so there is no contact on a conscious level between the self who dreams and the dream world which has its own independent existence.
quotes.translation: 现在。我说过，你们中的任何一个人和所有人，都创造了一个具有有效性、真实性、持久性，并且为了菲利普起见，还具有自主决定性的梦之宇宙，其方式与存有投射其各种人格的实相相同。正如存有与普通意识自我之间没有接触一样，做梦的自我与拥有独立存在的梦世界之间，在意识层面上也没有接触。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: First I want to make certain that action is understood, insofar as we have discussed it. Action of the inner vitality of all reality. It seeks to utilize itself in as many ways as possible. Its action, its attempts at outward materialization, however, must result in the creation of new inner vitality, for this is the stuff of which it is composed. And this new inner vitality will then seek materialization, and so the cycle is never completed.
quotes.translation: 首先，我想确保行动被理解，就我们目前讨论的范围而言。行动是一切实相的内在活力。它寻求以尽可能多的方式利用自身。然而，它的行动，它向外物质化的尝试，必然会导致新的内在活力的创造，因为这就是构成它的素材。而这种新的内在活力随后会寻求物质化，如此循环往复，永无止境。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: You are made basically of the same ingredients as a chair, a stone, a head of lettuce, a bird. In a gigantic cooperative endeavor, all consciousness joins together to make the forms that you perceive. Now, because this is known to us, we can change our environments and our own physical forms as we wish, and without confusion, for we perceive the reality that lies beneath.
quotes.translation: 你们的基本构成成分与椅子、石头、生菜头、鸟儿相同。在一场巨大的合作努力中，所有意识联合起来，制造出你们所感知到的形态。现在，因为我们知道这一点，我们可以随心所欲地改变我们的环境和我们自己的物质形态，而不会产生混乱，因为我们感知到了隐藏在表象之下的实相。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I was going to say that discipline is even more important in the use of the inner senses than you might think. It is true that focusing upon the inner reality at times requires a temporary lessening of outer focus, and this would sometimes give the appearance of letting go, but the inner concentration requires discipline and intent. It is quite possible to let the inner and the outer senses operate at the same time. It merely takes practice.
quotes.translation: 我本想说，纪律在内感官的运用上比你可能想象的更为重要。确实，聚焦于内在实相有时需要暂时减弱对外在的专注，这偶尔会显得像是放手，但内在的专注需要纪律和意图。让内在感官与外在感官同时运作是完全可能的，这仅仅需要练习。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: In a manner of speaking your experienced, practical reality is made up of events that seem entire to you, or relatively complete, while from my dimension it is apparent that your recognized events are simply portions of larger ones. I move naturally, then, in a realm of greater dimensionalized events.
quotes.translation: 从某种角度来说，你所经历的、实际的实相是由那些对你而言看似完整或相对完整的事件构成的，而从我的维度来看，显然你所认知的事件只是更大事件的一部分。因此，我自然而然地活动在一个更具维度性事件的领域中。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 744
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: I would make clear the nature and conditions in which I now have my existence, and explain some of the reasons for the various, often contradictory, statements made concerning life after death. Statements received by various mediums, in which quite different pictures of afterlife reality are received.
quotes.translation: 我想澄清我现在存在的本质与条件，并解释关于死后生命所做的各种、往往相互矛盾的陈述背后的一些原因。这些陈述由不同的灵媒接收，描绘出死后实相截然不同的画面。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: In that previous book I discussed the ways in which you form your private experience through your beliefs. You have certain pet ideas, therefore, and you use them to structure your own world view of the reality you know. It is important that you understand what your own beliefs are. Many of them might work quite well "at home," but when you begin to journey away from that home station you may find that those same ideas impede your progress.
quotes.translation: 在那本先前的书中，我讨论了你们如何通过信念形成个人经验。因此，你们有某些偏爱的想法，并用它们来构建你们所知的实相的世界观。理解你们自己的信念是什么很重要。其中许多信念在“家”里可能运作得很好，但当你们开始离开那个家园站点时，可能会发现这些同样的想法阻碍了你们的进步。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In denying life to yourself you end up by denying life to others. Now I wish this session was recorded so you could hear me, for I hold you, as Ruburt does, closely and dearly as a friend. But you are bound to misinterpret what I say in some sessions, and so it behooves me to speak more plainly. You do not realize now, you do not let yourself realize, the beauty and the complicated reality of your being. You do not let yourself realize the spontaneity and joyful burst that is your inner self, that results in this present human personality that you call yourself. Nor the effort and creative energy that has gone into your making, and that sings within your being like the first morning of creation.
quotes.translation: 否定自己的生命，最终会导致否定他人的生命。现在我希望这次课程被录下来，这样你就能听到我，因为我像鲁柏一样，将你视为亲密而珍贵的朋友。但你在某些课程中必然会误解我说的话，因此我更应该说得更直白些。你现在没有意识到，也不让自己意识到，你存在的美丽与复杂的实相。你没有让自己意识到你内在自发性与喜悦的迸发，正是这种迸发导致了你现在称之为自己的这个当前人格。你也没有意识到投入于你形成中的努力与创造能量，它们像创世的第一天早晨一样在你存在中歌唱。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The entity sees the whole event, the whole person-event, with the time element, or age in your terms, as simply another characteristic or dimension. The person-event is not cut off, however. Its greater reality simply cannot appear within three dimensions. It is instead composed of atoms and molecules that you do not perceive, both above and below the physical range of intensities -- and all of these in their own way possess consciousness.
quotes.translation: 存有看到整个事件，整个“人-事件”，其中时间元素，或你们所说的年龄，仅仅是另一个特征或维度。然而，“人-事件”并未被切断。它更大的实相根本无法在三维空间中显现。相反，它由你无法感知的原子和分子组成，这些原子和分子既在物理强度范围之上，也在其之下——而所有这些都以它们自己的方式拥有意识。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You, as you think of yourself, may have trouble following such concepts, just as you would have trouble trying to follow the "future" reality of the cells within your body at this moment. (Long pause.) You must understand that in greater terms there is no big or small. There is not a giant identity and a pygmy one. Each identity is inviolate. Each also unites with others while maintaining its individuality and developing its own potential.
quotes.translation: 你，就你对自己的认知而言，可能难以理解这些概念，就像你此刻难以理解你体内细胞的"未来"实相一样。（长停顿。）你必须明白，在更宏大的层面上，没有大与小之分。没有庞大的身份与侏儒的身份之分。每个身份都是不可侵犯的。每个身份也与其他身份结合，同时保持其个体性并发展其自身潜力。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The components of the electrical universe are drawn from all energy, in a manner that could be described as a cosmic juggling act. For this energy is never still, and it goes without saying that no electrical impulse is stable from one instant of reality to the next. That is, it is never the same or identical impulse. It is not identical. It is not therefore of the same intensity, though it may contain the same approximate electrical mass.
quotes.translation: 电性宇宙的组成部分取自所有能量，其方式可描述为一场宇宙杂耍。因为这能量永不停歇，且不言而喻，没有哪个电性脉冲在实相的一瞬间到下一瞬间是稳定的。也就是说，它从来不是相同或同一的脉冲。它不是同一的。因此，它也不具有相同的强度，尽管它可能包含大致相同的电性质量。

quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: For example, Mr. Foxs dream meetings were quite valid. This is what happens in such a case. Those who agree on such a meeting within dream reality must have certain abilities developed. They must be capable of taking waking consciousness into the dream state. They must be able to manipulate within it. Each of them therefore constructs, you see, the dream location at which the have agreed to meet, a point not thought of by Ruburt's Mr. Fox.
quotes.translation: 例如，福克斯先生的梦境会面是相当有效的。这就是在这种情况下发生的事情。那些同意在梦境实相中进行此类会面的人必须发展出某些能力。他们必须能够将清醒意识带入梦境状态。他们必须能够在其中操控。因此，你看，他们每个人都构建了约定会面的梦境地点，这是鲁柏的福克斯先生未曾想到的一点。

quotes.text: The clues which he does not look for would lead toward not mere dry generalized facts, but facts that are at the basis of the universe as he knows it. Facts indeed that make such a universe possible, facts that would revolutionize science, and most of all the science of medicine, and the field of psychology. The study of so-called extrasensory perception is now considered an isolated bizarre domain, unrelated to other fields of knowledge. The reason for this is that the data that would connect such a study to all other fields of interest is not recognized. The study of the inner workings of the self is closely connected to the study of the universe as it exists in all its levels of reality.
quotes.translation: 他不去寻找的线索，不仅会导向枯燥的一般性事实，而且会导向他所知的宇宙基础的事实。事实上，这些事实使这样的宇宙成为可能，这些事实将彻底改变科学，尤其是医学科学和心理学领域。对所谓超感官知觉的研究，如今被视为一个孤立的怪异领域，与其他知识领域无关。其原因是，能够将此类研究与所有其他兴趣领域联系起来的数据尚未被识别。对自我内在运作的研究，与对其所有实相层面存在的宇宙的研究密切相关。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: By recognizing these differences and honestly following the feelings through -- in other words, by riding the emotions -- you will be led to the beliefs behind them. A series of self-revelations will inevitably result, each leading you to further creative psychological activity. At each stage you will be closer to the reality of your experience than you have ever been.
quotes.translation: 通过识别这些差异并诚实地跟随感受——换句话说，通过驾驭情绪——你将被引向它们背后的信念。一系列自我启示将不可避免地发生，每一个都引导你进入进一步的创造性心理活动。在每个阶段，你都将比以往任何时候都更接近你经验的实相。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: In terms of probabilities, therefore, you choose certain acts, unconsciously transform these into physical events or objects, and then perceive them. But those unchosen events also go out from you and are projected into these other forms. Now the behavior of atoms and molecules is involved here, for again these are only present within your universe during certain stages. Their activity is perceived only during the range of particular vibratory rhythms. When your scientists examine them for example, they do not examine the nature, say, of an atom. They only explore the characteristics of an atom as it acts or shows itself within your system. Its greater reality completely escapes them.
quotes.translation: 因此，就可能性而言，你选择某些行为，无意识地将它们转化为物理事件或物体，然后感知它们。但那些未被选择的事件也离开你，并被投射到这些其他形式中。现在，原子和分子的行为也参与其中，因为它们再次只在你宇宙的特定阶段存在。它们的活动只在特定振动韵律的范围内被感知。例如，当你的科学家检查它们时，他们并不检查，比如说，一个原子的本质。他们只探索原子在你系统内运作或显现时的特性。它更宏大的实相完全逃过了他们的注意。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Your physicists know that time does not exist, basically, as a series of moments, one following the other. Therefore my earlier remark that physical reality was not dependent upon time as a series of moments should be obvious.
quotes.translation: 你们的物理学家知道，时间本质上并非由一连串前后相继的片刻所构成。因此我先前说物质实相并不依赖作为片刻序列的时间，这应该是显而易见的。

quotes.text: Consciousness, by its nature, continually expands. The nature of consciousness as you understand it as a species will, in one way or another, lead you beyond your limited ideas of reality, for your experience will set challenges that cannot be solved within your current framework. Those problems set by one level of consciousness will automatically cause breakthroughs into other areas of conscious activity, where solutions can be found.
quotes.translation: 意识就其本质而言，会持续扩展。你们作为人类物种所理解的意识本质，终将以某种方式引导你们超越对实相的有限认知，因为你们的经验将设置无法在现有框架内解决的挑战。某一意识层次所设定的问题，会自动引发突破进入其他意识活动领域，在那里可以找到解决方案。

It is not a property that belongs to physical matter, although it does appear in physical terms. （Pause.） The second and third projection forms have electromagnetic realities. （Pause.） We are going slow here to get this material through clearly.
quotes.translation: 电磁实相属于内在自体。它并非物质属性的特质，尽管它确实以物质形式显现。（停顿）第二和第三投射形态具有电磁实相。（停顿）我们在此放慢速度，以确保这些资料清晰传达。

quotes.text: Other concepts are really not basically workable even in your own physical reality. A rigid, dogmatic concept of good and evil will force you to perceive physical existence as a battleground of opposing forces, with the poor unwary soul almost as a buffer. Or you will think of the poor soul as a blackboard eraser, slapped between two hands -- one good and one evil.
quotes.translation: 其他概念即便在你们自身的物质实相中，本质上也无法真正运作。僵化教条的善恶观念，会迫使你们将物质存在视为对立力量的战场，而可怜的无知灵魂几乎成了缓冲带。或者你们会认为这可怜的灵魂如同黑板擦，被夹在善恶两掌之间拍打。

quotes.text: Now: You are, using an analogy again, sent out by a superself who strongly desired existence in physical form. You are no puppet of this superself. You will follow your own lines of development, and through means far too difficult to explain here, you add to the experience of the superself and also then extend the nature of its reality. You also insure your own development, and you are able to draw upon the abilities of the superself.
quotes.translation: 现在：再用一个比喻来说，你们是被一个强烈渴望以物质形态存在的超我派遣出来的。你们并非这个超我的傀儡。你们将遵循自身的发展路线，通过此处难以详述的复杂方式，既为超我增添经验，又扩展其实相的本质。你们同时确保自身的发展，并能汲取超我的能力。

quotes.text: The giant image of myself, never clearly glimpsed by Ruburt, represents my own greater reality. In a particular fashion, that identity cannot be fully expressed within the confines of any one form, any more than yours can. Period. Ruburt saw many miniature versions of me. In his inner vision these appeared as identical, simply so that he would identify them as portions of myself. They are actually quite different, one from the others.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏从未清晰瞥见的我的巨大形象，代表着我更宏大的实相。以特定方式而言，那个本体无法在任何单一形态的局限中完全展现，正如你们也无法做到。句号。鲁柏看见许多我的微缩版本。在他的内在视像中，这些版本看似完全相同，这只是为了让他辨识出它们是我的一部分。实际上它们彼此截然不同。

We will conclude our discussion concerning the nature of action, in so far as action is considered by itself. We will always be concerned with a discussion of action, even though other subjects are under consideration however, since it is impossible to divide action into various parts even for the purpose of discussion.
quotes.translation: 我们也将探讨人类人格在多重实相层面中的架构。我们将结束关于行动本质的讨论——就行动本身而言。尽管其他主题也在考量之中，但我们始终会涉及对行动的讨论，因为即使为了讨论之便，也无法将行动分割成不同部分。

quotes.text: You make your reality from your expectations, and this is one of the greatest truths. I can tell you no better. You must deal with the realities that you have made, or change them. There are no alternatives. I cannot make you believe anything, and I would not if I could. This is not my purpose.
quotes.translation: 你从自己的期望中创造你的实相，这是最伟大的真理之一。我无法告诉你更好的道理。你必须处理你所创造的实相，否则就改变它们。没有其他选择。我无法让你相信任何事，即使我能也不会这么做。这不是我的目的。

quotes.text: Now this is a mental camera we are using. There is a knack about being a good dream photographer, and you must learn how to operate the camera. In physical life, for example, a photographer knows that many conditions affect the picture he takes. Exterior situations then are important: You might get a very poor picture on a dark day, for instance. With our dream camera, however, the conditions themselves are mental. If you are in a dark mood, for example, then your picture of inner reality might be dim, poorly outlined, or foreboding. This would not necessarily mean that the dream itself had tragic overtones, simply that it was taken in the "poor light" of the psyche's mood.
quotes.translation: 现在我们使用的是心灵相机。成为优秀梦境摄影师有其诀窍，你必须学会如何操作相机。例如在物质生活中，摄影师知道许多条件会影响他拍摄的照片。外在情境很重要：比如在阴天你可能拍到很差的照片。然而使用我们的梦境相机时，条件本身是心灵性的。例如，如果你处于阴郁心境，那么你对内在实相的影像可能就会模糊、轮廓不清或充满不祥之兆。这未必意味着梦境本身带有悲剧色彩，只是它是在心灵情绪的"弱光"下拍摄的。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now there are some who resist the new existence, even though they chose it, as long as possible. To some extent they must be present at birth, but they can still escape any full identification with the born infant. They hover within and about the form, but half reluctantly. There are many reasons for such behavior. Some personalities simply prefer in-between-life existence and are much more concerned with the theoretical solving of problems than the practical application necessarily involved. Others have discovered that physical existence does not meet their needs as well as they thought it would, and they will progress much better in other fields of reality and existence.
quotes.translation: 有些人会尽可能抗拒新的存在，即使这是他们自己选择的。他们必须在某种程度上参与出生过程，但仍能逃避与新生婴儿的完全认同。他们徘徊在形体之内与周围，却半心半意。这种行为有许多原因。有些人格单纯更喜欢中间状态的存在，更关心问题的理论解决而非必然涉及的实际应用。另一些则发现物质存在并未如预期般满足他们的需求，他们将在其他实相与存在的领域中进展得更好。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The Speakers help you in the formation of dreams which are indeed multidimensional artistic productions of a kind -- dreams existing in more than one reality, with effects that dissect various stages of consciousness that are real, in your terms, to both the living and the dead and in which both the living and the dead may participate. It is for this reason that inspirations and revelations are so often a part of the dream condition.
quotes.translation: 传讯者协助你形成梦境，这些梦境确实是某种多维度的艺术创作——存在于多重实相中的梦境，其效应剖析了各种意识阶段，用你们的说法，这些阶段对生者与死者都是真实的，且生者与死者都可参与其中。正因如此，灵感与启示才如此频繁地成为梦境状态的一部分。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Again, the term God is used simply because it is an accepted term for the reality we are discussing. That which knows itself, which experiences itself within many forms and yet knows itself as something apart from the total of its sums, that left-over, unexplainable remnant you see, can be thought of as original action, original consciousness, prime mover, or consciousness as distinct from its own creations, of which it is also part.
quotes.translation: 再次强调，使用“神”这个术语，仅仅因为它是一个被普遍接受的术语，用以指代我们正在讨论的实相。那自知自身、在众多形式中体验自身、却又知晓自身有别于其总和之物——你瞧，那剩余、无法解释的残余——可被视为原初行动、原初意识、原动力，或意识有别于其自身创造物（它也是其中一部分）的状态。
