quotes.text: The so-called war of the sexes originates in the artificial divisions that you have placed about the nature of the self. The psyche's reality is beyond such misunderstandings. Its native language usually escapes you. It is closely connected with what can be loosely called the language of love.
quotes.translation: 所谓的两性战争，源于你们围绕自我本质所设立的人为划分。心灵的实相超越了此类误解。它的本然语言通常不为你们所理解。它与一种可粗略称为“爱的语言”紧密相连。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: (7:20.) It is impossible in our time scheme to intellectually know our own potentials without trying them out, without testing them against the world's edges. We must activate our impulses and desires, try out our abilities, seek out our strengths by joyfully advancing into the given world of physical energy, physical time and space. In the development of each individual we act and reenact the startling events that brought our own universe into existence. The universe was not created in some dim past, but is newly recreated by our own thoughts, dreams, and desires--so that reality happens at all possible levels at once. And in that living endeavor we each play our part.
quotes.translation: （7:20）在我们的时间架构中，若不亲身尝试、不将自身潜能与世界的边界碰撞，便无法从理智上认知它们。我们必须激发自身的冲动与欲望，尝试自身的能力，通过欢欣地进入既定的物质能量、物质时间与物质空间的世界，来探寻自身的力量。在每个人的发展过程中，我们都在重演并再次行动，那些曾将我们自己的宇宙带入存在的惊人事件。宇宙并非在某个遥远的过去被创造，而是被我们自己的思想、梦境和欲望重新创造——因此，实相在所有可能的层面上同时发生。在那活生生的努力中，我们每个人都扮演着自己的角色。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: --FROM SESSION 929, NOVEMBER 26, 1980
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 6: Genetic Heritage and Reincarnational Predilections

quotes.text: Even the physical planet, having vanished, basically would continue to exist. Those responsible for such a destruction would have destroyed only reality as they knew it, in the probable system. Other probable earths and other probable races of mankind coexist, and you are apart of these also. You would be dispensing with an experiment you were not able to handle.
quotes.translation: 即便是物质星球，即便消失，也基本上会继续存在。那些对此类毁灭负责的人，在可能系统中，只会毁灭他们所知的实相。其他可能的地球和其他可能的人类种族共存，你们也是其中的一部分。你们将放弃一个自己无法驾驭的实验。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Now personality, even as you know it, constantly changes, and not always in ways that are anticipated -- most often, in fact, in unpredictable ways. You insist upon focusing your attention upon the similarities that are woven through your own behavior; and upon these you build a theory that the self follows a pattern that you, instead, have transposed upon it. And the transposed pattern prevents you from seeing the self as it really is. Therefore, you also project this distorted viewpoint upon your conception of the reality of the soul. You think of the soul, therefore, in the light of erroneous conceptions that you hold regarding even the nature of your mortal selves.
quotes.translation: 人格，即便如你们所知，也在不断变化，而且并非总是以可预期的方式——事实上，最常见的是以不可预测的方式。你们坚持将注意力集中在贯穿自身行为的相似性上；并以此为基础构建一种理论，认为自我遵循着某种模式——而实际上，是你们将这种模式强加于它。这种强加的模式阻止你们看到自我的真实面貌。因此，你们也将这种扭曲的观点投射到对灵魂实相的概念上。因此，你们对灵魂的看法，是建立在你们对自身凡俗自我本质的错误观念之上的。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Sometime before the emergence of the Hebrew god these tendencies were apparent. In many ancient, now-forgotten tribal religions, recourse was also made to the gods to turn nature against the enemy. Before this time, however, man felt a part of nature, not separated from it. It was regarded as an extension of his being, as he felt an extension of its reality. One cannot use oneself as a weapon against oneself in those terms. (Pause.)
quotes.translation: 在希伯来神出现之前的某个时期，这些倾向就已显而易见。在许多古老、如今已被遗忘的部落宗教中，人们也求助于神灵，让自然之力转而对付敌人。然而，在此之前，人类感觉自己是大自然的一部分，而非与之分离。大自然被视为其存在的延伸，正如他感觉自己是其实相的延伸。一个人无法以那些术语将自己作为武器来对付自己。（停顿）
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: To pursue certain goals, you pretended that they did not exist. Now, however, your global situation as a race requires the new acquisition of some "ancient arts." These can help you become aware again of those inner idealizations that form your private reality and your mass world. They can permit you to become acquainted with other inward orders of events, and the rich bed of probabilities from which your physical existence emerges.
quotes.translation: 为了追求某些目标，你们假装它们并不存在。然而现在，作为种族，你们的全球处境要求重新习得某些"古老技艺"。这些技艺能帮助你们再次意识到那些构成个人实相与群体世界的内在理想化形态，让你们得以认识其他内在事件序列，以及孕育物质存在的丰饶概率之床。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 698
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Such tales are myths. They do indeed have power and strength. In those terms they represent the darker side of myths, however -- yet through their casts you presently view your world. You will interpret the private events of your lives, and the spectacular range of history, in the light of those assumptions about reality. They not only color your experience, but you create those events that more or less conform to those assumptions.
quotes.translation: 这类故事属于神话。它们确实具有力量与强度。但就这些术语而言，它们代表了神话的阴暗面——然而你们正是透过其框架来审视当前的世界。你们会依据这些关于实相的假设，解读个人生活事件与恢弘的历史全景。这些假设不仅为经验着色，更使你们创造出大致符合这些假设的事件。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 821, FEBRUARY 20, 1978
9:30 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Some are entirely legitimate. Often, however, the suggestion involved in a dream brings about the event, so it seems when the dream becomes real that you have looked into a future that already existed. Instead you have formed the event, not realizing that it had its origins at the time you slept. The question cannot be answered simply for there are many ramifications, but from this instant of reality you form and change not only the future, but the past. In the operation of probabilities this has great significance, for this means that you change and affect all events, and that your books are a delightful fiction that tell you only your current ideas about the past.
quotes.translation: 有些梦境完全合理。但通常，梦中蕴含的暗示会促成事件发生，因此当梦境成真时，仿佛你们窥见了已然存在的未来。实则你们塑造了事件，却未察觉其起源就在睡眠时刻。这个问题无法简单回答，因其涉及诸多分支，但在这个实相瞬间里，你们不仅形成并改变未来，也在重塑过去。在概率运作中这具有重大意义——这意味着你们改变并影响所有事件，而你们的历史书籍不过是反映当前对过往认知的愉悦虚构。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now your own physical image is the materialization of your idea of yourself within the properties of matter. Without the idea of yourself, your physical image would not be; yet often it is all you are aware of. The initial power and energy of that idea of yourself keeps your image alive. Ideas, then, are far more important than you realize. If you will try to accept the idea that your own existence is multidimensional, that you dwell within the medium of infinite probabilities, then you may catch a slight glimpse of the reality that is behind the word "god," and you may understand why it is almost impossible to capture a true understanding of that concept in words.
quotes.translation: 此刻你们的物质形象，是你们在物质属性范围内对自我概念的具现化。没有自我概念，物质形象便不复存在；然而这形象往往成为你们唯一觉察之物。自我概念的原初力量与能量维系着形象的存续。因此，观念远比你们认知的重要。若你们尝试接受自身存在具有多维性、栖居于无限概率媒介中的概念，或许能瞥见"神"字背后的实相，并理解为何几乎无法用言语捕捉该概念的真正内涵。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It is only because, particularly in your times, you have trained yourselves to limit the nature of your own consciousnesses that such ideas seem strange. You have thus far believed that you must train your great imaginations and your intelligences to confine themselves and their activities to the physical world as you have been told it exists. In childhood, before you so leashed your imaginations, however, you each had your own dreams--dreams that awakened you to other portions of your own identities. There are many experiences open to you now--if you can be free enough to allow them--that will give you glimpses of those other intervals in which you have a reality.
quotes.translation: 尤其在你们这个时代，只因你们训练自己限制意识本质，这类观念才显得怪异。你们迄今相信必须约束伟大的想象力与智慧，使其活动局限于被告知的物质世界。然而在童年时期，在你们尚未束缚想象力之前，每个人都拥有自己的梦境——那些唤醒你们对自身其他面向认知的梦。现在仍有诸多经验向你们敞开——只要你们足够自由去接纳它们——这些经验将让你们瞥见自身具有实相的那些其他间隙。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You try to maintain a constant, relatively permanent physical and subjective self in order to maintain a relatively constant, relatively permanent environment. So you are always in a position of ignoring such changes. Those that you refuse to acknowledge are precisely those that would give you a much better understanding of the true nature of reality, individual subjectivity, and the physical environment that seems to surround you.
quotes.translation: 你们试图维持恒定、相对持久的物质与主观自我，以维系相对恒定、相对持久的环境。因此你们始终处于忽视这类变化的状态。那些被你们拒绝承认的变化，恰恰能赋予你们对实相本质、个体主观性及看似环绕的物质环境更深刻的理解。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: As I mentioned earlier, you will not become more spiritual by denying your flesh. (See Chapter Seven.) This is the life you are living! Trust the living that flows through you. By doing so, other realities will make themselves known. They will add dimension and depth to your present reality.
quotes.translation: 如我先前所说，你不会因否定肉身而变得更灵性。（见第七章。）这就是你正在活的人生！信任流经你的生命。如此，其他实相自会显现。它们将为你当前的实相增添维度与深度。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Realities merge, one into the other. Personalities, or any type of individualized energy, may pass through various realities. The appearance of energy in one form could be said to end in that form were it not for the existence of the spacious present, in which all realities are simultaneous.
quotes.translation: 实相彼此融合，一个进入另一个。人格或任何类型的个体化能量，可能穿越各种实相。若非广袤现在的存在——其中所有实相都是同时性的——能量以一种形式显现，可以说在该形式中终结。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Each reader, being presently ensconced within a physical form, I presume (humorously), knows only a small portion of himself -- as I mentioned earlier. The entity is the overall identity of which his personality is one manifestation -- an independent and eternally valid portion. In these communications, therefore, Ruburt's consciousness expands, and yet focuses in a different dimension, a dimension between his reality and mine, a field relatively free of distraction. Here I impress certain concepts upon him, with his permission and assent. They are not neutral, in that all knowledge or information bears the stamp of the personality who holds it or passes it on.
quotes.translation: 我假设（幽默地）每位读者目前都安处于肉身形态中，只认识自己的一小部分——如我先前所说。存有是整体身份，其人格是其中一个显现——一个独立且永恒有效的部分。在这些沟通中，因此，鲁柏的意识扩展，却聚焦于另一个维度，一个介于他实相与我实相之间的维度，一个相对不受干扰的场域。在此，我经他允许与同意，将某些概念印刻于他。它们并非中立，因为所有知识或信息都带有持有或传递它的人格的印记。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It is very difficult to try to assign anything like human motivation to All That Is. I can only say that it is possessed by "the need" to lovingly create from its own being; to lovingly transform its own reality in such a way that each most slight probable consciousness can come to be (long pause); and with the need to see that any and all possible orchestrations of consciousness have the chance to emerge, to perceive and to love.
quotes.translation: 试图将类似人类的动机赋予一切万有极为困难。我只能说，它被“需要”所拥有——需要从其自身存在中慈爱地创造；需要以如此方式慈爱地转化其自身实相，使每一个最微小的可能意识都能得以存在（长停顿）；并且需要确保所有可能意识的一切编排都有机会浮现、感知与爱。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Say for example that our individual "A" wanted to transmit this thought to "B". The thought is as much a reality as the landscape. It is as much a part of individual "A" as the landscape is part of the physical earth. Our imaginary artist could not rip the landscape out of the earth, or bring it to his studio. He could not create an identical landscape because he did not have at his command the perspectives or materials necessary.
quotes.translation: 举例来说，假设我们个体的“甲”想将此思想传递给“乙”。思想与风景一样是实相。它如同风景是物质地球的一部分那样，是个体“甲”的一部分。我们虚构的艺术家无法将风景从大地中撕扯出来，或将其带到画室。他无法创造完全相同的风景，因为他没有掌握必要的视角或材料。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: For to the whole self all personalities that compose it exist simultaneously, and personalities that would appear to you as future personalities are experienced by the whole self in the same dimension as it experiences personalities that you would call past personalities. For all your ideas of time are illusion, not merely philosophical illusions, but delusions as far as any basic reality is concerned.
quotes.translation: 因为对整体自己而言，构成它的所有人格同时存在；那些在你看来是未来人格的人格，被整体自己在同一维度中体验，如同它体验你称为过去人格的人格。因为你们所有的时间观念都是幻相，不仅是哲学上的幻相，就任何基本实相而言，更是错觉。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Matter has little effect within the electrical system, but the effects of the electrical system upon physical matter are great. Now. Thoughts, human thought, has an electric reality; a human thought exists as an independent electrical action, and as such it continues to exist within the electrical system long after it has left its point of origin. This is another important piece of information that will also be referred to later.
quotes.translation: 物质在电性系统内影响甚微，但电性系统对物质实相的影响却极为显著。须知，思想——人类的思想——具有电性实相；人类思想作为独立的电性活动存在，在离开其发源点后仍会在电性系统内长期存续。这是另一项重要信息，日后亦将再次提及。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: As you create physical matter constantly without knowing that you do so, so also you create constantly a dream universe, and this dream universe is as individual as your environment in the physical world. There is also a chemical reaction here, for without dreaming the physical organism could not exist. With your outer senses, you perceive but the camouflage physical reality which the physical senses are equipped to perceive. In the dream universe you are however free, and familiar, with both space and time in a manner which is denied you in the waking state. Where indeed are your dream locations? Where in space is the street upon which you walk in a dream?
quotes.translation: 正如你们在不知不觉中持续创造物质实相，你们同样持续创造着梦境宇宙，这个梦境宇宙与物质世界的环境一样具有个体性。此处亦存在化学反应，因为没有梦境，物质有机体便无法存续。凭借外在感官，你们仅能感知物质感官所配备感知的伪装物质实相。然而在梦境宇宙中，你们却得以自由且熟悉地运用空间与时间——这种运用方式在清醒状态是被禁止的。你们梦境中的地点究竟何在？梦中行走的街道又存在于空间的何处？
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The outer senses perceive only certain given distinctions within an open, infinite system, and these distinctions therefore become the apparent boundaries of the system. A closed system is, in other words, the result of the limitations of the outward senses, whose nature it is to distinguish as a meaningful reality only one portion of an open infinite system.
quotes.translation: 外在感官仅能感知开放无限系统内的特定区分，这些区分因此成为系统的表面边界。换言之，封闭系统实为外在感官局限性的产物——其本质是从开放无限系统中仅将某一部分辨识为有意义的实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: In formal hypnosis you make an agreement with the hypnotist: For a while you will accept his ideas about reality instead of your own. If he tells you that there is a pink elephant in front of you, then you will see it and believe it is there, and act according to the suggestions given. If you are a good subject and your hypnotist a good practitioner, then blisters can arise on your skin if he tells you that you have been burned.
quotes.translation: 在正式催眠中，你与催眠师达成协议：暂时接受他对实相的看法而非你自己的。若他告知你面前有只粉红大象，你便会看见并相信其存在，且依暗示行动。若你是优秀受试者而催眠师技艺精湛，当他告知你曾被灼伤时，你皮肤上甚至会出现水泡。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: In those terms, the great religions of your civilizations rise from myths that change their character through the centuries, even as mountain ranges rise and fall. You can see mountain ranges. It would be ridiculous to ignore their reality. You see your myths somewhat less directly, yet they are apparent within all of your activities, and they form the inner structures of all of your civilizations with their multitudinous parts.
quotes.translation: 以此观之，你们文明中的伟大宗教皆源于神话——这些神话历经数世纪改变其特质，正如山脉的隆起与沉陷。你们能看见山脉，若忽视其实相便显得荒谬。你们对神话的观察虽不那么直接，但它们却显现在所有活动中，构成所有文明及其繁复组成部分的内在结构。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: eth's statement at the end of dictation for the 815th session, "I will try to begin work on our book in a more predictable fashion," reflected his good intent, but things didn't turn out that way. Jane and I let the holiday season intervene to some extent, and Session 816, which came through the day after Christmas, didn't concern book work at all. Then, starting in early January, we held eight sessions having to do with questions we'd put off dealing with for a long time; some were personal and some were not. We stayed with our Monday-Saturday evening session routine, though, and kept busy with all of our other projects.1
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Any creative work involves you in a cooperative process in which you learn to dip into these other streams of consciousness, and come up with a perception that has far more dimensions than one arising from the one narrow, usual stream of consciousness that you know. Great creativity is then multidimensional for this reason. Its origin is not from one reality, but from many, and it is tinged with the multiplicity of that origin.
quotes.translation: 任何创造性工作都使你参与合作过程：学习潜入这些其他意识之流，浮现出比源自单一狭窄惯常意识之流更具维度的感知。伟大创造力因此具有多维性——其源头并非来自单一实相，而是来自多重实相，并沾染着那源头的多重性。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now: Your beliefs and intents cause you to pick, from an unpredictable group of actions, those that you want to happen. You experience those events. (To me:) "Your" desire to live straddled the death of the child in an operation. The child's desire to die chose that event. People are as free as atoms are. Give us a moment ... In no way could you predict what would happen to the child in that photograph of yourself.6 In no way now can you "predict" what will happen to you now. You can choose to accept as your reality any number of given unpredictable events. In that respect, the choice is yours, but all the events you do not accept occur nevertheless.
quotes.translation: 现在：你的信念与意图会促使你从一组不可预测的行动中，挑选出那些你希望发生的事件。你体验这些事件。（对我而言：）“你”求生的欲望跨越了那个孩子在手术中的死亡。而那个孩子求死的欲望则选择了那个事件。人和原子一样自由。请稍等片刻……你绝无可能预测那张你童年照片中的孩子会遭遇什么。如今你也绝无可能“预测”此刻将发生在你身上的事。你可以选择接受任何数量的特定不可预测事件作为你的实相。就这点而言，选择权在你手中，但所有你不接受的事件依然会发生。

quotes.text: (11:33.) The ability to dream presupposes the existence of experience that is not defined as physical fact. It presupposes a far greater freedom in which perception is not dependent upon space or time, a reality in which objects appear or are dismissed with equal ease, a subjective framework in which the individual freely expresses what he or she will in the most direct of fashions, yet without physical contact in usual terms.
quotes.translation: （11:33）做梦的能力预设了不被定义为物理事实的经验之存在。它预设了一种远为更大的自由——其中感知不依赖空间或时间，一种物体能同样轻易地出现或消失的实相，一种主观架构——个体在其中以最直接的方式自由表达其意愿，却无需通常意义上的物理接触。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Despite all appearances, conditions of an exterior nature do not cause wars, or poverty, or disease, or any of the unfortunate circumstances apparent in the world. Your beliefs form your reality. Your thoughts generate practical experience. When these change, conditions will change. To add your own energy, focus, and concentration to dire circumstances in other portions of the world does not help, but adds to, such situations.
quotes.translation: 尽管表象如此，外在环境条件并不会导致战争、贫穷、疾病或世上任何明显的不幸境况。你的信念形成你的实相。你的思想生成实际经验。当这些改变时，境况就会改变。将你的能量、专注与聚焦加诸世界其他地区的悲惨境况，非但无益，反而助长了这类情形。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The very idea of protection, however, as you know, implies a threat--so if you believe in threats you had better have protection. It was not necessary for Ruburt to see the form again--merely to sense the reality of that powerful energy, and realize that it worked on his behalf. In a fashion the form also represented the innocent and powerful inner self, or spontaneous self, or naturally magical self--the terms are synonymous.
quotes.translation: 然而，正如你所知，保护这个概念本身就暗示着威胁——所以如果你相信威胁的存在，最好还是要有保护。鲁柏无需再次看到那个形象——只需感知那股强大能量的实相，并意识到它在为他运作。在某种意义上，那个形象也代表了纯真而强大的内在自我，或自发性自我，或自然神奇的自我——这些术语是同义词。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: You are truly multidimensional personalities, as I have said before. At some point in your development you will become more and more aware of the true nature of your identity. There is, for example, a part of you who is very aware of the pulsations you have just been discussing, and who is aware of the pulse-like nature of memory. When the pulse is in this physical reality, then you, as you know yourselves, have memory of this existence. When the pulse is in another dimension, there is memory of that existence. Now a portion of your entire identity has memory of both. The entire personality structure dwells in many dimensions, and simultaneously.
quotes.translation: 正如我之前说过的，你们确实是多维度的存有。在你们发展的某个阶段，你们会越来越意识到自己身份的真实本质。例如，你有一部分非常清楚你们刚才讨论的那些脉动，也清楚记忆那类似脉冲的本质。当脉冲处于这个物质实相时，你们所认知的自己便拥有对这一存在的记忆。当脉冲处于另一个维度时，便拥有对那个存在的记忆。而你们整体身份的一部分同时拥有这两者的记忆。整个人格结构栖居于许多维度之中，而且是同时进行的。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: "In these side pockets, memory, so-called, is not so structured. Its ever-present living elements are apparent; and its growth. Its material is ever-fresh. Here the past still happens. Usually we experience it through neurological connections; that's when it seems vivid or alive, but actually it's that way all the time. Past motion and acts still go on, not recurring -- it's hard to explain -- but those past actions are still exploring other probabilities, while our nervous structure focuses us in the one (physical) probable reality we've chosen. To us those other actions seem terminated ... but that's only because usually we can't follow them.
quotes.translation: “在这些侧袋中，所谓的记忆并非如此结构化。其永远鲜活的元素显而易见；其成长也显而易见。其素材永远新鲜。在这里，过去仍在发生。通常我们通过神经连接来体验它；那时它显得生动或鲜活，但实际上它一直如此。过去的运动与行为仍在继续，并非重演——这很难解释——但那些过去的行为仍在探索其他可能性，而我们的神经结构将我们聚焦于我们选择的那一个（物质）可能实相。对我们而言，那些其他行为似乎已经终止……但那只是因为我们通常无法追随它们。”
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The emergence of consciousness into those physical conditions automatically alters them -- a fact not recognized by astrologers. Each child born alters the entire universe,7 and changes the world of its time and birth by bringing into it action not there earlier, in your terms, and by impressing the universe with the stamp -- the indelible stamp -- of its reality. Each child chooses its own probable version of any given birthdate. Such dates are obviously not just points in time, pinpointed in space. In the first place, since all time is simultaneous, you are always dying and being born, and your later experience affects the time of your birth.
quotes.translation: 意识进入那些物质条件时，会自动改变它们——这是占星家未能认识到的事实。每个出生的孩子都会改变整个宇宙，并以你们所谓之前不存在的行动，以及用其现实那不可磨灭的印记烙印在宇宙上，从而改变其出生时代和世界。每个孩子都会选择任何特定出生日期的可能版本。这样的日期显然不仅仅是空间中的时间点。首先，由于所有时间都是同时存在的，你始终在死亡和出生，而你后来的经验会影响你出生的时间。

quotes.text: This book had no chapters [in order] to further disrupt your accepted notions of what a book should be. There are different kinds of organizations present, however, and in any given section of the book, several levels of consciousness are appealed to at once. (Intently:) The threads of the work are interwoven so that various portions of your consciousness are sent out, so to speak, on separate journeys of thought and imagination. Yet these side trips are also related. They intertwine, not only through the psychic organization that I have given to "Unknown" Reality, but because of the great uniting nature within the consciousness of each reader.
quotes.translation: 这本书没有章节，是为了进一步打破你对书籍应有样子的固有观念。然而，书中存在不同的组织方式，在任何特定部分，都会同时唤起多个意识层次。（专注地：）作品的线索交织在一起，可以说，你意识的各个部分被派往思想和想象的不同旅程。然而，这些旁支旅程也是相互关联的。它们交织在一起，不仅通过我赋予《未知的实相》的心灵组织，还因为每个读者意识中伟大的统一本质。

quotes.text: Emotions also are electrically coded, and also have an independent electrical reality. On a physical level they are both chemical and electrical. The validity and strength of emotions cannot be overestimated, for they represent in only slightly tinged form the uncamouflaged vitality of the universe as it passes through the inner self. Emotions represent, therefore, this vitality before it has been constructed into camouflage.
quotes.translation: 情绪也被电性编码，并具有独立的电性现实。在物质层面上，它们既是化学性的也是电性的。情绪的有效性和强度不可低估，因为它们以略微染色的形式代表了宇宙未经伪装的生命力，当它通过内在自我时。因此，情绪代表了这种生命力在被构建成伪装之前的形态。

quotes.text: This is a voluntary arrangement. The boy needs the additional vitality. I do not believe the boy will live to old age. The father loaned this vitality and helps the boy, knowing beforehand the boy's difficulty. The boy is unable to relate fully to physical reality. When the man called your father dies his energy will return to the self who is waiting. That self will then call back the energy which it has loaned to Ruth's son.
quotes.translation: 这是一种自愿的安排。男孩需要额外的生命力。我不认为这个男孩会活到老年。父亲借出了这种生命力并帮助男孩，事先知道男孩的困难。男孩无法完全与物质现实建立联系。当你称为父亲的人去世时，他的能量将回到等待的自我那里。那个自我随后会召回它借给露丝儿子的能量。

quotes.translation: 这些联系甚至未被怀疑，仅仅因为你们的科学家没有认真考虑物质现实是任何此类相互关联的结果。然而，事实是神经系统的全部功能尚不为人所知，因为正确的问题尚未被提出。

quotes.text: Your thinking mind, as you consider it, is the top of your mountain. In certain terms you can see "more" than your cells can, though they are also conscious of their realities. Were it not for their lives you would not be at the top of your psychological mountain. Even the trees at the highest tip of the hillside send sturdy roots into the ground, and receive from it nourishment and vitality -- and there is a great give-and-take between the smallest sapling in the foothills and the most ancient pine. No single blade of grass dies but that it affects the entire mountain. The energy within the grass sinks into the earth, and in your terms is again reborn. Trees, rocks, and grass constantly exchange places as energy changes form (very forcefully, leaning forward, eyes wide and dark).
quotes.translation: 你所以为的思维心智，是你山峰的顶端。在某些方面，你能比你的细胞“看到更多”，尽管它们也意识到自己的现实。若非它们的存在，你就不会站在你心理山峰的顶端。即使是山坡最高处的树木，也将强壮的根扎入大地，从中汲取养分和生命力——山麓最小的树苗与最古老的松树之间存在着巨大的给予与索取。没有一片草叶死去而不影响整座山。草叶内的能量沉入大地，在你们看来，它再次重生。树木、岩石和草叶不断交换位置，因为能量改变形态（非常有力地，身体前倾，眼睛又大又黑）。

quotes.text: Again, I do not mean to deny the validity of that experience, but to point out its specialized nature. By its nature, however, that precise specialization and tuning of consciousness in to space and time largely precludes other less-specialized encounters with realities. Dreams often present you with what seems to be an ambiguity, an opaqueness, since they lack the immediate impact of psychological activity with space and time. From your viewpoint it seems often that dreams are not events, or that they happen but do not happen. The lack of normal time and space intersections means that you cannot share your dreams with others in the way that you can share waking events. Nor can you remember dream events -- or so it seems -- as you do your normal conscious experience. In actual fact you remember consciously only certain highlighted events of your lives, and ordinary details of your days vanish as dreams seem to.
quotes.translation: 我并非要否定那种体验的有效性，而是要指出其特殊性。意识对时空的精确调谐与专注，本质上在很大程度上排除了与其他现实形态进行非专门化接触的可能性。梦境常呈现看似模糊不清的特质，因其缺乏心理活动在时空中的直接冲击力。从你的视角看，梦境往往不像事件，或似发生又似未发生。缺乏正常时空交会点意味着你无法像分享清醒事件那样与他人分享梦境。你也无法像记住日常意识经验那样记住梦境事件——至少表面如此。事实上，你清醒时只记得生命中某些突出事件，日常琐碎细节如同梦境般消逝。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Physically then you perceive an approximation of an inner event. The inner event basically is not physical. (Pause.) Now, as simply as I can put this, thoughts and emotions form, of their own electromagnetic reality, vitalized physical products called atoms and molecules. This is basically what I told you in our early analogy with the mazes and the wires.
quotes.translation: 因此你从物质层面感知到内在事件的近似形态。内在事件本质上并非物质性的。（停顿）现在，让我尽可能简单地说明：思想和情感凭借其自身的电磁实相，形成了被称为原子与分子的活化物质产物。这本质上就是我早期用迷宫与电线所做的类比。

quotes.text: Unfortunately, the fundamentalists accept literal interpretations of intuitive realities in such a way that they further narrow the channels through which their psychic abilities can flow. The fundamental framework, in this period of time, for all of its fervor, is not rich -- as for example Christianity was in the past, with its numerous saints. It is instead a fanatical Puritan vein, peculiarly American in character, and restrictive rather than expansive, for the bursts of emotion are highly structured -- that is, the emotions are limited in most areas of life, permitted only an explosive religious expression under certain conditions, when they are not so much spontaneously expressed as suddenly released from the dam of usual repression.
quotes.translation: 不幸的是，原教旨主义者以如此方式对直觉实相进行字面解读，反而进一步收窄了其心灵能力流通的渠道。在这个时代，原教旨主义框架纵使充满狂热，却缺乏丰富性——例如基督教过去拥有众多圣徒时那般。它反而呈现为狂热的清教徒脉络，具有独特的美国特质，是限制性而非扩展性的，因为情感爆发被高度结构化——也就是说，情感在生活大多数领域受到限制，只允许在特定条件下以爆炸性的宗教表达形式出现，这种表达与其说是自发流露，不如说是从日常压抑的堤坝中突然释放。

An animal knows unconsciously that it is unique and has a place in the scheme of being. Its sense of grace is built-in. Your free will allows for the freedom of any belief, including one that says you are unworthy, with no right to your existence.
quotes.translation: （11:21）然而，活在此刻的个体也将在这些决策中扮演角色。再次强调，由于你们是具自我意识的存在，你们的信念调控着实相。动物无意识地知晓自身独特性，并在存在体系中占有位置。其优雅感是与生俱来的。你们的自由意志允许任何信念的自由，包括那些声称你毫无价值、无权存在的信念。

quotes.text: （The death connection enters in because the bill was filled out by the worker at the lumberyard who obtained and cut the Masonite so it would fit into our station wagon. The worker--whose name we do not know, but could easily learn--became quite talkative when he learned I planned to use the Masonite as support for paintings. He described to us in some detail how he had a portrait of himself drawn during the Second World War, when he was overseas. War...death. The conversation was unusual in that the worker explained how the artist drew his face as though it was symmetrical, whereas in reality it is quite asymmetrical, with an impaired eye.
quotes.translation: （死亡关联之所以出现，是因为那张账单由木材场的工人填写——他为我们获取并切割了梅森奈特纤维板，使其能放入旅行车。这位不知名（但易查知）的工人得知我计划用纤维板作画板时变得健谈。他详细描述二战期间在海外时如何请人绘制肖像。战争...死亡。这段对话的特殊之处在于，工人解释画家将他的脸画得对称，而实际上他的脸相当不对称，还有一只眼睛有缺陷。

We have indeed spoken much concerning the focus of energy, and a sufferer is truly entranced with the idea of his illness, and it is only this which basically allows the illness to continue. He focuses upon it both consciously and unconsciously.
quotes.translation: 现在确实是时候探讨所谓"暗示"的真实本质与实相了。我们确实已多次讨论能量聚焦，而患者确实沉迷于自身疾病的观念，正是这种沉迷从根本上使疾病得以持续。他有意识与无意识地都在聚焦于此。

quotes.text: Even the duration of an event or object in space or time is determined by the intensity of the thoughts or emotions that gave it birth. Duration in space is not the same as duration in time, however, though it may seem that this is the case. I am speaking in your terms now. An event or object that exists briefly in space may have a much greater duration in time. It may have far greater importance and intensity, existing in your memory, for example, long after it has disappeared in space. Such an event or object does not merely exist symbolically within your mind or memory -- but in your terms its actual reality continues as a time event.
quotes.translation: 事件或物体在空间或时间中的存续时长，甚至取决于赋予其生命的思维或情感的强度。然而，空间中的存续时长与时间中的存续时长并不相同，尽管表面上看似如此。我现在用你们的说法来解释。一个在空间中短暂存在的事件或物体，可能在时间中拥有更长的存续时长。它可能具有远为重要的强度和意义，例如，在它从空间消失很久之后，仍存在于你们的记忆中。这样的事件或物体并不仅仅象征性地存在于你们的心智或记忆中——而是用你们的说法，它作为时间事件的实际实相仍在延续。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Even if a direct solution does not appear, rejuvenation will of itself begin to point you in the proper direction. If you are a woman in an unhappy marriage, for example, you may begin by imagining yourself with a fine suitor. Now: No Sir Galahad may appear, but if the exercise is pursued properly you will automatically begin to feel loved, and therefore worthy of love, and lovable, where before you felt rejected, unworthy and inferior. This feeling of being loved will alter your reality, drawing love to you. You will act loved. Your spouse may then find you exhibiting characteristics of a most pleasant nature, and he himself may change.
quotes.translation: 即使直接解决方案没有出现，焕发活力本身就会开始将你引向正确的方向。例如，如果你是一位身处不幸婚姻中的女性，你可以从想象自己有一位优秀追求者开始。注意：可能不会有加拉哈德爵士出现，但如果正确进行这个练习，你会自动开始感受到被爱，因而觉得自己值得被爱、可爱，而在此之前你感到被拒绝、不值得和卑微。这种被爱的感受会改变你的实相，将爱吸引到你身边。你会表现出被爱的行为。你的配偶随后可能会发现你展现出最令人愉悦的特质，他自己也可能因此改变。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: I do not mean to idealize him either, or others of his kind, but to point out that you can use your imaginations and intellects in other fashions than you do. In fact, such fashions are not only genetically possible, but genetically probable--a matter I will discuss later in the book. The imagination, of course, deals with the implied universe, those vast areas of reality that are not physically manifest, while reason usually deals with the evidence of the world that is before it. That statement is generally true, but specifically, of course, any act of the imagination involves reasoning, and any [act] of reason involves the imagination.
quotes.translation: 我并非要理想化他或其他同类人，而是要指出，你们可以用不同于现在的方式运用想象力和智力。事实上，这样的方式不仅在基因上是可能的，而且在基因上是或然的——这一点我将在本书后面讨论。当然，想象力处理的是隐含的宇宙，那些未以物质形式显现的广阔实相领域，而理性通常处理的是眼前世界的证据。这个说法大体正确，但具体而言，任何想象行为都涉及推理，任何推理行为也都涉及想象。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The material is delivered to the body, as I have said, from the inner world by means of these inner senses. This inner reality data is received by the mind. This is extremely important. The mind, being un-camouflaged, is the receiving station for the data brought to it by the inner senses. What you almost have here is an inner nervous and communication system closely resembling the outer systems with which you are familiar.
quotes.translation: 如我所说，物质通过内在感官从内在世界传递给身体。这种内在实相数据被心智接收。这一点极其重要。心智，由于未经伪装，是接收内在感官所传递数据的接收站。你们这里几乎拥有一个内在神经系统和通讯系统，与你们熟悉的外在系统非常相似。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: You are bombarded with such "alien intrusions" constantly. The focus of your consciousness blots these out while you are in the normal waking state. There are falling stars everywhere tumbling through the heavens, for example, though you only see some of these in the night sky. It is important during the day that a screening process be used, so that the precision of your actions can be maintained. Again, however, that fine precision rests upon an endless amount of information that impinges onto other levels of your psychological reality. Those data then become the raw material, so to speak, from which your physical events are formed.
quotes.translation: 你们不断受到此类"异类入侵"的冲击。在正常清醒状态下，意识的焦点将这些屏蔽。例如，天空中到处都有流星坠落，尽管你们只在夜空中看到其中一些。在白天使用筛选过程很重要，这样你们行动的精确性才能得以维持。然而，这种精确性又依赖于源源不断的信息，这些信息冲击着你们心理实相的其他层面。可以说，这些数据随后成为形成你们物质事件的原始材料。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The knowledge of probabilities4 brings forth present time and reality. Voices speak through the genes and chromosomes that connect the future and the past in a balance that you call the present form. The history of the private psyche and the mass experience of the species, again, resides in each individual. The archaeology of the past and the future alike is alive within the layers of consciousness that compose your being.
quotes.translation: 对或然性的认知带来了当下时间和实相。声音通过基因和染色体说话，将未来与过去连接在你们称为当下形态的平衡中。私密心灵的历史和物种的集体经验，同样存在于每个个体中。过去与未来的考古学都活生生地存在于构成你们存有的意识层次中。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 727
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: The chair is being fully constructed simultaneously and instantaneously. Now take the blade of grass, and the seed. Energy and the consciousness within continually constructs itself into completely new constructions; because of the various speeds I have spoken of, and because you do not perceive the full reality, you do not notice the simultaneous constructions, and think them continuous, rather than separate and ever new.
quotes.translation: 椅子正在被同时且瞬间地完整建构。现在来看这片草叶与种子。能量与内在意识持续将自身建构为全新的结构；由于我提及的各种速度，且因你未能感知完整的实相，你便察觉不到这些同时性的建构，反而认为它们是连续的，而非独立且常新的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: In some personalities they will lead to what you call a mystic experience. They will carry the personality into new realms of perception. They will momentarily break up usual patterned organizations of perception. You will see with new inner eyes. To some extent you will view some aspects of reality apart from the usual physical structures that you impose upon it as a whole. There is therefore a freedom for the inner self.
quotes.translation: 在某些人格中，这些将导向你们所谓的神秘体验。它们会将人格带入全新的感知领域。它们会暂时打破惯常的感知组织模式。你将用新的内在之眼观看。在某种程度上，你将看到实相的某些面向，脱离了你通常强加于其上的整体物理结构。因此，内在自体获得了自由。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Cats in the wild were, in those terms of time, exploring one kind of nature. In that kind of nature, with a natural population taken care of in the environment, there would be far fewer cats than there are now. Your cats would not exist. Why does it seem antinatural, even slightly perverse, for a household cat to, say, prefer fine cat food from a can, when it seems that he should be eating mice, perhaps, or dining upon grasshoppers? The household cat is exploring a different kind of nature, in which he has a certain relationship to human consciousness, a relationship that changes the reality of his particular kind of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 以那些时间术语而言，野猫曾探索着一种自然。在那种自然中，若环境能照料自然种群，猫的数量会比现在少得多。你们的家猫根本不会存在。当家猫似乎本应吃老鼠或蝗虫，却偏爱罐头精制猫粮时，为何这显得反自然甚至有些反常？家猫正在探索另一种自然，在此自然中，它们与人类意识存在特定关系——这种关系改变了其特定意识种类的实相。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: And from Session 613 for September 11, 1972: "Interactions with others do occur, of course, yet there are none that you do not accept or draw to you by your thoughts, attitudes, or emotions. This applies in each area of life. In your terms, it applies both before life and after it. In the most miraculous fashion you are given the gift of creating your experience."
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 6: Genetic Heritage and Reincarnational Predilections

quotes.text: (A one-minute pause at 11:21.) Learned behavior therefore alters not only present and future but also past conduct. Your power as a rational consciousness focused in the present provides you with opportunities for creativity that you are but vaguely learning to understand. As you do learn, you will automatically begin to appreciate the multidimensional nature of not only your own species but of others as well. The moment as you think of it, then, is the creative framework through which you, the nonphysical self, constantly form corporeal reality; and through that window into earthly existence you form both its future and its past.
quotes.translation: （11:21停顿一分钟）因此，习得行为不仅改变现在与未来，也改变过去行为。你作为聚焦于当下的理性意识所拥有的力量，为你提供了你仅模糊开始理解的创造力机遇。当你真正学习时，你将自动开始欣赏不仅是你自己物种，也包括其他物种的多维本质。那么，你所认为的"当下时刻"，正是你这位非肉身自体持续形成物质实相的创造性框架；通过这扇通往尘世存在的窗口，你同时形成了它的未来与过去。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: In your terms, while you live, and in the most pertinent terms of intimate sensation, your reality must be what you perceive in the framework of your time, and what you create within that framework as it is experienced. Therefore, I entreat you not to behave as if man will destroy himself in some future -- not to behave as if man is an imbecile, doomed to extinction, a dimwitted, half-crazy animal with a brain gone amuck.
quotes.translation: 以你们的说法，在你活着时，以最切身的亲密感受而言，你的实相必然是你于时间框架内所感知之物，以及你在体验该框架时于其中所创造之物。因此，我恳请你不要表现得仿佛人类将在未来自我毁灭——不要表现得仿佛人类是注定灭绝的蠢货，是头脑错乱的半疯动物。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It is not quite as simple a matter as just deciding what events you want to materialize as reality, since you have, in your terms, a body of probabilities of one kind or another already established as the raw materials for the coming year. It would be quite improbable for you, Joseph (as Seth calls me), to suddenly turn into a tailor, for example, for none of your choices with probabilities have led toward such an action.
quotes.translation: 这并非简单到只需决定你想让哪些事件物质化为实相，因为以你们的说法，你已拥有某种概率体作为来年的原材料。例如，约瑟（赛斯对我的称呼）突然变成裁缝就极不可能，因为你与概率相关的选择从未导向此类行动。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You cannot examine reality without examining yourself, in other words. You cannot hold encounters with All That Is apart from yourself, and you cannot separate yourself from your experience. (Pause.) You cannot use "truth." It cannot be manipulated. Whoever thinks he is manipulating truth is manipulating himself. You are truth. Then discover yourself.
quotes.translation: 你无法在不审视自己的情况下审视实相，换言之。你无法脱离自身与一切万有相遇，也无法将自身与经验分离。（停顿。）你不能“利用”真理。真理无法被操纵。任何自认为在操纵真理的人，其实是在操纵自己。你就是真理。那么，去发现自己吧。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In art, you manage sometimes to put into a framework of space something which usually has no existence in space. The crucifixion has no existence in space. It has no existence basically in time, in that it did not occur to any particular person, per se, at any particular time per se. Nevertheless it is a reality on your plane, and it exists within it.
quotes.translation: 在艺术中，你有时能将通常不存在于空间中的事物纳入空间的框架。十字架受难并不存在于空间中。它基本上也不存在于时间中，因为它并未发生在任何特定的人身上，也未发生在任何特定的时间本身。然而，它在你的层面上是一种实相，并存在于其中。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: There are alterations taken into your calculations, so astronauts know ahead of time that they can expect to encounter weightlessness, for example. Your ideas and experiences with space and matter, however, are determined by your own sense apparatus. What is matter to you might be "empty space" for beings equipped in an entirely different fashion. Your conscious mind as you understand it is the "psychological structure" that deals with conditions on a physical basis. Sense data are served up, so to speak, more or less already packaged. The greater inner reality of the psyche, however, is as extensive as outer space seems to be.
quotes.translation: 你的计算中已纳入各种变化，因此宇航员事先知道他们会遇到失重现象，例如。然而，你对空间和物质的观念与经验，是由你自己的感官装置决定的。对你而言是物质的东西，对装备完全不同的存在来说可能是“空无空间”。你所理解的意识心，是处理物质基础上条件的“心理结构”。感官资料可以说是或多或少已经包装好才呈现出来。然而，心灵的内在实相更为广阔，正如外在空间看似的那样。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The inner senses are equipped to perceive data that is not physical. They are not deceived by the images that you project in three-dimensional reality. Now, they can perceive physical objects. Your physical senses are extensions of these inner methods of perception, and after death it is upon these that you will rely. They are used in out-of-body experiences. They operate constantly beneath normal waking consciousness...
quotes.translation: 内在感官能够感知非物质的资料。它们不会被你在三维实相中投射的影像所欺骗。它们也能感知物质物体。你的肉体感官是这些内在感知方法的延伸，死后你将依赖这些内在感官。它们在出体经验中被使用。它们在正常的清醒意识之下持续运作……
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Various portions of the self therefore face physical reality at any given time as the ego. The process is constant. An attempt to maintain the status quo is of course natural on the part of the ego, but when this becomes a stubborn effort to maintain dominancy then the difficulties arise. The self throws up those portions of itself that it considers most able to handle the changing physical environment at any given time.
quotes.translation: 因此，自我的不同部分在任何特定时刻以自我的身份面对物质实相。这个过程是持续的。自我方面维持现状的努力当然是自然的，但当这变成顽固地试图维持主导地位时，困难便会出现。自我会抛出它认为在任何特定时刻最能处理变化中的物质环境的那部分自己。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: All manner of insects, birds and beasts cooperate in this venture, producing the natural environment. This is as normal and inevitable as the fact that your breath causes a mist to form on glass if you breathe upon it. All consciousness creates the world, rising out of feeling-tone. It is a natural product of what your consciousness is. Feelings and emotions emerge into reality in certain specific ways. Thoughts appear, growing on the bed already laid. The seasons spring up, formed by ancient feeling-tones, having deep and abiding rhythms. They are the result, again, of innate creative aspects that are a portion of all life.
quotes.translation: 各种昆虫、鸟类和野兽都参与这项冒险，共同产生自然环境。这就像你对着玻璃呼气会在上面形成雾气一样正常且不可避免。所有意识都从感受基调中升起，创造世界。这是你意识本质的自然产物。感受和情绪以特定方式浮现为实相。思想出现，在已铺好的床上生长。季节涌现，由古老的感受基调形成，具有深沉而持久的韵律。它们再次是作为所有生命一部分的先天创造面向的结果。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: These brief definitions are very general: A paranoid is afflicted with systematic, logically-reasoned delusions of grandeur or persecution; the personality can be relatively stable otherwise. A schizophrenic suffers from a division between thought processes and emotions. The cause of schizophrenia is unknown, and the victim usually ends up hospitalized because of the severity of symptoms, which can include motor malfunctions, perceptual distortions involving hallucinations and delusions, strange behavior, and a withdrawal from reality. Yet the schizophrenic can also keep the use of his or her primary intellectual capacities.
quotes.translation: 这些简要的定义非常笼统：妄想症患者受系统性的、逻辑推理出的夸大或被害妄想所苦；除此之外，人格可能相对稳定。精神分裂症患者则遭受思维过程与情感之间的分裂。精神分裂症的病因未知，患者通常因症状严重而最终住院，这些症状可能包括运动功能障碍、涉及幻觉和妄想的知觉扭曲、怪异行为以及脱离现实。然而，精神分裂症患者仍能保持其基本智力能力的使用。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: NOTES: SESSION 863
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: This is most difficult to explain, but the capacity for full conscious life is inherent in each portion of the body itself. Otherwise, in fact, its smooth synchronicity would be impossible. The brain has abilities you do not use consciously because your beliefs prevent you from initiating the proper neural habits. Certain portions of the brain seem dominant only because of those neural habits that are adopted in any given civilization or time. But other cultures in your past have experienced reality quite differently as a result of encouraging different neural patterns, and putting experience together through other focuses.
quotes.translation: 这极难解释，但完全有意识的生命能力内在于身体自身的每个部分。事实上，否则其顺畅的同步性将不可能。大脑拥有你并未有意识使用的能力，因为你的信念阻止你启动适当的神经习惯。大脑的某些部分之所以显得主导，仅仅是因为任何特定文明或时代所采用的神经习惯。但在你过去的其他文化中，由于鼓励不同的神经模式并通过其他焦点整合经验，它们对现实的体验截然不同。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Later, Jane suggested that I ask Seth to comment upon the ideas in a session. There would also be a number of other reasons for double dreams, of course. I briefly wrote about them in Volume I of "Unknown" Reality; see the notes for Session 692.)

quotes.text: Now, in one sense, all selves are projections of the whole or that which is. New universes such as your own constantly come into existence. Systems and planes far different develop and in turn form other systems. Infinity has meaning only in subjective and psychic terms. The physical brain cannot contain the idea of infinity. The concept will not fit into three dimensional terms. The whole self, the inner self moves within the concept of infinity as you move within physical reality through space. Infinity contains all that will ever be known, and, of course, all that will ever be known is known within the spacious present .
quotes.translation: 现在，从某种意义上说，所有自我都是整体或“那存在者”的投射。像你们这样的新宇宙不断生成。截然不同的系统和层面发展出来，并反过来形成其他系统。无限只在主观和心灵层面有意义。物理大脑无法容纳无限的概念。这个概念无法适应三维的术语。整体自我、内在自我在无限的概念中移动，就像你在物理现实中通过空间移动一样。无限包含了所有将被知晓的事物，当然，所有将被知晓的事物都在广阔当下中被知晓。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: When women give birth in a crowded world they also know, and with a portion of their conscious minds, that a violation is involved. When your species sees that it is destroying other species and disrupting the natural balance, then it is consciously aware of its violation. When such natural guilt is not faced there are other mechanisms that must be employed. Again at the risk of repeating myself: Many of your problems result from the fact that you do not accept the responsibility of your own consciousness. It is meant to assess the reality that is unconsciously formed in direct replica of your thoughts and expectations.
quotes.translation: 当女性在一个拥挤的世界中生育时，她们也知道，并且用她们有意识心智的一部分，知道这涉及一种侵犯。当你们的物种看到它在毁灭其他物种并破坏自然平衡时，那么它就有意识地意识到自己的侵犯。当这种自然的内疚不被面对时，就必须采用其他机制。再次冒着重复的风险：你们的许多问题源于你们不接受自己意识的责任这一事实。它本应评估那无意识形成的、直接复制你们思想和期望的现实。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Following our analogy, you will be his guest and from his rooms look down into your own with some greater objectivity. This is possible, feasible, since you are all portions, in our analogy, of this same inner self who maintains all of the rooms. While each of you are egotistically focused within your own reality, the deeper layers of the self are aware of the quote "family" relationship. Now give me a moment. (Pause.)
quotes.translation: 按照我们的类比，你将是他的客人，从他的房间向下俯视你自己的房间，带着更大的客观性。这是可能的、可行的，因为在我们的类比中，你们都是同一个维持所有房间的内在自我的部分。当你们每个人以自我为中心聚焦于自己的现实时，自我的更深层意识到所谓的“家族”关系。现在给我一点时间。（停顿。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: They contained within themselves intense and yet blurred talents that were used as energy sources by the children. They came together precisely to give birth to the family, and for no other main reason as far as their joint reality was concerned. They seeded a generation, then.1
quotes.translation: 他们自身包含了强烈而模糊的才能，这些才能被孩子们用作能量来源。他们聚在一起正是为了生育这个家族，就他们的共同现实而言，没有其他主要理由。那么，他们播下了一代人的种子。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

source_raw.title: SESSION 680

quotes.text: Now, in the three-dimensional reality in which your ego has its main focus, becoming presupposes arrival, or a destination -- an ending to that which has been in a state of becoming. But the soul or entity has its existence basically in other dimensions, and in these, fulfillment is not dependent upon arrivals at any points, spiritual or otherwise.
quotes.translation: 现在，在你们自我主要聚焦的三维实相中，"成为"预设了抵达或终点——即处于"成为"状态之事的终结。但灵魂或本体基本上存在于其他维度，在这些维度中，圆满并不依赖于任何精神或其他层面的终点。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: He was determined to find the kind of mate that would best suit him and his own unique characteristics. That intent was in his mind. When that challenge was met he used his will and mobilized all of his power to fulfill his abilities, and to bring about conditions in which he hoped Joseph (as Seth calls me) could also fulfill his. The will, again, operates according to the personality's beliefs about reality, so its desires are sometimes tempered as those beliefs change. In his own way Ruburt always concentrated upon one challenge at a time -- boring in, so to speak, and ignoring anything else that might distract him.
quotes.translation: 他决心找到最适合自己独特特质的伴侣。这个意图始终在他心中。当这个挑战被克服后，他运用意志调动全部力量来实现自身能力，并创造他期望约瑟夫（赛斯对我的称呼）也能实现其能力的环境。意志再次根据人格对实相的信念运作，因此当这些信念改变时，其欲望有时会被调和。鲁柏总是以自己独特的方式一次专注于一个挑战——可以说是深入钻研，忽略任何可能分散注意力的事物。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Now: Man has a far greater leeway. He forms his reality according to his conscious beliefs, even while its basis lies in the deep unconscious nature of the earth in corporeal terms. Man's "I am," [seemingly] apart from nature -- a characteristic necessary for the development of his kind of consciousness -- led him into value judgments, and also necessitated some break with the deep inner certainties of other species.
quotes.translation: 现在：人类拥有远为广阔的自由度。他根据自己有意识的信念塑造实相，即便其基础存在于地球物质层面的深层无意识本质中。人类看似与自然分离的"我是"——这种对其意识发展必要的特性——导致他陷入价值判断，也必然与其他物种深层的内在确定性产生某种断裂。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The brain is a camouflage pattern. It takes up space. It exists in time. The mind takes up no space, it does not have its basic existence in time. The reality of the inner universe does not take up space, nor does it have its basic existence in time. Your camouflage universe, on the other hand, takes up space and has an existence in time, but it is not the real and basic universe, any more than the brain is the mind.
quotes.translation: 大脑是一种伪装模式。它占据空间，存在于时间中。心智不占据空间，其基本存在不在时间中。内在宇宙的实相不占据空间，其基本存在也不在时间中。而你们的伪装宇宙则占据空间并存在于时间中，但它并非真实和基本的宇宙，正如大脑并非心智。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: This does not mean that you do not exist however in the apparent interval. Our discussion concerning the nature of matter is important here, for I explained the pulsations that occur in atoms. Your perception of time causes many difficulties when you try to examine reality as it exists independent of matter. The limitations of verbal communications make some explanations difficult. （See Volumes I and 2, for example.）
quotes.translation: 但这并不意味着你们在明显的间隔中不存在。我们关于物质本质的讨论在此很重要，因为我解释过原子中发生的脉动。当你们试图检视独立于物质存在的实相时，对时间的感知会造成许多困难。语言沟通的局限性使某些解释变得困难。（例如参见第一、二卷。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: Now my relationship with you [and Ruburt] is indeed a strange one, since you do not relate to me as you do to each other. The -- I hope -- delightfully human egotistical characteristics that I show help calm your fears and show you that the self as you think of it continues to exist [after physical death]. I have a reservoir of personality banks upon which I can draw, and as a teacher I use the one that is most effective in any given system of reality; this is the one I use here. It is a portion of myself that is the most closely connected with earthly existence, and it is a self that I liked very well, indeed.
quotes.translation: 现在我与你们（和鲁柏）的关系确实奇特，因为你们不像彼此相处那样与我相处。我所展现的——我希望是——令人愉悦的人性自我特质，能帮助平息你们的恐惧，并展示你们所认为的自我（在肉体死亡后）继续存在。我拥有可调取的人格银行储备，作为教师，我会在特定实相系统中使用最有效的那个；这就是我在此处使用的。这是我与尘世存在最紧密相连的那部分自我，而且确实是我非常喜爱的那个自我。

quotes.text: Many times such constructions are used as inner visual patterns, for example. Visually they often bear a similarity to the inner architecture of the cells, and to planets. Your dream images are biologically structured, then. The experiences behind them bring you in contact with the deepest portions of your nonphysical reality, and it is the unconscious who translates these for you into recognizable images and forms.
quotes.translation: 例如，这类建构常被用作内在视觉模式。它们在视觉上常与细胞的内在结构及行星相似。因此，你的梦境意象具有生物结构。其背后的体验使你接触到非物质实相的最深层部分，而潜意识将这些体验转化为你可辨识的意象与形态。

quotes.text: There is much I have not told you about your city, for you will have to discover it for yourselves. I am merely encouraging you to focus your joint energies in that direction ... You will be dealing with symbols, yet you will learn that symbols are reality, for you are symbols of yourselves that live and speak. You do not think of yourselves as symbols [but] there is no symbol that does not have its individual life.
quotes.translation: 关于你们的城市，我还有许多未告知之事，因为你们必须自行发现。我只是鼓励你们将共同能量聚焦于那个方向……你们将处理象征符号，但终将明白象征即实相——因为你们就是自身活生生、会言说的象征符号。你们不把自己视为象征，但没有任何象征不拥有其个体生命。

quotes.text: You must of course interpret events in a personal manner. You create them. Yet there is also a meeting ground of more or less shared physical encounters, a sense plateau that offers firm-enough footing for the agreement of a mass-shared world. With most mental aberrations, you are dealing with people whose private symbols are so heavily thrust over prime sense data that even those data sometimes become almost invisible. These individuals often use the physical world in the way that most people use the dream world, so that for them it is difficult to distinguish between a private and a publicly-shared reality.
quotes.translation: 你当然必须以个人方式诠释事件，因为是你创造了它们。然而，也存在一个或多或少共享物理遭遇的交汇场域，一个为大众共享世界的共识提供足够坚实立足点的感知高原。面对大多数精神异常者，你是在与那些私人象征如此强烈地覆盖原始感官数据的人打交道，以至于有时连这些数据都近乎不可见。这些人常以多数人使用梦境世界的方式使用物理世界，因此对他们而言，区分私人实相与公众共享实相变得困难。

source_raw.title: hen he originally gave us the 812th session some 16 months ago, Seth told us it would "be part of a chapter later in the book...." [See my opening notes for Session 814.] The 812th session was held following an encounter Jane and I had with an unexpected visitor, although these excerpts aren't personal at all. They fit in well with material in Mass Events, however -- indeed, as soon as Seth confirmed Jane's headings for Part 3 and Chapter 6, we knew that we'd found the place to present this material. We made our decision without consulting Seth, by the way.

quotes.text: Now give us a moment ... One brief note: Ruburt was momentarily upset before the session -- cranky. He thought he did not feel like having a session at 9:30 P.M. to try to solve the world's problems. He just wanted to watch television and forget it all, and hidden in that crankiness is a good point: The sessions are an expression of your private and joint curiosity, a high and excellent curiosity about the nature of reality, a result of your desire to know; to know whether or not the knowledge can be held in your hands like a fruit, whether or not the knowledge can be dosed out to an ailing world as medicine.
quotes.translation: 现在请稍候……简短说明：鲁柏在课前短暂烦躁——闹脾气。他觉得自己不想在晚上9:30上课来解决世界问题，只想看电视忘掉一切。这烦躁中暗藏一个要点：课程正是你们私人及共同好奇心的表达——一种对实相本质崇高而卓越的好奇心，源于你们渴望知晓：知识能否如水果般握在手中？知识能否作为药物施予病态的世界？

quotes.text: In the light of such ideals, surely you seem wanting -- yet your reality is one in which the greatest freedoms have been allowed. This means that you have given yourselves full range so that all probabilities could be explored, and none left out that were physically feasible.
quotes.translation: 以这些理想为参照，你们显然有所欠缺——但你们的实相正是被允许最大自由的实相。这意味着你们已给予自己充分空间，得以探索所有可能性，不遗漏任何物理上可行的路径。

quotes.text: The authors instead tell you to be positive, compassionate, strong, optimistic, filled with joy and enthusiasm, without telling you what to do to get out of the predicament you may be in, and without understanding the vicious circle that may seem to entrap you. Such books, again, while sometimes of value, do not explain how thoughts and emotions cause reality. They do not take into consideration the multidimensional aspects of the self or the fact that ultimately each personality, while following definite general laws, must still find and follow his or her own way of adapting these to personal circumstances.
quotes.translation: 这些作者反而告诉你要积极、慈悲、坚强、乐观、充满喜悦与热忱，却不告诉你如何摆脱可能身陷的困境，也不理解那看似困住你的恶性循环。这类书籍虽有时具有价值，却未解释思想与情感如何创造实相。它们既不考虑自我的多维度面向，也不考虑一个事实：每个个体在遵循明确普遍法则的同时，最终仍需找到并遵循自己将法则应用于个人境遇的独特方式。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: Basically you create your experience through your beliefs about yourself and the nature of reality. Another way to understand this is to realize that you create your experiences through your expectations. Your feeling-tones are your emotional attitudes toward yourself and life in general, and these generally govern the large areas of experience.
quotes.translation: 基本上，你通过对自己和实相本质的信念创造你的经验。另一种理解方式是，你通过你的期望创造你的经验。你的感觉基调是你对自己和生命整体的情感态度，这些通常主导着经验的大范围领域。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: I am using you here and the physician probability as an example. Art, you see, is also closely connected with healing. The projections of which I spoke do happen occasionally and spontaneously on both of your parts. Various aspects of the personality are being developed, you see. Reincarnation is but a part of this probability system, the part that falls within your particular universe. This all involves value fulfillment, which is at its basis. It is obvious then that you perceive consciously only a small part of your own overall reality. The doctor obviously has his own ego, though not within your system.
quotes.translation: 我在这里用你和那位医师的概率作为例子。你看，艺术也与疗愈紧密相连。我提到的那些投射确实偶尔会在你们双方身上自发发生。你看，人格的各个面向正在被发展。转世只是这个概率系统的一部分，是落在你们特定宇宙内的那部分。这一切都涉及价值完成，这是其基础。那么显而易见，你有意识地只感知到你自己整体实相的一小部分。那位医师显然有他自己的自我，尽管不在你们的系统内。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: We will resume dictation ... You will react, therefore, to all the information that you receive according to your conscious beliefs concerning the nature of reality. The deeper portions of the self do not have to take the ego's idea of time into consideration, so these portions of the self also deal with data that would ordinarily escape the ego's perception, perhaps until a certain "point" of ego time was reached.
quotes.translation: 我们将继续口述……因此，你会根据你对实相本质的有意识信念，对你接收到的所有信息做出反应。自我的较深部分不必考虑自我对时间的观念，所以自我的这些部分也处理那些通常会逃脱自我感知的数据，也许直到达到自我时间的某个"点"。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: I have wanted to cover this because I can prove the existence of this dream world to you, and its continuity, always within the spacious present. You must understand psychological reality, psychological time, psychological experience, and the dream existence before you can learn to utilize many abilities, since in all the mentioned aspects, you use your abilities, that is your inner senses, on a subconscious level.
quotes.translation: 我一直想涵盖这个，因为我可以向你证明这个梦境世界的存在及其连续性，始终在广袤的当下之内。你必须理解心理实相、心理时间、心理经验以及梦境存在，然后才能学会运用许多能力，因为在所有提到的这些方面，你都在潜意识层面上运用你的能力，也就是你的内在感官。

quotes.text: To some extent that emotional reality is also expressed at other levels--as your own is--in periods of dreaming, in which animals, like men, participate in a vast cooperative venture that helps to form the psychological atmosphere in which your lives must first of all exist.
quotes.translation: 在某种程度上，那种情感实相也在其他层面被表达——就像你们自己的实相一样——在梦境期间，动物和人类一样，参与一项庞大的合作事业，这有助于形成你们的生命首先必须存在的心理氛围。

source_raw.title: While Jane has been enjoying many periods of ease and relaxation, she's also had bouts of blueness over her general stiffness and walking difficulties [her "symptoms"]. But she's finally dispensed with the last stages of her cold, which had turned out to be much heavier than mine, and is feeling a resurgence of energy for the sessions. Her own work has been going well for the most part, however--she expects to finish Chapter 12 of God of Jane tomorrow. Because of her concentration on that hook she hasn't done much on her book of poetry, If We Live Again, since late February; and as I mentioned in the Preface for Dreams, she laid aside her third Seven novel, Oversoul Seven and the Museum of Time, in May 1979 when she began God of Jane. As for myself, I've progressed to working with Session 860 [for June 13, 1979], which bridges chapters 8 and 9 in Mass Events.

quotes.text: Therefore your manipulations are extremely superficial, as far as their effect on the larger reality is concerned. This is the first time, I believe, that this particular subject has come into our discussions. Yet our fifth-dimensional early session hinted in this direction.
quotes.translation: 因此，就其对更大实相的影响而言，你们的操控是极其肤浅的。我相信，这是这个特定主题首次进入我们的讨论。然而，我们第五维度的早期课程已朝这个方向暗示过。

quotes.text: Your cats are as alive in all ways inside of the house as out. They understand their relationship with your human reality. They enjoy contributing in your life as much as any wild animal enjoys being a part of its group. Their consciousnesses lean in a new direction, feel about the edges of concepts, sense openings of awareness of a different kind, and form alliances of consciousness quite as natural as any other.
quotes.translation: 你的猫在屋内与屋外同样充满生机。它们理解自身与你们人类实相的关系。它们享受参与你们生活的程度，不亚于任何野生动物享受成为群体一员。它们的意识转向新方向，摸索概念的边缘，感知不同种类的觉知开口，并形成与其他意识同样自然的联盟。

This applies in each area of life. In your terms, it applies both before life and after it. In the most miraculous fashion you are given the gift of creating your experience."
